text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses in the wilderness of Sinai, in the tent of meeting, on the first day of the second month, in the second year after they had come out of the land of Egypt, saying:
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses in the desert of Sinai in the tent of meeting,—on the first of the second month in the second year by their coming forth out of the land of Egypt saying:
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses in the wilderness of Sinai, in the tabernacle of the congregation, on the first day of the second month, in the second year after they were come out of the land of Egypt, saying,
text.NABRE: In the second year after the Israelites’ departure from the land of Egypt, on the first day of the second month, the Lord said to Moses at the tent of meeting in the wilderness of Sinai:
text.DRB: And our Lord spake to Moyses in the desert of Sinai in the tabernacle of covenant, the first day of the second month, the second year of their going out of Ægypt, saying:

text.NRSV: Take a census of the whole congregation of Israelites, in their clans, by ancestral houses, according to the number of names, every male individually;
text.Rotherham: Reckon ye up the sum of all the assembly of the sons of Israel, by their families by their ancestral houses,—in the counting of names, every male, by their polls;
text.KJV: Take ye the sum of all the congregation of the children of Israel, after their families, by the house of their fathers, with the number of their names, every male by their polls;
text.NABRE: Take a census of the whole community of the Israelites, by clans and ancestral houses, registering by name each male individually.
text.DRB: Take the sum of the whole assembly of the children of Israel by their kindreds, & houses, and the names of every one, whatsoever of the male sex,

text.NRSV: from twenty years old and upward, everyone in Israel able to go to war. You and Aaron shall enroll them, company by company.
text.Rotherham: from twenty years old and upwards, every one able to go forth to war in Israel,—ye shall number them by their hosts, thou and Aaron;
text.KJV: From twenty years old and upward, all that are able to go forth to war in Israel: thou and Aaron shall number them by their armies.
text.NABRE: You and Aaron shall enroll in companies all the men in Israel of twenty years or more who are fit for military service.
text.DRB: from the twentieth year and upward, of all the strong men of Israel, and you shall number them by their troops, thou and Aaron.

text.NRSV: A man from each tribe shall be with you, each man the head of his ancestral house.
text.Rotherham: and with you, shall be one man for each tribe,—each man, head of his ancestral house, shall be.
text.KJV: And with you there shall be a man of every tribe; every one head of the house of his fathers.
text.NABRE: Moses’ Assistants. With you there shall be a man from each tribe, each the head of his ancestral house.
text.DRB: And there shall be with you the Princes of the tribes, and of the houses in their kindreds,

text.NRSV: These are the names of the men who shall assist you: From Reuben, Elizur son of Shedeur.
text.Rotherham: These, then, are the names of the men who shall stand with you,—Of Reuben, Elizur, son of Shedeur;
text.KJV: And these are the names of the men that shall stand with you: of the tribe of Reuben; Elizur the son of Shedeur.
text.NABRE: These are the names of those who are to assist you: from Reuben: Elizur, son of Shedeur;
text.DRB: whose names are these: Of Ruben, Elizur the son of Sedeur.

text.NRSV: From Simeon, Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai.
text.Rotherham: Of Simeon, Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai;
text.KJV: Of Simeon; Shelumiel the son of Zurishaddai.
text.NABRE: from Simeon: Shelumiel, son of Zurishaddai;
text.DRB: Of Simeon, Salamiel the son of Surisaddai.

text.NRSV: From Judah, Nahshon son of Amminadab.
text.Rotherham: Of Judah, Nahshon, son of Amminadab;
text.KJV: Of Judah; Nahshon the son of Amminadab.
text.NABRE: from Judah: Nahshon, son of Amminadab;
text.DRB: Of Juda, Nahasson, the son of Aminadab.

text.NRSV: From Issachar, Nethanel son of Zuar.
text.Rotherham: Of Issachar, Nethanel, son of Zuar;
text.KJV: Of Issachar; Nethaneel the son of Zuar.
text.NABRE: from Issachar: Nethanel, son of Zuar;
text.DRB: Of Issachar, Nathanael the son of Suar.

text.NRSV: From Zebulun, Eliab son of Helon.
text.Rotherham: Of Zebulun, Eliab, son of Helon;
text.KJV: Of Zebulun; Eliab the son of Helon.
text.NABRE: from Zebulun: Eliab, son of Helon;
text.DRB: Of Zabulon, Eliab the son of Helon.

text.NRSV: From the sons of Joseph: from Ephraim, Elishama son of Ammihud; from Manasseh, Gamaliel son of Pedahzur.
text.Rotherham: Of the sons of Joseph,—Of Ephraim, Elishama son of Ammihud; Of Manasseh, Gamaliel, son of Pedahzur;
text.KJV: Of the children of Joseph: of Ephraim; Elishama the son of Ammihud: of Manasseh; Gamaliel the son of Pedahzur.
text.NABRE: for the descendants of Joseph: from Ephraim: Elishama, son of Ammihud; and from Manasseh: Gamaliel, son of Pedahzur;
text.DRB: And of the sons of Joseph, of Ephraim, Elisama the son of Ammiud. Of Manasses, Gamaliel, the son of Phadassur.

text.NRSV: From Benjamin, Abidan son of Gideoni.
text.Rotherham: Of Benjamin, Abidan son of Gideoni;
text.KJV: Of Benjamin; Abidan the son of Gideoni.
text.NABRE: from Benjamin: Abidan, son of Gideoni;
text.DRB: Of Benjamin, Abidan the son of Gedeon.

text.NRSV: From Dan, Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai.
text.Rotherham: Of Dan, Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai;
text.KJV: Of Dan; Ahiezer the son of Ammishaddai.
text.NABRE: from Dan: Ahiezer, son of Ammishaddai;
text.DRB: Of Dan, Ahiezer the son of Ammisaddai.

text.NRSV: From Asher, Pagiel son of Ochran.
text.Rotherham: Of Asher, Pagiel, son of Ochran;
text.KJV: Of Asher; Pagiel the son of Ocran.
text.NABRE: from Asher: Pagiel, son of Ochran;
text.DRB: Of Aser, Phegiel the son of Ochran.

text.NRSV: From Gad, Eliasaph son of Deuel.
text.Rotherham: Of Gad, Eliasaph son of Deuel;
text.KJV: Of Gad; Eliasaph the son of Deuel.
text.NABRE: from Gad: Eliasaph, son of Reuel;
text.DRB: Of Gad, Eliazaph the son of Duel.

text.NRSV: From Naphtali, Ahira son of Enan.
text.Rotherham: Of Naphtali, Ahira son of Enan.
text.KJV: Of Naphtali; Ahira the son of Enan.
text.NABRE: from Naphtali: Ahira, son of Enan.
text.DRB: Of Nephthali, Ahira the son of Enan.

text.NRSV: These were the ones chosen from the congregation, the leaders of their ancestral tribes, the heads of the divisions of Israel.
text.Rotherham: These, are they who had been summoned by the assembly, being princes of the tribes of their fathers,—heads of the thousands of Israel.
text.KJV: These were the renowned of the congregation, princes of the tribes of their fathers, heads of thousands in Israel.
text.NABRE: These were the elect of the community, leaders of their ancestral tribes, heads of the clans of Israel.
text.DRB: These are the most noble Princes of the multitude by their tribes & kindreds, and the heads of the host of Israel:

text.NRSV: Moses and Aaron took these men who had been designated by name,
text.Rotherham: So then Moses and Aaron took these men, who were distinguished by name;
text.KJV: And Moses and Aaron took these men which are expressed by their names:
text.NABRE: So Moses and Aaron took these men who had been designated by name,
text.DRB: whom Moyses and Aaron took with all the multitude of the common people:

text.NRSV: and on the first day of the second month they assembled the whole congregation together. They registered themselves in their clans, by their ancestral houses, according to the number of names from twenty years old and upward, individually,
text.Rotherham: all the assembly also, called they together on the first of the second month,—and they declared their pedigree according to their families by their ancestral houses,—in the counting of names from twenty years old and upwards by their polls.
text.KJV: And they assembled all the congregation together on the first day of the second month, and they declared their pedigrees after their families, by the house of their fathers, according to the number of the names, from twenty years old and upward, by their polls.
text.NABRE: and assembled the whole community on the first day of the second month. Every man of twenty years or more then registered individually his name and lineage according to clan and ancestral house,
text.DRB: and assembled them the first day of the second month, reckoning them by the kindreds, and houses, and families, and heads, and names of every one from the twentieth year and upward,

text.NRSV: as the Lord commanded Moses. He enrolled them in the wilderness of Sinai.
text.Rotherham: As Yahweh commanded Moses, so he mustered them in the desert of Sinai.
text.KJV: As the Lord commanded Moses, so he numbered them in the wilderness of Sinai.
text.NABRE: as the Lord had commanded Moses. So he enrolled them in the wilderness of Sinai.
text.DRB: as our Lord had commanded Moyses. And they were numbered in the desert of Sinai.

text.NRSV: The descendants of Reuben, Israel's firstborn, their lineages, by their clans, by their ancestral houses, according to the number of names, individually, every male from twenty years old and upward, everyone able to go to war:
text.Rotherham: And they were [as followeth] —The sons of Reuben, the firstborn of Israel, in their pedigrees—by their families, by their ancestral houses, in the counting of names, by their polls, every male, from twenty years old and upwards, every one able to go forth to war;
text.KJV: And the children of Reuben, Israel’s eldest son, by their generations, after their families, by the house of their fathers, according to the number of the names, by their polls, every male from twenty years old and upward, all that were able to go forth to war;
text.NABRE: Count of the Twelve Tribes. Of the descendants of Reuben, the firstborn of Israel, registered individually by name and lineage according to their clans and ancestral houses, every male of twenty years or more, everyone fit for military service:
text.DRB: Of Ruben the first begotten of Israel, by their generations and families and houses, and names of every head, all that is of the male sex, from the twentieth year & upward, of them that go forth to war,

text.NRSV: those enrolled of the tribe of Reuben were forty-six thousand five hundred.
text.Rotherham: such as were numbered of them as belonging to the tribe of Reuben, forty-six thousand and five hundred.
text.KJV: Those that were numbered of them, even of the tribe of Reuben, were forty and six thousand and five hundred.
text.NABRE: those enrolled from the tribe of Reuben were forty-six thousand five hundred.
text.DRB: forty six thousand five hundred.

text.NRSV: The descendants of Simeon, their lineages, by their clans, by their ancestral houses, those of them that were numbered, according to the number of names, individually, every male from twenty years old and upward, everyone able to go to war:
text.Rotherham: Of the sons of Simeon, in their pedigrees by their families by their ancestral houses,—such as were numbered of him, in the counting of names by their polls, every male from twenty years old and upwards, every one able to go forth to war;
text.KJV: Of the children of Simeon, by their generations, after their families, by the house of their fathers, those that were numbered of them, according to the number of the names, by their polls, every male from twenty years old and upward, all that were able to go forth to war;
text.NABRE: Of the descendants of Simeon, registered individually by name and lineage according to their clans and ancestral houses, every male of twenty years or more, everyone fit for military service:
text.DRB: Of the sons of Simeon by the generations, and families, and houses of their kindreds, were reckoned by the names & heads of every one, all that is of the male sex from the twentieth year and upward, of them that go forth to war,

text.NRSV: those enrolled of the tribe of Simeon were fifty-nine thousand three hundred.
text.Rotherham: such as were numbered of them as belonging to the tribe of Simeon, fifty-nine thousand and three hundred.
text.KJV: Those that were numbered of them, even of the tribe of Simeon, were fifty and nine thousand and three hundred.
text.NABRE: those enrolled from the tribe of Simeon were fifty-nine thousand three hundred.
text.DRB: fifty nine thousand three hundred.

text.NRSV: The descendants of Gad, their lineages, by their clans, by their ancestral houses, according to the number of the names, from twenty years old and upward, everyone able to go to war:
text.Rotherham: Of the sons of Gad, in their pedigrees—by their families by their ancestral houses, in the counting of names from twenty years old and upwards, every one able to go forth to war:
text.KJV: Of the children of Gad, by their generations, after their families, by the house of their fathers, according to the number of the names, from twenty years old and upward, all that were able to go forth to war;
text.NABRE: Of the descendants of Gad, registered by name and lineage according to their clans and ancestral houses, every male of twenty years or more, everyone fit for military service:
text.DRB: Of the sons of Gad, by the generations and families and houses of their kindreds were reckoned by the names of every one from twenty years and upward, all that went forth to war,

text.NRSV: those enrolled of the tribe of Gad were forty-five thousand six hundred fifty.
text.Rotherham: such as were numbered of them as belonging to the tribe of Gad, forty-five thousand and six hundred and fifty.
text.KJV: Those that were numbered of them, even of the tribe of Gad, were forty and five thousand six hundred and fifty.
text.NABRE: those enrolled from the tribe of Gad were forty-five thousand six hundred and fifty.
text.DRB: forty five thousand six hundred fifty.

text.Rotherham: Of the sons of Judah, in their pedigrees by their families by their ancestral houses,—in the counting of names, from twenty years old and upwards, every one able to go forth to war;
text.KJV: Of the children of Judah, by their generations, after their families, by the house of their fathers, according to the number of the names, from twenty years old and upward, all that were able to go forth to war;
text.NABRE: Of the descendants of Judah, registered by name and lineage according to their clans and ancestral houses, every male of twenty years or more, everyone fit for military service:
text.DRB: Of the sons of Juda by the generations and families and houses of their kindreds, by the names of every one from the twentieth year and upward, all that could go forth to war,

text.Rotherham: such as were numbered of them as belonging to the tribe of Judah, seventy-four thousand and six hundred.

text.NABRE: those enrolled from the tribe of Judah were seventy-four thousand six hundred.

text.DRB: Of the sons of Issachar, by the generations and families and houses of their kindreds, by the names of every one from the twentieth year & upward, all that went forth to war,

text.DRB: Of the sons of Zabulon, by the generations, & families and houses of their kindreds, were reckoned by names of every one from the twentieth year and upward, all that could go forth to war,

text.Rotherham: such as were numbered of them as belonging to the tribe of Zebulun, fifty-seven thousand and four hundred.

text.Rotherham: Of the sons of Joseph—Of the sons of Ephraim, in their pedigrees—by their families by their ancestral houses,—in the counting of names, from twenty years old, and upwards, every one able to go forth to war;
text.KJV: Of the children of Joseph, namely, of the children of Ephraim, by their generations, after their families, by the house of their fathers, according to the number of the names, from twenty years old and upward, all that were able to go forth to war;
text.NABRE: Of the descendants of Joseph: Of the descendants of Ephraim, registered by name and lineage according to their clans and ancestral houses, every male of twenty years or more, everyone fit for military service:
text.DRB: Of the sons of Joseph, namely of the sons of Ephraim by the generations, families and houses of their kindreds were reckoned by the names of every one, from the twentieth year & upward, all that could go forth to war,

text.NRSV: those enrolled of the tribe of Ephraim were forty thousand five hundred.
text.Rotherham: such as were numbered of them, as belonging to the tribe of Ephraim, forty thousand and five hundred.
text.KJV: Those that were numbered of them, even of the tribe of Ephraim, were forty thousand and five hundred.
text.NABRE: those enrolled from the tribe of Ephraim were forty thousand five hundred.
text.DRB: forty thousand five hundred.

text.Rotherham: Of the sons of Manasseh, in their pedigrees—by their families by their ancestral houses,—in the counting of names from twenty years old and upwards, every one able to go forth to war;
text.KJV: Of the children of Manasseh, by their generations, after their families, by the house of their fathers, according to the number of the names, from twenty years old and upward, all that were able to go forth to war;
text.NABRE: Of the descendants of Manasseh, registered by name and lineage according to their clans and ancestral houses, every male of twenty years or more, everyone fit for military service:
text.DRB: Moreover of the sons of Manasses, by the generations and families and houses of their kindreds, were reckoned by the names of every one from twenty years and upward, all that could go forth to war,

text.Rotherham: such as were numbered of them as belonging to the tribe of Manasseh, thirty-two thousand, and two hundred.
text.KJV: Those that were numbered of them, even of the tribe of Manasseh, were thirty and two thousand and two hundred.
text.NABRE: those enrolled from the tribe of Manasseh were thirty-two thousand two hundred.
text.DRB: thirty two thousand two hundred.

text.Rotherham: Of the sons of Benjamin, in their pedigrees by their families, by their ancestral houses,—in the counting of names, from twenty years old, and upwards, every one able to go forth to war;
text.KJV: Of the children of Benjamin, by their generations, after their families, by the house of their fathers, according to the number of the names, from twenty years old and upward, all that were able to go forth to war;
text.NABRE: Of the descendants of Benjamin, registered by name and lineage according to their clans and ancestral houses, every male of twenty years or more, everyone fit for military service:
text.DRB: Of the sons of Benjamin by the generations & families and houses of their kindreds, were reckoned by names of every one from the twentieth year and upward, all that could go forth to war,

text.Rotherham: such as were numbered of them as belonging to the tribe of Benjamin, thirty-five thousand and four hundred.
text.KJV: Those that were numbered of them, even of the tribe of Benjamin, were thirty and five thousand and four hundred.
text.NABRE: those enrolled from the tribe of Benjamin were thirty-five thousand four hundred.
text.DRB: thirty five thousand four hundred.

text.Rotherham: Of the sons of Dan, in their pedigrees by their families by their ancestral houses, in the counting of names from twenty years old and upwards, every one able to go forth to war;
text.KJV: Of the children of Dan, by their generations, after their families, by the house of their fathers, according to the number of the names, from twenty years old and upward, all that were able to go forth to war;
text.NABRE: Of the descendants of Dan, registered by name and lineage according to their clans and ancestral houses, every male of twenty years or more, everyone fit for military service:
text.DRB: Of the sons of Dan, by the generations and families and houses of their kindreds, were reckoned by the names of every one from twenty years and upward, all that could go forth to war,

text.Rotherham: such as were numbered of them as belonging to the tribe of Dan, sixty-two thousand, and seven hundred.
text.KJV: Those that were numbered of them, even of the tribe of Dan, were threescore and two thousand and seven hundred.
text.NABRE: those enrolled from the tribe of Dan were sixty-two thousand seven hundred.
text.DRB: sixty two thousand seven hundred.

text.Rotherham: Of the sons of Asher, in their pedigrees—by their families by their ancestral houses,—in the counting of names, from twenty years old, and upwards, every one able to go forth to war;

text.Rotherham: such as were numbered of them as belonging to the tribe of Asher, forty-one thousand, and five hundred.

text.NABRE: those enrolled from the tribe of Asher were forty-one thousand five hundred.
text.DRB: forty thousand and a thousand five hundred.

text.NRSV: The descendants of Naphtali, their lineages, by their clans, by their ancestral houses, according to the number of names, from twenty years old and upward, everyone able to go to war:
text.Rotherham: The sons of Naphtali, in their pedigrees by their families by their ancestral houses,—in the counting of names, from twenty years old, and upwards, every one able to go forth to war;
text.KJV: Of the children of Naphtali, throughout their generations, after their families, by the house of their fathers, according to the number of the names, from twenty years old and upward, all that were able to go forth to war;
text.NABRE: Of the descendants of Naphtali, registered by name and lineage according to their clans and ancestral houses, every male of twenty years or more, everyone fit for military service:
text.DRB: Of the sons of Nephthali, by the generations, and families, and houses of their kindreds were reckoned by the names of every one from the twentieth year and upward, all that could go forth to war,

text.Rotherham: such as were numbered of them, as belonging to the tribe of Naphtali, fifty-three thousand, and four hundred.
text.KJV: Those that were numbered of them, even of the tribe of Naphtali, were fifty and three thousand and four hundred.
text.NABRE: those enrolled from the tribe of Naphtali were fifty-three thousand four hundred.
text.DRB: fifty three thousand four hundred.

text.NRSV: These are those who were enrolled, whom Moses and Aaron enrolled with the help of the leaders of Israel, twelve men, each representing his ancestral house.
text.Rotherham: These, are they who were numbered, whom Moses and Aaron and the twelve princes of Israel did number, the princes acting each one for his ancestral house.
text.KJV: These are those that were numbered, which Moses and Aaron numbered, and the princes of Israel, being twelve men: each one was for the house of his fathers.
text.NABRE: It was these who were enrolled, each according to his ancestral house, by Moses and Aaron and the twelve leaders of Israel.
text.DRB: These are they, whom Moyses and Aaron numbered, and the twelve Princes of Israel, every one by the houses of their kindreds.

text.NRSV: So the whole number of the Israelites, by their ancestral houses, from twenty years old and upward, everyone able to go to war in Israel—
text.Rotherham: So then all they who were numbered of the sons of Israel by their ancestral houses, from twenty years old and upwards, every one able to go forth to war in Israel;
text.KJV: So were all those that were numbered of the children of Israel, by the house of their fathers, from twenty years old and upward, all that were able to go forth to war in Israel;
text.NABRE: The total enrollment of the Israelites of twenty years or more, according to their ancestral houses, everyone fit for military service in Israel—
text.DRB: And the whole number of the children of Israel by their houses and families, from the twentieth year and upward, that could go to war,

text.NRSV: their whole number was six hundred three thousand five hundred fifty.
text.Rotherham: so then all they who were numbered were six hundred and three thousand and five hundred and fifty.
text.KJV: Even all they that were numbered were six hundred thousand and three thousand and five hundred and fifty.
text.NABRE: the total enrollment was six hundred and three thousand, five hundred and fifty.
text.DRB: were six hundred three thousand men five hundred fifty.

text.NRSV: The Levites, however, were not numbered by their ancestral tribe along with them.
text.Rotherham: But the Levites by the tribe of their fathers were not numbered in their midst.
text.KJV: But the Levites after the tribe of their fathers were not numbered among them.
text.NABRE: Levites Omitted in the Census. Now the Levites were not enrolled by their ancestral tribe with the others.
text.DRB: But the Levites in the tribe of their families were not numbered with them.

text.NRSV: The Lord had said to Moses:
text.Rotherham: For Yahweh spake unto Moses saying:
text.KJV: For the Lord had spoken unto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: For the Lord had told Moses,
text.DRB: And our Lord spake to Moyses, saying:

text.NRSV: Only the tribe of Levi you shall not enroll, and you shall not take a census of them with the other Israelites.
text.Rotherham: Only the tribe of Levi, shalt thou not number, nor the sum of them, shalt thou reckon up, in the midst of the sons of Israel.
text.KJV: Only thou shalt not number the tribe of Levi, neither take the sum of them among the children of Israel:
text.NABRE: The tribe of Levi alone you shall not enroll nor include in the census along with the other Israelites.
text.DRB: Number not the tribe of Levi, neither shalt thou put the sum of them with the children of Israel:

text.NRSV: Rather you shall appoint the Levites over the tabernacle of the covenant, and over all its equipment, and over all that belongs to it; they are to carry the tabernacle and all its equipment, and they shall tend it, and shall camp around the tabernacle.
text.Rotherham: But do, thou thyself, put the Levites in charge over the habitation of the testimony, and over all the furniture thereof and over all that pertaineth thereto, they, shall bear the habitation, and all the furniture thereof, and they, shall attend thereupon; and, round about the habitation, shall they encamp.
text.KJV: But thou shalt appoint the Levites over the tabernacle of testimony, and over all the vessels thereof, and over all things that belong to it: they shall bear the tabernacle, and all the vessels thereof; and they shall minister unto it, and shall encamp round about the tabernacle.
text.NABRE: You are to give the Levites charge of the tabernacle of the covenant with all its equipment and all that belongs to it. It is they who shall carry the tabernacle with all its equipment and who shall be its ministers; and they shall camp all around the tabernacle.
text.DRB: but appoint them over the tabernacle of testimony, and all the vessels thereof, and whatsoever pertaineth to the ceremonies. They shall carry the tabernacle and all the furniture thereof: and they shall be in the ministry, and shall pitch round about the tabernacle.

text.NRSV: When the tabernacle is to set out, the Levites shall take it down; and when the tabernacle is to be pitched, the Levites shall set it up. And any outsider who comes near shall be put to death.
text.Rotherham: And, when the habitation is to set forward, the Levites shall take it down, and when the habitation is to be pitched, the Levites, shall set it up, but the stranger that draweth near, shall be put to death.
text.KJV: And when the tabernacle setteth forward, the Levites shall take it down: and when the tabernacle is to be pitched, the Levites shall set it up: and the stranger that cometh nigh shall be put to death.
text.NABRE: When the tabernacle is to move on, the Levites shall take it down; when the tabernacle is to be pitched, it is the Levites who shall set it up. Any unauthorized person who comes near it shall be put to death.
text.DRB: When you are to go forward, the Levites shall take down the tabernacle: when you are to camp, they shall set it up. What stranger soever cometh to it, he shall be slain.

text.NRSV: The other Israelites shall camp in their respective regimental camps, by companies;
text.Rotherham: So then the sons of Israel shall pitch their tents, every man, near his own camp, and, every man, near his own standard, by their hosts;
text.KJV: And the children of Israel shall pitch their tents, every man by his own camp, and every man by his own standard, throughout their hosts.
text.NABRE: The other Israelites shall camp according to their companies, each in their own divisional camps,
text.DRB: And the children of Israel shall camp every man by his troops and bands and host.

text.NRSV: but the Levites shall camp around the tabernacle of the covenant, so that there may be no wrath on the congregation of the Israelites; and the Levites shall perform the guard duty of the tabernacle of the covenant.
text.Rotherham: but the Levites, shall pitch their tents round about the habitation of the testimony, that there be no wrath upon the assembly of the sons of Israel,—so shall the Levites keep the watch of the habitation of the testimony.
text.KJV: But the Levites shall pitch round about the tabernacle of testimony, that there be no wrath upon the congregation of the children of Israel: and the Levites shall keep the charge of the tabernacle of testimony.
text.NABRE: but the Levites shall camp around the tabernacle of the covenant to ensure that God’s wrath will not fall upon the Israelite community. The Levites shall keep guard over the tabernacle of the covenant.
text.DRB: Moreover the Levites shall pitch their tents round about the tabernacle.

text.NRSV: The Israelites did so; they did just as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: Thus did the sons of Israel,—according to all that Yahweh commanded Moses, so, did they.
text.KJV: And the children of Israel did according to all that the Lord commanded Moses, so did they.
text.NABRE: The Israelites complied; they did just as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.DRB: The children of Israel thereof did according to all things which our Lord had commanded Moyses.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses and Aaron, saying:
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh onto Moses and onto Aaron saying:
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses and unto Aaron, saying,
text.NABRE: The Lord said to Moses and Aaron:
text.DRB: And our Lord spake to Moyses and Aaron saying:

text.NRSV: The Israelites shall camp each in their respective regiments, under signs by their ancestral houses; they shall camp facing the tent of meeting on every side.
text.Rotherham: Every man—near his standard with the ensigns belonging to their ancestral houses, shall the sons of Israel encamp,—at a distance round about the tent of meeting, shall they encamp.
text.KJV: Every man of the children of Israel shall pitch by his own standard, with the ensign of their father’s house: far off about the tabernacle of the congregation shall they pitch.
text.NABRE: The Israelites shall camp, each in their own divisions, under the ensigns of their ancestral houses. They shall camp at some distance all around the tent of meeting.
text.DRB: Every one of the children of Israel by the troops, ensigns, and standards, and houses of their kindreds shall camp round about the tabernacle of covenant.

text.NRSV: Those to camp on the east side toward the sunrise shall be of the standard of the camp of Judah by companies; the leader of the people of Judah being Nahshon son of Amminadab,
text.Rotherham: And they who encamp eastwards towards sunrise, shall be the standard of the camp of Judah by their hosts,—even the prince of the sons of Judah, Nashon, son of Amminadab;
text.KJV: And on the east side toward the rising of the sun shall they of the standard of the camp of Judah pitch throughout their armies: and Nahshon the son of Amminadab shall be captain of the children of Judah.
text.NABRE: Encamped on the east side, toward the sunrise, shall be the divisional camp of Judah, arranged in companies. The leader of the Judahites is Nahshon, son of Amminadab,
text.DRB: On the east Judas shall pitch his tents by the troops of his band: and the Prince of his sons shall be Nahasson the son of Aminadab.

text.NRSV: with a company as enrolled of seventy-four thousand six hundred.
text.Rotherham: and his host, even they who are numbered of them,—seventy-four thousand and six hundred,
text.KJV: And his host, and those that were numbered of them, were threescore and fourteen thousand and six hundred.
text.NABRE: and the enrollment of his company is seventy-four thousand six hundred.
text.DRB: And the whole sum of the warriors of his stock, seventy four thousand six hundred.

text.NRSV: Those to camp next to him shall be the tribe of Issachar, the leader of the Issacharites being Nethanel son of Zuar,
text.Rotherham: Then they who encamp by him the tribe of Issachar,—even the prince of the sons of Issachar, Nethanel, son of Zuar;
text.KJV: And those that do pitch next unto him shall be the tribe of Issachar: and Nethaneel the son of Zuar shall be captain of the children of Issachar.
text.NABRE: Encamped beside it is the tribe of Issachar. The leader of the Issacharites is Nethanel, son of Zuar,
text.DRB: Beside him camped they of the tribe of Issachar, whose Prince was Nathanael the son of Suar:

text.NRSV: with a company as enrolled of fifty-four thousand four hundred.
text.Rotherham: and his host even they who are numbered of them,—fifty-four thousand and four hundred.
text.KJV: And his host, and those that were numbered thereof, were fifty and four thousand and four hundred.
text.NABRE: and the enrollment of his company is fifty-four thousand four hundred.
text.DRB: and all the number of his warriors fifty four thousand four hundred.

text.NRSV: Then the tribe of Zebulun: the leader of the Zebulunites being Eliab son of Helon,
text.Rotherham: The tribe of Zebulun, even the prince of the sons of Zebulun, Eliab, son of Helon;
text.KJV: Then the tribe of Zebulun: and Eliab the son of Helon shall be captain of the children of Zebulun.
text.NABRE: Also the tribe of Zebulun. The leader of the Zebulunites is Eliab, son of Helon,
text.DRB: In the tribe of Zabulon the Prince was Eliab the Son of Helon.

text.NRSV: with a company as enrolled of fifty-seven thousand four hundred.
text.Rotherham: and his host, even they who are numbered of him, fifty-seven thousand and four hundred.
text.KJV: And his host, and those that were numbered thereof, were fifty and seven thousand and four hundred.
text.NABRE: and the enrollment of his company is fifty-seven thousand four hundred.
text.DRB: And all the host of warriors of his stock, fifty seven thousand four hundred.

text.NRSV: The total enrollment of the camp of Judah, by companies, is one hundred eighty-six thousand four hundred. They shall set out first on the march.
text.Rotherham: All they who are num-bered to the camp of Judah. a hundred and eighty-six thousand four hundred, by their hosts, shall first set forward.
text.KJV: All that were numbered in the camp of Judah were an hundred thousand and fourscore thousand and six thousand and four hundred, throughout their armies. These shall first set forth.
text.NABRE: The total enrollment of the camp of Judah by companies is one hundred and eighty-six thousand four hundred. They shall be first on the march.
text.DRB: All that were numbered in the camp of Judas, were an hundred eighty six thousand four hundred: and they by their troops shall march first.

text.NRSV: On the south side shall be the standard of the camp of Reuben by companies; the leader of the Reubenites being Elizur son of Shedeur,
text.Rotherham: The standard of the camp of Reuben southwards, by their hosts,—even the prince of the sons of Reuben, Elizur son of Shedeur;
text.KJV: On the south side shall be the standard of the camp of Reuben according to their armies: and the captain of the children of Reuben shall be Elizur the son of Shedeur.
text.NABRE: The divisional camp of Reuben shall be on the south side, by companies. The leader of the Reubenites is Elizur, son of Shedeur,
text.DRB: In the camp of the sons of Ruben on the south side the Prince shall be Elisur the son of Sedeur:

text.NRSV: with a company as enrolled of forty-six thousand five hundred.
text.Rotherham: and his host, even they who are numbered of him,—forty-six thousand, and five hundred.
text.KJV: And his host, and those that were numbered thereof, were forty and six thousand and five hundred.
text.NABRE: and the enrollment of his company is forty-six thousand five hundred.
text.DRB: and the whole host of his warriors, that were numbered, forty six thousand five hundred.

text.NRSV: And those to camp next to him shall be the tribe of Simeon, the leader of the Simeonites being Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai,
text.Rotherham: Then they who encamp by him, the tribe of Simeon,—even the prince of the sons of Simeon, Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai;
text.KJV: And those which pitch by him shall be the tribe of Simeon: and the captain of the children of Simeon shall be Shelumiel the son of Zurishaddai.
text.NABRE: Encamped beside it is the tribe of Simeon. The leader of the Simeonites is Shelumiel, son of Zurishaddai,
text.DRB: Beside him camped they of the tribe of Simeon: whose Prince was Salamiel the son of Surisaddai,

text.NRSV: with a company as enrolled of fifty-nine thousand three hundred.
text.Rotherham: and his host, even they who are numbered of them,—fifty-nine thousand and three hundred.
text.KJV: And his host, and those that were numbered of them, were fifty and nine thousand and three hundred.
text.NABRE: and the enrollment of his company is fifty-nine thousand three hundred.
text.DRB: and the whole host of his warriors, that were numbered, fifty nine thousand three hundred.

text.NRSV: Then the tribe of Gad: the leader of the Gadites being Eliasaph son of Reuel,
text.Rotherham: Then the tribe of Gad, even the prince of the sons of Gad, Eliasaph son of Reuel;
text.KJV: Then the tribe of Gad: and the captain of the sons of Gad shall be Eliasaph the son of Reuel.
text.NABRE: Next is the tribe of Gad. The leader of the Gadites is Eliasaph, son of Reuel,
text.DRB: In the tribe of Gad, the Prince was Eliasaph the son of Duel,

text.NRSV: with a company as enrolled of forty-five thousand six hundred fifty.
text.Rotherham: and his host, even they who are numbered of them,—forty-five thousand, and six hundred and fifty.
text.KJV: And his host, and those that were numbered of them, were forty and five thousand and six hundred and fifty.
text.NABRE: and the enrollment of his company is forty-five thousand six hundred and fifty.
text.DRB: and the whole host of his warriors, that were numbered, forty five thousand six hundred fifty.

text.NRSV: The total enrollment of the camp of Reuben, by companies, is one hundred fifty-one thousand four hundred fifty. They shall set out second.
text.Rotherham: All they who are num-bered to the camp of Reuben,—a hundred and fifty-one thousand and four hundred and fifty, by their hosts, and they in the second rank, shall set forward.
text.KJV: All that were numbered in the camp of Reuben were an hundred thousand and fifty and one thousand and four hundred and fifty, throughout their armies. And they shall set forth in the second rank.
text.NABRE: The total enrollment of the camp of Reuben by companies is one hundred and fifty-one thousand four hundred and fifty. They shall be second on the march.
text.DRB: All that were reckoned in the camp of Ruben, an hundred fifty thousand and a thousand four hundred fifty by their troops: they shall march in the second place.

text.NRSV: The tent of meeting, with the camp of the Levites, shall set out in the center of the camps; they shall set out just as they camp, each in position, by their regiments.
text.Rotherham: Then shall set forward the tent of meeting—the camp of the Levites in the midst of the camps,—as they encamp, so, shall they set forward, every man at the side thereof by their standards.
text.KJV: Then the tabernacle of the congregation shall set forward with the camp of the Levites in the midst of the camp: as they encamp, so shall they set forward, every man in his place by their standards.
text.NABRE: Then the tent of meeting and the camp of the Levites shall set out in the midst of the divisions. As they camp, so also they will march, each in place, by their divisions.
text.DRB: But the tabernacle of testimony shall be lifted up by the offices of the Levites and their troops. As it shall be set up, so shall it be taken down. Every one shall march by their places, and orders.

text.NRSV: On the west side shall be the standard of the camp of Ephraim by companies; the leader of the people of Ephraim being Elishama son of Ammihud,
text.Rotherham: the standard of the camp of Ephraim, by their hosts westwards,—even the prince of the sons of Ephraim, Elishama son of Ammihud;
text.KJV: On the west side shall be the standard of the camp of Ephraim according to their armies: and the captain of the sons of Ephraim shall be Elishama the son of Ammihud.
text.NABRE: The divisional camp of Ephraim shall be on the west side, by companies. The leader of the Ephraimites is Elishama, son of Ammihud,
text.DRB: On the west side shall be the camp of the sons of Ephraim, whose Prince was Elisama the son of Ammiud.

text.NRSV: with a company as enrolled of forty thousand five hundred.
text.Rotherham: and his host, even they who are numbered of them,—forty thousand, and five hundred.
text.KJV: And his host, and those that were numbered of them, were forty thousand and five hundred.
text.NABRE: and the enrollment of his company is forty thousand five hundred.
text.DRB: The whole host of his warriors, that were numbered, forty thousand five hundred.

text.NRSV: Next to him shall be the tribe of Manasseh, the leader of the people of Manasseh being Gamaliel son of Pedahzur,
text.Rotherham: Then by him, the tribe of Manasseh, even the prince of the sons of Manasseh, Gamaliel, son of Pedahzur;
text.KJV: And by him shall be the tribe of Manasseh: and the captain of the children of Manasseh shall be Gamaliel the son of Pedahzur.
text.NABRE: Beside it shall be the tribe of Manasseh. The leader of the Manassites is Gamaliel, son of Pedahzur,
text.DRB: And with them the tribes of the sons of Manasses, whose Prince was Gamaliel the son of Phadassur,

text.NRSV: with a company as enrolled of thirty-two thousand two hundred.
text.Rotherham: and his host, even they who are numbered of them,—thirty-two thousand and two hundred.
text.KJV: And his host, and those that were numbered of them, were thirty and two thousand and two hundred.
text.NABRE: and the enrollment of his company is thirty-two thousand two hundred.
text.DRB: and the whole host of his warriors, that were numbered, thirty two thousand two hundred.

text.NRSV: Then the tribe of Benjamin: the leader of the Benjaminites being Abidan son of Gideoni,
text.Rotherham: Then the tribe of Benjamin, even the prince of the sons of Benjamin, Abidan, son of Gideoni;
text.KJV: Then the tribe of Benjamin: and the captain of the sons of Benjamin shall be Abidan the son of Gideoni.
text.NABRE: Also the tribe of Benjamin. The leader of the Benjaminites is Abidan, son of Gideoni,
text.DRB: In the tribe of the sons of Benjamin the Prince was Abidan the son of Gedeon,

text.NRSV: with a company as enrolled of thirty-five thousand four hundred.
text.Rotherham: and his host, even they who are numbered of them,—thirty-five thousand and four hundred.
text.KJV: And his host, and those that were numbered of them, were thirty and five thousand and four hundred.
text.NABRE: and the enrollment of his company is thirty-five thousand four hundred.
text.DRB: and the whole host of his warriors, that were reckoned, thirty five thousand four hundred.

text.NRSV: The total enrollment of the camp of Ephraim, by companies, is one hundred eight thousand one hundred. They shall set out third on the march.
text.Rotherham: All they who are numbered to the camp of Ephraim a hundred and eight thousand and one hundred by their hosts, and they in the third rank, shall set forward.
text.KJV: All that were numbered of the camp of Ephraim were an hundred thousand and eight thousand and an hundred, throughout their armies. And they shall go forward in the third rank.
text.NABRE: The total enrollment of the camp of Ephraim by companies is one hundred and eight thousand one hundred. They shall be third on the march.
text.DRB: All that were numbered in the camp of Ephraim, an hundred eight thousand one hundred by their troops: they shall march the third.

text.NRSV: On the north side shall be the standard of the camp of Dan by companies; the leader of the Danites being Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai,
text.Rotherham: the standard of the camp of Dan, north-wards by their hosts, even the prince of the sons of Dan, Ahiezer, son of Ammishaddai:
text.KJV: The standard of the camp of Dan shall be on the north side by their armies: and the captain of the children of Dan shall be Ahiezer the son of Ammishaddai.
text.NABRE: The divisional camp of Dan shall be on the north side, by companies. The leader of the Danites is Ahiezer, son of Ammishaddai,
text.DRB: On the north part camped the sons of Dan: whose Prince was Ahiezar the son of Ammisaddai.

text.NRSV: with a company as enrolled of sixty-two thousand seven hundred.
text.Rotherham: and his host, even they who are numbered of them,—sixty-two thousand and seven hundred.
text.KJV: And his host, and those that were numbered of them, were threescore and two thousand and seven hundred.
text.NABRE: and the enrollment of his company is sixty-two thousand seven hundred.
text.DRB: The whole host of his warriors, that were numbered, sixty two thousand seven hundred.

text.NRSV: Those to camp next to him shall be the tribe of Asher, the leader of the Asherites being Pagiel son of Ochran,
text.Rotherham: Then they who encamp by him the tribe of Asher,—even the prince of the sons of Asher, Pagiel, son of Ochran;
text.KJV: And those that encamp by him shall be the tribe of Asher: and the captain of the children of Asher shall be Pagiel the son of Ocran.
text.NABRE: Encamped beside it shall be the tribe of Asher. The leader of the Asherites is Pagiel, son of Ochran,
text.DRB: Besides him they of the tribe of Aser pitched their tents: whose Prince was Phegiel the son of Ochran:

text.NRSV: with a company as enrolled of forty-one thousand five hundred.
text.Rotherham: and his host, even they who are numbered of them,—forty-one thousand and five hundred.
text.KJV: And his host, and those that were numbered of them, were forty and one thousand and five hundred.
text.NABRE: and the enrollment of his company is forty-one thousand five hundred.
text.DRB: the whole host of his warriors, that were numbered, forty thousand and a thousand five hundred.

text.NRSV: Then the tribe of Naphtali: the leader of the Naphtalites being Ahira son of Enan,
text.Rotherham: Then the tribe of Naphtali,—even the prince of the sons of Naphtali, Ahira, son of Enan;
text.KJV: Then the tribe of Naphtali: and the captain of the children of Naphtali shall be Ahira the son of Enan.
text.NABRE: Also the tribe of Naphtali. The leader of the Naphtalites is Ahira, son of Enan,
text.DRB: Of the tribe of the sons of Nephthali the Prince was Ahira the son of Enan.

text.NRSV: with a company as enrolled of fifty-three thousand four hundred.
text.Rotherham: and his host, even they who are numbered of them,—fifty-three thousand and four hundred,
text.KJV: And his host, and those that were numbered of them, were fifty and three thousand and four hundred.
text.NABRE: and the enrollment of his company is fifty-three thousand four hundred.
text.DRB: The whole host of his warriors, fifty three thousand four hundred.

text.NRSV: The total enrollment of the camp of Dan is one hundred fifty-seven thousand six hundred. They shall set out last, by companies.
text.Rotherham: All they who are numbered to the camp of Dan, a hundred and fifty-seven thousand and six hundred, in the hindmost rank, shall they set forward, by their standards.
text.KJV: All they that were numbered in the camp of Dan were an hundred thousand and fifty and seven thousand and six hundred. They shall go hindmost with their standards.
text.NABRE: The total enrollment of the camp of Dan is one hundred and fifty-seven thousand six hundred. They shall be the last on the march, by divisions.
text.DRB: All that were numbered in the camp of Dan, were an hundred fifty seven thousand six hundred: and they shall march last.

text.NRSV: This was the enrollment of the Israelites by their ancestral houses; the total enrollment in the camps by their companies was six hundred three thousand five hundred fifty.
text.Rotherham: These, are they who were numbered of the sons of Israel by their ancestral houses,—all they who were numbered of the camps by their hosts were six hundred and three thousand and five hundred and fifty.
text.KJV: These are those which were numbered of the children of Israel by the house of their fathers: all those that were numbered of the camps throughout their hosts were six hundred thousand and three thousand and five hundred and fifty.
text.NABRE: These are the enrollments of the Israelites according to their ancestral houses. The total enrollment of the camps by companies is six hundred and three thousand five hundred and fifty.
text.DRB: This is the number of the children of Israel by the houses of their kindreds and troops of the host being divided, six hundred three thousand five hundred fifty.

text.NRSV: But the Levites were not enrolled among the other Israelites, as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: But the Levites, were not numbered in the midst of the sons of Israel,—As Yahweh commanded Moses.
text.KJV: But the Levites were not numbered among the children of Israel; as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.NABRE: The Levites, however, were not enrolled with the other Israelites, just as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.DRB: And the Levites were not numbered among the children of Israel: for so our Lord had commanded Moyses.

text.NRSV: The Israelites did just so; they encamped by their standards, and so they set out, everyone in his clan, according to his ancestral house.
text.Rotherham: Thus did the sons of Israel,—according to all that Yahweh commanded Moses, so, did they encamp by their standards, and so, did they set forward, every one by his families near his ancestral house.
text.KJV: And the children of Israel did according to all that the Lord commanded Moses: so they pitched by their standards, and so they set forward, every one after their families, according to the house of their fathers.
text.NABRE: The Israelites did just as the Lord had commanded Moses; both in camp and on the march they were in their own divisions, everyone by clan and according to ancestral house.
text.DRB: And the children of Israel did according to all things that our Lord had commanded. They camped by their troops, and marched by their families and houses of their fathers.

text.NRSV: This is the lineage of Aaron and Moses at the time when the Lord spoke with Moses on Mount Sinai.
text.Rotherham: Now, these, were the pedigrees of Aaron and Moses,—in the day when Yahweh spake with Moses in Mount Sinai.
text.KJV: These also are the generations of Aaron and Moses in the day that the Lord spake with Moses in mount Sinai.
text.NABRE: These are the offspring of Aaron and Moses at the time the Lord spoke to Moses on Mount Sinai.
text.DRB: These are the generations of Aaron and Moyses in the day that our Lord spake to Moyses in Mount Sinai.

text.NRSV: These are the names of the sons of Aaron: Nadab the firstborn, and Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar;
text.Rotherham: And these, are the names of the sons of Aaron—the firstborn Nadab,—and Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.
text.KJV: And these are the names of the sons of Aaron; Nadab the firstborn, and Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.
text.NABRE: These are the names of Aaron’s sons: Nadab, the firstborn, Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.
text.DRB: And these be the names of the sons of Aaron: his first begotten Nadab, then Abiu, and Eleazar, and Ithamar.

text.NRSV: these are the names of the sons of Aaron, the anointed priests, whom he ordained to minister as priests.
text.Rotherham: These, are the names of the sons of Aaron, the priests that were anointed,—who were installed to minister as priests.
text.KJV: These are the names of the sons of Aaron, the priests which were anointed, whom he consecrated to minister in the priest’s office.
text.NABRE: These are the names of Aaron’s sons, the anointed priests whom he ordained to serve as priests.
text.DRB: These are the names of the sons of Aaron the Priests that were anointed, and whose hands were filled and consecrated, to do the function of the priesthood.

text.NRSV: But Nadab and Abihu died before the Lord when they offered unholy fire before the Lord in the wilderness of Sinai, and they had no children. So Eleazar and Ithamar served as priests in the lifetime of their father Aaron.
text.Rotherham: But Nadab and Abihu died before Yahweh when they brought near strange fire before Yahweh in the desert of Sinai, and sons, had they none,—so then Eleazar and Ithamar ministered as priests, in the presence of Aaron their father.
text.KJV: And Nadab and Abihu died before the Lord, when they offered strange fire before the Lord, in the wilderness of Sinai, and they had no children: and Eleazar and Ithamar ministered in the priest’s office in the sight of Aaron their father.
text.NABRE: But Nadab and Abihu died in the presence of the Lord when they offered unauthorized fire before the Lord in the wilderness of Sinai; and they left no sons. So only Eleazar and Ithamar served as priests during the lifetime of their father Aaron.
text.DRB: For Nadab and Abiu died, when they offered the strange fire in the sight of our Lord, in the desert of Sinai, without children: and Eleazar and Ithamar did the function of priesthood in the presence of Aaron their father.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses, saying—
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: Levites in Place of the Firstborn. Now the Lord said to Moses:
text.DRB: And our Lord spake to Moyses, saying:

text.NRSV: Bring the tribe of Levi near, and set them before Aaron the priest, so that they may assist him.
text.Rotherham: Bring near the tribe of Levi, and thou shalt cause it to stand before Aaron the priest,—so shall they wait upon him;
text.KJV: Bring the tribe of Levi near, and present them before Aaron the priest, that they may minister unto him.
text.NABRE: Summon the tribe of Levi and station them before Aaron the priest to serve him.
text.DRB: Bring the tribe of Levi, & make them stand in the sight of Aaron the Priest to minister unto him, and let them watch,

text.NRSV: They shall perform duties for him and for the whole congregation in front of the tent of meeting, doing service at the tabernacle;
text.Rotherham: so shall they keep his charge, and the charge of all the assembly, before the tent of meeting,—to do the laborious work of the habitation;
text.KJV: And they shall keep his charge, and the charge of the whole congregation before the tabernacle of the congregation, to do the service of the tabernacle.
text.NABRE: They shall discharge his obligations and those of the whole community before the tent of meeting by maintaining the tabernacle.
text.DRB: and observe whatsoever pertaineth to the service of the multitude before the tabernacle of testimony,

text.NRSV: they shall be in charge of all the furnishings of the tent of meeting, and attend to the duties for the Israelites as they do service at the tabernacle.
text.Rotherham: so shall they have charge of all the furniture of the tent of meeting, even the charge of the sons of Israel,—to do the laborious work of the habitation.
text.KJV: And they shall keep all the instruments of the tabernacle of the congregation, and the charge of the children of Israel, to do the service of the tabernacle.
text.NABRE: They shall have responsibility for all the furnishings of the tent of meeting and discharge the obligations of the Israelites by maintaining the tabernacle.
text.DRB: and let them keep the vessels of the tabernacle, serving in the ministry thereof.

text.NRSV: You shall give the Levites to Aaron and his descendants; they are unreservedly given to him from among the Israelites.
text.Rotherham: Thus shalt thou give the Levites unto Aaron and unto his sons,—given, given, they are unto him, from among the sons of Israel;
text.KJV: And thou shalt give the Levites unto Aaron and to his sons: they are wholly given unto him out of the children of Israel.
text.NABRE: You shall assign the Levites to Aaron and his sons; they have been assigned unconditionally to him from among the Israelites.
text.DRB: And thou shalt give the Levites for a gift,

text.NRSV: But you shall make a register of Aaron and his descendants; it is they who shall attend to the priesthood, and any outsider who comes near shall be put to death.
text.Rotherham: but unto Aaron, and unto his sons, shalt thou give oversight, so shall they keep charge of their priesthood,—and, the stranger who cometh near, shall be put to death.
text.KJV: And thou shalt appoint Aaron and his sons, and they shall wait on their priest’s office: and the stranger that cometh nigh shall be put to death.
text.NABRE: But you will appoint only Aaron and his descendants to exercise the priesthood. Any unauthorized person who comes near shall be put to death.
text.DRB: to Aaron and to his sons, to whom they are delivered of the children of Israel. But Aaron and his sons thou shalt appoint over the service of priesthood. The stranger, that approacheth to minister, shall die.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses, saying—
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: The Lord said to Moses:
text.DRB: And our Lord spake to Moyses, saying:

text.NRSV: I hereby accept the Levites from among the Israelites as substitutes for all the firstborn that open the womb among the Israelites. The Levites shall be mine,
text.Rotherham: I, therefore, lo! I have taken the Levites out of the midst of the sons of Israel, instead of every firstborn that a mother beareth, from among the sons of Israel,—so shall the Levites be mine;
text.KJV: And I, behold, I have taken the Levites from among the children of Israel instead of all the firstborn that openeth the matrix among the children of Israel: therefore the Levites shall be mine;
text.NABRE: I hereby take the Levites from the Israelites in place of every firstborn that opens the womb among the Israelites. The Levites, therefore, are mine,
text.DRB: I have taken the Levites from the children of Israel, for every first born, that openeth the matrice among the children of Israel, and the Levites shall be mine.

text.NRSV: for all the firstborn are mine; when I killed all the firstborn in the land of Egypt, I consecrated for my own all the firstborn in Israel, both human and animal; they shall be mine. I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: for, mine, is every firstborn, in the day when I smote every firstborn in the land of Egypt, I hallowed unto myself every firstborn in Israel, both of man and of beast,—mine, did they become, and belong unto me, Yahweh.
text.KJV: Because all the firstborn are mine; for on the day that I smote all the firstborn in the land of Egypt I hallowed unto me all the firstborn in Israel, both man and beast: mine shall they be: I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: because every firstborn is mine. When I struck down all the firstborn in the land of Egypt, I consecrated to me every firstborn in Israel, human being and beast alike. They belong to me; I am the Lord.
text.DRB: For the first born is mine: since I struck the first born in the Land of Ægypt: I have sanctified to me whatsoever is first born in Israel from man unto beast, they are mine: I the Lord.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord spoke to Moses in the wilderness of Sinai, saying:
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses, in the desert of Sinai, saying:—
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses in the wilderness of Sinai, saying,
text.NABRE: Census of the Levites. The Lord said to Moses in the wilderness of Sinai:
text.DRB: And our Lord spake to Moyses in the desert of Sinai, saying:

text.NRSV: Enroll the Levites by ancestral houses and by clans. You shall enroll every male from a month old and upward.
text.Rotherham: Number thou the sons of Levi, by their ancestral houses, by their families,—every male, from one month old and upwards, shalt thou number them.
text.KJV: Number the children of Levi after the house of their fathers, by their families: every male from a month old and upward shalt thou number them.
text.NABRE: Enroll the Levites by their ancestral houses and clans, enrolling every male of a month or more.
text.DRB: Number the sons of Levi by the houses of their fathers and their families, every male from one month & upward.

text.NRSV: So Moses enrolled them according to the word of the Lord, as he was commanded.
text.Rotherham: So then Moses numbered them according to the bidding of Yahweh,—as he was commanded.
text.KJV: And Moses numbered them according to the word of the Lord, as he was commanded.
text.NABRE: Moses, therefore, enrolled them at the direction of the Lord, just as the Lord had charged.
text.DRB: Moyses numbered, as our Lord had commanded,

text.NRSV: The following were the sons of Levi, by their names: Gershon, Kohath, and Merari.
text.Rotherham: And these were the sons of Levi, by their names,—Gershon, and Kohath, and Merari.
text.KJV: And these were the sons of Levi by their names; Gershon, and Kohath, and Merari.
text.NABRE: These were the sons of Levi by name: Gershon, Kohath and Merari.
text.DRB: & there were found the sons of Levi by their names, Gerson & Caath and Merari.

text.NRSV: These are the names of the sons of Gershon by their clans: Libni and Shimei.
text.Rotherham: And these, the names of the sons of Gershon by their families,—Libni and Shimei.
text.KJV: And these are the names of the sons of Gershon by their families; Libni, and Shimei.
text.NABRE: These were the names of the sons of Gershon, by their clans: Libni and Shimei.
text.DRB: The sons of Gerson: Lebni & Semei.

text.NRSV: The sons of Kohath by their clans: Amram, Izhar, Hebron, and Uzziel.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Kohath, by their families Amram, and Izhar, Hebron and Uzziel.
text.KJV: And the sons of Kohath by their families; Amram, and Izehar, Hebron, and Uzziel.
text.NABRE: The sons of Kohath, by their clans, were Amram, Izhar, Hebron and Uzziel.
text.DRB: The sons of Caath: Amram, and Iesaar, Hebron & Oziel.

text.NRSV: The sons of Merari by their clans: Mahli and Mushi. These are the clans of the Levites, by their ancestral houses.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Merari by their families, Mahli and Mushi. These, are they—the families of the Levites by their ancestral houses.
text.KJV: And the sons of Merari by their families; Mahli, and Mushi. These are the families of the Levites according to the house of their fathers.
text.NABRE: The sons of Merari, by their clans, were Mahli and Mushi. These were the clans of the Levites by their ancestral houses.
text.DRB: The sons of Merari: Moholi & Musi.

text.NRSV: To Gershon belonged the clan of the Libnites and the clan of the Shimeites; these were the clans of the Gershonites.
text.Rotherham: To, Gershon, belonged the family of the Libnites, and the family of the Shimeites,—these, are they—the families of the Gershonites.
text.KJV: Of Gershon was the family of the Libnites, and the family of the Shimites: these are the families of the Gershonites.
text.NABRE: Duties of the Levitical Clans. To Gershon belonged the clan of the Libnites and the clan of the Shimeites; these were the clans of the Gershonites.
text.DRB: Of Gerson were two families, the Lebnitical, & Semeitical:

text.NRSV: Their enrollment, counting all the males from a month old and upward, was seven thousand five hundred.
text.Rotherham: They who were numbered of them in the counting of every male, from one month old, and upwards,—they who were num-bered of them, were seven thousand and five hundred.
text.KJV: Those that were numbered of them, according to the number of all the males, from a month old and upward, even those that were numbered of them were seven thousand and five hundred.
text.NABRE: Their enrollment, registering every male of a month or more, was seven thousand five hundred.
text.DRB: of whom were numbered the people of male sex from one month & upward, seven thousand five hundred.

text.NRSV: The clans of the Gershonites were to camp behind the tabernacle on the west,
text.Rotherham: the families of the Gershonites, to the rear of the habitation, were to encamp, westward.
text.KJV: The families of the Gershonites shall pitch behind the tabernacle westward.
text.NABRE: The clans of the Gershonites camped behind the tabernacle, to the west.
text.DRB: These shall pitch behind the tabernacle on the West,

text.NRSV: with Eliasaph son of Lael as head of the ancestral house of the Gershonites.
text.Rotherham: And, the prince of the ancestral house of the Gershonites, was Eliasaph son of Lael.
text.KJV: And the chief of the house of the father of the Gershonites shall be Eliasaph the son of Lael.
text.NABRE: The leader of the ancestral house of the Gershonites was Eliasaph, son of Lael.
text.DRB: under their Prince Heliasaph, the son of Lael.

text.NRSV: The responsibility of the sons of Gershon in the tent of meeting was to be the tabernacle, the tent with its covering, the screen for the entrance of the tent of meeting,
text.Rotherham: And, the charge, of the sons of Gershon, was over the tent of meeting, the habitation, and the tent,—the covering thereof, and the screen for the entrance of the tent of meeting;
text.KJV: And the charge of the sons of Gershon in the tabernacle of the congregation shall be the tabernacle, and the tent, the covering thereof, and the hanging for the door of the tabernacle of the congregation,
text.NABRE: At the tent of meeting their responsibility was the tabernacle: the tent and its covering, the curtain at the entrance of the tent of meeting,
text.DRB: And their charge shall be in the tabernacle of covenant,

text.NRSV: the hangings of the court, the screen for the entrance of the court that is around the tabernacle and the altar, and its cords—all the service pertaining to these.
text.Rotherham: and the hangings of the court, and the screen for the entrance of the court which was near the habitation and near the altar, round about,—and the cords thereof, as to all the laborious work thereof.
text.KJV: And the hangings of the court, and the curtain for the door of the court, which is by the tabernacle, and by the altar round about, and the cords of it for all the service thereof.
text.NABRE: the hangings of the court, the curtain at the entrance of the court enclosing both the tabernacle and the altar, and the ropes—whatever pertained to their maintenance.
text.DRB: the tabernacle it-self and the cover thereof, the hanging that is drawn before the doors of the roof of covenant, and the curtains of the court: the hanging also that is hanged in the entry of the court of the tabernacle, and whatsoever pertaineth to the rite of the altar, the cords of the tabernacle, and all the furniture thereof.

text.NRSV: To Kohath belonged the clan of the Amramites, the clan of the Izharites, the clan of the Hebronites, and the clan of the Uzzielites; these are the clans of the Kohathites.
text.Rotherham: And to Kohath, belonged the family of the Amramites and the family of the Izharites, and the family of the Hebronites, and the family of the Uzzielites,—these, are they—the families of the Kohathites.
text.KJV: And of Kohath was the family of the Amramites, and the family of the Izeharites, and the family of the Hebronites, and the family of the Uzzielites: these are the families of the Kohathites.
text.NABRE: To Kohath belonged the clans of the Amramites, the Izharites, the Hebronites, and the Uzzielites; these were the clans of the Kohathites.
text.DRB: The kindred of Caath shall have the peoples of the Amramites, and Iesaarites, and Hebronites, and Ozielites. These are the families of the Caathites reckoned by their names:

text.NRSV: Counting all the males, from a month old and upward, there were eight thousand six hundred, attending to the duties of the sanctuary.
text.Rotherham: In, the counting of every male, from one month old and upwards, there were eight thousand and six hundred, to keep the charge of the sanctuary.
text.KJV: In the number of all the males, from a month old and upward, were eight thousand and six hundred, keeping the charge of the sanctuary.
text.NABRE: Their enrollment, registering every male of a month or more, was eight thousand three hundred. They were the ones who performed the duties of the sanctuary.
text.DRB: all of the male sex from one month and upward, eight thousand six hundred, they shall have the guard of the Sanctuary,

text.NRSV: The clans of the Kohathites were to camp on the south side of the tabernacle,
text.Rotherham: The, families of the sons of Kohath, were to encamp,—on the side of the habitation, south-ward.
text.KJV: The families of the sons of Kohath shall pitch on the side of the tabernacle southward.
text.NABRE: The clans of the Kohathites camped on the south side of the tabernacle.
text.DRB: and shall camp on the south side.

text.NRSV: with Elizaphan son of Uzziel as head of the ancestral house of the clans of the Kohathites.
text.Rotherham: And the prince of the ancestral house of the families of the Kohathites, was Elizaphan, son of Uzziel.
text.KJV: And the chief of the house of the father of the families of the Kohathites shall be Elizaphan the son of Uzziel.
text.NABRE: And the leader of their ancestral house of the clan of the Kohathites was Elizaphan, son of Uzziel.
text.DRB: And their Prince shall be Elizaphan the son of Oziel:

text.NRSV: Their responsibility was the ark, the table, the lampstand, the altars, the vessels of the sanctuary with which the priests minister, and the screen—all the service pertaining to these.
text.Rotherham: And their charge, was—the ark and the table and the lampstand and the altars, and the utensils of the sanctuary wherewith they were to minister,—and the screen, as to all the laborious work thereof.
text.KJV: And their charge shall be the ark, and the table, and the candlestick, and the altars, and the vessels of the sanctuary wherewith they minister, and the hanging, and all the service thereof.
text.NABRE: Their responsibility was the ark, the table, the menorah, the altars, the utensils of the sanctuary with which the priests minister, the veil, and everything pertaining to their maintenance.
text.DRB: and they shall keep the ark, and table, and the candlestick, the altars, and the vessels of the Sanctuary, wherein the ministration is, and the veil, and all such kind of implements.

text.NRSV: Eleazar son of Aaron the priest was to be chief over the leaders of the Levites, and to have oversight of those who had charge of the sanctuary.
text.Rotherham: And the prince of the princes of the Levites, was Eleazar, son of Aaron the priest,—to have the oversight of them that were to keep the charge of the sanctuary.
text.KJV: And Eleazar the son of Aaron the priest shall be chief over the chief of the Levites, and have the oversight of them that keep the charge of the sanctuary.
text.NABRE: The chief of the leaders of the Levites, however, was Eleazar, son of Aaron the priest; he was in charge of those who performed the duties of the sanctuary.
text.DRB: And the Prince of the Princes of the Levites, Eliazar, the son of Aaron the Priest, shall be over them that watch for the custody of the Sanctuary.

text.NRSV: To Merari belonged the clan of the Mahlites and the clan of the Mushites; these are the clans of Merari.
text.Rotherham: To Merari, belonged the family of the Mahlites, and the family of the Mushites,—these, are they—the families of Merari.
text.KJV: Of Merari was the family of the Mahlites, and the family of the Mushites: these are the families of Merari.
text.NABRE: To Merari belonged the clans of the Mahlites and the Mushites; these were the clans of Merari.
text.DRB: But of Merari shall be the peoples of the Moholites, and Musites, reckoned by their names:

text.NRSV: Their enrollment, counting all the males from a month old and upward, was six thousand two hundred.
text.Rotherham: And they who were numbered of them in the counting of every male, from one month old and upwards, were six thousand, and two hundred.
text.KJV: And those that were numbered of them, according to the number of all the males, from a month old and upward, were six thousand and two hundred.
text.NABRE: Their enrollment, registering every male of a month or more, was six thousand two hundred.
text.DRB: all of the male kind from one month & upward, six thousand two hundred.

text.NRSV: The head of the ancestral house of the clans of Merari was Zuriel son of Abihail; they were to camp on the north side of the tabernacle.
text.Rotherham: And the prince of the ancestral house of the families of Merari, was Zuriel son of Abihail,—on the side of the habitation, were they to encamp northward.
text.KJV: And the chief of the house of the father of the families of Merari was Zuriel the son of Abihail: these shall pitch on the side of the tabernacle northward.
text.NABRE: The leader of the ancestral house of the clans of Merari was Zuriel, son of Abihail. They camped at the north side of the tabernacle.
text.DRB: There Prince Suriel the son of Abihaiel: they shall camp on the north side.

text.NRSV: The appointed responsibility of the sons of Merari was the frames of the tabernacle, the bars, the pillars, the bases, and all their accessories—all the service pertaining to these;
text.Rotherham: And the oversight of the charge of the sons of Merari, was—the boards of the habitation, and the bars thereof and the pillars thereof and the sockets thereof,—and all the furniture thereof, as to all the laborious work thereof;
text.KJV: And under the custody and charge of the sons of Merari shall be the boards of the tabernacle, and the bars thereof, and the pillars thereof, and the sockets thereof, and all the vessels thereof, and all that serveth thereto,
text.NABRE: The Merarites were assigned responsibility for the boards of the tabernacle, its bars, columns, pedestals, and all its fittings—and everything pertaining to their maintenance,
text.DRB: Under their custody shall be the boards of the tabernacle, and the bars, and the pillars and their feet, and all things that pertain to this kind of service:

text.NRSV: also the pillars of the court all around, with their bases and pegs and cords.
text.Rotherham: also the pillars of the court round about and their sockets,—and their pins and their cords.
text.KJV: And the pillars of the court round about, and their sockets, and their pins, and their cords.
text.NABRE: as well as the columns of the surrounding court with their pedestals, pegs and ropes.
text.DRB: and the pillars of the court round about with their feet, and the pins with the cords.

text.NRSV: Those who were to camp in front of the tabernacle on the east—in front of the tent of meeting toward the east—were Moses and Aaron and Aaron's sons, having charge of the rites within the sanctuary, whatever had to be done for the Israelites; and any outsider who came near was to be put to death.
text.Rotherham: But, they who were to encamp before the habitation eastwards before the tent of meeting, towards sunrise, were Moses and Aaron and his sons. to keep the charge of the sanctuary, as the charge of the sons of Israel and, the stranger that came near, was to be put to death.
text.KJV: But those that encamp before the tabernacle toward the east, even before the tabernacle of the congregation eastward, shall be Moses, and Aaron and his sons, keeping the charge of the sanctuary for the charge of the children of Israel; and the stranger that cometh nigh shall be put to death.
text.NABRE: East of the tabernacle, that is, in front of the tent of meeting, toward the sunrise, were camped Moses and Aaron and the latter’s sons, performing the duties of the sanctuary incumbent upon the Israelites. Any unauthorized person who came near was to be put to death.
text.DRB: Before the tabernacle of covenant, that is to say, on the east side, shall Moyses and Aaron camp, with their sons, having the custody of the Sanctuary, in the midst of the children of Israel. What stranger soever cometh thereto, shall die.

text.NRSV: The total enrollment of the Levites whom Moses and Aaron enrolled at the commandment of the Lord, by their clans, all the males from a month old and upward, was twenty-two thousand.
text.Rotherham: All they who were numbered of the Levites whom Moses [and Aaron] numbered at the bidding of Yahweh by their families,—every male, from one month old and upwards, were two-and-twenty thousand.
text.KJV: All that were numbered of the Levites, which Moses and Aaron numbered at the commandment of the Lord, throughout their families, all the males from a month old and upward, were twenty and two thousand.
text.NABRE: The total enrollment of the Levites whom Moses and Aaron enrolled at the direction of the Lord, by clans, every male a month old or more, was twenty-two thousand.
text.DRB: All the Levites, that Moyses and Aaron numbered, according to the precept of our Lord by their families in the male kind from one month and upward, were twenty two thousand.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said to Moses: Enroll all the firstborn males of the Israelites, from a month old and upward, and count their names.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said unto Moses: Number thou every firstborn male belonging to the sons of Israel, from one month old and upwards,—and reckon up the number of their names;
text.KJV: And the Lord said unto Moses, Number all the firstborn of the males of the children of Israel from a month old and upward, and take the number of their names.
text.NABRE: Census and Ransom of Firstborn. The Lord then said to Moses: Enroll every firstborn male of the Israelites a month old or more, and count the number of their names.
text.DRB: And our Lord said to Moyses: Number the first-born of the male sex of the children of Israel, from one month and upward, and thou shalt have the sum of them.

text.NRSV: But you shall accept the Levites for me—I am the Lord—as substitutes for all the firstborn among the Israelites, and the livestock of the Levites as substitutes for all the firstborn among the livestock of the Israelites.
text.Rotherham: then shalt thou take the Levites for me, me Yahweh, instead of every firstborn among the sons of Israel,—also the cattle of the Levites, instead of every firstling among the cattle of the sons of Israel.
text.KJV: And thou shalt take the Levites for me (I am the Lord) instead of all the firstborn among the children of Israel; and the cattle of the Levites instead of all the firstlings among the cattle of the children of Israel.
text.NABRE: Then take the Levites for me—I am the Lord —in place of all the firstborn of the Israelites, as well as the Levites’ cattle, in place of all the firstborn among the cattle of the Israelites.
