text.NABRE: the divisional camp of the Judahites, arranged in companies, was the first to set out. Over its whole company was Nahshon, son of Amminadab,
text.DRB: The sons of Judas by their troops: whose Prince was Nahasson the son of Aminadab.

text.NRSV: Over the company of the tribe of Issachar was Nethanel son of Zuar;
text.Rotherham: and, over the host of the tribe of the sons of Issachar, Nethanel, son of Zuar;
text.KJV: And over the host of the tribe of the children of Issachar was Nethaneel the son of Zuar.
text.NABRE: with Nethanel, son of Zuar, over the company of the tribe of Issacharites,
text.DRB: In the tribe of the sons of Issachar, the Prince was Nathanael the son of Suar.

text.NRSV: and over the company of the tribe of Zebulun was Eliab son of Helon.
text.Rotherham: and, over the host of the tribe of the sons of Zebulun, Eliab, son of Helon.
text.KJV: And over the host of the tribe of the children of Zebulun was Eliab the son of Helon.
text.NABRE: and Eliab, son of Helon, over the company of the tribe of Zebulunites.
text.DRB: In the tribe of Zabulon, the Prince was Eliab the son of Helon.

text.NRSV: Then the tabernacle was taken down, and the Gershonites and the Merarites, who carried the tabernacle, set out.
text.Rotherham: Then was taken down the habitation,—and the sons of Gershon and the sons of Merari set forward bearing the habitation.
text.KJV: And the tabernacle was taken down; and the sons of Gershon and the sons of Merari set forward, bearing the tabernacle.
text.NABRE: Then, after the tabernacle was dismantled, the Gershonites and Merarites who carried the tabernacle set out.
text.DRB: And the tabernacle was taken down, which the sons of Gerson and Merari carrying, marched.

text.NRSV: Next the standard of the camp of Reuben set out, company by company; and over the whole company was Elizur son of Shedeur.
text.Rotherham: Then set forward the standard of the camp of Reuben, by their hosts,—and over his own host, Elizur, son of Shedeur;
text.KJV: And the standard of the camp of Reuben set forward according to their armies: and over his host was Elizur the son of Shedeur.
text.NABRE: The divisional camp of the Reubenites, arranged in companies, was the next to set out. Over its whole company was Elizur, son of Shedeur,
text.DRB: And the sons of Ruben also marched, by their troops and order, whose Prince was Helisur the son of Sedeur.

text.NRSV: Over the company of the tribe of Simeon was Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai,
text.Rotherham: and, over the host of the tribe of the sons of Simeon, Shelumiel, son of Zurishaddai;
text.KJV: And over the host of the tribe of the children of Simeon was Shelumiel the son of Zurishaddai.
text.NABRE: with Shelumiel, son of Zurishaddai, over the company of the tribe of Simeonites,
text.DRB: And in the tribe of Simeon, the Prince was Salamiel the son of Surisaddai.

text.NRSV: and over the company of the tribe of Gad was Eliasaph son of Deuel.
text.Rotherham: and, over the host of the tribe of the sons of Gad, Eliasaph, son of Deuel.
text.KJV: And over the host of the tribe of the children of Gad was Eliasaph the son of Deuel.
text.NABRE: and Eliasaph, son of Reuel, over the company of the tribe of Gadites.
text.DRB: Moreover in the tribe of Gad, the Prince was Eliasaph the son of Duel.

text.NRSV: Then the Kohathites set out, carrying the holy things. (The tabernacle was set up before their arrival.)
text.Rotherham: Then set forward the Kohathites, bearing the sanctuary,—and so the habitation was reared by the time they came in.
text.KJV: And the Kohathites set forward, bearing the sanctuary: and the other did set up the tabernacle against they came.
text.NABRE: The Kohathites, who carried the sacred objects, then set out. Before their arrival the tabernacle would be erected.
text.DRB: And the Caathites also marched carrying the Sanctuary. So long was the tabernacle carried, till they came to the place of erecting it.

text.NRSV: Next the standard of the Ephraimite camp set out, company by company, and over the whole company was Elishama son of Ammihud.
text.Rotherham: Then set forward the standard of the camp of the sons of Ephraim by their hosts,—and over his own host, was Elishama son of Ammihud;
text.KJV: And the standard of the camp of the children of Ephraim set forward according to their armies: and over his host was Elishama the son of Ammihud.
text.NABRE: The divisional camp of the Ephraimites set out next, arranged in companies. Over its whole company was Elishama, son of Ammihud,
text.DRB: The sons of Ephraim also moved their camp by their troops, in whose host the Prince was Elisama the son of Ammiud.

text.NRSV: Over the company of the tribe of Manasseh was Gamaliel son of Pedahzur,
text.Rotherham: and, over the host of the tribe of the sons of Manasseh, Gamaliel, son of Pedahzur;
text.KJV: And over the host of the tribe of the children of Manasseh was Gamaliel the son of Pedahzur.
text.NABRE: with Gamaliel, son of Pedahzur, over the company of the tribe of Manassites,
text.DRB: And in the tribe of the sons of Manasses, the Prince was Gamaliel the son of Phadassur.

text.NRSV: and over the company of the tribe of Benjamin was Abidan son of Gideoni.
text.Rotherham: and, over the host of the tribe of the sons of Benjamin, Abidan, son of Gideoni.
text.KJV: And over the host of the tribe of the children of Benjamin was Abidan the son of Gideoni.
text.NABRE: and Abidan, son of Gideoni, over the company of the tribe of Benjaminites.
text.DRB: And in the tribe of Benjamin the Prince was Abidan the son of Gedeon.

text.NRSV: Then the standard of the camp of Dan, acting as the rear guard of all the camps, set out, company by company: Over the whole company was Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai.
text.Rotherham: Then set forward the standard of the camp of the sons of Dan, bringing up the rear of all the camps, by their hosts,—and over his own host, Ahiezur, son of Ammishaddai;
text.KJV: And the standard of the camp of the children of Dan set forward, which was the rereward of all the camps throughout their hosts: and over his host was Ahiezer the son of Ammishaddai.
text.NABRE: Finally, as rear guard for all the camps, the divisional camp of the Danites set out, arranged in companies. Over its whole company was Ahiezer, son of Ammishaddai,
text.DRB: The last of all the camp marched the sons of Dan by their troops, in whose host the Prince was Ahiezer the son of Ammisaddai.

text.NRSV: Over the company of the tribe of Asher was Pagiel son of Ochran,
text.Rotherham: and, over the host of the tribe of the sons of Asher, Pagiel, son of Ochran;
text.KJV: And over the host of the tribe of the children of Asher was Pagiel the son of Ocran.
text.NABRE: with Pagiel, son of Ochran, over the company of the tribe of Asherites,
text.DRB: And in the tribe of the sons of Aser, the Prince was Phegiel the son of Ochran.

text.NRSV: and over the company of the tribe of Naphtali was Ahira son of Enan.
text.Rotherham: and, over the host of the tribe of the sons of Naphtali, Ahira, son of Enan.
text.KJV: And over the host of the tribe of the children of Naphtali was Ahira the son of Enan.
text.NABRE: and Ahira, son of Enan, over the company of the tribe of Naphtalites.
text.DRB: And in the tribe of the sons of Nephthali the Prince was Ahira the son of Enan.

text.NRSV: This was the order of march of the Israelites, company by company, when they set out.
text.Rotherham: These, were the settings-forward of the sons of Israel by their hosts,—thus did they set forward.
text.KJV: Thus were the journeyings of the children of Israel according to their armies, when they set forward.
text.NABRE: This was the order of march for the Israelites, company by company, when they set out.
text.DRB: These are the camps, and the marchings of the children of Israel by their troops when they marched.

text.NRSV: Moses said to Hobab son of Reuel the Midianite, Moses' father-in-law, "We are setting out for the place of which the Lord said, 'I will give it to you'; come with us, and we will treat you well; for the Lord has promised good to Israel."
text.Rotherham: Then said Moses to Hobab, son of Raguel the Midianite, father-in-law of Moses: Setting forward, are we unto the place of which Yahweh hath said, The same, will I give unto you,—Oh come with us and we will do thee good, for, Yahweh, hath spoken good, concerning Israel.
text.KJV: And Moses said unto Hobab, the son of Raguel the Midianite, Moses’ father in law, We are journeying unto the place of which the Lord said, I will give it you: come thou with us, and we will do thee good: for the Lord hath spoken good concerning Israel.
text.NABRE: Hobab as Guide. Moses said to Hobab, son of Reuel the Midianite, Moses’ father-in-law, “We are setting out for the place concerning which the Lord has said, ‘I will give it to you.’ Come with us, and we will be generous toward you, for the Lord has promised prosperity to Israel.”
text.DRB: And Moyses said to Hobab the son of Raguel the Madianite, his allied: We march toward the place, which our Lord will give us: come with us, that we may do thee good: for our Lord hath promised good things to Israel.

text.NRSV: But he said to him, "I will not go, but I will go back to my own land and to my kindred."
text.Rotherham: And he said unto him, I will not go,—but unto my own land, and unto my own kindred, will I go.
text.KJV: And he said unto him, I will not go; but I will depart to mine own land, and to my kindred.
text.NABRE: But he answered, “No, I will not come. I am going instead to the land of my birth.”
text.DRB: To whom he answered: I will not go with thee, but I will return to my country, wherein I was born.

text.NRSV: He said, "Do not leave us, for you know where we should camp in the wilderness, and you will serve as eyes for us.
text.Rotherham: Then said he: I beseech thee, do not forsake us,—for, on this account, hast thou come to know of our encamping in the desert, so shalt thou be unto us as eyes;
text.KJV: And he said, Leave us not, I pray thee; forasmuch as thou knowest how we are to encamp in the wilderness, and thou mayest be to us instead of eyes.
text.NABRE: Moses said, “Please, do not leave us; you know where we can camp in the wilderness, and you can serve as our guide.
text.DRB: And he said: Do not forsake us: for thou knowest in what places through the desert we may camp, and thou shalt be our guide.

text.NRSV: Moreover, if you go with us, whatever good the Lord does for us, the same we will do for you."
text.Rotherham: and it shall be if thou wilt go with us,—yea it shall be that with the very good wherewith Yahweh shall do us good, will we do good unto thee.
text.KJV: And it shall be, if thou go with us, yea, it shall be, that what goodness the Lord shall do unto us, the same will we do unto thee.
text.NABRE: If you come with us, we will share with you the prosperity the Lord will bestow on us.”
text.DRB: And when thou comest with us, whatsoever shall be best of the riches, which our Lord shall deliver us, we will give thee.

text.NRSV: So they set out from the mount of the Lord three days' journey with the ark of the covenant of the Lord going before them three days' journey, to seek out a resting place for them,
text.Rotherham: So then they went forward, from the mountain of Yahweh, a journey of three days,—and the ark of the covenant of Yahweh, was going before them a journey of three days, to search out for them a resting-place,
text.KJV: And they departed from the mount of the Lord three days’ journey: and the ark of the covenant of the Lord went before them in the three days’ journey, to search out a resting place for them.
text.NABRE: Into the Wilderness. From the mountain of the Lord they made a journey of three days, and the ark of the covenant of the Lord went before them for the three-day journey to seek out a resting place for them.
text.DRB: They marched therefore from the Mount of our Lord three days' journey, and the ark of the covenant of our Lord went before them, for three days providing a place for the camp.

text.NRSV: the cloud of the Lord being over them by day when they set out from the camp.
text.Rotherham: And the cloud of Yahweh, was over them by day,—when they set forward out of the camp.
text.KJV: And the cloud of the Lord was upon them by day, when they went out of the camp.
text.NABRE: And the cloud of the Lord was over them by day when they set out from camp.
text.DRB: The cloud also of our Lord was over them by day when they marched.

text.NRSV: Whenever the ark set out, Moses would say, "Arise, O Lord, let your enemies be scattered, and your foes flee before you."
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass when the ark set forward, that Moses said: Arise, O Yahweh! and scattered be thy foes, Let them that hate thee flee before thee;
text.KJV: And it came to pass, when the ark set forward, that Moses said, Rise up, Lord, and let thine enemies be scattered; and let them that hate thee flee before thee.
text.NABRE: Whenever the ark set out, Moses would say, “Arise, O Lord, may your enemies be scattered, and may those who hate you flee before you.”
text.DRB: And when the ark was lifted up, Moyses said: Arise Lord, and be thine enemies dispersed, and let them fly that hate thee, from thy face.

text.NRSV: And whenever it came to rest, he would say, "Return, O Lord of the ten thousand thousands of Israel."
text.Rotherham: But when it rested, he said,—Return, O Yahweh! unto the myriads of the thousands of Israel.
text.KJV: And when it rested, he said, Return, O Lord, unto the many thousands of Israel.
text.NABRE: And when it came to rest, he would say, “Bring back, O Lord, the myriads of Israel’s troops!”
text.DRB: And when it was set down, he said: Return Lord to the multitude of the host of Israel.

text.NRSV: Now when the people complained in the hearing of the Lord about their misfortunes, the Lord heard it and his anger was kindled. Then the fire of the Lord burned against them, and consumed some outlying parts of the camp.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass that when the people were giving themselves up to murmuring, it was grievous in the ears of Yahweh,—so Yahweh hearkened, and kindled was his anger, and the fire of Yahweh burned among them, and consumed the uttermost part of the camp.
text.KJV: And when the people complained, it displeased the Lord: and the Lord heard it; and his anger was kindled; and the fire of the Lord burnt among them, and consumed them that were in the uttermost parts of the camp.
text.NABRE: Now the people complained bitterly in the hearing of the Lord; and when he heard it his wrath flared up, so that the Lord ’s fire burned among them and consumed the outskirts of the camp.
text.DRB: In the mean time there arose a murmuring of the people, as it were repining for labour, against our Lord. Which when our Lord had heard, he was angry. And the fire of our Lord being kindled against them, devoured the uttermost part of the camp.

text.NRSV: But the people cried out to Moses; and Moses prayed to the Lord, and the fire abated.
text.Rotherham: Then did the people make outcry unto Moses,—and Moses prayed unto Yahweh, and the fire sank down.
text.KJV: And the people cried unto Moses; and when Moses prayed unto the Lord, the fire was quenched.
text.NABRE: But when the people cried out to Moses, he prayed to the Lord and the fire died out.
text.DRB: And when the people had cried to Moyses, Moyses prayed to our Lord, and the fire was quenched.

text.NRSV: So that place was called Taberah, because the fire of the Lord burned against them.
text.Rotherham: So he called the name of that place, Taberah [="A Burning"] because there burned among them the fire of Yahweh.
text.KJV: And he called the name of the place Taberah: because the fire of the Lord burnt among them.
text.NABRE: Hence that place was called Taberah, because there the fire of the Lord burned among them.
text.DRB: And he called the name of the place, Kindling: for that the fire of our Lord had been kindled against them.

text.NRSV: The rabble among them had a strong craving; and the Israelites also wept again, and said, "If only we had meat to eat!
text.Rotherham: Moreover the mixed multitude that was in their midst concealed not their lusting,—and so even the sons of Israel, fell away and wept, and said: Who will grant us to eat flesh?
text.KJV: And the mixt multitude that was among them fell a lusting: and the children of Israel also wept again, and said, Who shall give us flesh to eat?
text.NABRE: The riffraff among them were so greedy for meat that even the Israelites lamented again, “If only we had meat for food!
text.DRB: For the common vulgar people that came up with them, burned with desire, sitting and weeping, the children of Israel being joined together with them, and said: Who shall give us flesh to eat?

text.NRSV: We remember the fish we used to eat in Egypt for nothing, the cucumbers, the melons, the leeks, the onions, and the garlic;
text.Rotherham: We remember the fish that we did eat in Egypt, without money,—the cucumbers, and the water-melons, and the leeks and the onions, and the garlick.
text.KJV: We remember the fish, which we did eat in Egypt freely; the cucumbers, and the melons, and the leeks, and the onions, and the garlick:
text.NABRE: We remember the fish we used to eat without cost in Egypt, and the cucumbers, the melons, the leeks, the onions, and the garlic.
text.DRB: We remember the fishes that we did eat in Ægypt gratis: the cucumbers come unto our mind, and the melons, and leeks and onions and garlic.

text.NRSV: but now our strength is dried up, and there is nothing at all but this manna to look at."
text.Rotherham: But, now, our soul is dried up, there is nothing at all,—unless, unto the manna, [we turn] our eyes.
text.KJV: But now our soul is dried away: there is nothing at all, beside this manna, before our eyes.
text.NABRE: But now we are famished; we have nothing to look forward to but this manna.”
text.DRB: Our soul is dry, our eyes behold nothing else but Manna.

text.NRSV: Now the manna was like coriander seed, and its color was like the color of gum resin.
text.Rotherham: Now, the manna, was like coriander seed, and the appearance thereof like the appearance of bdellium,
text.KJV: And the manna was as coriander seed, and the colour thereof as the colour of bdellium.
text.NABRE: Manna was like coriander seed and had the appearance of bdellium.
text.DRB: And the Manna was as it were the seed of Coriander, of the colour of bdellion.

text.NRSV: The people went around and gathered it, ground it in mills or beat it in mortars, then boiled it in pots and made cakes of it; and the taste of it was like the taste of cakes baked with oil.
text.Rotherham: The people used to go about and pick it up, and grind it with a pair of mill-stones, or pound it in a mortar, and boil it in a pot, and make it into round cakes,—then was the taste thereof like the taste of a sweet cake made with oil.
text.KJV: And the people went about, and gathered it, and ground it in mills, or beat it in a mortar, and baked it in pans, and made cakes of it: and the taste of it was as the taste of fresh oil.
text.NABRE: When they had gone about and gathered it up, the people would grind it between millstones or pound it in a mortar, then cook it in a pot and make it into loaves, with a rich creamy taste.
text.DRB: And the people went about, & gathering it, ground it in a mill, or brayed it in a mortar, boiling it in a pot, and making cakes thereof of the taste as it were of oiled bread.

text.NRSV: When the dew fell on the camp in the night, the manna would fall with it.
text.Rotherham: When the dew came down upon the camp by night, the manna came down thereupon.
text.KJV: And when the dew fell upon the camp in the night, the manna fell upon it.
text.NABRE: At night, when the dew fell upon the camp, the manna also fell.
text.DRB: And when the dew fell in the night upon the camp, the Manna also fell withal.

text.NRSV: Moses heard the people weeping throughout their families, all at the entrances of their tents. Then the Lord became very angry, and Moses was displeased.
text.Rotherham: So then Moses heard the people weeping by their families, every one at the entrance of his tent,—then kindled the anger of Yahweh fiercely, and in the eyes of Moses, it was grievous.
text.KJV: Then Moses heard the people weep throughout their families, every man in the door of his tent: and the anger of the Lord was kindled greatly; Moses also was displeased.
text.NABRE: When Moses heard the people, family after family, crying at the entrance of their tents, so that the Lord became very angry, he was grieved.
text.DRB: Moyses therefore heard the people weeping by their families, every one at the doors of his tent. And the fury of our Lord was exceeding wrath: but to Moyses also it seemed an intolerable thing.

text.NRSV: So Moses said to the Lord, "Why have you treated your servant so badly? Why have I not found favor in your sight, that you lay the burden of all this people on me?
text.Rotherham: And Moses said unto Yahweh—Wherefore hast thou let thy servant come to grief, and wherefore have I not found favour in thine eyes,—that thou shouldest lay the burden of all this people upon me?
text.KJV: And Moses said unto the Lord, Wherefore hast thou afflicted thy servant? and wherefore have I not found favour in thy sight, that thou layest the burden of all this people upon me?
text.NABRE: “Why do you treat your servant so badly?” Moses asked the Lord. “Why are you so displeased with me that you burden me with all this people?
text.DRB: And he said to our Lord: Why hast thou afflicted thy servant? wherefore do I not find grace before thee? and why hast thou laid the weight of all this people upon me?

text.NRSV: Did I conceive all this people? Did I give birth to them, that you should say to me, 'Carry them in your bosom, as a nurse carries a sucking child,' to the land that you promised on oath to their ancestors?
text.Rotherham: Did, I, conceive all this people, or, I, beget them,—that thou shouldest say unto me, Carry them in thy bosom, as a nursing father carrieth a suckling, unto the soil which thou didst swear unto their fathers?
text.KJV: Have I conceived all this people? have I begotten them, that thou shouldest say unto me, Carry them in thy bosom, as a nursing father beareth the sucking child, unto the land which thou swarest unto their fathers?
text.NABRE: Was it I who conceived all this people? or was it I who gave them birth, that you tell me to carry them at my breast, like a nurse carrying an infant, to the land you have promised under oath to their fathers?
text.DRB: Have I conceived all this multitude, or begotten them, that thou shouldest say to me: Carry them in thy bosom as the nurse is wont to carry the little infant, and bear them into the land, for the which thou hast sworn to their fathers?

text.NRSV: Where am I to get meat to give to all this people? For they come weeping to me and say, 'Give us meat to eat!'
text.Rotherham: Whence should, I, have flesh to give to all this people,—for they keep weeping by me saying, Oh give us flesh that we may eat!
text.KJV: Whence should I have flesh to give unto all this people? for they weep unto me, saying, Give us flesh, that we may eat.
text.NABRE: Where can I get meat to give to all this people? For they are crying to me, ‘Give us meat for our food.’
text.DRB: Whence shall I have flesh to give to so great a multitude? They whine against me, saying: Give us flesh that we may eat.

text.NRSV: I am not able to carry all this people alone, for they are too heavy for me.
text.Rotherham: Unable, am, I, by myself, to carry all this people,—for they are too heavy for me.
text.KJV: I am not able to bear all this people alone, because it is too heavy for me.
text.NABRE: I cannot carry all this people by myself, for they are too heavy for me.
text.DRB: I alone cannot sustain all this people, because it is heavy for me.

text.NRSV: If this is the way you are going to treat me, put me to death at once—if I have found favor in your sight—and do not let me see my misery."
text.Rotherham: But if, in this way, thou art going to deal with me, slay me, I beseech thee, slay, if I have found favour in thine eyes,—and let me not see my grief,
text.KJV: And if thou deal thus with me, kill me, I pray thee, out of hand, if I have found favour in thy sight; and let me not see my wretchedness.
text.NABRE: If this is the way you will deal with me, then please do me the favor of killing me at once, so that I need no longer face my distress.”
text.DRB: But if it seem unto thee otherwise, I beseech thee to kill me, and let me find grace in thine eyes, that I be not molested with so great evils.

text.NRSV: So the Lord said to Moses, "Gather for me seventy of the elders of Israel, whom you know to be the elders of the people and officers over them; bring them to the tent of meeting, and have them take their place there with you.
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto Moses—Gather thou unto me seventy men, from among the elders of Israel, of whom thou knowest that they are elders of the people and their overseers,—then shalt thou take them unto the tent of meeting, and they shall station themselves there with thee.
text.KJV: And the Lord said unto Moses, Gather unto me seventy men of the elders of Israel, whom thou knowest to be the elders of the people, and officers over them; and bring them unto the tabernacle of the congregation, that they may stand there with thee.
text.NABRE: The Seventy Elders. Then the Lord said to Moses: Assemble for me seventy of the elders of Israel, whom you know to be elders and authorities among the people, and bring them to the tent of meeting. When they are in place beside you,
text.DRB: And our Lord said to Moyses: Gather me seventy men of the ancients of Israel, whom thou knowest to be the ancients of the people and masters: and thou shalt bring them to the door of the tabernacle of covenant, and shalt make them to stand there with thee,

text.NRSV: I will come down and talk with you there; and I will take some of the spirit that is on you and put it on them; and they shall bear the burden of the people along with you so that you will not bear it all by yourself.
text.Rotherham: Then will I come down, and speak with thee there, and will take of the spirit that is upon thee and put upon them,—and they shall carry, with thee, the burden of the people, and, thou, shalt not carry it by thyself.
text.KJV: And I will come down and talk with thee there: and I will take of the spirit which is upon thee, and will put it upon them; and they shall bear the burden of the people with thee, that thou bear it not thyself alone.
text.NABRE: I will come down and speak with you there. I will also take some of the spirit that is on you and will confer it on them, that they may share the burden of the people with you. You will then not have to bear it by yourself.
text.DRB: that I may descend & speak to thee: and I will take of thy spirit, and will deliver to them, that they may sustain with thee the burden of the people, and thou only be not burdened.

text.NRSV: And say to the people: Consecrate yourselves for tomorrow, and you shall eat meat; for you have wailed in the hearing of the Lord, saying, 'If only we had meat to eat! Surely it was better for us in Egypt.' Therefore the Lord will give you meat, and you shall eat.
text.Rotherham: And, unto the people, shalt thou say: Hallow yourselves by to-morrow, and ye shall eat flesh, for ye have wept in the ears of Yahweh saying—Who will grant us to eat flesh? for it was well with us in Egypt,—so then Yahweh will give you flesh and ye shall eat.
text.KJV: And say thou unto the people, Sanctify yourselves against to morrow, and ye shall eat flesh: for ye have wept in the ears of the Lord, saying, Who shall give us flesh to eat? for it was well with us in Egypt: therefore the Lord will give you flesh, and ye shall eat.
text.NABRE: To the people, however, you shall say: “Sanctify yourselves for tomorrow, when you shall have meat to eat. For in the hearing of the Lord you have cried, ‘If only we had meat for food! Oh, how well off we were in Egypt!’ Therefore the Lord will give you meat to eat,
text.DRB: To the people also thou shalt say: Be sanctified: tomorrow you shall eat flesh: for I have heard you say: Who shall give us meats of flesh? It was well with us in Ægypt. That the Lord may give you flesh, and you may eat:

text.NRSV: You shall eat not only one day, or two days, or five days, or ten days, or twenty days,
text.Rotherham: Not one day, shall ye eat nor two days,—nor five days nor ten days, nor twenty days:
text.KJV: Ye shall not eat one day, nor two days, nor five days, neither ten days, nor twenty days;
text.NABRE: and you will eat it, not for one day, or two days, or five, or ten, or twenty days,
text.DRB: not one day, nor two, or five, or ten, no nor twenty,

text.NRSV: but for a whole month—until it comes out of your nostrils and becomes loathsome to you—because you have rejected the Lord who is among you, and have wailed before him, saying, 'Why did we ever leave Egypt?'"
text.Rotherham: for a month of days until it cometh forth out of your nostrils, so shall it become to you a loathsome thing,—because ye have refused Yahweh who is in your midst, and have wept before him, saying, Wherefore now came we forth out of Egypt?
text.KJV: But even a whole month, until it come out at your nostrils, and it be loathsome unto you: because that ye have despised the Lord which is among you, and have wept before him, saying, Why came we forth out of Egypt?
text.NABRE: but for a whole month—until it comes out of your very nostrils and becomes loathsome to you. For you have rejected the Lord who is in your midst, and in his presence you have cried, ‘Why did we ever leave Egypt?’”
text.DRB: but even to a month of days, till it go out of your nostrils, & be turned to loathsomeness, because you have rejected the Lord, who is in the midst of you, and have whined before him, saying: Wherefore came we out of Ægypt?

text.NRSV: But Moses said, "The people I am with number six hundred thousand on foot; and you say, 'I will give them meat, that they may eat for a whole month'!
text.Rotherham: And Moses said, Six hundred thousand footmen, are the people in whose midst am, I, yet thou hast said—Flesh, will I give unto them, and they shall eat for a month of days.
text.KJV: And Moses said, The people, among whom I am, are six hundred thousand footmen; and thou hast said, I will give them flesh, that they may eat a whole month.
text.NABRE: But Moses said, “The people around me include six hundred thousand soldiers; yet you say, ‘I will give them meat to eat for a whole month.’
text.DRB: And Moyses said: There are six hundred thousand footmen of this people, and sayest thou: I will give them flesh to eat a whole month?

text.NRSV: Are there enough flocks and herds to slaughter for them? Are there enough fish in the sea to catch for them?"
text.Rotherham: Shall the flocks and herds, be slaughtered for them, that it may suffice for them? Shall all the fishes of the sea, be gathered together unto them that it may suffice for them?
text.KJV: Shall the flocks and the herds be slain for them, to suffice them? or shall all the fish of the sea be gathered together for them, to suffice them?
text.NABRE: Can enough sheep and cattle be slaughtered for them? If all the fish of the sea were caught for them, would they have enough?”
text.DRB: Why, shall a multitude of sheep and oxen be killed, that it may suffice for meat? Or shall all the fishes of the sea be gathered together, for to fill them?

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Moses, "Is the Lord's power limited? Now you shall see whether my word will come true for you or not."
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto Moses, Shall the hand of Yahweh, fail? Now, shalt thou see whether my word come to pass unto thee or not.
text.KJV: And the Lord said unto Moses, Is the Lord’s hand waxed short? thou shalt see now whether my word shall come to pass unto thee or not.
text.NABRE: The Lord answered Moses: Is this beyond the Lord ’s reach? You shall see now whether or not what I have said to you takes place.
text.DRB: To whom our Lord answered: Why, is the hand of the Lord unable? Now presently thou shalt see whether my word shall be accomplished in deed.

text.NRSV: So Moses went out and told the people the words of the Lord; and he gathered seventy elders of the people, and placed them all around the tent.
text.Rotherham: And Moses went forth and spake unto the people the words of Yahweh,—and gathered together seventy men from among the elders of the people, and caused them to stand round about the tent.
text.KJV: And Moses went out, and told the people the words of the Lord, and gathered the seventy men of the elders of the people, and set them round about the tabernacle.
text.NABRE: The Spirit on the Elders. So Moses went out and told the people what the Lord had said. Gathering seventy elders of the people, he had them stand around the tent.
text.DRB: Moyses therefore came, and told the people the words of our Lord, assembling seventy men of the ancients of Israel, whom he caused to stand about the tabernacle.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord came down in the cloud and spoke to him, and took some of the spirit that was on him and put it on the seventy elders; and when the spirit rested upon them, they prophesied. But they did not do so again.
text.Rotherham: Then Yahweh came down, in the cloud, and spake unto him, and took of the spirit that was upon him, and gave unto the seventy men—the elders. And it came to pass that when the spirit rested upon them, they prophesied, and then did so no more.
text.KJV: And the Lord came down in a cloud, and spake unto him, and took of the spirit that was upon him, and gave it unto the seventy elders: and it came to pass, that, when the spirit rested upon them, they prophesied, and did not cease.
text.NABRE: The Lord then came down in the cloud and spoke to him. Taking some of the spirit that was on Moses, he bestowed it on the seventy elders; and as the spirit came to rest on them, they prophesied but did not continue.
text.DRB: And our Lord descended in a cloud, and spake to him, taking away of the spirit that was in Moyses, and giving to the seventy men. And when the spirit had rested on them, they prophesied, neither ceased they any more.

text.NRSV: Two men remained in the camp, one named Eldad, and the other named Medad, and the spirit rested on them; they were among those registered, but they had not gone out to the tent, and so they prophesied in the camp.
text.Rotherham: Now there were two men left behind in the camp—the name of the one, was Eldad and the name of the other, Medad, so then the spirit, rested on them—they, being among them who were written, though they had not gone forth unto the tent,—but they prophesied in the camp.
text.KJV: But there remained two of the men in the camp, the name of the one was Eldad, and the name of the other Medad: and the spirit rested upon them; and they were of them that were written, but went not out unto the tabernacle: and they prophesied in the camp.
text.NABRE: Now two men, one named Eldad and the other Medad, had remained in the camp, yet the spirit came to rest on them also. They too had been on the list, but had not gone out to the tent; and so they prophesied in the camp.
text.DRB: And there had remained in the camp two men, of the which one was called Eldad, and the other Medad, upon whom the spirit rested. For they also had been enrolled, & were not gone forth to the tabernacle.

text.NRSV: And a young man ran and told Moses, "Eldad and Medad are prophesying in the camp."
text.Rotherham: And there ran a young man, and told Moses and said,—Eldad and Medad, are prophesying in the camp!
text.KJV: And there ran a young man, and told Moses, and said, Eldad and Medad do prophesy in the camp.
text.NABRE: So, when a young man ran and reported to Moses, “Eldad and Medad are prophesying in the camp,”
text.DRB: And when they prophesied in the camp, there ran a boy, and told Moyses, saying: Eldad and Medad do prophesy in the camp.

text.NRSV: And Joshua son of Nun, the assistant of Moses, one of his chosen men, said, "My lord Moses, stop them!"
text.Rotherham: Then responded Joshua son of Nun the attendant of Moses from his youth, and said,—My lord Moses forbid them!
text.KJV: And Joshua the son of Nun, the servant of Moses, one of his young men, answered and said, My lord Moses, forbid them.
text.NABRE: Joshua, son of Nun, who from his youth had been Moses’ aide, said, “My lord, Moses, stop them.”
text.DRB: Forthwith Josue the Nun, the minister of Moyses, and chosen of many, said: My Lord Moyses forbid them.

text.NRSV: But Moses said to him, "Are you jealous for my sake? Would that all the Lord's people were prophets, and that the Lord would put his spirit on them!"
text.Rotherham: But Moses said unto him, Art, thou, jealous for, me? Oh would that, all the people of Yahweh, were prophets! Yea let Yahweh put his spirit upon them!
text.KJV: And Moses said unto him, Enviest thou for my sake? would God that all the Lord’s people were prophets, and that the Lord would put his spirit upon them!
text.NABRE: But Moses answered him, “Are you jealous for my sake? If only all the people of the Lord were prophets! If only the Lord would bestow his spirit on them!”
text.DRB: But he said: Why hast thou emulation for me? O that all the people might prophesy, and that our Lord would give them his spirit!

text.NRSV: And Moses and the elders of Israel returned to the camp.
text.Rotherham: Then was Moses received back into the camp,—he and the elders of Israel.
text.KJV: And Moses gat him into the camp, he and the elders of Israel.
text.NABRE: Then Moses retired to the camp, along with the elders of Israel.
text.DRB: And Moyses returned, and the ancients of Israel into the camp.

text.NRSV: Then a wind went out from the Lord, and it brought quails from the sea and let them fall beside the camp, about a day's journey on this side and a day's journey on the other side, all around the camp, about two cubits deep on the ground.
text.Rotherham: Now, a wind, had sprung up, from Yahweh, and cut off quails from the sea, and let them lie over the camp—as it were a days journey here and a days journey there, round about the camp,—and as it were two cubits on the face of the land.
text.KJV: And there went forth a wind from the Lord, and brought quails from the sea, and let them fall by the camp, as it were a day’s journey on this side, and as it were a day’s journey on the other side, round about the camp, and as it were two cubits high upon the face of the earth.
text.NABRE: The Quail. There arose a wind from the Lord that drove in quail from the sea and left them all around the camp site, to a distance of a day’s journey and at a depth of two cubits upon the ground.
text.DRB: And a wind coming forth from our Lord, taking quails over the sea brought them, and let them fall into the camp the space of one day's journey, on every side of the camp round about, and they did fly in the air two cubits high over the earth.

text.NRSV: So the people worked all that day and night and all the next day, gathering the quails; the least anyone gathered was ten homers; and they spread them out for themselves all around the camp.
text.Rotherham: And the people rose up all that day and all the night and all the next day, and gathered the quails, he that did least, gathered ten homers,—and they spread them out for themselves spreading away, round about the camp.
text.KJV: And the people stood up all that day, and all that night, and all the next day, and they gathered the quails: he that gathered least gathered ten homers: and they spread them all abroad for themselves round about the camp.
text.NABRE: So all that day, all night, and all the next day the people set about to gather in the quail. Even the one who got the least gathered ten homers of them. Then they spread them out all around the camp.
text.DRB: The people therefore rising up all that day, and the night, and the next day, gathered together a multitude of quails, he that did least, ten cores: and they dried them round about the camp.

text.NRSV: But while the meat was still between their teeth, before it was consumed, the anger of the Lord was kindled against the people, and the Lord struck the people with a very great plague.
text.Rotherham: The flesh, was yet between their teeth, not yet chewed,—when the anger of Yahweh, had kindled upon the people, and Yahweh smote the people, with an exceeding great plague.
text.KJV: And while the flesh was yet between their teeth, ere it was chewed, the wrath of the Lord was kindled against the people, and the Lord smote the people with a very great plague.
text.NABRE: But while the meat was still between their teeth, before it could be chewed, the Lord ’s wrath flared up against the people, and the Lord struck them with a very great plague.
text.DRB: As yet the flesh was in their teeth, neither had that kind of meat failed: & behold the fury of our Lord being provoked against the people, struck them with an exceeding great plague.

text.NRSV: So that place was called Kibroth-hattaavah, because there they buried the people who had the craving.
text.Rotherham: And the name of that place was called Kibroth-hattaavah—because there, they buried the people who had lusted,
text.KJV: And he called the name of that place Kibroth–hattaavah: because there they buried the people that lusted.
text.NABRE: So that place was named Kibroth-hattaavah, because it was there that the greedy people were buried.
text.DRB: And that place was called, The Sepulchres of concupiscence: for there they buried the people that had lusted. And departing from the Sepulchres of concupiscence, they came unto Haseroth, and tarried there.

text.NRSV: From Kibroth-hattaavah the people journeyed to Hazeroth.
text.Rotherham: From Kibroth-hattaavah, the people set forward to Hazeroth,—and they remained in Hazeroth.
text.KJV: And the people journeyed from Kibroth–hattaavah unto Hazeroth; and abode at Hazeroth.
text.NABRE: From Kibroth-hattaavah the people set out for Hazeroth, where they stayed.

text.NRSV: While they were at Hazeroth, Miriam and Aaron spoke against Moses because of the Cushite woman whom he had married (for he had indeed married a Cushite woman);
text.Rotherham: And Miriam and Aaron spake against Moses, on account of the Cushite woman whom he had taken,—for, a Cushite woman, had he taken.
text.KJV: And Miriam and Aaron spake against Moses because of the Ethiopian woman whom he had married: for he had married an Ethiopian woman.
text.NABRE: Miriam and Aaron spoke against Moses on the pretext of the Cushite woman he had married; for he had in fact married a Cushite woman.
text.DRB: And Mary and Aaron spake against Moyses, for his wife the Æthiopian,

text.NRSV: and they said, "Has the Lord spoken only through Moses? Has he not spoken through us also?" And the Lord heard it.
text.Rotherham: And they said: Is it only and solely with Moses, that Yahweh hath spoken? With us also, hath he not spoken? And Yahweh heard it.
text.KJV: And they said, Hath the Lord indeed spoken only by Moses? hath he not spoken also by us? And the Lord heard it.
text.NABRE: They complained, “Is it through Moses alone that the Lord has spoken? Has he not spoken through us also?” And the Lord heard this.
text.DRB: and they said: hath our Lord spoken by Moyses only? Hath he not spoken to us also in like manner? Which when our Lord had heard,

text.NRSV: Now the man Moses was very humble, more so than anyone else on the face of the earth.
text.Rotherham: Now, the man Moses, was patient, exceedingly,—above all the sons of earth, who were on the face of the ground.
text.KJV: (Now the man Moses was very meek, above all the men which were upon the face of the earth.)
text.NABRE: Now the man Moses was very humble, more than anyone else on earth.
text.DRB: ( For Moyses was the mildest man above all men that dwelt upon the earth)

text.NRSV: Suddenly the Lord said to Moses, Aaron, and Miriam, "Come out, you three, to the tent of meeting." So the three of them came out.
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh suddenly, unto Moses, and unto Aaron, and unto Miriam, Come out ye three unto the tent of meeting. And they three went out.
text.KJV: And the Lord spake suddenly unto Moses, and unto Aaron, and unto Miriam, Come out ye three unto the tabernacle of the congregation. And they three came out.
text.NABRE: So at once the Lord said to Moses and Aaron and Miriam: Come out, you three, to the tent of meeting. And the three of them went.
text.DRB: immediately he spake to him, and to Aaron and Mary: Go forth you three only to the tabernacle of covenant. And when they were come forth,

text.NRSV: Then the Lord came down in a pillar of cloud, and stood at the entrance of the tent, and called Aaron and Miriam; and they both came forward.
text.Rotherham: Then Yahweh came down in a pillar of cloud, and stood at the entrance of the tent,—and called Aaron and Miriam, and they both went forth.
text.KJV: And the Lord came down in the pillar of the cloud, and stood in the door of the tabernacle, and called Aaron and Miriam: and they both came forth.
text.NABRE: Then the Lord came down in a column of cloud, and standing at the entrance of the tent, called, “Aaron and Miriam.” When both came forward,
text.DRB: our Lord descended in the pillar of a cloud, and stood in the entry of the tabernacle calling Aaron & Mary. Who going unto him,

text.NRSV: And he said, "Hear my words: When there are prophets among you, I the Lord make myself known to them in visions; I speak to them in dreams.
text.Rotherham: Then said he Hear, I beseech you my words. When ye have your prophet, As Yahweh in a vision, will I make myself known, unto him; In a dream, will I speak with him.
text.KJV: And he said, Hear now my words: If there be a prophet among you, I the Lord will make myself known unto him in a vision, and will speak unto him in a dream.
text.NABRE: the Lord said: Now listen to my words: If there are prophets among you, in visions I reveal myself to them, in dreams I speak to them;
text.DRB: he said to them: Hear my words: If there shall be among you a Prophet of the Lord, in vision will I appear to him, or in sleep I will speak to him.

text.NRSV: Not so with my servant Moses; he is entrusted with all my house.
text.Rotherham: Not so, my servant Moses,—In all my house, trusty, is he:
text.KJV: My servant Moses is not so, who is faithful in all mine house.
text.NABRE: Not so with my servant Moses! Throughout my house he is worthy of trust:
text.DRB: But my servant Moyses is not such an one, who in all my house is most faithful:

text.NRSV: With him I speak face to face—clearly, not in riddles; and he beholds the form of the Lord. Why then were you not afraid to speak against my servant Moses?"
text.Rotherham: Mouth to mouth, do I speak with him And plainly—not in dark sayings, And the form of Yahweh, doth he discern,—Wherefore then, were ye not afraid to speak against my servant—against Moses?
text.KJV: With him will I speak mouth to mouth, even apparently, and not in dark speeches; and the similitude of the Lord shall he behold: wherefore then were ye not afraid to speak against my servant Moses?
text.NABRE: face to face I speak to him, plainly and not in riddles. The likeness of the Lord he beholds. Why, then, do you not fear to speak against my servant Moses?
text.DRB: for mouth to mouth I speak to him: and plainly, and not by riddles and figures doth he see the Lord. Why therefore did you not fear to detract from my servant Moyses?

text.NRSV: And the anger of the Lord was kindled against them, and he departed.
text.Rotherham: And the anger of Yahweh kindled upon them and he departed.
text.KJV: And the anger of the Lord was kindled against them; and he departed.
text.NABRE: And so the Lord ’s wrath flared against them, and he departed.
text.DRB: And being wrath against them, he went away:

text.NRSV: When the cloud went away from over the tent, Miriam had become leprous, as white as snow. And Aaron turned towards Miriam and saw that she was leprous.
text.Rotherham: And, when, the cloud, removed from off the tent,—lo! Miriam, leprous, like snow! And Aaron turned unto Miriam and lo! …leprous!
text.KJV: And the cloud departed from off the tabernacle; and, behold, Miriam became leprous, white as snow: and Aaron looked upon Miriam, and, behold, she was leprous.
text.NABRE: Miriam’s Punishment. Now the cloud withdrew from the tent, and there was Miriam, stricken with a scaly infection, white as snow! When Aaron turned toward Miriam and saw her stricken with snow-white scales,
text.DRB: the cloud also departed that was over the tabernacle: and behold Mary appeared white with leprosy as it were snow. And when Aaron had looked on her, and saw her wholly covered with leprosy,

text.NRSV: Then Aaron said to Moses, "Oh, my lord, do not punish us for a sin that we have so foolishly committed.
text.Rotherham: Then said Aaron unto Moses,—Oh my lord, do not I beseech thee lay upon us sin, although we have made ourselves foolish, and although we have sinned.
text.KJV: And Aaron said unto Moses, Alas, my lord, I beseech thee, lay not the sin upon us, wherein we have done foolishly, and wherein we have sinned.
text.NABRE: he said to Moses, “Ah, my lord! Please do not charge us with the sin that we have foolishly committed!
text.DRB: he said to Moyses: I beseech thee my Lord, lay not upon us this sin which we have foolishly committed,

text.NRSV: Do not let her be like one stillborn, whose flesh is half consumed when it comes out of its mother's womb."
text.Rotherham: Let her not, I beseech thee, remain like the still-born,—which when it is born of its mother, the half of its flesh is consumed.
text.KJV: Let her not be as one dead, of whom the flesh is half consumed when he cometh out of his mother’s womb.
text.NABRE: Do not let her be like the stillborn baby that comes forth from its mother’s womb with its flesh half consumed.”
text.DRB: let not this woman be as it were dead, and as an abortive that is cast forth of the mother's womb. Lo now the one half of her flesh is devoured with the leprosy.

text.NRSV: And Moses cried to the Lord, "O God, please heal her."
text.Rotherham: Then Moses made outcry unto Yahweh, saying,—O GOD, I beseech thee grant healing. I beseech thee unto her.
text.KJV: And Moses cried unto the Lord, saying, Heal her now, O God, I beseech thee.
text.NABRE: Then Moses cried to the Lord, “Please, not this! Please, heal her!”
text.DRB: And Moyses cried to our Lord, saying: God, I beseech thee, heal her.

text.NRSV: But the Lord said to Moses, "If her father had but spit in her face, would she not bear her shame for seven days? Let her be shut out of the camp for seven days, and after that she may be brought in again."
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said unto Moses: If, her own father, had, but spat, in her face, would she not, have acknowledged the shame for seven days? Let her shut herself up for seven days, outside the camp, and afterwards, let her be received back.
text.KJV: And the Lord said unto Moses, If her father had but spit in her face, should she not be ashamed seven days? let her be shut out from the camp seven days, and after that let her be received in again.
text.NABRE: But the Lord answered Moses: Suppose her father had spit in her face, would she not bear her shame for seven days? Let her be confined outside the camp for seven days; afterwards she may be brought back.
text.DRB: To whom our Lord answered: If her father had spit upon her face, ought she not to have been ashamed seven days at the least? Let her be separated seven days without the camp, and afterwards she shall be called again.

text.NRSV: So Miriam was shut out of the camp for seven days; and the people did not set out on the march until Miriam had been brought in again.
text.Rotherham: So Miriam shut herself up, outside the camp, for seven days,—and, the people, set not forward, until Miriam had been received back.
text.KJV: And Miriam was shut out from the camp seven days: and the people journeyed not till Miriam was brought in again.
text.NABRE: So Miriam was confined outside the camp for seven days, and the people did not start out again until she was brought back.
text.DRB: Mary therefore was shut forth without the camp seven days: and the people moved not from that place, until Mary was called again.

text.NRSV: After that the people set out from Hazeroth, and camped in the wilderness of Paran.
text.Rotherham: Then afterwards, did the people set forward from Hazeroth, and encamped in the desert of Paran.
text.KJV: And afterward the people removed from Hazeroth, and pitched in the wilderness of Paran.
text.NABRE: After that the people set out from Hazeroth and encamped in the wilderness of Paran.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Moses,
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: The Lord said to Moses:
text.DRB: And the people marched from Haseroth pitching their tents in the desert of Pharan.

text.NRSV: "Send men to spy out the land of Canaan, which I am giving to the Israelites; from each of their ancestral tribes you shall send a man, every one a leader among them."
text.Rotherham: Send for thee men and let them spy out the land of Canaan, which I am giving unto the sons of Israel,—one man each for the tribe of his fathers, shalt thou send, each one a prince among them.
text.KJV: Send thou men, that they may search the land of Canaan, which I give unto the children of Israel: of every tribe of their fathers shall ye send a man, every one a ruler among them.
text.NABRE: Send men to reconnoiter the land of Canaan, which I am giving the Israelites. You shall send one man from each ancestral tribe, every one a leader among them.
text.DRB: And there our Lord spake to Moyses, saying:

text.NRSV: So Moses sent them from the wilderness of Paran, according to the command of the Lord, all of them leading men among the Israelites.
text.Rotherham: So then Moses sent them out of the desert of Paran, at the bidding of Yahweh,—all of them, great men, heads of the sons of Israel, were they.
text.KJV: And Moses by the commandment of the Lord sent them from the wilderness of Paran: all those men were heads of the children of Israel.
text.NABRE: So Moses sent them from the wilderness of Paran, at the direction of the Lord. All of them were leaders among the Israelites.
text.DRB: Send men that may view the Land of Chanaan, which I will give to the children of Israel, one of every tribe, of the Princes.

text.NRSV: These were their names: From the tribe of Reuben, Shammua son of Zaccur;
text.Rotherham: And, these, are their names,—Of the tribe of Reuben, Shammua, son of Zaccur;
text.KJV: And these were their names: of the tribe of Reuben, Shammua the son of Zaccur.
text.NABRE: These were their names: from the tribe of Reuben, Shammua, son of Zaccur;
text.DRB: Moyses did that which our Lord had commanded, from the desert of Pharan sending principal men, whose names be these.

text.NRSV: from the tribe of Simeon, Shaphat son of Hori;
text.Rotherham: Of the tribe of Simeon, Shaphat, son of Hori;
text.KJV: Of the tribe of Simeon, Shaphat the son of Hori.
text.NABRE: from the tribe of Simeon, Shaphat, son of Hori;
text.DRB: Of the tribe of Ruben, Sammua the son of Zechur.

text.NRSV: from the tribe of Judah, Caleb son of Jephunneh;
text.Rotherham: Of the tribe of Judah, Caleb, son of Jephunneh;
text.KJV: Of the tribe of Judah, Caleb the son of Jephunneh.
text.NABRE: from the tribe of Judah, Caleb, son of Jephunneh;
text.DRB: Of the tribe of Simeon, Saphat the son of Huri.

text.NRSV: from the tribe of Issachar, Igal son of Joseph;
text.Rotherham: Of the tribe of Issachar, Igal, son of Joseph;
text.KJV: Of the tribe of Issachar, Igal the son of Joseph.
text.NABRE: from the tribe of Issachar, Igal;
text.DRB: Of the tribe of Juda, Caleb the son of Jephone.

text.NRSV: from the tribe of Ephraim, Hoshea son of Nun;
text.Rotherham: Of the tribe of Ephraim, Hoshea, son of Nun;
text.KJV: Of the tribe of Ephraim, Oshea the son of Nun.
text.NABRE: for the Josephites, from the tribe of Ephraim, Hoshea, son of Nun;
text.DRB: Of the tribe of Issachar, Igal the son of Joseph.

text.NRSV: from the tribe of Benjamin, Palti son of Raphu;
text.Rotherham: Of the tribe of Benjamin, Palti, son of Raphu;
text.KJV: Of the tribe of Benjamin, Palti the son of Raphu.
text.NABRE: from the tribe of Benjamin, Palti, son of Raphu;
text.DRB: Of the tribe of Ephraim, Osee the son of Nun.

text.NRSV: from the tribe of Zebulun, Gaddiel son of Sodi;
text.Rotherham: Of the tribe of Zebulun, Gaddiel, son of Sodi;
text.KJV: Of the tribe of Zebulun, Gaddiel the son of Sodi.
text.NABRE: from the tribe of Zebulun, Gaddiel, son of Sodi;
text.DRB: Of the tribe of Benjamin, Phalti the son of Raphu.

text.NRSV: from the tribe of Joseph (that is, from the tribe of Manasseh), Gaddi son of Susi;
text.Rotherham: Of the tribe of Joseph—of the tribe of Manasseh, Gaddi son of Susi;
text.KJV: Of the tribe of Joseph, namely, of the tribe of Manasseh, Gaddi the son of Susi.
text.NABRE: for the Josephites, from the tribe of Manasseh, Gaddi, son of Susi;
text.DRB: Of the tribe of Zabulon, Geddiel the son of Sodi.

text.NRSV: from the tribe of Dan, Ammiel son of Gemalli;
text.Rotherham: Of the tribe of Dan, Ammiel, son of Gemalli;
text.KJV: Of the tribe of Dan, Ammiel the son of Gemalli.
text.NABRE: from the tribe of Dan, Ammiel, son of Gemalli;
text.DRB: Of the tribe of Joseph, of the sceptre of Manasses, Gaddi, the son of Susi.

text.NRSV: from the tribe of Asher, Sethur son of Michael;
text.Rotherham: Of the tribe of Asher, Sethur, son of Michael;
text.KJV: Of the tribe of Asher, Sethur the son of Michael.
text.NABRE: from the tribe of Asher, Sethur, son of Michael;
text.DRB: Of the tribe of Dan, Ammiel the son of Gemalli.

text.NRSV: from the tribe of Naphtali, Nahbi son of Vophsi;
text.Rotherham: Of the tribe of Naphtali, Nahbi, son of Vophsi;
text.KJV: Of the tribe of Naphtali, Nahbi the son of Vophsi.
text.NABRE: from the tribe of Naphtali, Nahbi, son of Vophsi;
text.DRB: Of the tribe of Aser, Sthur the son of Michael.

text.NRSV: from the tribe of Gad, Geuel son of Machi.
text.Rotherham: Of the tribe of Gad, Geuel, son of Machi.
text.KJV: Of the tribe of Gad, Geuel the son of Machi.
text.NABRE: from the tribe of Gad, Geuel, son of Machi.
text.DRB: Of the tribe of Nephthali, Nahabi the son of Vapsi.

text.NRSV: These were the names of the men whom Moses sent to spy out the land. And Moses changed the name of Hoshea son of Nun to Joshua.
text.Rotherham: These, are the names of the men, whom Moses sent to spy out the land,—but Moses called Hoshea son of Nun, Joshua.
text.KJV: These are the names of the men which Moses sent to spy out the land. And Moses called Oshea the son of Nun Jehoshua.
text.NABRE: These are the names of the men whom Moses sent to reconnoiter the land. But Hoshea, son of Nun, Moses called Joshua.
text.DRB: Of the tribe of Gad, Guel the son of Machi.

text.NRSV: Moses sent them to spy out the land of Canaan, and said to them, "Go up there into the Negeb, and go up into the hill country,
text.Rotherham: So Moses sent them, to spy out the land of Canaan,—and said unto them—Go up, here in the South, Yea ye shall go up the mountain;
text.KJV: And Moses sent them to spy out the land of Canaan, and said unto them, Get you up this way southward, and go up into the mountain:
text.NABRE: In sending them to reconnoiter the land of Canaan, Moses said to them, “Go up there in the Negeb, up into the highlands,
text.DRB: These are the names of the men, whom Moyses sent to view the Land: and he called Osee the son of Nun, Josue.

text.NRSV: and see what the land is like, and whether the people who live in it are strong or weak, whether they are few or many,
text.Rotherham: And shall see the land, what it is,—And the people that dwell thereupon, Whether they are, strong, or weak, Whether they are, few or many;
text.KJV: And see the land, what it is; and the people that dwelleth therein, whether they be strong or weak, few or many;
text.NABRE: and see what kind of land it is and whether the people living there are strong or weak, few or many.
text.DRB: Moyses therefore sent them to view the Land of Chanaan, and said to them: Go up by the south side. And when you shall come to the mountains,

text.NRSV: and whether the land they live in is good or bad, and whether the towns that they live in are unwalled or fortified,
text.Rotherham: And what, the land, is, wherein they dwell, Whether it is, good, or, bad,—And the cities within which they dwell, Whether in camps or in strongholds;
text.KJV: And what the land is that they dwell in, whether it be good or bad; and what cities they be that they dwell in, whether in tents, or in strong holds;
text.NABRE: Is the country in which they live good or bad? Are the towns in which they dwell open or fortified?
text.DRB: view the Land, what it is: and the people that are the inhabitants thereof, whether they be strong or weak: few in number or many:

text.NRSV: and whether the land is rich or poor, and whether there are trees in it or not. Be bold, and bring some of the fruit of the land." Now it was the season of the first ripe grapes.
text.Rotherham: And what the land is, Whether, fat, or, lean, Whether there is timber therein or is not, Then shall ye pluck up courage and take of the fruit of the land. Now the days, were the days of the first-fruits of grapes.
text.KJV: And what the land is, whether it be fat or lean, whether there be wood therein, or not. And be ye of good courage, and bring of the fruit of the land. Now the time was the time of the firstripe grapes.
text.NABRE: Is the soil fertile or barren, wooded or clear? And do your best to get some of the fruit of the land.” It was then the season for early grapes.
text.DRB: the land it-self, whether it be good or bad: what manner of cities, walled or without walls:

text.NRSV: So they went up and spied out the land from the wilderness of Zin to Rehob, near Lebo-hamath.
text.Rotherham: So they went up, and spied out the land,—from the wilderness of Zin unto Rehob at the entering in of Hamath.
text.KJV: So they went up, and searched the land from the wilderness of Zin unto Rehob, as men come to Hamath.
text.NABRE: So they went up and reconnoitered the land from the wilderness of Zin as far as where Rehob adjoins Lebo-hamath.
text.DRB: the ground, fat or barren, woody or without trees. Be of good courage, and bring us of the fruits of the Land. And it was the time when now the first ripe grapes are to be eaten.

text.NRSV: They went up into the Negeb, and came to Hebron; and Ahiman, Sheshai, and Talmai, the Anakites, were there. (Hebron was built seven years before Zoan in Egypt.)
text.Rotherham: Yea they went up in the South and entered as far as Hebron, and there, were Ahiman Sheshai and Talmai descendants of the Anak. Now, Hebron, was built seven years, before Zoan of Egypt.
text.KJV: And they ascended by the south, and came unto Hebron; where Ahiman, Sheshai, and Talmai, the children of Anak, were. (Now Hebron was built seven years before Zoan in Egypt.)
text.NABRE: Going up by way of the Negeb, they reached Hebron, where Ahiman, Sheshai and Talmai, descendants of the Anakim, were. (Now Hebron had been built seven years before Zoan in Egypt.)
text.DRB: And when they were gone up, they viewed the Land from the desert of Sin, unto Rohob as you enter to Emath.

text.NRSV: And they came to the Wadi Eshcol, and cut down from there a branch with a single cluster of grapes, and they carried it on a pole between two of them. They also brought some pomegranates and figs.
text.Rotherham: And they entered as far as the ravine of Eschol, and cut down, from thence a branch with one cluster of grapes, and bare it on a pole between two,—also of the pomegranates and of the figs.
text.KJV: And they came unto the brook of Eshcol, and cut down from thence a branch with one cluster of grapes, and they bare it between two upon a staff; and they brought of the pomegranates, and of the figs.
text.NABRE: They also reached the Wadi Eshcol, where they cut down a branch with a single cluster of grapes on it, which two of them carried on a pole, as well as some pomegranates and figs.
text.DRB: And they went up at the south side, & came to Hebron, where were Achiman and Sisai & Tholmai the sons of Enac, for Hebron was built seven years before Tanis the city of Ægypt.

text.NRSV: That place was called the Wadi Eshcol, because of the cluster that the Israelites cut down from there.
text.Rotherham: That place, was called, The Ravine of Eschol [="Cluster-ravine"]—on account of the cluster, which was cut down from thence by the sons of Israel.
text.KJV: The place was called the brook Eshcol, because of the cluster of grapes which the children of Israel cut down from thence.
text.NABRE: It was because of the cluster the Israelites cut there that they called the place Wadi Eshcol.
text.DRB: And going forward as far as the Torrent of cluster, they cut off a branch with the grapes thereof, which two men carried upon a lever. They took of the pomegranates also & of the figs of that place:

text.NRSV: At the end of forty days they returned from spying out the land.
text.Rotherham: And they returned from spying out the land,—at the end of forty days.
text.KJV: And they returned from searching of the land after forty days.
text.NABRE: Their Report. They returned from reconnoitering the land forty days later.
text.DRB: which was called Nehelescol, that is to say, the Torrent of cluster, for that thence the children of Israel had carried a cluster.

text.NRSV: And they came to Moses and Aaron and to all the congregation of the Israelites in the wilderness of Paran, at Kadesh; they brought back word to them and to all the congregation, and showed them the fruit of the land.
text.Rotherham: So they took their journey, and came in unto Moses and unto Aaron and unto all the assembly of the sons of Israel into the wilderness of Paran, towards Kadesh,—and brought back word—unto them and unto all the assembly, and showed them the fruit of the land.
text.KJV: And they went and came to Moses, and to Aaron, and to all the congregation of the children of Israel, unto the wilderness of Paran, to Kadesh; and brought back word unto them, and unto all the congregation, and shewed them the fruit of the land.
text.NABRE: Proceeding directly to Moses and Aaron and the whole community of the Israelites in the wilderness of Paran at Kadesh, they made a report to them and to the whole community, showing them the fruit of the land.
text.DRB: And the discoverers of the Land returning after forty days, having circuited all the country,

text.NRSV: And they told him, "We came to the land to which you sent us; it flows with milk and honey, and this is its fruit.
text.Rotherham: And they recounted unto him, and said, We entered into the land whither thou didst send us, and surely it doth flow with milk and honey, and, this, is the fruit thereof.
text.KJV: And they told him, and said, We came unto the land whither thou sentest us, and surely it floweth with milk and honey; and this is the fruit of it.
text.NABRE: They told Moses: “We came to the land to which you sent us. It does indeed flow with milk and honey, and here is its fruit.
text.DRB: came to Moyses & Aaron and to all the assembly of the children of Israel into the desert of Pharan; which is in Cades. And speaking to them and to all the multitude they shewed the fruits of the Land:

text.NRSV: Yet the people who live in the land are strong, and the towns are fortified and very large; and besides, we saw the descendants of Anak there.
text.Rotherham: Nevertheless surely mighty, are the people that dwell in the land,—and, the cities, walled in, exceeding great, moreover also, the descendants of the Anak, saw we there.
text.KJV: Nevertheless the people be strong that dwell in the land, and the cities are walled, and very great: and moreover we saw the children of Anak there.
text.NABRE: However, the people who are living in the land are powerful, and the towns are fortified and very large. Besides, we saw descendants of the Anakim there.
text.DRB: and reported, saying: We came into the Land to which thou didst send us, which in very deed floweth with milk and honey, as by these fruits may be known:

text.NRSV: The Amalekites live in the land of the Negeb; the Hittites, the Jebusites, and the Amorites live in the hill country; and the Canaanites live by the sea, and along the Jordan."
text.Rotherham: Amalek, dwelleth in the land of the South; And, the Hittite, and the Jebusite and the Amorite, dwell in the mountain And the Canaanite, dwelleth by the sea, and by the side of the Jordan.
text.KJV: The Amalekites dwell in the land of the south: and the Hittites, and the Jebusites, and the Amorites, dwell in the mountains: and the Canaanites dwell by the sea, and by the coast of Jordan.
text.NABRE: Amalekites live in the region of the Negeb; Hittites, Jebusites and Amorites dwell in the highlands, and Canaanites along the sea and the banks of the Jordan.”
text.DRB: but it hath very strong inhabitants, and cities great and walled. The stock of Enac we saw there.

text.NRSV: But Caleb quieted the people before Moses, and said, "Let us go up at once and occupy it, for we are well able to overcome it."
text.Rotherham: And Caleb stilled the people unto Moses,—and said unto them—Let us go straight up, and possess it, for we shall, surely prevail, against it.
text.KJV: And Caleb stilled the people before Moses, and said, Let us go up at once, and possess it; for we are well able to overcome it.
text.NABRE: Caleb, however, quieted the people before Moses and said, “We ought to go up and seize the land, for we can certainly prevail over it.”
text.DRB: Amalec dwelleth in the south, the Hetheite and the Jebusite and the Amorrhite in the mountains: but the Chananite abideth beside the sea and about the streams of Jordan.

text.NRSV: Then the men who had gone up with him said, "We are not able to go up against this people, for they are stronger than we."
text.Rotherham: But the men who went up with him said, We cannot go up against the people,—for stronger, are they than we.
text.KJV: But the men that went up with him said, We be not able to go up against the people; for they are stronger than we.
text.NABRE: But the men who had gone up with him said, “We cannot attack these people; they are too strong for us.”
text.DRB: Among these things Caleb appeasing the murmuring of the people that rose against Moyses, said: Let us go up and possess the Land because we may obtain it.

text.NRSV: So they brought to the Israelites an unfavorable report of the land that they had spied out, saying, "The land that we have gone through as spies is a land that devours its inhabitants; and all the people that we saw in it are of great size.
text.Rotherham: So they sent forth a rumour of the land which they had spied out, among the sons of Israel, saying,—As touching the land through which we passed to spy it out, it is, land that eateth up them who dwell therein, And all the people that we saw in the midst thereof, were men of great stature.
text.KJV: And they brought up an evil report of the land which they had searched unto the children of Israel, saying, The land, through which we have gone to search it, is a land that eateth up the inhabitants thereof; and all the people that we saw in it are men of a great stature.
text.NABRE: They spread discouraging reports among the Israelites about the land they had reconnoitered, saying, “The land that we went through and reconnoitered is a land that consumes its inhabitants. And all the people we saw there are huge.
text.DRB: But the others, that had been with him, said: No, we are not able to go up to this people, because it is stronger than we.

text.NRSV: There we saw the Nephilim (the Anakites come from the Nephilim); and to ourselves we seemed like grasshoppers, and so we seemed to them."
text.Rotherham: And, there, saw we the giants, sons of Anak [descended] of the giants,—And we were in our own eyes, as grass-hoppers, And, so, were we in their eyes.
text.KJV: And there we saw the giants, the sons of Anak, which come of the giants: and we were in our own sight as grasshoppers, and so we were in their sight.
text.NABRE: There we saw the Nephilim (the Anakim are from the Nephilim); in our own eyes we seemed like mere grasshoppers, and so we must have seemed to them.”
text.DRB: And they detracted from the Land, which they had viewed, before the children of Israel, saying: The Land, which we have viewed, devoureth her inhabitants: the people, that we beheld, is of a tall stature.

text.NRSV: Then all the congregation raised a loud cry, and the people wept that night.
text.Rotherham: Then did all the assembly lift up, and give forth their voice,—and the people wept throughout that night.
text.KJV: And all the congregation lifted up their voice, and cried; and the people wept that night.
text.NABRE: At this, the whole community broke out with loud cries, and the people wept into the night.
text.DRB: Therefore all the Multitude crying out wept that night,

text.NRSV: And all the Israelites complained against Moses and Aaron; the whole congregation said to them, "Would that we had died in the land of Egypt! Or would that we had died in this wilderness!
text.Rotherham: And all the sons of Israel, murmured against Moses and against Aaron,—and all the assembly said unto them—Oh that we had died in the land of Egypt, Or in this wilderness, oh that we had died!
text.KJV: And all the children of Israel murmured against Moses and against Aaron: and the whole congregation said unto them, Would God that we had died in the land of Egypt! or would God we had died in this wilderness!
text.NABRE: All the Israelites grumbled against Moses and Aaron, the whole community saying to them, “If only we had died in the land of Egypt,” or “If only we would die here in the wilderness!
text.DRB: and all the children of Israel murmured against Moyses and Aaron, saying:

text.NRSV: Why is the Lord bringing us into this land to fall by the sword? Our wives and our little ones will become booty; would it not be better for us to go back to Egypt?"
text.Rotherham: Wherefore then is Yahweh bringing us into this land, to fall by the sword? Our wives and little ones, will become a prey! Would it not be well for us to go back towards Egypt?
text.KJV: And wherefore hath the Lord brought us unto this land, to fall by the sword, that our wives and our children should be a prey? were it not better for us to return into Egypt?
text.NABRE: Why is the Lord bringing us into this land only to have us fall by the sword? Our wives and little ones will be taken as spoil. Would it not be better for us to return to Egypt?”
text.DRB: Would God we had died in Ægypt: and in this vast wilderness would God we might die, and that our Lord would not bring us into this Land, lest we fall by the sword, and our wives and children be led captive. Is it not better to return into Ægypt?

text.NRSV: So they said to one another, "Let us choose a captain, and go back to Egypt."
text.Rotherham: And they said, one to another,—Let us appoint a head and let us go back towards Egypt.
text.KJV: And they said one to another, Let us make a captain, and let us return into Egypt.
text.NABRE: So they said to one another, “Let us appoint a leader and go back to Egypt.”
text.DRB: And one said to another: Let us appoint a captain, & let us return into Ægypt.

text.NRSV: Then Moses and Aaron fell on their faces before all the assembly of the congregation of the Israelites.
text.Rotherham: Then did Moses and Aaron fall upon their faces,—before all the convocation of the assembly of the sons of Israel.
text.KJV: Then Moses and Aaron fell on their faces before all the assembly of the congregation of the children of Israel.
text.NABRE: But Moses and Aaron fell prostrate before the whole assembled community of the Israelites;
text.DRB: Which Moyses & Aaron hearing fell flat upon the ground before all the multitude of the children of Israel.

text.NRSV: And Joshua son of Nun and Caleb son of Jephunneh, who were among those who had spied out the land, tore their clothes
text.Rotherham: But, Joshua son of Nun, and Caleb son of Jephunneh, of them who spied out the land, rent their clothes;
text.KJV: And Joshua the son of Nun, and Caleb the son of Jephunneh, which were of them that searched the land, rent their clothes:
text.NABRE: while Joshua, son of Nun, and Caleb, son of Jephunneh, who had been among those that reconnoitered the land, tore their garments
text.DRB: But Josue the son of Nun, and Caleb the son of Jephone, who themselves also had viewed the land, rent their garments,

text.NRSV: and said to all the congregation of the Israelites, "The land that we went through as spies is an exceedingly good land.
text.Rotherham: and spake unto all the assembly of the sons of Israel, saying,—As for the land through which we passed to spy it out, Good, is the land, exceedingly,
text.KJV: And they spake unto all the company of the children of Israel, saying, The land, which we passed through to search it, is an exceeding good land.
text.NABRE: and said to the whole community of the Israelites, “The land which we went through and reconnoitered is an exceedingly good land.
text.DRB: and spake to all the multitude of the children of Israel: The Land, which we have circuited, is very good.

text.NRSV: If the Lord is pleased with us, he will bring us into this land and give it to us, a land that flows with milk and honey.
text.Rotherham: If Yahweh delight in us, then will he bring us into this land, and give it unto us,—A land which doth flow with milk and honey.
text.KJV: If the Lord delight in us, then he will bring us into this land, and give it us; a land which floweth with milk and honey.
text.NABRE: If the Lord is pleased with us, he will bring us in to this land and give it to us, a land which flows with milk and honey.
text.DRB: If our Lord be propitious, he shall bring us into it, and deliver us a ground flowing with milk and honey.

text.NRSV: Only, do not rebel against the Lord; and do not fear the people of the land, for they are no more than bread for us; their protection is removed from them, and the Lord is with us; do not fear them."
text.Rotherham: Only against Yahweh, do not rebel. Do not, ye, fear the people of the land, For our food, they are,—Their shade hath departed, from over them. And Yahweh is with us, Do not fear them!
text.KJV: Only rebel not ye against the Lord, neither fear ye the people of the land; for they are bread for us: their defence is departed from them, and the Lord is with us: fear them not.
text.NABRE: Only do not rebel against the Lord! You need not be afraid of the people of the land, for they are but food for us! Their protection has left them, but the Lord is with us. Do not fear them.”
text.DRB: Be not rebellious against our Lord: neither fear ye the people of this land, for even as bread so we may devour them, all aid is gone from them: our Lord is with us, fear ye not.

text.NRSV: But the whole congregation threatened to stone them. Then the glory of the Lord appeared at the tent of meeting to all the Israelites.
text.Rotherham: But when all the assembly bade stone them with stones, the glory of Yahweh, appeared, in the tent of meeting, unto all the sons of Israel.
