text.KJV: But all the congregation bade stone them with stones. And the glory of the Lord appeared in the tabernacle of the congregation before all the children of Israel.
text.NABRE: The Lord’s Sentence. The whole community threatened to stone them. But the glory of the Lord appeared at the tent of meeting to all the Israelites.
text.DRB: And when all the multitude cried, and would have stoned them, the glory of our Lord appeared over the roof of covenant in the sight of all the children of Israel.

text.NRSV: And the Lord said to Moses, "How long will this people despise me? And how long will they refuse to believe in me, in spite of all the signs that I have done among them?"
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto Moses How long! must this people, despise me? And how long can they not believe in me. in view of all the signs which I have done in their midst?
text.KJV: And the Lord said unto Moses, How long will this people provoke me? and how long will it be ere they believe me, for all the signs which I have shewed among them?
text.NABRE: And the Lord said to Moses: How long will this people spurn me? How long will they not trust me, despite all the signs I have performed among them?
text.DRB: And our Lord said to Moyses: How long will this people detract me? How long will they not believe me in all the signs that I have done before them?

text.NRSV: I will strike them with pestilence and disinherit them, and I will make of you a nation greater and mightier than they."
text.Rotherham: Let me smite them with pestilence, and disinherit them,—And make thee into a nation greater and more mighty than they!
text.KJV: I will smite them with the pestilence, and disinherit them, and will make of thee a greater nation and mightier than they.
text.NABRE: I will strike them with pestilence and disown them. Then I will make of you a nation greater and mightier than they.
text.DRB: I will strike them therefore with pestilence, and will consume them: but thee I will make Prince over a great nation and a stronger than this is.

text.NRSV: But Moses said to the Lord, "Then the Egyptians will hear of it, for in your might you brought up this people from among them,"
text.Rotherham: Then said Moses unto Yahweh,—So shall the Egyptians hear, For thou hast brought up. in thy might, this people out of their midst;
text.KJV: And Moses said unto the Lord, Then the Egyptians shall hear it, (for thou broughtest up this people in thy might from among them;)
text.NABRE: But Moses said to the Lord: “The Egyptians will hear of this, for by your power you brought out this people from among them.
text.DRB: And Moyses said to our Lord: That the Ægyptians, from the midst of whom thou hast brought forth this people,

text.NRSV: and they will tell the inhabitants of this land. They have heard that you, O Lord, are in the midst of this people; for you, O Lord, are seen face to face, and your cloud stands over them and you go in front of them, in a pillar of cloud by day and in a pillar of fire by night.
text.Rotherham: And will tell it unto the inhabitants of this land: They have heard, That, thou, Yahweh, art in the midst of this people,—That eye to eye, is he seen—thou, Yahweh, And thy cloud, is standing over them, And in a pillar of cloud, thou thyself, art going on before them by day, And in a pillar of fire, by night.
text.KJV: And they will tell it to the inhabitants of this land: for they have heard that thou Lord art among this people, that thou Lord art seen face to face, and that thy cloud standeth over them, and that thou goest before them, by day time in a pillar of a cloud, and in a pillar of fire by night.
text.NABRE: They will tell the inhabitants of this land, who have heard that you, Lord, are in the midst of this people; you, Lord, who directly revealed yourself! Your cloud stands over them, and you go before them by day in a column of cloud and by night in a column of fire.
text.DRB: and the inhabitants of this Land, which have heard that thou Lord art among this people, and art seen face to face, and thy cloud protecteth them, and in a pillar of a cloud thou goest before them by day, and in a pillar of fire by night,

text.NRSV: Now if you kill this people all at one time, then the nations who have heard about you will say,
text.Rotherham: As soon, therefore, as thou hast put to death this people as one man, so soon will the nations who have heard thy fame speak saying:
text.KJV: Now if thou shalt kill all this people as one man, then the nations which have heard the fame of thee will speak, saying,
text.NABRE: If now you slay this people all at once, the nations who have heard such reports of you will say,
text.DRB: may hear that thou hast killed so great a multitude as it were one man, and may say:

text.NRSV: 'It is because the Lord was not able to bring this people into the land he swore to give them that he has slaughtered them in the wilderness.'
text.Rotherham: Because Yahweh, was not able, to bring in this people into the land which he had sworn unto them, therefore did he slay them in the desert.
text.KJV: Because the Lord was not able to bring this people into the land which he sware unto them, therefore he hath slain them in the wilderness.
text.NABRE: ‘The Lord was not able to bring this people into the land he swore to give them; that is why he slaughtered them in the wilderness.’
text.DRB: He could not bring in the people into the Land, for which he had sworn, therefore did he kill them in the wilderness.

text.NRSV: And now, therefore, let the power of the Lord be great in the way you promised when you spoke, saying,
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, I beseech thee let the might of My Lord, be magnified,—according as thou didst speak saying:
text.KJV: And now, I beseech thee, let the power of my Lord be great, according as thou hast spoken, saying,
text.NABRE: Now then, may my Lord’s forbearance be great, even as you have said,
text.DRB: Let therefore the strength of our Lord be magnified as thou hast sworn, saying:

text.NRSV: 'The Lord is slow to anger, and abounding in steadfast love, forgiving iniquity and transgression, but by no means clearing the guilty, visiting the iniquity of the parents upon the children to the third and the fourth generation.'
text.Rotherham: Yahweh—slow to anger and great in lovingkindness, Forgiving iniquity and transgression: Though he will not leave, wholly unpunished, Visiting the iniquity of fathers upon sons, Upon a third generation, and upon a fourth.
text.KJV: The Lord is longsuffering, and of great mercy, forgiving iniquity and transgression, and by no means clearing the guilty, visiting the iniquity of the fathers upon the children unto the third and fourth generation.
text.NABRE: ‘The Lord is slow to anger and abounding in kindness, forgiving iniquity and rebellion; yet certainly not declaring the guilty guiltless, but punishing children to the third and fourth generation for their parents’ iniquity.’
text.DRB: The Lord is patient and full of mercy, taking away iniquity and wicked deeds, and leaving no man innocent, which visitest the sins of the fathers upon the children unto the third and fourth generation.

text.NRSV: Forgive the iniquity of this people according to the greatness of your steadfast love, just as you have pardoned this people, from Egypt even until now."
text.Rotherham: Pardon I beseech thee, the iniquity of this people, According to the greatness of thy lovingkindness,—And according as thou hast forgiven this people, from Egypt even thus far.
text.KJV: Pardon, I beseech thee, the iniquity of this people according unto the greatness of thy mercy, and as thou hast forgiven this people, from Egypt even until now.
text.NABRE: Pardon, then, the iniquity of this people in keeping with your great kindness, even as you have forgiven them from Egypt until now.”
text.DRB: Forgive, I beseech thee, the sin of this thy people, according to the greatness of thy mercy, as thou hast been propitious to them since their going out of Ægypt unto this place.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said, "I do forgive, just as you have asked;
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said, I have pardoned according to thy word;
text.KJV: And the Lord said, I have pardoned according to thy word:
text.NABRE: The Lord answered: I pardon them as you have asked.
text.DRB: And our Lord said: I have forgiven it according to thy word.

text.NRSV: nevertheless—as I live, and as all the earth shall be filled with the glory of the Lord—
text.Rotherham: Nevertheless as I live,—and that all the earth may be filled with the glory of Yahweh,
text.KJV: But as truly as I live, all the earth shall be filled with the glory of the Lord.
text.NABRE: Yet, by my life and the Lord ’s glory that fills the whole earth,
text.DRB: Live I: and the whole earth shall be replenished with the glory of the Lord.

text.NRSV: none of the people who have seen my glory and the signs that I did in Egypt and in the wilderness, and yet have tested me these ten times and have not obeyed my voice,
text.Rotherham: Verily, none of the men who have been beholding my glory and my signs, which I have done in Egypt and in the desert,—and have put me to the proof these ten times, and have not hearkened unto my voice,
text.KJV: Because all those men which have seen my glory, and my miracles, which I did in Egypt and in the wilderness, and have tempted me now these ten times, and have not hearkened to my voice;
text.NABRE: of all the people who have seen my glory and the signs I did in Egypt and in the wilderness, and who nevertheless have put me to the test ten times already and have not obeyed me,
text.DRB: But yet all the men that have seen my majesty, and the signs that I have done in Ægypt, and in the wilderness, and have tempted me now ten times, neither have obeyed my voice,

text.NRSV: shall see the land that I swore to give to their ancestors; none of those who despised me shall see it.
text.Rotherham: shall see the land, which I sware to their fathers,—yea, none of my despisers, shall see it.
text.KJV: Surely they shall not see the land which I sware unto their fathers, neither shall any of them that provoked me see it:
text.NABRE: not one shall see the land which I promised on oath to their ancestors. None of those who have spurned me shall see it.
text.DRB: they shall not see the Land for the which I sware to their fathers, neither shall any of them that hath detracted me, behold it.

text.NRSV: But my servant Caleb, because he has a different spirit and has followed me wholeheartedly, I will bring into the land into which he went, and his descendants shall possess it.
text.Rotherham: But my servant Caleb—because there was another spirit with him, and he followed me fully, therefore will I bring him into the land whereinto he entered, and his seed, shall possess it.
text.KJV: But my servant Caleb, because he had another spirit with him, and hath followed me fully, him will I bring into the land whereinto he went; and his seed shall possess it.
text.NABRE: But as for my servant Caleb, because he has a different spirit and follows me unreservedly, I will bring him into the land which he entered, and his descendants shall possess it.
text.DRB: My servant Caleb, who being full of another spirit hath followed me, will I bring in unto this Land which he hath circuited: and his seed shall possess it.

text.NRSV: Now, since the Amalekites and the Canaanites live in the valleys, turn tomorrow and set out for the wilderness by the way to the Red Sea."
text.Rotherham: Now the Amalekites and the Canaanites, are dwelling in the valley,—to-morrow, turn ye away; and set yourselves forward towards the desert by the way of the Red Sea.
text.KJV: (Now the Amalekites and the Canaanites dwelt in the valley.) To morrow turn you, and get you into the wilderness by the way of the Red sea.
text.NABRE: But now, since the Amalekites and Canaanites are living in the valleys, turn away tomorrow and set out into the wilderness by way of the Red Sea road.
text.DRB: Because the Amalecite and the Cananeite dwell in the valleys. Tomorrow remove the camp, and return into the wilderness by the way of the Red sea.

text.NRSV: And the Lord spoke to Moses and to Aaron, saying:
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses and unto Aaron, saying:
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses and unto Aaron, saying,
text.NABRE: The Lord also said to Moses and Aaron:
text.DRB: And our Lord spake to Moyses and Aaron, saying:

text.NRSV: How long shall this wicked congregation complain against me? I have heard the complaints of the Israelites, which they complain against me.
text.Rotherham: How long, as regardeth this evil assembly, are they to be murmuring against me? The murmuring of the sons of Israel which, they, have been murmuring against me, have I heard,
text.KJV: How long shall I bear with this evil congregation, which murmur against me? I have heard the murmurings of the children of Israel, which they murmur against me.
text.NABRE: How long will this wicked community grumble against me? I have heard the grumblings of the Israelites against me.
text.DRB: How long doth this ungracious multitude murmur against me? I have heard the complaints of the children of Israel.

text.NRSV: Say to them, "As I live," says the Lord, "I will do to you the very things I heard you say:
text.Rotherham: Say unto them: As I live, is the oracle of Yahweh: Surely as ye have spoken in mine ears, so, will I do unto you:—
text.KJV: Say unto them, As truly as I live, saith the Lord, as ye have spoken in mine ears, so will I do to you:
text.NABRE: Tell them: “By my life”—oracle of the Lord —“I will do to you just what I have heard you say.
text.DRB: Say therefore to them: live I, sayeth our Lord: According as you have spoken I hearing it, so will I do to you.

text.NRSV: your dead bodies shall fall in this very wilderness; and of all your number, included in the census, from twenty years old and upward, who have complained against me,
text.Rotherham: In this desert, shall your dead bodies fall And none of you who were numbered in all your counting, from twenty years old and upwards,—who have murmured against me,
text.KJV: Your carcases shall fall in this wilderness; and all that were numbered of you, according to your whole number, from twenty years old and upward, which have murmured against me,
text.NABRE: Here in the wilderness your dead bodies shall fall. Of all your men of twenty years or more, enrolled in your registration, who grumbled against me,
text.DRB: In this wilderness shall your carcasses lie. All you that are numbered from twenty years & upward, and have murmured against me,

text.NRSV: not one of you shall come into the land in which I swore to settle you, except Caleb son of Jephunneh and Joshua son of Nun.
text.Rotherham: none of you, shall enter into the land, as to which I uplifted my hand to give you an abode therein,—save Caleb son of Jephunneh, and Joshua, son of Nun:
text.KJV: Doubtless ye shall not come into the land, concerning which I sware to make you dwell therein, save Caleb the son of Jephunneh, and Joshua the son of Nun.
text.NABRE: not one of you shall enter the land where I solemnly swore to settle you, except Caleb, son of Jephunneh, and Joshua, son of Nun.
text.DRB: you shall not enter into the Land, over the which I have lifted up my hand to make you inhabit it, except Caleb the son of Jephone, and Josue the son of Nun.

text.NRSV: But your little ones, who you said would become booty, I will bring in, and they shall know the land that you have despised.
text.Rotherham: whereas, your little ones, of whom ye said, they should become, a prey, I will bring them in, and they shall acknowledge the land which ye refused.
text.KJV: But your little ones, which ye said should be a prey, them will I bring in, and they shall know the land which ye have despised.
text.NABRE: Your little ones, however, who you said would be taken as spoil, I will bring in, and they shall know the land you rejected.
text.DRB: But your little ones, of whom you said, that they should be a prey to the enemies, will I bring in: that they may see the Land that hath misliked you.

text.NRSV: But as for you, your dead bodies shall fall in this wilderness.
text.Rotherham: But, your own dead bodies, shall fall in this desert;
text.KJV: But as for you, your carcases, they shall fall in this wilderness.
text.NABRE: But as for you, your bodies shall fall here in the wilderness,
text.DRB: Your carcasses shall lie in the wilderness.

text.NRSV: And your children shall be shepherds in the wilderness for forty years, and shall suffer for your faithlessness, until the last of your dead bodies lies in the wilderness.
text.Rotherham: and your sons, shall be shepherds in the desert forty years, and shall bear your unchastities,—until your dead bodies are consumed in the desert.
text.KJV: And your children shall wander in the wilderness forty years, and bear your whoredoms, until your carcases be wasted in the wilderness.
text.NABRE: while your children will wander for forty years, suffering for your infidelity, till the last of you lies dead in the wilderness.
text.DRB: Your children shall wander in the desert forty years, and shall bear your fornication, until the carcasses of their fathers be consumed in the desert,

text.NRSV: In accordance with the number of the days in which you spied out the land, forty days, for every day a year, you shall bear your iniquity, forty years, and you shall know my displeasure."
text.Rotherham: By the number of the days wherein ye spied out the land—forty days, each day for a year, shall they bear your iniquities—forty years,—so shall ye know my opposition.
text.KJV: After the number of the days in which ye searched the land, even forty days, each day for a year, shall ye bear your iniquities, even forty years, and ye shall know my breach of promise.
text.NABRE: Corresponding to the number of days you spent reconnoitering the land—forty days—you shall bear your punishment one year for each day: forty years. Thus you will realize what it means to oppose me.
text.DRB: according to the number of the forty days, wherein you viewed the land: a year shall be reputed for a day. And forty years you shall receive your iniquities, and shall know my revenge:

text.NRSV: I the Lord have spoken; surely I will do thus to all this wicked congregation gathered together against me: in this wilderness they shall come to a full end, and there they shall die.
text.Rotherham: I—Yahweh, have spoken, Verily this, will I do to all this evil assembly, who have conspired against me,—In this desert, shall they be consumed And there, shall they die.
text.KJV: I the Lord have said, I will surely do it unto all this evil congregation, that are gathered together against me: in this wilderness they shall be consumed, and there they shall die.
text.NABRE: I, the Lord, have spoken; and I will surely do this to this entire wicked community that conspired against me: here in the wilderness they shall come to their end and there they will die.”
text.DRB: for as I have spoken, so will I do to all this wicked multitude, that hath risen together against me: in this wilderness shall it fail, and die,

text.NRSV: And the men whom Moses sent to spy out the land, who returned and made all the congregation complain against him by bringing a bad report about the land—
text.Rotherham: Now as for the men whom Moses sent to spy out the land,—who returned and caused all the assembly to murmur against him by giving out a rumour against the land,
text.KJV: And the men, which Moses sent to search the land, who returned, and made all the congregation to murmur against him, by bringing up a slander upon the land,
text.NABRE: And the men whom Moses had sent to reconnoiter the land and who on returning had set the whole community grumbling against him by spreading discouraging reports about the land—
text.DRB: Therefore all the men, whom Moyses had sent to view the Land, and which returning had made all the multitude to murmur against him, detracting from the Land that it was nought,

text.NRSV: the men who brought an unfavorable report about the land died by a plague before the Lord.
text.Rotherham: they died—the men who gave out an evil rumour of the land,—by the plague, before Yahweh.
text.KJV: Even those men that did bring up the evil report upon the land, died by the plague before the Lord.
text.NABRE: these men who had spread discouraging reports about the land were struck down by the Lord and died.
text.DRB: died and were strucken in the sight of our Lord.

text.NRSV: But Joshua son of Nun and Caleb son of Jephunneh alone remained alive, of those men who went to spy out the land.
text.Rotherham: But, Joshua son of Nun, and Caleb son of Jephunneh! remained alive—of those men who went to spy out the land.
text.KJV: But Joshua the son of Nun, and Caleb the son of Jephunneh, which were of the men that went to search the land, lived still.
text.NABRE: Only Joshua, son of Nun, and Caleb, son of Jephunneh, survived of all the men who had gone to reconnoiter the land.
text.DRB: But Josue the son of Nun, and Caleb the son of Jephone lived of all them, that had gone to view the Land.

text.NRSV: When Moses told these words to all the Israelites, the people mourned greatly.
text.Rotherham: And Moses spake these words unto all the sons of Israel, and the people were greatly depressed.
text.KJV: And Moses told these sayings unto all the children of Israel: and the people mourned greatly.
text.NABRE: Unsuccessful Invasion. When Moses repeated these words to all the Israelites, the people mourned greatly.
text.DRB: And Moyses spake all these words to all the children of Israel, and the people mourned exceedingly.

text.NRSV: They rose early in the morning and went up to the heights of the hill country, saying, "Here we are. We will go up to the place that the Lord has promised, for we have sinned."
text.Rotherham: So they arose early in the morning, and went up into the top of the mountain, saying,—We are here—therefore will we go up into the place as to which Yahweh hath said that we have sinned.
text.KJV: And they rose up early in the morning, and gat them up into the top of the mountain, saying, Lo, we be here, and will go up unto the place which the Lord hath promised: for we have sinned.
text.NABRE: Early the next morning they started up high into the hill country, saying, “Here we are, ready to go up to the place that the Lord spoke of: for we did wrong.”
text.DRB: And behold very early in the morning rising they went up to the top of the mountain, and said: We are ready to go up to the place, whereof our Lord hath spoken: for we have sinned.

text.NRSV: But Moses said, "Why do you disobey the command of the Lord? It will not succeed.
text.Rotherham: But Moses said, Wherefore is it that, ye, are going beyond the bidding of Yahweh,—since that cannot prosper?
text.KJV: And Moses said, Wherefore now do ye transgress the commandment of the Lord? but it shall not prosper.
text.NABRE: But Moses said, “Why are you now transgressing the Lord ’s order? This cannot succeed.
text.DRB: To whom Moyses said: Why transgress you the word of our Lord, which shall not succeed prosperously with you?

text.NRSV: Do not go up, for the Lord is not with you; do not let yourselves be struck down before your enemies.
text.Rotherham: Do not go up, For Yahweh is not in your midst,—Lest ye be smitten down before your enemies.
text.KJV: Go not up, for the Lord is not among you; that ye be not smitten before your enemies.
text.NABRE: Do not go up, because the Lord is not in your midst; do not allow yourself to be struck down by your enemies.
text.DRB: Go not up, for our Lord is not with you: lest you fall before your enemies.

text.NRSV: For the Amalekites and the Canaanites will face you there, and you shall fall by the sword; because you have turned back from following the Lord, the Lord will not be with you."
text.Rotherham: For, the Amalekites and the Canaanites, are there before you, So shall ye fall by the sword,—For inasmuch as ye have turned back from following Yahweh, Therefore will Yahweh not be with you.
text.KJV: For the Amalekites and the Canaanites are there before you, and ye shall fall by the sword: because ye are turned away from the Lord, therefore the Lord will not be with you.
text.NABRE: For there the Amalekites and Canaanites will face you, and you will fall by the sword. You have turned back from following the Lord; therefore the Lord will not be with you.”
text.DRB: The Amalecite and the Chananite are before you, by whose sword you shall fall, for that you would not consent to our Lord, neither will our Lord be with you.

text.NRSV: But they presumed to go up to the heights of the hill country, even though the ark of the covenant of the Lord, and Moses, had not left the camp.
text.Rotherham: But they presumptuously ascended into the top of the mountain,—though, neither, the ark of the covenant of Yahweh nor Moses moved out of the midst of the camp.
text.KJV: But they presumed to go up unto the hill top: nevertheless the ark of the covenant of the Lord, and Moses, departed not out of the camp.
text.NABRE: Yet they dared to go up high into the hill country, even though neither the ark of the covenant of the Lord nor Moses left the camp.
text.DRB: But they being blinded went up to the top of the mountain. But the ark of the testament of our Lord & Moyses departed not from the camp.

text.NRSV: Then the Amalekites and the Canaanites who lived in that hill country came down and defeated them, pursuing them as far as Hormah.
text.Rotherham: So ten the Amalekites and the Canaanites, that dwelt in that mountain, came down,—and smote them and routed them even unto Hormah.
text.KJV: Then the Amalekites came down, and the Canaanites which dwelt in that hill, and smote them, and discomfited them, even unto Hormah.
text.NABRE: And the Amalekites and Canaanites who dwelt in that hill country came down and defeated them, beating them back as far as Hormah.
text.DRB: And the Amalecite came down and the Cananeite, that dwelt in the mountain: and striking and hewing them, pursued them as far as Horma.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: The Lord spoke to Moses:
text.DRB: And our Lord spake to Moyses, saying:

text.NRSV: Speak to the Israelites and say to them: When you come into the land you are to inhabit, which I am giving you,
text.Rotherham: Speak unto the sons of Israel, and thou shall say unto them,—When ye shall enter into the land of your dwelling-places, which, I, am giving unto you;
text.KJV: Speak unto the children of Israel, and say unto them, When ye be come into the land of your habitations, which I give unto you,
text.NABRE: Speak to the Israelites and say to them: When you enter the land that I am giving you for your settlements,
text.DRB: Speak to the children of Israel, and thou shalt say to them: When you shall be entered into the Land of your habitation, which I will give you,

text.NRSV: and you make an offering by fire to the Lord from the herd or from the flock—whether a burnt offering or a sacrifice, to fulfill a vow or as a freewill offering or at your appointed festivals—to make a pleasing odor for the Lord,
text.Rotherham: and ye would offer, as an altar-flame unto Yahweh an ascending-offering or a sacrifice, for celebrating a vow or as a freewill-offering, or in your appointed seasons,—by way of offering a satisfying odour unto Yahweh, from the herd or from the flock,
text.KJV: And will make an offering by fire unto the Lord, a burnt offering, or a sacrifice in performing a vow, or in a freewill offering, or in your solemn feasts, to make a sweet savour unto the Lord, of the herd, or of the flock:
text.NABRE: if you make to the Lord an oblation from the herd or from the flock—either a burnt offering or a sacrifice, to fulfill a vow, or as a voluntary offering, or for one of your festivals—to produce a pleasing aroma for the Lord,
text.DRB: and shall make oblation to the Lord for an holocaust, or victim, paying vows, or voluntarily offering gifts, or in your solemnities burning a sweet savour unto the Lord, of oxen or of sheep:

text.NRSV: then whoever presents such an offering to the Lord shall present also a grain offering, one-tenth of an ephah of choice flour mixed with one-fourth of a hin of oil.
text.Rotherham: then shall he that bringeth near his oblation bring near unto Yahweh a meal-offering, of fine meal a tenth part [of an ephah] overflowed with the fourth part of a hin of oil;
text.KJV: Then shall he that offereth his offering unto the Lord bring a meat offering of a tenth deal of flour mingled with the fourth part of an hin of oil.
text.NABRE: the one presenting the offering shall also present to the Lord a grain offering, a tenth of a measure of bran flour mixed with a fourth of a hin of oil,
text.DRB: whosoever immolateth the victim, shall offer a sacrifice of flour, the tenth part of an ephi tempered with oil, which shall have in measure the fourth part of an hin:

text.NRSV: Moreover, you shall offer one-fourth of a hin of wine as a drink offering with the burnt offering or the sacrifice, for each lamb.
text.Rotherham: wine also for a drink-offering, the fourth part of a hin, shalt thou offer with the ascending-offering or with the sacrifice,—for each he-lamb.
text.KJV: And the fourth part of an hin of wine for a drink offering shalt thou prepare with the burnt offering or sacrifice, for one lamb.
text.NABRE: as well as wine for a libation, a fourth of a hin. You will do this with the burnt offering or the sacrifice, for each lamb.
text.DRB: and wine of the same measure to pour the libaments shall he give for the holocaust or for the victim. For every lamb

text.NRSV: For a ram, you shall offer a grain offering, two-tenths of an ephah of choice flour mixed with one-third of a hin of oil;
text.Rotherham: Or, with a ram, thou shalt offer a meal-offering, of fine meal, two tenths,—overflowed with oil, the third of a hin;
text.KJV: Or for a ram, thou shalt prepare for a meat offering two tenth deals of flour mingled with the third part of an hin of oil.
text.NABRE: Alternatively for a ram you shall make a grain offering of two tenths of a measure of bran flour mixed with a third of a hin of oil,
text.DRB: and ram there shall be a sacrifice of flour of two tenths, which shall be tempered with oil the third part of an hin:

text.NRSV: and as a drink offering you shall offer one-third of a hin of wine, a pleasing odor to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: wine also, for a drink-offering, the third of a hin, shalt thou bring near as a satisfying odour unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: And for a drink offering thou shalt offer the third part of an hin of wine, for a sweet savour unto the Lord.
text.NABRE: and for a libation, a third of a hin of wine, thereby presenting a pleasing aroma to the Lord.
text.DRB: and wine for the libament, the third part of the same measure, shall he offer for a sweet savour to the Lord.

text.NRSV: When you offer a bull as a burnt offering or a sacrifice, to fulfill a vow or as an offering of well-being to the Lord,
text.Rotherham: And when thou wouldest offer a choice young bullock as an ascending-offering or sacrifice,—for celebrating a vow or as a peace-offering unto Yahweh,
text.KJV: And when thou preparest a bullock for a burnt offering, or for a sacrifice in performing a vow, or peace offerings unto the Lord:
text.NABRE: If you make an offering from the herd—either a burnt offering, or a sacrifice, to fulfill a vow, or as a communion offering to the Lord,
text.DRB: But when thou makest an holocaust or host of oxen, to fulfill thy vow, or for pacific victims,

text.NRSV: then you shall present with the bull a grain offering, three-tenths of an ephah of choice flour mixed with half a hin of oil,
text.Rotherham: then shall he bring near with his choice young bullock, a meal-offering, of fine meal three tenths,—overflowed with half a hin of oil;
text.KJV: Then shall he bring with a bullock a meat offering of three tenth deals of flour mingled with half an hin of oil.
text.NABRE: with it a grain offering of three tenths of a measure of bran flour mixed with half a hin of oil will be presented;
text.DRB: thou shalt give for every oxe three tenths of flour tempered with oil, which shall have half the measure of a hin:

text.NRSV: and you shall present as a drink offering half a hin of wine, as an offering by fire, a pleasing odor to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: wine also, shalt thou bring near for a drink-offering, half a hin,—for an altar-flame of satisfying odour unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: And thou shalt bring for a drink offering half an hin of wine, for an offering made by fire, of a sweet savour unto the Lord.
text.NABRE: and you will present for a libation, half a hin of wine—a sweet-smelling oblation to the Lord.
text.DRB: and wine to pour libaments of the same measure for an oblation of most sweet savour to the Lord.

text.NRSV: Thus it shall be done for each ox or ram, or for each of the male lambs or the kids.
text.Rotherham: Thus and thus, shall it be done, for each ox, and for each ram,—and for each young one among the sheep, or among the goats:
text.KJV: Thus shall it be done for one bullock, or for one ram, or for a lamb, or a kid.
text.NABRE: The same is to be done for each ox, ram, lamb or goat.
text.DRB: So shalt thou do

text.NRSV: According to the number that you offer, so you shall do with each and every one.
text.Rotherham: according to the number which ye shall offer, thus and thus, shall ye do for each one according to their number.
text.KJV: According to the number that ye shall prepare, so shall ye do to every one according to their number.
text.NABRE: Whatever the number you offer, do the same for each of them.
text.DRB: for every oxe and ram and lamb & buck goat.

text.NRSV: Every citizen shall do thus in presenting an offering by fire, a pleasing odor to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Every one born in the land, thus and thus, shall offer these things,—when bringing near an altar-flame of a satisfying odour unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: All that are born of the country shall do these things after this manner, in offering an offering made by fire, of a sweet savour unto the Lord.
text.NABRE: All the native-born shall make these offerings in this way, whenever they present a sweet-smelling oblation to the Lord.
text.DRB: As well they that are born in the country as the strangers

text.NRSV: An alien who lives with you, or who takes up permanent residence among you, and wishes to offer an offering by fire, a pleasing odor to the Lord, shall do as you do.
text.Rotherham: And when there shall sojourn with you a sojourner, or one who is in your midst to your generations, and he would offer an altar-flame of a satisfying odour unto Yahweh, as ye offer, so, shall he offer,
text.KJV: And if a stranger sojourn with you, or whosoever be among you in your generations, and will offer an offering made by fire, of a sweet savour unto the Lord; as ye do, so he shall do.
text.NABRE: Likewise, in any future generation, any alien residing with you or anyone else in your midst, who presents an oblation of pleasing aroma to the Lord, must do as you do.
text.DRB: after one rite shall offer sacrifices.

text.NRSV: As for the assembly, there shall be for both you and the resident alien a single statute, a perpetual statute throughout your generations; you and the alien shall be alike before the Lord.
text.Rotherham: In the convocation, one statute, shall there be for you and for the sojourner who sojourneth,—an age-abiding statute unto your generations, as ye are, so, the sojourner, shall be before Yahweh:
text.KJV: One ordinance shall be both for you of the congregation, and also for the stranger that sojourneth with you, an ordinance for ever in your generations: as ye are, so shall the stranger be before the Lord.
text.NABRE: There is but one statute for you and for the resident alien, a perpetual statute throughout your generations. You and the resident alien will be alike before the Lord;
text.DRB: There shall be all one precept & judgment as well to yourselves as to the strangers of the land.

text.NRSV: You and the alien who resides with you shall have the same law and the same ordinance.
text.Rotherham: one law and one regulation, shall there be for yourselves and for the sojourner that sojourneth with you.
text.KJV: One law and one manner shall be for you, and for the stranger that sojourneth with you.
text.NABRE: you and the alien residing with you will have the same rule and the same application of it.
text.DRB: Our Lord spake to Moyses, saying:

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: The Lord spoke to Moses:
text.DRB: Speak to the children of Israel, & thou shalt say to them:

text.NRSV: Speak to the people of Israel and say to them: When you come into the land to which I bring you,
text.Rotherham: Speak unto the sons of Israel, and thou shalt say unto them,—When ye enter into the land whereinto I, am bringing you,
text.KJV: Speak unto the children of Israel, and say unto them, When ye come into the land whither I bring you,
text.NABRE: Speak to the Israelites and say to them: When you enter the land into which I am bringing you
text.DRB: When you are come into the land, which I will give you,

text.NRSV: and when you eat of the bread of the land, you shall present a donation to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: then shall it be, when ye eat the bread of the land, that ye shall heave up a heave-offering unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: Then it shall be, that, when ye eat of the bread of the land, ye shall offer up an heave offering unto the Lord.
text.NABRE: and eat of the bread of the land, you shall offer the Lord a contribution.
text.DRB: and shall eat of the breads of that country, you shall separate first fruits to the Lord

text.NRSV: From your first batch of dough you shall present a loaf as a donation; you shall present it like the donation from the threshing floor.
text.Rotherham: Of the first of your meal, shall ye heave up, a cake, as a heave-offering,—like the heave-offering of grain, so shall ye heave it.
text.KJV: Ye shall offer up a cake of the first of your dough for an heave offering: as ye do the heave offering of the threshingfloor, so shall ye heave it.
text.NABRE: A round loaf from your first batch of dough you shall offer as a contribution. Just like a contribution from the threshing floor you shall offer it.
text.DRB: of your meats. As of your barn floors you separate first fruits,

text.NRSV: Throughout your generations you shall give to the Lord a donation from the first of your batch of dough.
text.Rotherham: Of the first of your meal, shall ye give unto Yahweh a heave-offering,—unto your generations.
text.KJV: Of the first of your dough ye shall give unto the Lord an heave offering in your generations.
text.NABRE: Throughout your generations you shall give a contribution to the Lord from your first batch of dough. Purification Offerings.
text.DRB: so of your pasts shall you give first fruits to the Lord.

text.NRSV: But if you unintentionally fail to observe all these commandments that the Lord has spoken to Moses—
text.Rotherham: And when ye shall make a mistake, and not observe all these commandments,—which Yahweh hath spoken unto Moses;
text.KJV: And if ye have erred, and not observed all these commandments, which the Lord hath spoken unto Moses,
text.NABRE: If through inadvertence you fail to do any of these commandments which the Lord has given to Moses—
text.DRB: And if through ignorance you omit any of these things, which the Lord hath spoken to Moyses,

text.NRSV: everything that the Lord has commanded you by Moses, from the day the Lord gave commandment and thereafter, throughout your generations—
text.Rotherham: even all that Yahweh hath given unto you in command, by the hand of Moses,—from the day that Yahweh gave command, and onward unto your generations,
text.KJV: Even all that the Lord hath commanded you by the hand of Moses, from the day that the Lord commanded Moses, and henceforward among your generations;
text.NABRE: anything the Lord commanded you through Moses from the time the Lord first gave the command down through your generations—
text.DRB: and by him hath commanded you, from the day that he began to command and so forward,

text.NRSV: if it was done unintentionally without the knowledge of the congregation, the whole congregation shall offer one young bull for a burnt offering, a pleasing odor to the Lord, together with its grain offering and its drink offering, according to the ordinance, and one male goat for a sin offering.
text.Rotherham: then shall it be—if, any from the eyes of the assembly, it hath been done by mistake, that all the assembly shall offer one choice young bullock for an ascending-sacrifice as a satisfying odour unto Yahweh, with the meal-offering thereof and the drink-offering thereof, according to the regulation,—and one young he-goat as a sin-bearer.
text.KJV: Then it shall be, if ought be committed by ignorance without the knowledge of the congregation, that all the congregation shall offer one young bullock for a burnt offering, for a sweet savour unto the Lord, with his meat offering, and his drink offering, according to the manner, and one kid of the goats for a sin offering.
text.NABRE: if it was done inadvertently without the community’s knowledge, the whole community shall sacrifice one bull from the herd as a burnt offering of pleasing aroma to the Lord, along with its prescribed grain offering and libation, as well as one he-goat as a purification offering.
text.DRB: and the multitude have forgotten to do it: they shall offer a calf out of the herd, an holocaust for a most sweet savour to the Lord, and the sacrifice and libaments thereof, as the ceremonies require, and a buck goat for sin:

text.NRSV: The priest shall make atonement for all the congregation of the Israelites, and they shall be forgiven; it was unintentional, and they have brought their offering, an offering by fire to the Lord, and their sin offering before the Lord, for their error.
text.Rotherham: So shall the priest put a propitiatory, covering over all the assembly of the sons of Israel and pardon shall be granted unto them,—because a mistake, it was, they, therefore have brought in their offering—an altar-flame unto Yahweh and their sin-bearer before Yahweh for their mistake:
text.KJV: And the priest shall make an atonement for all the congregation of the children of Israel, and it shall be forgiven them; for it is ignorance: and they shall bring their offering, a sacrifice made by fire unto the Lord, and their sin offering before the Lord, for their ignorance:
text.NABRE: Then the priest shall make atonement for the whole Israelite community; and they will be forgiven, since it was inadvertence, and for their inadvertence they have brought their offering: an oblation to the Lord as well as their purification offering before the Lord.
text.DRB: and the Priest shall pray for all the multitude of the children of Israel: and it shall be forgiven them, because they sinned not wittingly, offering notwithstanding burnt sacrifice to the Lord for themselves and for their sin and error:

text.NRSV: All the congregation of the Israelites shall be forgiven, as well as the aliens residing among them, because the whole people was involved in the error.
text.Rotherham: and pardon shall be granted unto all the assembly of the sons of Israel and unto the sojourner that sojourneth in their midst,—for, in the case of all the people, it was, by mistake.
text.KJV: And it shall be forgiven all the congregation of the children of Israel, and the stranger that sojourneth among them; seeing all the people were in ignorance.
text.NABRE: Not only the whole Israelite community but also the aliens residing among you shall be forgiven, since the inadvertent fault affects all the people.
text.DRB: and it shall be forgiven all the people of the children of Israel, and the strangers, that sojourn among them: because it is the fault of all the people through ignorance.

text.NRSV: An individual who sins unintentionally shall present a female goat a year old for a sin offering.
text.Rotherham: And if, any one person, shall sin by mistake, then shall he bring near a she-goat of the first year as a sin-bearer;
text.KJV: And if any soul sin through ignorance, then he shall bring a she goat of the first year for a sin offering.
text.NABRE: If it is an individual who sins inadvertently, this person shall bring a yearling she-goat as a purification offering.
text.DRB: But if one soul shall sin unwitting, he shall offer a she goat of a year old for his sin:

text.NRSV: And the priest shall make atonement before the Lord for the one who commits an error, when it is unintentional, to make atonement for the person, who then shall be forgiven.
text.Rotherham: and the priest shall put a propitiatory-covering over the person who hath made the mistake, when he hath sinned by mistake before Yahweh, even put a propitiatory-covering over him, and pardon shall be granted him.
text.KJV: And the priest shall make an atonement for the soul that sinneth ignorantly, when he sinneth by ignorance before the Lord, to make an atonement for him; and it shall be forgiven him.
text.NABRE: And the priest shall make atonement before the Lord for the one who erred, since the sin was inadvertent, making atonement for the person to secure forgiveness.
text.DRB: and the Priest shall pray for him, because he sinned unwitting before the Lord: and he shall obtain him pardon, and it shall be forgiven him.

text.NRSV: For citizen and alien who reside among you there shall be one law for anyone who does anything in error.
text.Rotherham: For the native born among the sons of Israel, and for the sojourner that sojourneth in their midst, one law, shall there be unto you—for acting by mistake.
text.KJV: Ye shall have one law for him that sinneth through ignorance, both for him that is born among the children of Israel, and for the stranger that sojourneth among them.
text.NABRE: You shall have but one rule for the person who sins inadvertently, whether a native-born Israelite or an alien residing among you.
text.DRB: As well to them that are born in the country as to the strangers one law shall be for all, that sin by ignorance.

text.NRSV: But whoever acts high-handedly, whether a native or an alien, affronts the Lord, and shall be cut off from among the people.
text.Rotherham: But, as for the person who acteth with a high hand—of the native born or of the sojourners, Yahweh himself, he, reproacheth,—therefore shall that person be cut off out of the midst of his people.
text.KJV: But the soul that doeth ought presumptuously, whether he be born in the land, or a stranger, the same reproacheth the Lord; and that soul shall be cut off from among his people.
text.NABRE: But anyone who acts defiantly, whether a native or an alien, reviles the Lord, and shall be cut off from among the people.
text.DRB: But the soul, that through pride committeth any thing, whether he be born in the country, or a stranger, (because he hath been rebellious against the Lord) shall perish out of his people:

text.NRSV: Because of having despised the word of the Lord and broken his commandment, such a person shall be utterly cut off and bear the guilt.
text.Rotherham: Because the word of Yahweh, hath he despised, and his commandment, hath he broken, that person shall be surely cut off, his iniquity, is in himself.
text.KJV: Because he hath despised the word of the Lord, and hath broken his commandment, that soul shall utterly be cut off; his iniquity shall be upon him.
text.NABRE: For having despised the word of the Lord and broken his commandment, he must be cut off entirely and bear the punishment.
text.DRB: for he hath contemned the word of the Lord, and made his precept of no effect: therefore shall he be destroyed, and shall bear his iniquity.

text.NRSV: When the Israelites were in the wilderness, they found a man gathering sticks on the sabbath day.
text.Rotherham: While the sons of Israel were in the desert, they found a man gathering sticks on the sabbath day.
text.KJV: And while the children of Israel were in the wilderness, they found a man that gathered sticks upon the sabbath day.
text.NABRE: The Sabbath-breaker. While the Israelites were in the wilderness, a man was discovered gathering wood on the sabbath day.
text.DRB: And it came to pass, when the children of Israel were in the wilderness, and had found a man gathering sticks on the Sabbath day,

text.NRSV: Those who found him gathering sticks brought him to Moses, Aaron, and to the whole congregation.
text.Rotherham: And they who found him gathering sticks, brought him near, unto Moses and unto Aaron and unto all the assembly.
text.KJV: And they that found him gathering sticks brought him unto Moses and Aaron, and unto all the congregation.
text.NABRE: Those who caught him at it brought him to Moses and Aaron and the whole community.
text.DRB: they presented him to Moyses and Aaron and the whole multitude.

text.NRSV: They put him in custody, because it was not clear what should be done to him.
text.Rotherham: And they put him in ward,—because it was not clear what they should do unto him.
text.KJV: And they put him in ward, because it was not declared what should be done to him.
text.NABRE: But they put him in custody, for there was no clear decision as to what should be done with him.
text.DRB: Who shut him into prison, not knowing what they should do with him.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said to Moses, "The man shall be put to death; all the congregation shall stone him outside the camp."
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto Moses, The man shall be, put to death,—all the assembly, stoning him with stones, outside the camp.
text.KJV: And the Lord said unto Moses, The man shall be surely put to death: all the congregation shall stone him with stones without the camp.
text.NABRE: Then the Lord said to Moses: This man shall be put to death; let the whole community stone him outside the camp.
text.DRB: And our Lord said to Moyses, dying let this man die, let all the multitude stone him without the camp.

text.NRSV: The whole congregation brought him outside the camp and stoned him to death, just as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: So then all the assembly, put him forth, outside the camp, and stoned him with stones, and he died,—As Yahweh commanded Moses.
text.KJV: And all the congregation brought him without the camp, and stoned him with stones, and he died; as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.NABRE: So the whole community led him outside the camp and stoned him to death, as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.DRB: And when they had brought him out, they stoned him, and he died as our Lord had commanded.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Moses:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses saying:
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: Tassels on the Cloak. The Lord said to Moses:
text.DRB: Our Lord also said to Moyses:

text.NRSV: Speak to the Israelites, and tell them to make fringes on the corners of their garments throughout their generations and to put a blue cord on the fringe at each corner.
text.Rotherham: Speak unto the sons of Israel, and thou shalt say unto them, That they shall make them fringes on the corners of their garments unto their generations—and shall put upon the fringe of the corner a cord of blue:
text.KJV: Speak unto the children of Israel, and bid them that they make them fringes in the borders of their garments throughout their generations, and that they put upon the fringe of the borders a ribband of blue:
text.NABRE: Speak to the Israelites and tell them that throughout their generations they are to make tassels for the corners of their garments, fastening a violet cord to each corner.
text.DRB: Speak to the children of Israel, and thou shalt say to them, that they make themselves fringes in the corners of their garments, putting in them ribands of hyacinth:

text.NRSV: You have the fringe so that, when you see it, you will remember all the commandments of the Lord and do them, and not follow the lust of your own heart and your own eyes.
text.Rotherham: so shall they serve you as fringes, and when ye see then, then shall ye call to mind all the commandments of Yahweh, and shall do them,—and shall not spy out, after your own hearts, and after your own eyes for things, after which, ye, are ready to go unchastely away:
text.KJV: And it shall be unto you for a fringe, that ye may look upon it, and remember all the commandments of the Lord, and do them; and that ye seek not after your own heart and your own eyes, after which ye use to go a whoring:
text.NABRE: When you use these tassels, the sight of the cord will remind you of all the commandments of the Lord and you will do them, without prostituting yourself going after the desires of your hearts and your eyes.
text.DRB: which when they shall see, they may remember all the commandments of the Lord, and not follow their own cogitations and eyes fornicating after diverse things,

text.NRSV: So you shall remember and do all my commandments, and you shall be holy to your God.
text.Rotherham: to the intent ye may call to mind, and do all my commandments, and be holy unto your God.
text.KJV: That ye may remember, and do all my commandments, and be holy unto your God.
text.NABRE: Thus you will remember to do all my commandments and you will be holy to your God.
text.DRB: but rather mindful of the precepts of the Lord may do them, and be holy to their God.

text.NRSV: I am the Lord your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt, to be your God: I am the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: I—Yahweh, am your God—who brought you forth out of the land of Egypt, to become your God,—I,—Yahweh, am your God.
text.KJV: I am the Lord your God, which brought you out of the land of Egypt, to be your God: I am the Lord your God.
text.NABRE: I, the Lord, am your God who brought you out of the land of Egypt to be your God: I, the Lord your God.
text.DRB: I the Lord your God, that brought you out of the Land of Ægypt, that I might be your God.

text.NRSV: Now Korah son of Izhar son of Kohath son of Levi, along with Dathan and Abiram sons of Eliab, and On son of Peleth—descendants of Reuben—took
text.Rotherham: Then took Korah, son of Izhar son of Kohath son of Levi,—and Dathan and Abiram sons of Eliab, and On, son of Peleth sons of Reuben;
text.KJV: Now Korah, the son of Izhar, the son of Kohath, the son of Levi, and Dathan and Abiram, the sons of Eliab, and On, the son of Peleth, sons of Reuben, took men:
text.NABRE: Korah, son of Izhar, son of Kohath, son of Levi, and the Reubenites Dathan and Abiram, sons of Eliab, and On, son of Peleth, son of Reuben took
text.DRB: And behold Core the son of Isaar, the son of Caath, the son of Levi, and Dathan and Abiron the sons of Eliab, Hon also the son of Pheleth of the children of Ruben,

text.NRSV: two hundred fifty Israelite men, leaders of the congregation, chosen from the assembly, well-known men, and they confronted Moses.
text.Rotherham: and rose up before Moses, with certain men of the sons of Israel, two hundred and fifty,—princes of assembly, called to the stated meeting, men of renown;
text.KJV: And they rose up before Moses, with certain of the children of Israel, two hundred and fifty princes of the assembly, famous in the congregation, men of renown:
text.NABRE: two hundred and fifty Israelites who were leaders in the community, members of the council and men of note, and confronted Moses.
text.DRB: rose against Moyses, & other of the children of Israel, two hundred fifty men, princes of the synagogue, and which in the time of assembly were called by name.

text.NRSV: They assembled against Moses and against Aaron, and said to them, "You have gone too far! All the congregation are holy, every one of them, and the Lord is among them. So why then do you exalt yourselves above the assembly of the Lord?"
text.Rotherham: and gathered themselves together against Moses and against Aaron and said unto them Too much for you, when, all the assembly, are, all of them, holy, and in the midst of them, is Yahweh,—Wherefore, then, should ye set yourselves up, above the convocation of Yahweh?
text.KJV: And they gathered themselves together against Moses and against Aaron, and said unto them, Ye take too much upon you, seeing all the congregation are holy, every one of them, and the Lord is among them: wherefore then lift ye up yourselves above the congregation of the Lord?
text.NABRE: Holding an assembly against Moses and Aaron, they said, “You go too far! The whole community, all of them, are holy; the Lord is in their midst. Why then should you set yourselves over the Lord ’s assembly?”
text.DRB: And when they had stood up against Moyses and Aaron, they said: Let it suffice you, that all the multitude consisteth of holy ones, and our Lord is among them: Why lift you up yourselves above the people of our Lord?

text.NRSV: When Moses heard it, he fell on his face.
text.Rotherham: And, when Moses heard it, he fell upon his face.
text.KJV: And when Moses heard it, he fell upon his face:
text.NABRE: When Moses heard this, he fell prostrate.
text.DRB: Which when Moyses had heard, he fell flat on his face:

text.NRSV: Then he said to Korah and all his company, "In the morning the Lord will make known who is his, and who is holy, and who will be allowed to approach him; the one whom he will choose he will allow to approach him.
text.Rotherham: Then spake he unto Korah, and unto all his assembly, saying, In the morning, will Yahweh make known who are his, and who is the holy one and will cause him to come near unto himself,—yea him whom he shall choose, will he cause to come near unto himself.
text.KJV: And he spake unto Korah and unto all his company, saying, Even to morrow the Lord will shew who are his, and who is holy; and will cause him to come near unto him: even him whom he hath chosen will he cause to come near unto him.
text.NABRE: Then he said to Korah and to all his faction, “May the Lord make known tomorrow morning who belongs to him and who is the holy one and whom he will have draw near to him! The one whom he chooses, he will have draw near to him.
text.DRB: and speaking to Core and all the multitude, he said: In the morning our Lord will make it known who pertain to him, and the holy he will join to himself: and whom he shall choose, they shall approach to him.

text.NRSV: Do this: take censers, Korah and all your company,"
text.Rotherham: This do,—Take to you censers, Korah and all his assembly;
text.KJV: This do; Take you censers, Korah, and all his company;
text.NABRE: Do this: take your censers, Korah and all his faction,
text.DRB: This do therefore: Take every man their censers, thou Core, and all thy council:

text.NRSV: and tomorrow put fire in them, and lay incense on them before the Lord; and the man whom the Lord chooses shall be the holy one. You Levites have gone too far!"
text.Rotherham: and place therein fire, and put thereon incense before Yahweh, to-morrow, So shall it be—that, the man whom Yahweh shall choose, he shall be the holy one. Too much for you ye sons of Levi!
text.KJV: And put fire therein, and put incense in them before the Lord to morrow: and it shall be that the man whom the Lord doth choose, he shall be holy: ye take too much upon you, ye sons of Levi.
text.NABRE: and put fire in them and place incense in them before the Lord tomorrow. He whom the Lord then chooses is the holy one. You Levites go too far!”
text.DRB: and taking fire in them tomorrow, put upon it incense before our Lord: and whom soever he shall choose, the same shall be holy: you do much exalt yourselves ye sons of Levi.

text.NRSV: Then Moses said to Korah, "Hear now, you Levites!
text.Rotherham: And Moses said unto Korah,—Hear, I pray you, ye sons of Levi!
text.KJV: And Moses said unto Korah, Hear, I pray you, ye sons of Levi:
text.NABRE: Moses also said to Korah, “Hear, now, you Levites!
text.DRB: And he said again to Core: Hear ye sons of Levi:

text.NRSV: Is it too little for you that the God of Israel has separated you from the congregation of Israel, to allow you to approach him in order to perform the duties of the Lord's tabernacle, and to stand before the congregation and serve them?
text.Rotherham: Is it too small a thing for you that the God of Israel hath separated you from the assembly of Israel, to bring you near unto himself,—to do the laborious work of the habitation of Yahweh, and to stand before the assembly, to wait upon them?
text.KJV: Seemeth it but a small thing unto you, that the God of Israel hath separated you from the congregation of Israel, to bring you near to himself to do the service of the tabernacle of the Lord, and to stand before the congregation to minister unto them?
text.NABRE: Are you not satisfied that the God of Israel has singled you out from the community of Israel, to have you draw near him to maintain the Lord ’s tabernacle, and to attend upon the community and to serve them?
text.DRB: Is it a small thing unto you, that the God of Israel hath separated you from all the people, and joined you to himself, that you should serve him in the service of the tabernacle, and should stand before the full assembly of the people, & should minister to him?

text.NRSV: He has allowed you to approach him, and all your brother Levites with you; yet you seek the priesthood as well?"
text.Rotherham: But having brought thee near, and all thy brethren the sons of Levi with thee, must ye seek priesthood also?
text.KJV: And he hath brought thee near to him, and all thy brethren the sons of Levi with thee: and seek ye the priesthood also?
text.NABRE: He has allowed you and your Levite kinsmen with you to approach him, and yet you seek the priesthood too.
text.DRB: did he therefore make thee and all thy brethren the sons of Levi to approach unto him, that you should challenge unto you the priesthood also,

text.NRSV: Therefore you and all your company have gathered together against the Lord. What is Aaron that you rail against him?"
text.Rotherham: Wherefore thou and all thine assembly, are conspiring against Yahweh,—But Aaron, what is he, that ye should murmur against him?
text.KJV: For which cause both thou and all thy company are gathered together against the Lord: and what is Aaron, that ye murmur against him?
text.NABRE: It is therefore against the Lord that you and all your faction are conspiring. As for Aaron, what has he done that you should grumble against him?”
text.DRB: & all thy company should stand against our Lord? For what is Aaron that you murmur against him?

text.NRSV: Moses sent for Dathan and Abiram sons of Eliab; but they said, "We will not come!
text.Rotherham: Then sent Moses, to call for Dathan and for Abiram sons of Eliab,—and they said We will not come up:
text.KJV: And Moses sent to call Dathan and Abiram, the sons of Eliab: which said, We will not come up:
text.NABRE: Rebellion of Dathan and Abiram. Moses summoned Dathan and Abiram, sons of Eliab, but they answered, “We will not go.
text.DRB: Moyses therefore sent to call Dathan and Abiron the sons of Eliab. Who answered: We come not.

text.NRSV: Is it too little that you have brought us up out of a land flowing with milk and honey to kill us in the wilderness, that you must also lord it over us?"
text.Rotherham: Was it, a small thing, that thou didst bring us up out of a land flowing with milk and honey, to put us to death in the desert but thou must, continue even to lord it, over us?
text.KJV: Is it a small thing that thou hast brought us up out of a land that floweth with milk and honey, to kill us in the wilderness, except thou make thyself altogether a prince over us?
text.NABRE: Are you not satisfied that you have brought us here from a land flowing with milk and honey to have us perish in the wilderness, that now you must also lord it over us?
text.DRB: Why, is it a small matter to thee that thou hast brought us out of a land, that flowed with milk and honey, to kill us in the desert, unless thou rule also like a Lord over us?

text.NRSV: It is clear you have not brought us into a land flowing with milk and honey, or given us an inheritance of fields and vineyards. Would you put out the eyes of these men? We will not come!"
text.Rotherham: Certainly not into a land flowing with milk and honey, hast thou brought us, nor given unto us an inheritance of field and vineyard,—The eyes of those men, wilt thou dig out? We will not come up.
text.KJV: Moreover thou hast not brought us into a land that floweth with milk and honey, or given us inheritance of fields and vineyards: wilt thou put out the eyes of these men? we will not come up.
text.NABRE: Far from bringing us to a land flowing with milk and honey, or giving us fields and vineyards for our inheritance, will you gouge out our eyes? No, we will not go.”
text.DRB: Indeed hast thou brought us into a land, that floweth with rivers of milk and honey, and hast thou given us possessions of fields and vineyards? What, wilt thou pluck out our eyes also? We come not.

text.NRSV: Moses was very angry and said to the Lord, "Pay no attention to their offering. I have not taken one donkey from them, and I have not harmed any of them."
text.Rotherham: Then was Moses very angry, and he said unto Yahweh, Do not thou have respect unto their meal-offering. Not one ass, from them, have I taken, neither have I wronged one of them.
text.KJV: And Moses was very wroth, and said unto the Lord, Respect not thou their offering: I have not taken one ass from them, neither have I hurt one of them.
text.NABRE: Then Moses became very angry and said to the Lord, “Pay no attention to their offering. I have never taken a single donkey from them, nor have I wronged any one of them.”
text.DRB: Moyses therefore being very wrath, said to our Lord: Respect not their sacrifices: thou knowest that I have not taken of them so much as a little ass at any time, neither have afflicted any of them.

text.NRSV: And Moses said to Korah, "As for you and all your company, be present tomorrow before the Lord, you and they and Aaron;"
text.Rotherham: And Moses said unto Korah, Thou, and all thine assembly, come ye before Yahweh,—thou, and they, and Aaron to-morrow.
text.KJV: And Moses said unto Korah, Be thou and all thy company before the Lord, thou, and they, and Aaron, to morrow:
text.NABRE: Korah. Moses said to Korah, “You and all your faction shall appear before the Lord tomorrow—you and they and Aaron too.
text.DRB: And he said to Core: Thou, and all thy congregation stand ye apart before our Lord, and Aaron tomorrow apart.

text.NRSV: and let each one of you take his censer, and put incense on it, and each one of you present his censer before the Lord, two hundred fifty censers; you also, and Aaron, each his censer."
text.Rotherham: And take ye each man his censer and put thereon incense, then shall ye bring near before Yahweh, each man his censer, two hundred and fifty Censers,—and, thou and Aaron, each man his censer.
text.KJV: And take every man his censer, and put incense in them, and bring ye before the Lord every man his censer, two hundred and fifty censers; thou also, and Aaron, each of you his censer.
text.NABRE: Then each of you take his own censer, put incense in it, and present it before the Lord, two hundred and fifty censers; and you and Aaron, each with his own censer, do the same.”
text.DRB: Take every one your censers, and put incense upon them, offering to our Lord two hundred fifty censers: Let Aaron also hold his censer.

text.NRSV: So each man took his censer, and they put fire in the censers and laid incense on them, and they stood at the entrance of the tent of meeting with Moses and Aaron.
text.Rotherham: So they took each man his censer and put thereon fire, and placed thereon incense,—and took their stand at the entrance of the tent of meeting with Moses and Aaron.
text.KJV: And they took every man his censer, and put fire in them, and laid incense thereon, and stood in the door of the tabernacle of the congregation with Moses and Aaron.
text.NABRE: So each of them took their censers, and laying incense on the fire they had put in them, they took their stand by the entrance of the tent of meeting along with Moses and Aaron.
text.DRB: Which when they had done, Moyses and Aaron standing,

text.NRSV: Then Korah assembled the whole congregation against them at the entrance of the tent of meeting. And the glory of the Lord appeared to the whole congregation.
text.Rotherham: And Korah gathered together against them all the assembly, unto the entrance of the tent of meeting,—then appeared the glory of Yahweh, unto all the assembly.
text.KJV: And Korah gathered all the congregation against them unto the door of the tabernacle of the congregation: and the glory of the Lord appeared unto all the congregation.
text.NABRE: Then, when Korah had assembled all the community against them at the entrance of the tent of meeting, the glory of the Lord appeared to the entire community,
text.DRB: & had heaped together all the multitude against them to the door of the tabernacle, the glory of our Lord appeared to them all.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord spoke to Moses and to Aaron, saying:
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses and unto Aaron, saying:
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses and unto Aaron, saying,
text.NABRE: and the Lord said to Moses and Aaron:
text.DRB: And our Lord speaking to Moyses and Aaron, said:

text.NRSV: Separate yourselves from this congregation, so that I may consume them in a moment.
text.Rotherham: Separate yourselves out of the midst of this assembly,—that I may consume them in a moment.
text.KJV: Separate yourselves from among this congregation, that I may consume them in a moment.
text.NABRE: Stand apart from this community, that I may consume them at once.
text.DRB: Separate yourselves from the midst of this congregation, that I may suddenly destroy them.

text.NRSV: They fell on their faces, and said, "O God, the God of the spirits of all flesh, shall one person sin and you become angry with the whole congregation?"
text.Rotherham: And they fell upon their faces and said, O GOD, the God of the spirits of all flesh,—shall, one man, sin, and against all the assembly, wilt thou be wroth.
text.KJV: And they fell upon their faces, and said, O God, the God of the spirits of all flesh, shall one man sin, and wilt thou be wroth with all the congregation?
text.NABRE: But they fell prostrate and exclaimed, “O God, God of the spirits of all living creatures, if one man sins will you be angry with the whole community?”
text.DRB: Who fell flat on their face, and said: Most mighty God of the spirits of all flesh, when one sinneth, shall thy wrath rage against all?

text.NRSV: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying,
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: The Lord answered Moses:
text.DRB: And our Lord said to Moyses:

text.NRSV: Say to the congregation: Get away from the dwellings of Korah, Dathan, and Abiram.
text.Rotherham: Speak unto the assembly, saying,—Get ye up from round about the habitation of Korah, Dathan and Abiram.
text.KJV: Speak unto the congregation, saying, Get you up from about the tabernacle of Korah, Dathan, and Abiram.
text.NABRE: Speak to the community and tell them: Withdraw from the area around the tent of Korah, Dathan and Abiram.
text.DRB: Command the whole people that they separate themselves from the tabernacles of Core and Dathan and Abiron.

text.NRSV: So Moses got up and went to Dathan and Abiram; the elders of Israel followed him.
text.Rotherham: So Moses rose up, and went, unto Dathan and Abiram,—then went, after him the elders of Israel.
text.KJV: And Moses rose up and went unto Dathan and Abiram; and the elders of Israel followed him.
text.NABRE: Punishment of Dathan and Abiram. Moses, followed by the elders of Israel, arose and went to Dathan and Abiram.
text.DRB: And Moyses arose, and went to Dathan and Abiron: and the ancients of Israel following him,

text.NRSV: He said to the congregation, "Turn away from the tents of these wicked men, and touch nothing of theirs, or you will be swept away for all their sins."
text.Rotherham: And he spake unto the assembly, saying—Remove I pray you from beside the tents of these lawless men, and do not touch aught that pertaineth unto them,—lest ye be carried off, in all their sins.
text.KJV: And he spake unto the congregation, saying, Depart, I pray you, from the tents of these wicked men, and touch nothing of theirs, lest ye be consumed in all their sins.
text.NABRE: Then he spoke to the community, “Move away from the tents of these wicked men and do not touch anything that is theirs: otherwise you too will be swept away because of all their sins.”
text.DRB: he said to the multitude: Depart from the tabernacles of the impious men, and touch not the things that pertain to them, lest you be wrapped in their sins.

text.NRSV: So they got away from the dwellings of Korah, Dathan, and Abiram; and Dathan and Abiram came out and stood at the entrance of their tents, together with their wives, their children, and their little ones.
text.Rotherham: So they went up from beside the habitation of Korah, Dathan and Abiram on every side,—and, Dathan and Abiram, came out and stationed themselves at the entrance of their tents, with their wives and their sons and their little ones.
text.KJV: So they gat up from the tabernacle of Korah, Dathan, and Abiram, on every side: and Dathan and Abiram came out, and stood in the door of their tents, and their wives, and their sons, and their little children.
text.NABRE: So they withdrew from the area around the tents of Korah, Dathan and Abiram. When Dathan and Abiram had come out and were standing at the entrance of their tents with their wives, their children, and their little ones,
text.DRB: And when they were departed from their tents round about, Dathan and Abiron coming forth stood in the entry of their pavilions with their wives and children, and all the multitude.

text.NRSV: And Moses said, "This is how you shall know that the Lord has sent me to do all these works; it has not been of my own accord:
text.Rotherham: Then said Moses, Hereby, shall ye know, that, Yahweh hath sent me, to do all these works,—verily, not out of my own heart:
text.KJV: And Moses said, Hereby ye shall know that the Lord hath sent me to do all these works; for I have not done them of mine own mind.
text.NABRE: Moses said, “This is how you shall know that the Lord sent me to do all I have done, and that it was not of my own devising:
text.DRB: And Moyses said: In this you shall know that our Lord hath sent me to do all things that you see, and that I have not forged them of my own mind:

text.NRSV: If these people die a natural death, or if a natural fate comes on them, then the Lord has not sent me.
text.Rotherham: If, as all human beings die, these do die, and the visitation of all human beings, be visited upon them, Yahweh, hath not sent me.
text.KJV: If these men die the common death of all men, or if they be visited after the visitation of all men; then the Lord hath not sent me.
text.NABRE: if these die an ordinary death, merely suffering the fate common to all humanity, the Lord has not sent me.
text.DRB: If they die the accustomed death of men, and if the plague, wherewith others also are wont to be visited, do visit them, our Lord did not send me:

text.NRSV: But if the Lord creates something new, and the ground opens its mouth and swallows them up, with all that belongs to them, and they go down alive into Sheol, then you shall know that the Lord has despised these men."
text.Rotherham: But, if, a creation, Yahweh create, and the ground open wide her mouth and swallow them up, with all that pertain unto them, and so they go down alive unto hades, then shall ye know, that these men have despised Yahweh.
text.KJV: But if the Lord make a new thing, and the earth open her mouth, and swallow them up, with all that appertain unto them, and they go down quick into the pit; then ye shall understand that these men have provoked the Lord.
text.NABRE: But if the Lord makes a chasm, and the ground opens its mouth and swallows them with all belonging to them, and they go down alive to Sheol, then you will know that these men have spurned the Lord.”
text.DRB: but if our Lord do a new thing, that the earth opening her mouth swallow them down, and all things that pertain to them, and they descend quick into hell, you shall know that they have blasphemed our Lord.

text.NRSV: As soon as he finished speaking all these words, the ground under them was split apart.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass as he made an end of speaking all these words, that the ground which was under them, clave asunder;
text.KJV: And it came to pass, as he had made an end of speaking all these words, that the ground clave asunder that was under them:
text.NABRE: No sooner had he finished saying all this than the ground beneath them split open,
text.DRB: Immediately therefore as he ceased to speak, the earth brake a-sunder under their feet:

text.NRSV: The earth opened its mouth and swallowed them up, along with their households—everyone who belonged to Korah and all their goods.
text.Rotherham: and the earth opened her mouth, and swallowed up them, and their households,—and all the human beings who pertained unto Korah, with all their goods:
text.KJV: And the earth opened her mouth, and swallowed them up, and their houses, and all the men that appertained unto Korah, and all their goods.
text.NABRE: and the earth opened its mouth and swallowed them and their families and all of Korah’s people with all their possessions.
text.DRB: and opening her mouth, devoured them with their tabernacles & all their substance.

text.NRSV: So they with all that belonged to them went down alive into Sheol; the earth closed over them, and they perished from the midst of the assembly.
text.Rotherham: so, they, and all that pertained unto them, went down, alive unto hades,—and the earth, closed upon them, and they perished out of the midst of the convocation.
text.KJV: They, and all that appertained to them, went down alive into the pit, and the earth closed upon them: and they perished from among the congregation.
text.NABRE: They went down alive to Sheol with all belonging to them; the earth closed over them, and they disappeared from the assembly.
text.DRB: and they went down into hell quick covered with the ground, and perished out of the midst of the multitude.

text.NRSV: All Israel around them fled at their outcry, for they said, "The earth will swallow us too!"
text.Rotherham: And, all Israel who were round about them, fled at their cry,—for they said, Lest the earth swallow us up.
text.KJV: And all Israel that were round about them fled at the cry of them: for they said, Lest the earth swallow us up also.
text.NABRE: But all the Israelites near them fled at their shrieks, saying, “The earth might swallow us too!”
text.DRB: But all Israel, that stood round about, fled at the cry of them that perished, saying: Lest perhaps the earth swallow us also.

text.NRSV: And fire came out from the Lord and consumed the two hundred fifty men offering the incense.
