text.NABRE: Balaam recited his poem: Rise, Balak, and listen; give ear to my testimony, son of Zippor!
text.DRB: But he taking up his parable, said: Stand Balac, and hearken, hear thou son of Sephor:

text.NRSV: God is not a human being, that he should lie, or a mortal, that he should change his mind. Has he promised, and will he not do it? Has he spoken, and will he not fulfill it?"
text.Rotherham: GOD is, not a man, that he should lie, Nor a son of Adam, that he should repent,—Hath, he, said, and will not perform? Yea spoken, and will not make it stand fast?
text.KJV: God is not a man, that he should lie; neither the son of man, that he should repent: hath he said, and shall he not do it? or hath he spoken, and shall he not make it good?
text.NABRE: God is not a human being who speaks falsely, nor a mortal, who feels regret. Is God one to speak and not act, to decree and not bring it to pass?
text.DRB: God is not as man, that he may lie, nor as the son of man, that he may be changed. Hath he said then, and will he not do? Hath he spoken, and will he not fulfill?

text.NRSV: See, I received a command to bless; he has blessed, and I cannot revoke it."
text.Rotherham: Lo! to bless, have I received [command],—And I will bless, nor will I take it back.
text.KJV: Behold, I have received commandment to bless: and he hath blessed; and I cannot reverse it.
text.NABRE: I was summoned to bless; I will bless; I cannot revoke it!
text.DRB: I was brought to bless, the blessing I am not able to stay.

text.NRSV: He has not beheld misfortune in Jacob; nor has he seen trouble in Israel. The Lord their God is with them, acclaimed as a king among them."
text.Rotherham: He hath not discovered trouble in Jacob, Neither hath he seen wretchedness in Israel,—Yahweh, his God, is with him, And, the acclaim of a king, in his midst:
text.KJV: He hath not beheld iniquity in Jacob, neither hath he seen perverseness in Israel: the Lord his God is with him, and the shout of a king is among them.
text.NABRE: Misfortune I do not see in Jacob, nor do I see misery in Israel. The Lord, their God, is with them; among them is the war-cry of their King.
text.DRB: There is no idol in Jacob, neither is there simulachre to be seen in Israel. The Lord his God is with him, and the sound of the victory of the king in him.

text.NRSV: God, who brings them out of Egypt, is like the horns of a wild ox for them."
text.Rotherham: GOD having brought them forth out of Egypt, The very horns of the buffalo, are his:
text.KJV: God brought them out of Egypt; he hath as it were the strength of an unicorn.
text.NABRE: They have the like of a wild ox’s horns: God who brought them out of Egypt.
text.DRB: God hath brought him out of Ægypt, whose strength is like to the unicorn.

text.NRSV: Surely there is no enchantment against Jacob, no divination against Israel; now it shall be said of Jacob and Israel, 'See what God has done!'
text.Rotherham: Surely, there is no enchantment against Jacob, Nor divination against Israel,—Now, shall it be said—Of Jacob and of Israel, What hath GOD wrought!
text.KJV: Surely there is no enchantment against Jacob, neither is there any divination against Israel: according to this time it shall be said of Jacob and of Israel, What hath God wrought!
text.NABRE: No, there is no augury against Jacob, nor divination against Israel. Now it is said of Jacob, of Israel, “Look what God has done!”
text.DRB: There is no Soothsaying in Jacob, nor divination in Israel. In their times it shall be said to Jacob and Israel what God hath wrought.

text.NRSV: Look, a people rising up like a lioness, and rousing itself like a lion! It does not lie down until it has eaten the prey and drunk the blood of the slain."
text.Rotherham: Lo, a people, like a lioness, shall rise, And like a strong lion, shall rouse himself up; He will not lie down till he eat the torn, And the blood of the slain, he drink.
text.KJV: Behold, the people shall rise up as a great lion, and lift up himself as a young lion: he shall not lie down until he eat of the prey, and drink the blood of the slain.
text.NABRE: Here is a people that rises up like a lioness, and gets up like a lion; It does not rest till it has devoured its prey and has drunk the blood of the slain.
text.DRB: Behold the people shall rise up as a lioness and as a lion shall raise itself: It shall not lie down till it devour the prey, and drink the blood of the slain.

text.NRSV: Then Balak said to Balaam, "Do not curse them at all, and do not bless them at all."
text.Rotherham: Then said Balak unto Balaam, Thou shalt neither revile, him,—nor bless him.
text.KJV: And Balak said unto Balaam, Neither curse them at all, nor bless them at all.
text.NABRE: “Neither lay a curse on them nor bless them,” said Balak to Balaam.
text.DRB: And Balac said to Balaam: neither curse, nor bless him.

text.NRSV: But Balaam answered Balak, "Did I not tell you, 'Whatever the Lord says, that is what I must do'?"
text.Rotherham: And Balaam responded, and said unto Balak,—Spake I not unto thee, saying, All that Yahweh shall speak, that, must I do?
text.KJV: But Balaam answered and said unto Balak, Told not I thee, saying, All that the Lord speaketh, that I must do?
text.NABRE: But Balaam answered Balak, “Did I not tell you, ‘Everything the Lord tells me I must do’?”
text.DRB: And he said: Did I not tell thee, that whatsoever God should command me, that would I do?

text.NRSV: So Balak said to Balaam, "Come now, I will take you to another place; perhaps it will please God that you may curse them for me from there."
text.Rotherham: And Balak said unto Balaam, Come, I pray thee! let me take thee unto another place,—peradventure, it may be right in the eyes of God, that thou revile me him. from thence.
text.KJV: And Balak said unto Balaam, Come, I pray thee, I will bring thee unto another place; peradventure it will please God that thou mayest curse me them from thence.
text.NABRE: The Third Oracle. Then Balak said to Balaam, “Come, let me bring you to another place; perhaps God will approve of your laying a curse on them for me from there.”
text.DRB: And Balac said to him: Come, and I will bring thee to another place: if happenly it please God that thence thou mayest curse them.

text.NRSV: So Balak took Balaam to the top of Peor, which overlooks the wasteland.
text.Rotherham: So Balak took Balaam,—unto the head of Peor, that looketh over the face of the Jeshimon.
text.KJV: And Balak brought Balaam unto the top of Peor, that looketh toward Jeshimon.
text.NABRE: So he took Balaam to the top of Peor, that overlooks Jeshimon.
text.DRB: And when he had brought him upon the top of the mountain Phogor, which looketh to the wilderness,

text.NRSV: Balaam said to Balak, "Build me seven altars here, and prepare seven bulls and seven rams for me."
text.Rotherham: And Balaam said unto Balak, Build me here, seven altars,—and prepare me here, seven bullocks and seven rams.
text.KJV: And Balaam said unto Balak, Build me here seven altars, and prepare me here seven bullocks and seven rams.
text.NABRE: Balaam then said to Balak, “Build me seven altars here; and here prepare for me seven bulls and seven rams.”
text.DRB: Balaam said to him: Build me here seven altars, and prepare as many calves, and rams of the same number.

text.NRSV: Balak did as Balaam had said, and offered a bull and a ram on each altar.
text.Rotherham: And Balak did as Balaam said,—and caused to ascend a bullock and a ram on each altar.
text.KJV: And Balak did as Balaam had said, and offered a bullock and a ram on every altar.
text.NABRE: And Balak did as Balaam had ordered, offering a bull and a ram on each altar.
text.DRB: Balac did as Balaam had said: and he laid the calves and the rams on every altar.

text.NRSV: Now Balaam saw that it pleased the Lord to bless Israel, so he did not go, as at other times, to look for omens, but set his face toward the wilderness.
text.Rotherham: And when Balaam saw that it was good in the eyes of Yahweh to bless Israel, he went not as at other times to invoke enchantments,—but set towards the desert, his face.
text.KJV: And when Balaam saw that it pleased the Lord to bless Israel, he went not, as at other times, to seek for enchantments, but he set his face toward the wilderness.
text.NABRE: Balaam, however, perceiving that the Lord was pleased to bless Israel, did not go aside as before to seek omens, but turned his gaze toward the wilderness.
text.DRB: And when Balaam had seen that it pleased our Lord that he should bless Israel, he went not as before he had gone, to seek soothsaying: but directing his countenance against the desert,

text.NRSV: Balaam looked up and saw Israel camping tribe by tribe. Then the spirit of God came upon him,
text.Rotherham: So Balaam lifted up his eyes and saw Israel, dwelling, according to his tribes,—then came upon him the Spirit of God;
text.KJV: And Balaam lifted up his eyes, and he saw Israel abiding in his tents according to their tribes; and the spirit of God came upon him.
text.NABRE: When Balaam looked up and saw Israel encamped, tribe by tribe, the spirit of God came upon him,
text.DRB: and lifting up his eyes, he saw Israel abiding in their tents by their tribes: and the spirit of God coming vehemently upon him,

text.NRSV: and he uttered his oracle, saying: "The oracle of Balaam son of Beor, the oracle of the man whose eye is clear,"
text.Rotherham: and he took up his parable, and said,—The oracle of Balaam, son of Beor, Yea the oracle of the man, of opened eye;
text.KJV: And he took up his parable, and said, Balaam the son of Beor hath said, and the man whose eyes are open hath said:
text.NABRE: and he recited his poem: The oracle of Balaam, son of Beor, the oracle of the man whose eye is true,
text.DRB: taking up his parable he said: Balaam the son of Beor hath said: The man hath said whose eye is stopped:

text.NRSV: the oracle of one who hears the words of God, who sees the vision of the Almighty, who falls down, but with eyes uncovered:"
text.Rotherham: The oracle of one hearing sayings of GOD, Who the sight of the Almighty, receiveth in vision, Who falleth down but hath unveiled eyes:—
text.KJV: He hath said, which heard the words of God, which saw the vision of the Almighty, falling into a trance, but having his eyes open:
text.NABRE: The oracle of one who hears what God says, and knows what the Most High knows, Of one who sees what the Almighty sees, in rapture and with eyes unveiled:
text.DRB: The hearer of the words of God hath said, he that hath beholden the vision of the Omnipotent, he that falleth, and so his eyes are opened:

text.NRSV: How fair are your tents, O Jacob, your encampments, O Israel!"
text.Rotherham: How pleasing are thy tents, O Jacob,—Thy habitations, O Israel:
text.KJV: How goodly are thy tents, O Jacob, and thy tabernacles, O Israel!
text.NABRE: How pleasant are your tents, Jacob; your encampments, Israel!
text.DRB: How beautiful are thy tabernacles, o Jacob, and thy tents, o Israel!

text.NRSV: Like valleys that stretch afar, like gardens beside a river, like aloes that the Lord has planted, like cedar trees beside the waters."
text.Rotherham: Like ravines extended, Like gardens by a river,—Like aloe-trees Yahweh hath planted, Like cedars by waters:
text.KJV: As the valleys are they spread forth, as gardens by the river’s side, as the trees of lign aloes which the Lord hath planted, and as cedar trees beside the waters.
text.NABRE: Like palm trees spread out, like gardens beside a river, Like aloes the Lord planted, like cedars beside water;
text.DRB: As woody valleys, as watered gardens beside the rivers, as tabernacles which the Lord hath pitched, as cedars by the waters' side.

text.NRSV: Water shall flow from his buckets, and his seed shall have abundant water, his king shall be higher than Agag, and his kingdom shall be exalted."
text.Rotherham: He poureth forth water from his buckets, And, his seed, is among many waters,—And taller than Agag is his King, And exalted is his kingdom.
text.KJV: He shall pour the water out of his buckets, and his seed shall be in many waters, and his king shall be higher than Agag, and his kingdom shall be exalted.
text.NABRE: Water will drip from their buckets, their seed will have plentiful water; Their king will rise higher than Agag and their dominion will be exalted.
text.DRB: Water shall flow out of his bucket, and his seed shall be into many waters. For Agag shall his king be taken away, and his kingdom shall be taken away.

text.NRSV: God who brings him out of Egypt, is like the horns of a wild ox for him; he shall devour the nations that are his foes and break their bones in pieces, and pierce them through with his arrows."
text.Rotherham: GOD having brought him forth out of Egypt, The very horns of the buffalo, are his,—He eateth up nations that assail him And the bones of them, he breaketh And the loins of him, he crusheth:
text.KJV: God brought him forth out of Egypt; he hath as it were the strength of an unicorn: he shall eat up the nations his enemies, and shall break their bones, and pierce them through with his arrows.
text.NABRE: They have the like of a wild ox’s horns: God who brought them out of Egypt. They will devour hostile nations, break their bones, and crush their loins.
text.DRB: God hath brought him out of Ægypt, whose strength is like to the Rhinoceros. They shall devour nations his enemies, and break their bones, and pierce them with arrows.

text.NRSV: He crouched, he lay down like a lion, and like a lioness; who will rouse him up? Blessed is everyone who blesses you, and cursed is everyone who curses you."
text.Rotherham: He hath knelt—hath lain down, Like a strong lion, yea like a lioness, Who shall rouse him up? Such as bless thee are each one blessed, But, such, as curse thee, are each one cursed.
text.KJV: He couched, he lay down as a lion, and as a great lion: who shall stir him up? Blessed is he that blesseth thee, and cursed is he that curseth thee.
text.NABRE: Crouching, they lie like a lion, or like a lioness; who will arouse them? Blessed are those who bless you, and cursed are those who curse you!
text.DRB: Lying he hath slept as a lion, and as a lioness, whom none shall be bold to raise up. He that blesseth thee, himself also shall be blessed: he that curseth thee, shall be reputed accurst.

text.NRSV: Then Balak's anger was kindled against Balaam, and he struck his hands together. Balak said to Balaam, "I summoned you to curse my enemies, but instead you have blessed them these three times."
text.Rotherham: Then kindled the anger of Balak against Balaam, and he smote together his hands,—and Balak said unto Balaam—To revile my foes, I called thee, And lo! thou hast kept on blessing, these three times.
text.KJV: And Balak’s anger was kindled against Balaam, and he smote his hands together: and Balak said unto Balaam, I called thee to curse mine enemies, and, behold, thou hast altogether blessed them these three times.
text.NABRE: In a blaze of anger at Balaam, Balak clapped his hands and said to him, “It was to lay a curse on my foes that I summoned you here; yet three times now you have actually blessed them!
text.DRB: And Balac being angry against Balaam, clapping his hands together said: To curse mine enemies I called thee, whom thou contrary wise hast blessed the third time:

text.NRSV: Now be off with you! Go home! I said, 'I will reward you richly,' but the Lord has denied you any reward."
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, flee thou unto thy place,—I said, I will, highly honour, thee; but lo! Yahweh hath kept thee back from honour.
text.KJV: Therefore now flee thou to thy place: I thought to promote thee unto great honour; but, lo, the Lord hath kept thee back from honour.
text.NABRE: Now flee to your home. I promised to reward you richly, but the Lord has withheld the reward from you!”
text.DRB: return to thy place. I was determined verily to honour thee magnifically, but the Lord hath deprived thee of the honour appointed.

text.NRSV: And Balaam said to Balak, "Did I not tell your messengers whom you sent to me,"
text.Rotherham: And Balaam said unto Balak,—Was it not so that even unto thy messengers whom thou didst send unto me, I spake saying-
text.KJV: And Balaam said unto Balak, Spake I not also to thy messengers which thou sentest unto me, saying,
text.NABRE: Balaam replied to Balak, “Did I not even tell the messengers whom you sent to me,
text.DRB: Balaam made answer to Balac: did I not say to thy messengers, whom thou didst send to me:

text.NRSV: "'If Balak should give me his house full of silver and gold, I would not be able to go beyond the word of the Lord, to do either good or bad of my own will; what the Lord says, that is what I will say'?"
text.Rotherham: Though Balak would give me his house full of silver and gold, yet could I not go beyond the bidding of Yahweh, to, do good or ill, out of my own heart,—what Yahweh shall speak, that, must I speak?
text.KJV: If Balak would give me his house full of silver and gold, I cannot go beyond the commandment of the Lord, to do either good or bad of mine own mind; but what the Lord saith, that will I speak?
text.NABRE: ‘Even if Balak gave me his house full of silver and gold, I could not of my own accord do anything, good or evil, contrary to the command of the Lord ’? Whatever the Lord says I must say.
text.DRB: If Balac would give me his house full of silver and gold, I cannot pass the word of the Lord my God, to utter of my own mind either any good, or evil: but whatsoever the Lord shall say, that will I speak?

text.NRSV: So now, I am going to my people; let me advise you what this people will do to your people in days to come."
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, behold me! going to my own people,—Come now! let me advise thee, what this people shall do unto thy people in the after-part of the days.
text.KJV: And now, behold, I go unto my people: come therefore, and I will advertise thee what this people shall do to thy people in the latter days.
text.NABRE: The Fourth Oracle. “But now that I am about to go to my own people, let me warn you what this people will do to your people in the days to come.”
text.DRB: But yet going to my people, I will give thee counsel, what thy people shall do to this people in the last time.

text.NRSV: So he uttered his oracle, saying: "The oracle of Balaam son of Beor, the oracle of the man whose eye is clear,"
text.Rotherham: So he took up his parable and said,—The oracle of Balaam, son of Beor, Yea the oracle of the man, of opened eye;
text.KJV: And he took up his parable, and said, Balaam the son of Beor hath said, and the man whose eyes are open hath said:
text.NABRE: Then he recited his poem: The oracle of Balaam, son of Beor, the oracle of the man whose eye is true,
text.DRB: Therefore taking up his parable, again he said: Balaam the son of Beor hath said: The man whose eye is stopped, hath said:

text.NRSV: the oracle of one who hears the words of God, and knows the knowledge of the Most High, who sees the vision of the Almighty, who falls down, but with his eyes uncovered:"
text.Rotherham: The oracle of one hearing sayings of GOD, And knowing the knowledge of the Most High,—Who the sight of the Almighty, receiveth in vision, Who falleth down but hath unveiled eyes:—
text.KJV: He hath said, which heard the words of God, and knew the knowledge of the most High, which saw the vision of the Almighty, falling into a trance, but having his eyes open:
text.NABRE: The oracle of one who hears what God says, and knows what the Most High knows, Of one who sees what the Almighty sees, in rapture and with eyes unveiled.
text.DRB: the hearer of the words of God hath said, who knoweth the doctrine of the Highest, and seeth the visions of the Omnipotent, who falling hath his eyes opened:

text.NRSV: I see him, but not now; I behold him, but not near— a star shall come out of Jacob, and a scepter shall rise out of Israel; it shall crush the borderlands of Moab, and the territory of all the Shethites."
text.Rotherham: I see One, who is not now, I observe One, who is not nigh,—There hath marched forth a Star out of Jacob. And arisen a Sceptre out of Israel, That hath dishonoured the beard of Moab, Yea the crown of the head of all the tumultuous;
text.KJV: I shall see him, but not now: I shall behold him, but not nigh: there shall come a Star out of Jacob, and a Sceptre shall rise out of Israel, and shall smite the corners of Moab, and destroy all the children of Sheth.
text.NABRE: I see him, though not now; I observe him, though not near: A star shall advance from Jacob, and a scepter shall rise from Israel, That will crush the brows of Moab, and the skull of all the Sethites,
text.DRB: I shall see him, but not now: I shall behold him but not near. A star Shall Rise out of Jacob, and a rod shall arise from Israel: and shall strike the Dukes of Moab, and shall waste all the children of Seth.

text.NRSV: Edom will become a possession, Seir a possession of its enemies, while Israel does valiantly."
text.Rotherham: So Edom hath become a possession, Yea a possession is Seir to his foes,—But, Israel, is doing valiantly;
text.KJV: And Edom shall be a possession, Seir also shall be a possession for his enemies; and Israel shall do valiantly.
text.NABRE: Edom will be dispossessed, and no survivor is left in Seir. Israel will act boldly,
text.DRB: And Idumea shall be his possession: the inheritance of Seir shall come to their enemies: but Israel shall do manfully.

text.NRSV: One out of Jacob shall rule, and destroy the survivors of Ir."
text.Rotherham: Yea One wieldeth dominion out of Jacob,—Who hath destroyed the remnant out of the fortress.
text.KJV: Out of Jacob shall come he that shall have dominion, and shall destroy him that remaineth of the city.
text.NABRE: and Jacob will rule his foes.
text.DRB: Of Jacob shall he be that shall rule, and shall destroy the remains of the city.

text.NRSV: Then he looked on Amalek, and uttered his oracle, saying: "First among the nations was Amalek, but its end is to perish forever."
text.Rotherham: And, when he saw Amalek, he took up, his parable, and said—The beginning of nations,—Amalek, But his latter end is even to perish.
text.KJV: And when he looked on Amalek, he took up his parable, and said, Amalek was the first of the nations; but his latter end shall be that he perish for ever.
text.NABRE: Upon seeing Amalek, Balaam recited his poem: First of the nations is Amalek, but their end is to perish forever.
text.DRB: And when he had seen Amalec, taking up his parable, he said: Amalec the beginning of Gentiles, whose latter ends shall be destroyed.

text.NRSV: Then he looked on the Kenite, and uttered his oracle, saying: "Enduring is your dwelling place, and your nest is set in the rock;"
text.Rotherham: And when he saw the Kenite, he took up his parable, and said,—Enduring thy dwelling-place, Set thou then in the crag, thy nest;
text.KJV: And he looked on the Kenites, and took up his parable, and said, Strong is thy dwellingplace, and thou puttest thy nest in a rock.
text.NABRE: Upon seeing the Kenites, he recited his poem: Though your dwelling is safe, and your nest is set on a cliff;
text.DRB: He saw also the Cineite: and taking up his parable, he said: Thy habitation indeed is strong: but if thou build thy nest in a rock,

text.NRSV: yet Kain is destined for burning. How long shall Asshur take you away captive?"
text.Rotherham: Yet shall it be for destruction, O Kain,—How long shall Assyria hold thee captive?
text.KJV: Nevertheless the Kenite shall be wasted, until Asshur shall carry thee away captive.
text.NABRE: Yet Kain will be destroyed when Asshur takes you captive.
text.DRB: and thou be chosen of the stock of Cin, how long shalt thou be able to continue? For Assur shall take thee.

text.NRSV: Again he uttered his oracle, saying: "Alas, who shall live when God does this?"
text.Rotherham: And he took up his parable, and said,—Alas who shall survive its fulfillment by GOD;
text.KJV: And he took up his parable, and said, Alas, who shall live when God doeth this!
text.NABRE: Upon seeing [the Ishmaelites?] he recited his poem: Alas, who shall survive of Ishmael,
text.DRB: And taking up his parable again he spake: Alas, who shall live, when God shall do these things?

text.NRSV: But ships shall come from Kittim and shall afflict Asshur and Eber; and he also shall perish forever."
text.Rotherham: When, ships, [come] from the coast of the isles, And humble Assyria, and humble the Hebrew,—And he too, is even to perish?
text.KJV: And ships shall come from the coast of Chittim, and shall afflict Asshur, and shall afflict Eber, and he also shall perish for ever.
text.NABRE: to deliver them from the hands of the Kittim? When they have conquered Asshur and conquered Eber, They too shall perish forever.
text.DRB: They shall come in galleys from Italy, they shall overcome the Assyrians, and shall waste the Hebrews, and at the last themselves also shall perish.

text.NRSV: Then Balaam got up and went back to his place, and Balak also went his way.
text.Rotherham: Then Balaam arose, and went and returned unto his place,—and, Balak also, went his way.
text.KJV: And Balaam rose up, and went and returned to his place: and Balak also went his way.
text.NABRE: Then Balaam set out on his journey home; and Balak also went his way.
text.DRB: And Balaam rose, and returned into his place: Balac also returned the way that he came.

text.NRSV: While Israel was staying at Shittim, the people began to have sexual relations with the women of Moab.
text.Rotherham: And Israel remained among the acacias,—and the people began to go away unchastely unto the daughters of Moab;
text.KJV: And Israel abode in Shittim, and the people began to commit whoredom with the daughters of Moab.
text.NABRE: While Israel was living at Shittim, the people profaned themselves by prostituting themselves with the Moabite women.
text.DRB: And Israel at that time abode in Setim, and the people fornicated with the daughters of Moab,

text.NRSV: These invited the people to the sacrifices of their gods, and the people ate and bowed down to their gods.
text.Rotherham: who invited the people unto the sacrifices of their gods,—so the people did eat, and did bow themselves down unto their gods.
text.KJV: And they called the people unto the sacrifices of their gods: and the people did eat, and bowed down to their gods.
text.NABRE: These then invited the people to the sacrifices of their god, and the people ate of the sacrifices and bowed down to their god.
text.DRB: who called them to their sacrifices. And they did eat and adore their gods.

text.NRSV: Thus Israel yoked itself to the Baal of Peor, and the Lord's anger was kindled against Israel.
text.Rotherham: Thus Israel let himself be bound unto Baal-peor, and the anger of Yahweh kindled upon Israel.
text.KJV: And Israel joined himself unto Baal–peor: and the anger of the Lord was kindled against Israel.
text.NABRE: Israel thereby attached itself to the Baal of Peor, and the Lord ’s anger flared up against Israel.
text.DRB: And Israel was professed to Beelphegor, and our Lord being angry,

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Moses, "Take all the chiefs of the people, and impale them in the sun before the Lord, in order that the fierce anger of the Lord may turn away from Israel."
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said unto Moses—Take all the heads of the people, and crucify them unto Yahweh. in the face of the sun,—that the heat of the anger of Yahweh may turn away from Israel.
text.KJV: And the Lord said unto Moses, Take all the heads of the people, and hang them up before the Lord against the sun, that the fierce anger of the Lord may be turned away from Israel.
text.NABRE: The Lord said to Moses: Gather all the leaders of the people, and publicly execute them before the Lord, that the blazing wrath of the Lord may turn away from Israel.
text.DRB: said to Moyses: Take all the Princes of the people, and hang them up against the sun on gibbets: that my fury may be averted from Israel.

text.NRSV: And Moses said to the judges of Israel, "Each of you shall kill any of your people who have yoked themselves to the Baal of Peor."
text.Rotherham: And Moses said unto the judges of Israel,—Slay ye each one his men, who have let themselves he bound unto Baal-peor.
text.KJV: And Moses said unto the judges of Israel, Slay ye every one his men that were joined unto Baal–peor.
text.NABRE: So Moses told the Israelite judges, “Each of you kill those of his men who have attached themselves to the Baal of Peor.”
text.DRB: And Moyses said to the Judges of Israel: Kill every man his neighbours, that are professed to Beelphegor.

text.NRSV: Just then one of the Israelites came and brought a Midianite woman into his family, in the sight of Moses and in the sight of the whole congregation of the Israelites, while they were weeping at the entrance of the tent of meeting.
text.Rotherham: And lo! a man of the sons of Israel coming in, who brought near unto his brethren a Midianite woman, before the eyes of Moses, and before the eyes of all the assembly of the sons of Israel, when they were weeping at the entrance of the tent of meeting.
text.KJV: And, behold, one of the children of Israel came and brought unto his brethren a Midianitish woman in the sight of Moses, and in the sight of all the congregation of the children of Israel, who were weeping before the door of the tabernacle of the congregation.
text.NABRE: Zeal of Phinehas. At this a certain Israelite came and brought in a Midianite woman to his kindred in the view of Moses and of the whole Israelite community, while they were weeping at the entrance of the tent of meeting.
text.DRB: And behold one of the children of Israel entered in before the face of his brethren to a whore a Madianite in the sight of Moyses, and of all the multitude of the children of Israel, who wept before the doors of the tabernacle.

text.NRSV: When Phinehas son of Eleazar, son of Aaron the priest, saw it, he got up and left the congregation. Taking a spear in his hand,
text.Rotherham: And Phinehas son of Eleazar, son of Aaron the priest saw it, so he rose up out of the midst of the assembly, and took a spear in his hand;
text.KJV: And when Phinehas, the son of Eleazar, the son of Aaron the priest, saw it, he rose up from among the congregation, and took a javelin in his hand;
text.NABRE: When Phinehas, son of Eleazar, son of Aaron the priest, saw this, he rose up from the assembly, and taking a spear in his hand,
text.DRB: Which thing when Phinees had seen the son of Eleazar the son of Aaron the Priest, he arose out of the midst of the multitude, and catching a dagger

text.NRSV: he went after the Israelite man into the tent, and pierced the two of them, the Israelite and the woman, through the belly. So the plague was stopped among the people of Israel.
text.Rotherham: and went in after the man of Israel into the pleasure-tent, and thrust both of them through, the man of Israel and the woman, in her parts of shame,—so the plague was restrained from against the sons of Israel.
text.KJV: And he went after the man of Israel into the tent, and thrust both of them through, the man of Israel, and the woman through her belly. So the plague was stayed from the children of Israel.
text.NABRE: followed the Israelite into the tent where he pierced the two of them, the Israelite and the woman. Thus the plague upon the Israelites was checked;
text.DRB: went in after the man of Israel into the brothel house, and thrust them through both together, to wit, the man and the woman in the genitals. And the plague ceased from the children of Israel,

text.NRSV: Nevertheless those that died by the plague were twenty-four thousand.
text.Rotherham: Then was it found that they who had died by the plague were four and twenty thousand.
text.KJV: And those that died in the plague were twenty and four thousand.
text.NABRE: but the dead from the plague were twenty-four thousand.
text.DRB: and there were slain four and twenty thousand men.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses saying:
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: Then the Lord said to Moses:
text.DRB: And our Lord said to Moyses:

text.NRSV: Phinehas son of Eleazar, son of Aaron the priest, has turned back my wrath from the Israelites by manifesting such zeal among them on my behalf that in my jealousy I did not consume the Israelites.
text.Rotherham: Phinehas son of Eleazar son of Aaron the priest, hath turned back my wrath from against the sons of Israel, in that he was jealous with my jealousy, in their midst, so that I made not an end of the sons of Israel. in my jealousy.
text.KJV: Phinehas, the son of Eleazar, the son of Aaron the priest, hath turned my wrath away from the children of Israel, while he was zealous for my sake among them, that I consumed not the children of Israel in my jealousy.
text.NABRE: Phinehas, son of Eleazar, son of Aaron the priest, has turned my anger from the Israelites by his being as jealous among them as I am; that is why I did not put an end to the Israelites in my jealousy.
text.DRB: Phinees the son of Eleazar the son of Aaron the Priest hath averted my wrath from the children of Israel: because he was moved with my zeal against them, that myself might not destroy the children of Israel in mine own zeal.

text.NRSV: Therefore say, 'I hereby grant him my covenant of peace."
text.Rotherham: Wherefore say,—Behold me! giving unto him my covenant of peace;
text.KJV: Wherefore say, Behold, I give unto him my covenant of peace:
text.NABRE: Announce, therefore, that I hereby give him my covenant of peace,
text.DRB: Therefore speak to him: Behold I give him the peace of my covenant,

text.NRSV: It shall be for him and for his descendants after him a covenant of perpetual priesthood, because he was zealous for his God, and made atonement for the Israelites.'"
text.Rotherham: so shall it prove to be unto him and unto his seed after him, the covenant of our age-abiding priesthood, because he was jealous for his God, and did put a propitiatory-covering over the sons of Israel.
text.KJV: And he shall have it, and his seed after him, even the covenant of an everlasting priesthood; because he was zealous for his God, and made an atonement for the children of Israel.
text.NABRE: which shall be for him and for his descendants after him the covenant of an everlasting priesthood, because he was jealous on behalf of his God and thus made expiation for the Israelites.
text.DRB: and there shall be as well to him as to his seed the covenant of priesthood for ever, because he hath been zealous for his God, and hath expiated the wicked fact of the children of Israel.

text.NRSV: The name of the slain Israelite man, who was killed with the Midianite woman, was Zimri son of Salu, head of an ancestral house belonging to the Simeonites.
text.Rotherham: Now, the name of the man of Israel that was smitten, who was smitten with the Midianitish woman, was Zimri son of Salu,—prince of an ancestral house of the Simeonites;
text.KJV: Now the name of the Israelite that was slain, even that was slain with the Midianitish woman, was Zimri, the son of Salu, a prince of a chief house among the Simeonites.
text.NABRE: The name of the slain Israelite, the one slain with the Midianite woman, was Zimri, son of Salu, prince of a Simeonite ancestral house.
text.DRB: And the name of the man of Israel, that was slain with the woman of Madian, was Zambri the son of Salu, a Prince of the kindred and tribe of Simeon.

text.NRSV: The name of the Midianite woman who was killed was Cozbi daughter of Zur, who was the head of a clan, an ancestral house in Midian.
text.Rotherham: and, the name of the woman who was smitten, the Midianitess, was Cozbi, daughter of Zur,—the head of his kindred, of an ancestral house in Midian, was he.
text.KJV: And the name of the Midianitish woman that was slain was Cozbi, the daughter of Zur; he was head over a people, and of a chief house in Midian.
text.NABRE: The name of the slain Midianite woman was Cozbi, daughter of Zur, who was head of a clan, an ancestral house, in Midian.
text.DRB: Moreover the Madianess that was slain with him, was called Cozbi the daughter of Sur a most noble Prince of the Madianites.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Moses,
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses saying:
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: Vengeance on the Midianites. The Lord then said to Moses:
text.DRB: And our Lord spake to Moyses, saying:

text.NRSV: "Harass the Midianites, and defeat them;"
text.Rotherham: Besiege the Midianites,—and ye shall smite them:
text.KJV: Vex the Midianites, and smite them:
text.NABRE: Treat the Midianites as enemies and strike them,
text.DRB: Let the Madianites find you their enemies, and strike you them:

text.NRSV: for they have harassed you by the trickery with which they deceived you in the affair of Peor, and in the affair of Cozbi, the daughter of a leader of Midian, their sister; she was killed on the day of the plague that resulted from Peor."
text.Rotherham: for, besiegers, were they unto you, with their wiles wherewith they beguiled you, over the matter of Peor,—and over he matter of Cozbi, daughter of a prince of Midian, their sister, who was smitten in the day of the plague, over the matter of Peor.
text.KJV: For they vex you with their wiles, wherewith they have beguiled you in the matter of Peor, and in the matter of Cozbi, the daughter of a prince of Midian, their sister, which was slain in the day of the plague for Peor’s sake.
text.NABRE: for they have been your enemies by the deceitful dealings they had with you regarding Peor and their kinswoman Cozbi, the daughter of a Midianite prince, who was slain at the time of the plague because of Peor.
text.DRB: Because they also have done like enemies against you, and have guilefully deceived you by the idol Phogor and Cozbi the daughter of the Duke of Madian their sister, who was strucken in the day of the plague for the sacrilege of Phogor.

text.NRSV: After the plague the Lord said to Moses and to Eleazar son of Aaron the priest,
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, after the plague, that Yahweh spake unto Moses, and unto Eleazar son of Aaron the priest, saying:
text.KJV: And it came to pass after the plague, that the Lord spake unto Moses and unto Eleazar the son of Aaron the priest, saying,
text.NABRE: the Lord said to Moses and Eleazar, son of Aaron the priest:
text.DRB: After the blood of the offenders was shed, our Lord said to Moyses and Eleazar the son of Aaron, the Priest:

text.NRSV: "Take a census of the whole congregation of the Israelites, from twenty years old and upward, by their ancestral houses, everyone in Israel able to go to war."
text.Rotherham: Reckon ye up the sum of all the assembly of the sons of Israel from twenty years old and upwards, by their ancestral houses,—every one able to go forth to war in Israel.
text.KJV: Take the sum of all the congregation of the children of Israel, from twenty years old and upward, throughout their fathers’ house, all that are able to go to war in Israel.
text.NABRE: Take a census, by ancestral houses, throughout the community of the Israelites of all those of twenty years or more who are eligible for military service in Israel.
text.DRB: Number the whole sum of the children of Israel from twenty years and upward, by their houses and kindreds, all that can go forth to wars.

text.NRSV: So Moses and Eleazar the priest spoke with them in the plains of Moab by the Jordan opposite Jericho, saying,
text.Rotherham: So then Moses and Eleazar the priest spake with them, in the waste plains of Moab,—by the Jordan near Jericho saying:
text.KJV: And Moses and Eleazar the priest spake with them in the plains of Moab by Jordan near Jericho, saying,
text.NABRE: So on the plains of Moab along the Jordan at Jericho, Moses and Eleazar the priest enrolled them,
text.DRB: Moyses therefore & Eleazar the Priest spake, in the champion country of Moab upon Jordan against Jericho, to them that were

text.NRSV: "Take a census of the people, from twenty years old and upward," as the Lord commanded Moses. The Israelites, who came out of the land of Egypt, were:
text.Rotherham: From twenty years old and upwards, As Yahweh commanded Moses, and the sons of Israel, who had come forth out of the land of Egypt.
text.KJV: Take the sum of the people, from twenty years old and upward; as the Lord commanded Moses and the children of Israel, which went forth out of the land of Egypt.
text.NABRE: those of twenty years or more, as the Lord had commanded Moses. The Israelites who came out of the land of Egypt were as follows:
text.DRB: from twenty years and upward, as our Lord had commanded, of whom this is the number:

text.NRSV: Reuben, the firstborn of Israel. The descendants of Reuben: of Hanoch, the clan of the Hanochites; of Pallu, the clan of the Palluites;
text.Rotherham: Reuben, the firstborn of Israel,—the sons of Reuben, To Hanoch, pertained the family of the Hanochites; To Pallu, the family of the Palluites;
text.KJV: Reuben, the eldest son of Israel: the children of Reuben; Hanoch, of whom cometh the family of the Hanochites: of Pallu, the family of the Palluites:
text.NABRE: Reuben, the firstborn of Israel. The descendants of Reuben by their clans were: through Hanoch, the clan of the Hanochites; through Pallu, the clan of the Palluites;
text.DRB: Ruben the first born of Israel, his son, Henoch, of whom is the family of the Henochites: and Phallu, of whom is the family of the Phalluites:

text.NRSV: of Hezron, the clan of the Hezronites; of Carmi, the clan of the Carmites.
text.Rotherham: To Hezron, the family of the Hezronites,—To Carmi, the family of the Carmites.
text.KJV: Of Hezron, the family of the Hezronites: of Carmi, the family of the Carmites.
text.NABRE: through Hezron, the clan of the Hezronites; through Carmi, the clan of the Carmites.
text.DRB: and Hesron, of whom is the family of the Hesronites: and Charmi, of whom is the family of the Charmites.

text.NRSV: These are the clans of the Reubenites; the number of those enrolled was forty-three thousand seven hundred thirty.
text.Rotherham: These, are the families of the Reubenites,—and they who were numbered of them were found to be—forty-three thousand, and seven hundred, and thirty.
text.KJV: These are the families of the Reubenites: and they that were numbered of them were forty and three thousand and seven hundred and thirty.
text.NABRE: These were the clans of the Reubenites, and those enrolled numbered forty-three thousand seven hundred and thirty.
text.DRB: These are the families of the stock of Ruben: whose number was found forty three thousand, and seven hundred thirty.

text.NRSV: And the descendants of Pallu: Eliab.
text.Rotherham: Now, the sons of Pallu, were Eliab;
text.KJV: And the sons of Pallu; Eliab.
text.NABRE: From Pallu descended Eliab.
text.DRB: The son of Phallu, Eliab:

text.NRSV: The descendants of Eliab: Nemuel, Dathan, and Abiram. These are the same Dathan and Abiram, chosen from the congregation, who rebelled against Moses and Aaron in the company of Korah, when they rebelled against the Lord,
text.Rotherham: and the sons of Eliab, Nemuel, and Dathan and Abiram,—The same, Dathan and Abiram, notable men of the assembly, who contended against Moses and against Aaron in the assembly of Korah, when they contended against Yahweh;
text.KJV: And the sons of Eliab; Nemuel, and Dathan, and Abiram. This is that Dathan and Abiram, which were famous in the congregation, who strove against Moses and against Aaron in the company of Korah, when they strove against the Lord:
text.NABRE: The sons of Eliab were Nemuel, Dathan, and Abiram —the same Dathan and Abiram, ones designated by the community, who contended with Moses and Aaron as part of Korah’s faction when they contended with the Lord.
text.DRB: his sons, Namuel and Dathan and Abiron. These are Dathan and Abiron the Princes of the people, that rose against Moyses and Aaron in the sedition of Core, when they rebelled against our Lord:

text.NRSV: and the earth opened its mouth and swallowed them up along with Korah, when that company died, when the fire devoured two hundred fifty men; and they became a warning.
text.Rotherham: and the earth opened her mouth, and swallowed them up—with Korah also, when the assembly died,—when the fire consumed two hundred and fifty men, and they became a warning;
text.KJV: And the earth opened her mouth, and swallowed them up together with Korah, when that company died, what time the fire devoured two hundred and fifty men: and they became a sign.
text.NABRE: The earth opened its mouth and swallowed them, along with Korah, as a warning. The faction was destroyed when the fire consumed two hundred and fifty men.
text.DRB: and the earth opening her mouth devoured Core, very many dying, when the fire burnt two hundred fifty men. And there was a great miracle done,

text.NRSV: Notwithstanding, the sons of Korah did not die.
text.Rotherham: But, the sons of Korah, died not.
text.KJV: Notwithstanding the children of Korah died not.
text.NABRE: The descendants of Korah, however, did not die out.
text.DRB: that Core perishing, his sons perished not.

text.NRSV: The descendants of Simeon by their clans: of Nemuel, the clan of the Nemuelites; of Jamin, the clan of the Jaminites; of Jachin, the clan of the Jachinites;
text.Rotherham: The sons of Simeon, by their families, To Nemuel, pertained the family of the Nemuelites, To Jamin, the family of the Jaminites,—To Jachin, the family of the Jachinites:
text.KJV: The sons of Simeon after their families: of Nemuel, the family of the Nemuelites: of Jamin, the family of the Jaminites: of Jachin, the family of the Jachinites:
text.NABRE: The descendants of Simeon by clans were: through Nemuel, the clan of the Nemuelites; through Jamin, the clan of the Jaminites; through Jachin, the clan of the Jachinites;
text.DRB: The sons of Simeon by their kindreds: Namuel, of him is the family of the Namuelites: Iamin, of him is the family of the Iaminites: Iachin, of him is the family of Iachinites:

text.NRSV: of Zerah, the clan of the Zerahites; of Shaul, the clan of the Shaulites.
text.Rotherham: To Zerah, the family of the Zerahites,—To Shaul, the family of the Shaulites:
text.KJV: Of Zerah, the family of the Zarhites: of Shaul, the family of the Shaulites.
text.NABRE: through Zerah, the clan of the Zerahites; through Shaul, the clan of the Shaulites.
text.DRB: Zare, of him is the family of the Zareites: Saul, of him is the family of the Saulites.

text.NRSV: These are the clans of the Simeonites, twenty-two thousand two hundred.
text.Rotherham: These, are the families of the Simeonites,—two and twenty thousand and two hundred.
text.KJV: These are the families of the Simeonites, twenty and two thousand and two hundred.
text.NABRE: These were the clans of the Simeonites, twenty-two thousand two hundred.
text.DRB: These are the families of the stock of Simeon, of which the whole number was two and twenty thousand two hundred.

text.NRSV: The children of Gad by their clans: of Zephon, the clan of the Zephonites; of Haggi, the clan of the Haggites; of Shuni, the clan of the Shunites;
text.Rotherham: The sons of Gad, by their families, To Zephon, pertained the family of the Zephonites; To Haggi, the family of the Haggites,—To Shuni, the family of the Shunites;
text.KJV: The children of Gad after their families: of Zephon, the family of the Zephonites: of Haggi, the family of the Haggites: of Shuni, the family of the Shunites:
text.NABRE: The descendants of Gad by clans were: through Zephon, the clan of the Zephonites; through Haggi, the clan of the Haggites; through Shuni, the clan of the Shunites;
text.DRB: The sons of Gad by their kindreds: Sephon, of him is the family of the Sephonites: Aggi, of him is the family of the Aggites: Suni, of him is the family of the Sunites:

text.NRSV: of Ozni, the clan of the Oznites; of Eri, the clan of the Erites;
text.Rotherham: To Ozni, the family of the Oznites,—To Eri, the family of the Erites;
text.KJV: Of Ozni, the family of the Oznites: of Eri, the family of the Erites:
text.NABRE: through Ozni, the clan of the Oznites; through Eri, the clan of the Erites;
text.DRB: Ozni, of him is the family of the Oznites: Her, of him is the family of the Herites:

text.NRSV: of Arod, the clan of the Arodites; of Areli, the clan of the Arelites.
text.Rotherham: To Arod, the family of the Arodites,—To Arelites, the family of the Arelites:
text.KJV: Of Arod, the family of the Arodites: of Areli, the family of the Arelites.
text.NABRE: through Arod, the clan of the Arodites; through Areli, the clan of the Arelites.
text.DRB: Arod, of him is the family of the Arodites: Ariel, of him is the family of the Arielites.

text.NRSV: These are the clans of the Gadites: the number of those enrolled was forty thousand five hundred.
text.Rotherham: These, are the families of the sons of Gad, as to them who were numbered of them,—forty thousand and five hundred.
text.KJV: These are the families of the children of Gad according to those that were numbered of them, forty thousand and five hundred.
text.NABRE: These were the clans of the descendants of Gad, of whom there were enrolled forty thousand five hundred.
text.DRB: These are the families of Gad, of which the whole number was forty thousand five hundred.

text.NRSV: The sons of Judah: Er and Onan; Er and Onan died in the land of Canaan.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Judah, Er and Onan,—but Er and Onan died in the land of Canaan.
text.KJV: The sons of Judah were Er and Onan: and Er and Onan died in the land of Canaan.
text.NABRE: The sons of Judah were Er and Onan. Er and Onan died in the land of Canaan.
text.DRB: The sons of Judas, Her, and Onan, who died both in the land of Chanaan.

text.NRSV: The descendants of Judah by their clans were: of Shelah, the clan of the Shelanites; of Perez, the clan of the Perezites; of Zerah, the clan of the Zerahites.
text.Rotherham: And so, as to the sons of Judah, by their families, it was found that—To Shelah, pertained the family of the Shelanites, To Perez, the family of the Perezites,—To Zerah, the family of the Zerahites;
text.KJV: And the sons of Judah after their families were; of Shelah, the family of the Shelanites: of Pharez, the family of the Pharzites: of Zerah, the family of the Zarhites.
text.NABRE: The descendants of Judah by their clans were: through Shelah, the clan of the Shelahites; through Perez, the clan of the Perezites; through Zerah, the clan of the Zerahites.
text.DRB: And the sons of Judas by their kindreds were: Sela, of whom is the family of the Selaites: Phares, of whom is the family of the Pharesites: Zare, of whom is the family of the Zareites.

text.NRSV: The descendants of Perez were: of Hezron, the clan of the Hezronites; of Hamul, the clan of the Hamulites.
text.Rotherham: And, as to the sons of Perez, it was found that—To Hezron, pertained the family of the Hezronites,—To Hamul, the family of the Hamulites:
text.KJV: And the sons of Pharez were; of Hezron, the family of the Hezronites: of Hamul, the family of the Hamulites.
text.NABRE: The descendants of Perez were: through Hezron, the clan of the Hezronites; through Hamul, the clan of the Hamulites.
text.DRB: Moreover the sons of Phares; Hesron, of whom is the family of the Hesronites: and Hamul, of whom is the family of the Hamulites.

text.NRSV: These are the clans of Judah: the number of those enrolled was seventy-six thousand five hundred.
text.Rotherham: These, are the families of Judah, as to them who were numbered of them,—seventy-six thousand, and five hundred,
text.KJV: These are the families of Judah according to those that were numbered of them, threescore and sixteen thousand and five hundred.
text.NABRE: These were the clans of Judah, of whom there were enrolled seventy-six thousand five hundred.
text.DRB: these are the families of Judas, of which the whole number was seventy six thousand five hundred.

text.NRSV: The descendants of Issachar by their clans: of Tola, the clan of the Tolaites; of Puvah, the clan of the Punites;
text.Rotherham: The sons of Issachar, by their families, Unto Tola, pertained the family of the Tolaites,—To Puvah, the family of the Puvites;
text.KJV: Of the sons of Issachar after their families: of Tola, the family of the Tolaites: of Pua, the family of the Punites:
text.NABRE: The descendants of Issachar by their clans were: through Tola, the clan of the Tolaites; through Puvah, the clan of the Puvahites;
text.DRB: The sons of Issachar, by their kindreds: Thola, of whom is the family of the Tholaites: Phua, of whom is the family of the Phuaites:

text.NRSV: of Jashub, the clan of the Jashubites; of Shimron, the clan of the Shimronites.
text.Rotherham: To Jashub, the family of the Jashubites,—To Shimron, the family of the Shimronites:
text.KJV: Of Jashub, the family of the Jashubites: of Shimron, the family of the Shimronites.
text.NABRE: through Jashub, the clan of the Jashubites; through Shimron, the clan of the Shimronites.
text.DRB: Jasub, of whom is the family of the Jasubites: Semran, of whom is the family of the Semranites.

text.NRSV: These are the clans of Issachar: sixty-four thousand three hundred enrolled.
text.Rotherham: These, are the families of Issachar. as to them who were numbered of them,—sixty-four thousand, and three hundred.
text.KJV: These are the families of Issachar according to those that were numbered of them, threescore and four thousand and three hundred.
text.NABRE: These were the clans of Issachar, of whom there were enrolled sixty-four thousand three hundred.
text.DRB: these are the kindreds of Issachar, whose number was sixty four thousand three hundred.

text.NRSV: The descendants of Zebulun by their clans: of Sered, the clan of the Seredites; of Elon, the clan of the Elonites; of Jahleel, the clan of the Jahleelites.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Zebulun, by their families, To Sered, pertained the family of the Seredites, To Elon, the family of the Elonites,—To Jahleel, the family of the Jahleelites:
text.KJV: Of the sons of Zebulun after their families: of Sered, the family of the Sardites: of Elon, the family of the Elonites: of Jahleel, the family of the Jahleelites.
text.NABRE: The descendants of Zebulun by their clans were: through Sered, the clan of the Seredites; through Elon, the clan of the Elonites; through Jahleel, the clan of the Jahleelites.
text.DRB: The sons of Zabulon by their kindreds: Sared, of whom is the family of the Saredites: Elon of whom is the family of the Elonites: Ialel of whom is the family of Ialelites.

text.NRSV: These are the clans of the Zebulunites; the number of those enrolled was sixty thousand five hundred.
text.Rotherham: These are the families of the Zebulunites as to them who were numbered of them,—sixty thousand, and five hundred.
text.KJV: These are the families of the Zebulunites according to those that were numbered of them, threescore thousand and five hundred.
text.NABRE: These were the clans of the Zebulunites, of whom there were enrolled sixty thousand five hundred.
text.DRB: These are the kindreds of Zabulon, whose number was sixty thousand five hundred:

text.NRSV: The sons of Joseph by their clans: Manasseh and Ephraim.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Joseph by their families,—Manasseh, and Ephraim.
text.KJV: The sons of Joseph after their families were Manasseh and Ephraim.
text.NABRE: The sons of Joseph were Manasseh and Ephraim.
text.DRB: The sons of Joseph by their kindreds, Manasses & Ephraim.

text.NRSV: The descendants of Manasseh: of Machir, the clan of the Machirites; and Machir was the father of Gilead; of Gilead, the clan of the Gileadites.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Manasseh, To Machir, pertained the family of the Machirites, and, Machir, begat Gilead,—To Gilead, pertained the family of the Gileadites.
text.KJV: Of the sons of Manasseh: of Machir, the family of the Machirites: and Machir begat Gilead: of Gilead come the family of the Gileadites.
text.NABRE: The descendants of Manasseh by clans were: through Machir, the clan of the Machirites. Now Machir begot Gilead: through Gilead, the clan of the Gileadites.
text.DRB: Of Manasses was born Machir, of whom is the family of the Machirites. Machir begat Galaad, of whom is the family of the Galaadites.

text.NRSV: These are the descendants of Gilead: of Iezer, the clan of the Iezerites; of Helek, the clan of the Helekites;
text.Rotherham: These are the sons of Gilead: Iezer, the family of the Iezerites,—To Helek, the family of the Helekites;
text.KJV: These are the sons of Gilead: of Jeezer, the family of the Jeezerites: of Helek, the family of the Helekites:
text.NABRE: The descendants of Gilead were: through Iezer, the clan of the Iezrites; through Helek, the clan of the Helekites;
text.DRB: Galaad had sons: Jezer, of whom is the family of the Jezerites: & Helec, of whom is the family of the Helecites:

text.NRSV: and of Asriel, the clan of the Asrielites; and of Shechem, the clan of the Shechemites;
text.Rotherham: And Asriel, the family of the Asrielites; And, Shechem, the family of the Shechemites;
text.KJV: And of Asriel, the family of the Asrielites: and of Shechem, the family of the Shechemites:
text.NABRE: through Asriel, the clan of the Asrielites; through Shechem, the clan of the Shechemites;
text.DRB: and Asriel, of whom is the family of the Asrielites: and Sechem, of whom is the family of the Sechemites:

text.NRSV: and of Shemida, the clan of the Shemidaites; and of Hepher, the clan of the Hepherites.
text.Rotherham: And Shemida, the family of the Shemidaites; And, Hepher, the family of the Hepherites.
text.KJV: And of Shemida, the family of the Shemidaites: and of Hepher, the family of the Hepherites.
text.NABRE: through Shemida, the clan of the Shemidaites; through Hepher, the clan of the Hepherites.
text.DRB: & Semida, of whom is the family of the Semidaites: and Hepher, of whom is the family of the Hepherites.

text.NRSV: Now Zelophehad son of Hepher had no sons, but daughters: and the names of the daughters of Zelophehad were Mahlah, Noah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Tirzah.
text.Rotherham: Now Zelophehad, son of Hepher, had no sons, but only, daughters,—and, the names of the daughters of Zelophehad, were Mahlah and Noah, Hoglah Milcah, and Tirzah.
text.KJV: And Zelophehad the son of Hepher had no sons, but daughters: and the names of the daughters of Zelophehad were Mahlah, and Noah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Tirzah.
text.NABRE: As for Zelophehad, son of Hepher—he had no sons, but only daughters. The names of the daughters of Zelophehad were Mahlah, Noah, Hoglah, Milcah and Tirzah.
text.DRB: And Hepher was the father of Salphaad, who had no sons, but only daughters, whose names are these: Maala, and Noa, and Hegla, and Melcha, and Tersa.

text.NRSV: These are the clans of Manasseh; the number of those enrolled was fifty-two thousand seven hundred.
text.Rotherham: These, are the families of Manasseh,—and, they who were numbered of them,—fifty-two thousand, and seven hundred.
text.KJV: These are the families of Manasseh, and those that were numbered of them, fifty and two thousand and seven hundred.
text.NABRE: These were the clans of Manasseh, and those enrolled numbered fifty-two thousand seven hundred.
text.DRB: These are the families of Manasses, & the number of them is fifty two thousand seven hundred.

text.NRSV: These are the descendants of Ephraim according to their clans: of Shuthelah, the clan of the Shuthelahites; of Becher, the clan of the Becherites; of Tahan, the clan of the Tahanites.
text.Rotherham: These are the sons of Ephraim by their families, To Shuthelah, pertained the family of the Shuthelahites, To Becher, the family of the Becherites,—To Tahan, the family of the Tahanites.
text.KJV: These are the sons of Ephraim after their families: of Shuthelah, the family of the Shuthalhites: of Becher, the family of the Bachrites: of Tahan, the family of the Tahanites.
text.NABRE: These were the descendants of Ephraim by their clans: through Shuthelah, the clan of the Shuthelahites; through Becher, the clan of the Becherites; through Tahan, the clan of the Tahanites.
text.DRB: And the sons of Ephraim by their kindreds were these: Suthala, of whom is the family of the Suthalaites: Becher, of whom is the family of the Becherites: Thehen, of whom is the family of the Thehenites.

text.NRSV: And these are the descendants of Shuthelah: of Eran, the clan of the Eranites.
text.Rotherham: And, these, are the sons of Shuthelah, To Eran, pertained the family of the Eranites.
text.KJV: And these are the sons of Shuthelah: of Eran, the family of the Eranites.
text.NABRE: These were the descendants of Shuthelah: through Eran, the clan of the Eranites.
text.DRB: Moreover the son of Suthala was Heran, of whom is the family of the Heranites.

text.NRSV: These are the clans of the Ephraimites: the number of those enrolled was thirty-two thousand five hundred. These are the descendants of Joseph by their clans.
text.Rotherham: These, are the families of the sons of Ephraim, as to them who were numbered of them,—thirty-two thousand, and five hundred, These, are the sons of Joseph by their families.
text.KJV: These are the families of the sons of Ephraim according to those that were numbered of them, thirty and two thousand and five hundred. These are the sons of Joseph after their families.
text.NABRE: These were the clans of the Ephraimites, of whom there were enrolled thirty-two thousand five hundred. These were the descendants of Joseph by their clans.
text.DRB: these are the kindreds of the sons of Ephraim: whose number was thirty two thousand five hundred.

text.NRSV: The descendants of Benjamin by their clans: of Bela, the clan of the Belaites; of Ashbel, the clan of the Ashbelites; of Ahiram, the clan of the Ahiramites;
text.Rotherham: The sons of Benjamin by their families, To Bela, pertained the family of the Belaites, To Ashbel, the family of the Ashbelites,—To Ahiram, the family of the Ahiramites;
text.KJV: The sons of Benjamin after their families: of Bela, the family of the Belaites: of Ashbel, the family of the Ashbelites: of Ahiram, the family of the Ahiramites:
text.NABRE: The descendants of Benjamin by their clans were: through Bela, the clan of the Belaites; through Ashbel, the clan of the Ashbelites; through Ahiram, the clan of the Ahiramites;
text.DRB: These are the sons of Joseph by their families. The sons of Benjamin in their kindreds: Bela, of whom is the family of the Belaites: Asbel, of whom is the family of the Asbelites: Ahiram, of whom is the family of the Ahiramites:

text.NRSV: of Shephupham, the clan of the Shuphamites; of Hupham, the clan of the Huphamites.
text.Rotherham: To Shephupham, the family of the Shuphamites,—To Hupham, the family of the Huphamites.
text.KJV: Of Shupham, the family of the Shuphamites: of Hupham, the family of the Huphamites.
text.NABRE: through Shupham, the clan of the Shuphamites; through Hupham, the clan of the Huphamites.
text.DRB: Supham, of whom is the family of the Suphamites: Hupham, of whom is the family of the Huphamites.

text.NRSV: And the sons of Bela were Ard and Naaman: of Ard, the clan of the Ardites; of Naaman, the clan of the Naamites.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Bela were Ard and Naaman,—[To Ard, pertained] the family of the Ardites, To Naaman, the family of the Naamites.
text.KJV: And the sons of Bela were Ard and Naaman: of Ard, the family of the Ardites: and of Naaman, the family of the Naamites.
text.NABRE: The sons of Bela were Ard and Naaman: through Ard, the clan of the Ardites; through Naaman, the clan of the Naamites.
text.DRB: The sons of Bela: Hered, and Noeman. Of Hered, the family of the Heredites: of Noeman, the family of the Noemanites.

text.NRSV: These are the descendants of Benjamin by their clans; the number of those enrolled was forty-five thousand six hundred.
text.Rotherham: These, are the sons of Benjamin by their families,—and they who were numbered of them,—were forty-five thousand and six hundred.
text.KJV: These are the sons of Benjamin after their families: and they that were numbered of them were forty and five thousand and six hundred.
text.NABRE: These were the descendants of Benjamin by their clans, of whom there were enrolled forty-five thousand six hundred.
text.DRB: These are the sons of Benjamin by their kindreds, whose number was forty five thousand six hundred.

text.NRSV: These are the descendants of Dan by their clans: of Shuham, the clan of the Shuhamites. These are the clans of Dan by their clans.
text.Rotherham: These, are the sons of Dan, by their families: To Shuham, pertained the family of the Shuhamites,—These, are the families of Dan by their families:
text.KJV: These are the sons of Dan after their families: of Shuham, the family of the Shuhamites. These are the families of Dan after their families.
text.NABRE: These were the descendants of Dan by their clans: through Shuham the clan of the Shuhamites. These were the clans of Dan,
text.DRB: The sons of Dan by their kindreds: Suham, of whom is the family of the Suhamites: these are the kindreds of Dan by their families.

text.NRSV: All the clans of the Shuhamites: sixty-four thousand four hundred enrolled.
text.Rotherham: All the families of the Shuhamites, as to them who were numbered of them, were, sixty-four thousand, and four hundred.
text.KJV: All the families of the Shuhamites, according to those that were numbered of them, were threescore and four thousand and four hundred.
text.NABRE: of whom there were enrolled sixty-four thousand four hundred.
text.DRB: All were Suhamites, whose number was sixty four thousand four hundred.

text.NRSV: The descendants of Asher by their clans: of Imnah, the clan of the Imnites; of Ishvi, the clan of the Ishvites; of Beriah, the clan of the Beriites.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Asher, by their families: To Imnah, pertained the family of the Imnites, To Ishvi, the family of the Ishvites,—To Beriah, the family of the Beriites;
text.KJV: Of the children of Asher after their families: of Jimna, the family of the Jimnites: of Jesui, the family of the Jesuites: of Beriah, the family of the Beriites.
text.NABRE: The descendants of Asher by their clans were: through Imnah, the clan of the Imnites; through Ishvi, the clan of the Ishvites; through Beriah, the clan of the Beriites;
text.DRB: The sons of Aser by their kindreds: Jemna, of whom is the family of the Jemnaites: Jessui, of whom is the family of the Jessuites: Brie, of whom is the family of the Brieites.

text.NRSV: Of the descendants of Beriah: of Heber, the clan of the Heberites; of Malchiel, the clan of the Malchielites.
text.Rotherham: To the sons of Beriah, To Heber, pertained the family of the Heberites, To Malchiel, the family of the Malchielites;
text.KJV: Of the sons of Beriah: of Heber, the family of the Heberites: of Malchiel, the family of the Malchielites.
text.NABRE: through Heber, the clan of the Heberites; through Malchiel, the clan of the Malchielites.
text.DRB: The sons of Brie: Heber, of whom is the family of the Heberites: and Melchiel, of whom is the family of the Melchielites.

text.NRSV: And the name of the daughter of Asher was Serah.
text.Rotherham: And, the name of the daughter of Asher, was Serah.
text.KJV: And the name of the daughter of Asher was Sarah.
text.NABRE: The name of Asher’s daughter was Serah.
text.DRB: And the name of the daughter of Aser, was Sara.

text.NRSV: These are the clans of the Asherites: the number of those enrolled was fifty-three thousand four hundred.
text.Rotherham: These, are the families of the sons of Asher as to them who were numbered of them,—fifty-three thousand and four hundred.
text.KJV: These are the families of the sons of Asher according to those that were numbered of them; who were fifty and three thousand and four hundred.
text.NABRE: These were the clans of the descendants of Asher, of whom there were enrolled fifty-three thousand four hundred.
text.DRB: These are the kindreds of the Sons of Aser, and their number fifty three thousand four hundred.

text.NRSV: The descendants of Naphtali by their clans: of Jahzeel, the clan of the Jahzeelites; of Guni, the clan of the Gunites;
text.Rotherham: The sons of Naphtali, by their families, To Jahzeel, pertained the family of the Jahzeelites; To Guni, the family of the Gunites:
text.KJV: Of the sons of Naphtali after their families: of Jahzeel, the family of the Jahzeelites: of Guni, the family of the Gunites:
text.NABRE: The descendants of Naphtali by their clans were: through Jahzeel, the clan of the Jahzeelites; through Guni, the clan of the Gunites;
text.DRB: The sons of Nephthali by their kindreds: Jesiel, of whom is the family of the Jesielites: Guni, of whom is the family of the Gunites:

text.NRSV: of Jezer, the clan of the Jezerites; of Shillem, the clan of the Shillemites.
text.Rotherham: To Jezer, the family of the Jezerites; To Shillem, the family of the Shillemites.
text.KJV: Of Jezer, the family of the Jezerites: of Shillem, the family of the Shillemites.
text.NABRE: through Jezer, the clan of the Jezerites; through Shillem, the clan of the Shillemites.
text.DRB: Ieser, of whom is the family of the Ieserites: Sellem, of whom is the family of the Sellemites.

text.NRSV: These are the Naphtalites by their clans: the number of those enrolled was forty-five thousand four hundred.
text.Rotherham: These are the families of Naphtali, by their families,—and they who were numbered of them, were forty-five thousand and four hundred.
text.KJV: These are the families of Naphtali according to their families: and they that were numbered of them were forty and five thousand and four hundred.
text.NABRE: These were the clans of Naphtali, of whom there were enrolled forty-five thousand four hundred.
text.DRB: these are kindreds of the sons of Nephthali by their families: whose number was forty five thousand four hundred.

text.NRSV: This was the number of the Israelites enrolled: six hundred one thousand seven hundred thirty.
text.Rotherham: These, are they who were numbered of the sons of Israel, six hundred and one thousand,—seven hundred and thirty,
text.KJV: These were the numbered of the children of Israel, six hundred thousand and a thousand seven hundred and thirty.
text.NABRE: These were the Israelites who were enrolled: six hundred and one thousand seven hundred and thirty. Allotment of the Land.
text.DRB: This is the sum of the children of Israel, that were reckoned, six hundred thousand, and a thousand seven hundred thirty.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: The Lord said to Moses:
text.DRB: And our Lord spake to Moyses saying:

text.NRSV: To these the land shall be allotted as an inheritance according to the number of names.
text.Rotherham: Unto these, shall be apportioned the land as an inheritance by the number of names.
text.KJV: Unto these the land shall be divided for an inheritance according to the number of names.
text.NABRE: Among these the land shall be divided as their heritage in keeping with the number of people named.
text.DRB: To these shall the land be divided according to the number of names for their possessions.

text.NRSV: To a large tribe you shall give a large inheritance, and to a small tribe you shall give a small inheritance; every tribe shall be given its inheritance in proportion to its enrollment.
text.Rotherham: For the large one, thou shalt make large his inheritance, and for the small one, thou shalt make small his inheritance,—unto each one, in proportion to them who were numbered of him, shall be given his inheritance.
text.KJV: To many thou shalt give the more inheritance, and to few thou shalt give the less inheritance: to every one shall his inheritance be given according to those that were numbered of him.
text.NABRE: To a large tribe you shall assign a large heritage, to a small tribe a small heritage, each receiving its heritage in proportion to the number enrolled in it.
text.DRB: To the greater number thou shalt give a greater portion, and to the fewer a less: to every one, as they have now been reckoned, shall possession be delivered:

text.NRSV: But the land shall be apportioned by lot; according to the names of their ancestral tribes they shall inherit.
text.Rotherham: Nevertheless by lot, shall the land be apportioned,—by the names of the tribes of their fathers, shall they inherit.
text.KJV: Notwithstanding the land shall be divided by lot: according to the names of the tribes of their fathers they shall inherit.
text.NABRE: But the land shall be divided by lot, all inheriting according to the lists of their ancestral tribes.
text.DRB: yet so that lot do divide the Land to the tribes and the families.

text.NRSV: Their inheritance shall be apportioned according to lot between the larger and the smaller.
text.Rotherham: At the bidding of the lot, shall be apportioned his inheritance,—between large and small.
text.KJV: According to the lot shall the possession thereof be divided between many and few.
text.NABRE: As the lot falls the heritage of each tribe, large or small, will be assigned.
text.DRB: Whatsoever shall chance by lot, that let either the more take, or the fewer.

text.NRSV: These are those enrolled of the Levites by their clans: of Gershon, the clan of the Gershonites; of Kohath, the clan of the Kohathites; of Merari, the clan of the Merarites.
text.Rotherham: And these, are they who were numbered of the Levites by their families, To Gershon, pertained the family of the Gershonites, To Kohath, the family of the Kohathites; To Merari, the family of the Merarites.
text.KJV: And these are they that were numbered of the Levites after their families: of Gershon, the family of the Gershonites: of Kohath, the family of the Kohathites: of Merari, the family of the Merarites.
text.NABRE: Census of the Levites. These were the Levites enrolled by clans: through Gershon, the clan of the Gershonites; through Kohath, the clan of the Kohathites; through Merari, the clan of the Merarites.
text.DRB: This also is the number of the sons of Levi by their families: Gerson, of whom the family of the Gersonites: Caath, of whom the family of the Caathites: Merari, of whom the family of the Merarites.

text.NRSV: These are the clans of Levi: the clan of the Libnites, the clan of the Hebronites, the clan of the Mahlites, the clan of the Mushites, the clan of the Korahites. Now Kohath was the father of Amram.
text.Rotherham: These, are the families of Levi—The family of the Libnites, The family of the Hebronites, The family of the Mahlites, The family of the Mushites, The family of the Korahites,—And, Kohath, begat Amram;
text.KJV: These are the families of the Levites: the family of the Libnites, the family of the Hebronites, the family of the Mahlites, the family of the Mushites, the family of the Korathites. And Kohath begat Amram.
text.NABRE: These were clans of Levi: the clan of the Libnites, the clan of the Hebronites, the clan of the Mahlites, the clan of the Mushites, the clan of the Korahites. Now Kohath begot Amram,
text.DRB: These are the families of Levi: The family of Lobni, the family of Hebroni, the family of Moholi, the family of Musi, the family of Core. Howbeit Caath begat Amram:

text.NRSV: The name of Amram's wife was Jochebed daughter of Levi, who was born to Levi in Egypt; and she bore to Amram: Aaron, Moses, and their sister Miriam.
text.Rotherham: and the name of the wife of Amram, was Jochebed daughter of Levi, who was born to Levi in Egypt,—and she bare to Amram Aaron and Moses, and Miriam their sister:
text.KJV: And the name of Amram’s wife was Jochebed, the daughter of Levi, whom her mother bare to Levi in Egypt: and she bare unto Amram Aaron and Moses, and Miriam their sister.
text.NABRE: whose wife was named Jochebed. She was the daughter of Levi, born to Levi in Egypt. To Amram she bore Aaron and Moses and Miriam their sister.
text.DRB: who had to wife Iochabed the daughter of Levi, who was born to him in Ægypt, She bare to Amram her husband sons, Aaron and Moyses, and Mary their sister.

text.NRSV: To Aaron were born Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.
text.Rotherham: And, there were born to Aaron, Nadab and Abihu,—Eleazar and Ithamar;
text.KJV: And unto Aaron was born Nadab, and Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.
text.NABRE: To Aaron were born Nadab and Abihu, Eleazar and Ithamar.
text.DRB: Of Aaron were born Nadab and Abiu, and Eleazar and Ithamar:

text.NRSV: But Nadab and Abihu died when they offered unholy fire before the Lord.
text.Rotherham: But Nadab and Abihu died,—when they brought near strange fire before Yahweh:
text.KJV: And Nadab and Abihu died, when they offered strange fire before the Lord.
text.NABRE: But Nadab and Abihu died when they offered unauthorized fire before the Lord.
text.DRB: of the which Nadab and Abiu died, when they had offered the strange fire before our Lord.

text.NRSV: The number of those enrolled was twenty-three thousand, every male from a month old and upward; for they were not enrolled among the Israelites because there was no allotment given to them among the Israelites.
text.Rotherham: And they who were numbered of them were found to be—twenty-three thousand, all the males from one month old and upwards—for they had not numbered themselves in the midst of the sons of Israel, because there was given unto them no inheritance, in the midst of the sons of Israel.
text.KJV: And those that were numbered of them were twenty and three thousand, all males from a month old and upward: for they were not numbered among the children of Israel, because there was no inheritance given them among the children of Israel.
text.NABRE: The Levites enrolled were twenty-three thousand, every male one month or more of age. They were not enrolled with the other Israelites, however, for no heritage was given them among the Israelites.
text.DRB: And all that were numbered were twenty three thousand of the male kind from one month and upward: who were not reckoned among the children of Israel, neither was their possession given with the rest.

text.NRSV: These were those enrolled by Moses and Eleazar the priest, who enrolled the Israelites in the plains of Moab by the Jordan opposite Jericho.
text.Rotherham: These, are they who were numbered by Moses, and Eleazar, the priest,—when they numbered the sons of Israel, in the waste plains of Moab, by Jordan, near Jericho.
text.KJV: These are they that were numbered by Moses and Eleazar the priest, who numbered the children of Israel in the plains of Moab by Jordan near Jericho.
text.NABRE: These, then, were those enrolled by Moses and Eleazar the priest, when they enrolled the Israelites on the plains of Moab along the Jordan at Jericho.
text.DRB: This is the number of the children of Israel, that were enrolled by Moyses and Eleazar the Priest, in the champion country of Moab upon Jordan against Jericho.

text.NRSV: Among these there was not one of those enrolled by Moses and Aaron the priest, who had enrolled the Israelites in the wilderness of Sinai.
text.Rotherham: And among these, was there not found a man of them who had been numbered by Moses, and Aaron the priest,—when they numbered the sons of Israel in the desert of Sinai.
text.KJV: But among these there was not a man of them whom Moses and Aaron the priest numbered, when they numbered the children of Israel in the wilderness of Sinai.
