text.NABRE: Among them there was not one of those who had been enrolled by Moses and Aaron the priest, when they enrolled the Israelites in the wilderness of Sinai.
text.DRB: Among whom there was none of them that were numbered before by Moyses and Aaron in the desert of Sinai.

text.NRSV: For the Lord had said of them, "They shall die in the wilderness." Not one of them was left, except Caleb son of Jephunneh and Joshua son of Nun.
text.Rotherham: For Yahweh had said of them, They shall surely die, in the desert. And there was not left of them a man, save only, Caleb son of Jephunneh, and Joshua son of Nun.
text.KJV: For the Lord had said of them, They shall surely die in the wilderness. And there was not left a man of them, save Caleb the son of Jephunneh, and Joshua the son of Nun.
text.NABRE: For the Lord had told them that they would surely die in the wilderness, and not one of them was left except Caleb, son of Jephunneh, and Joshua, son of Nun.
text.DRB: For our Lord had foretold that all should die in the wilderness. And none remained of them, but Caleb the son of Jephone, and Josue the son of Nun.

text.NRSV: Then the daughters of Zelophehad came forward. Zelophehad was son of Hepher son of Gilead son of Machir son of Manasseh son of Joseph, a member of the Manassite clans. The names of his daughters were: Mahlah, Noah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Tirzah.
text.Rotherham: Then came near the daughters of Zelophehad, son of Hepher, son of Gilead son of Machir son of Manasseh pertaining to the families of Manasseh, son of Joseph,—these being the names of his daughters, Mahlah Noah and Hoglah and Milcah, and Tirzah.
text.KJV: Then came the daughters of Zelophehad, the son of Hepher, the son of Gilead, the son of Machir, the son of Manasseh, of the families of Manasseh the son of Joseph: and these are the names of his daughters; Mahlah, Noah, and Hoglah, and Milcah, and Tirzah.
text.NABRE: The daughters of Zelophehad, son of Hepher, son of Gilead, son of Machir, son of Manasseh, came forward. (Zelophehad belonged to the clans of Manasseh, son of Joseph.) The names of his daughters were Mahlah, Noah, Hoglah, Milcah and Tirzah.
text.DRB: And there came the daughters of Salphaad, the son of Hepher, the son of Galaad, the son of Machir, the son of Manasses, who was the son of Joseph: whose names are, Maala, and Noa, and Hegla, and Melcha, and Thersa.

text.NRSV: They stood before Moses, Eleazar the priest, the leaders, and all the congregation, at the entrance of the tent of meeting, and they said,"
text.Rotherham: So they stood before Moses and before Eleazar the priest, and before the princes, and all the assembly,—at the entrance of the tent of meeting, saying:
text.KJV: And they stood before Moses, and before Eleazar the priest, and before the princes and all the congregation, by the door of the tabernacle of the congregation, saying,
text.NABRE: Standing before Moses, Eleazar the priest, the princes, and the whole community at the entrance of the tent of meeting, they said:
text.DRB: And they stood before Moyses and Eleazar the Priest, and all the Princes of the people at the door of the tabernacle of covenant, and said:

text.NRSV: Our father died in the wilderness; he was not among the company of those who gathered themselves together against the Lord in the company of Korah, but died for his own sin; and he had no sons."
text.Rotherham: Our father, died in the desert, although, he, was not among the assembly that conspired against Yahweh, in the assembly of Korah,—but, in his own sin, died he; and sons, had he none.
text.KJV: Our father died in the wilderness, and he was not in the company of them that gathered themselves together against the Lord in the company of Korah; but died in his own sin, and had no sons.
text.NABRE: “Our father died in the wilderness. Although he did not join the faction of those who conspired against the Lord, Korah’s faction, he died for his own sin without leaving any sons.
text.DRB: Our father died in the desert, neither was he in the sedition, that was raised against our Lord under Core, but he died in his own sin: he had no men children. Why is his name taken away out of his family, because he hath not a son? Give us possession among the kin of our father.

text.NRSV: Why should the name of our father be taken away from his clan because he had no son? Give to us a possession among our father's brothers."
text.Rotherham: Wherefore should the name of our father be withdrawn out of the midst of his family, because he had no son? Give ye unto us a possession, in the midst of the brethren of our father.
text.KJV: Why should the name of our father be done away from among his family, because he hath no son? Give unto us therefore a possession among the brethren of our father.
text.NABRE: But why should our father’s name be cut off from his clan merely because he had no son? Give us land among our father’s kindred.” Laws Concerning Heiresses.
text.DRB: And Moyses referred their cause to the judgment of our Lord.

text.NRSV: Moses brought their case before the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And Moses brought near their cause before Yahweh.
text.KJV: And Moses brought their cause before the Lord.
text.NABRE: So Moses laid their case before the Lord,
text.DRB: Who said to him:

text.NRSV: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: and the Lord said to him:
text.DRB: The daughters of Salphaad require a just thing: give them possession among their father's kin, and let them succeed him in the inheritance.

text.NRSV: The daughters of Zelophehad are right in what they are saying; you shall indeed give them possession of an inheritance among their father's brothers and pass the inheritance of their father on to them.
text.Rotherham: A right thing, are the daughters of Zelophehad speaking: Thou shalt, surely give, them a possession for an inheritance, in the midst of the brethren of their father,—and shalt cause the inheritance of their father to pass over unto them.
text.KJV: The daughters of Zelophehad speak right: thou shalt surely give them a possession of an inheritance among their father’s brethren; and thou shalt cause the inheritance of their father to pass unto them.
text.NABRE: The plea of Zelophehad’s daughters is just; you shall give them hereditary land among their father’s kindred and transfer their father’s heritage to them.
text.DRB: And to the children of Israel thou shalt speak these things:

text.NRSV: You shall also say to the Israelites, "If a man dies, and has no son, then you shall pass his inheritance on to his daughter."
text.Rotherham: And unto the sons of Israel, shalt thou speak, saying,—When, any man, shall die, having no son, then shall ye cause his inheritance to pass over to his daughter.
text.KJV: And thou shalt speak unto the children of Israel, saying, If a man die, and have no son, then ye shall cause his inheritance to pass unto his daughter.
text.NABRE: Tell the Israelites: If a man dies without leaving a son, you shall transfer his heritage to his daughter;
text.DRB: When a man dieth without a son, his inheritance shall pass to his daughter.

text.NRSV: If he has no daughter, then you shall give his inheritance to his brothers.
text.Rotherham: And, if he hath no daughter, then shall ye give his inheritance unto his, brethren.
text.KJV: And if he have no daughter, then ye shall give his inheritance unto his brethren.
text.NABRE: if he has no daughter, you shall give his heritage to his brothers;
text.DRB: If he have no daughter, he shall have his brethren his successors.

text.NRSV: If he has no brothers, then you shall give his inheritance to his father's brothers.
text.Rotherham: And if he hath no brethren, then shall ye give his inheritance unto the brethren of his father.
text.KJV: And if he have no brethren, then ye shall give his inheritance unto his father’s brethren.
text.NABRE: if he has no brothers, you shall give his heritage to his father’s brothers;
text.DRB: And if he have no brethren neither, you shall give the inheritance to his father's brethren.

text.NRSV: And if his father has no brothers, then you shall give his inheritance to the nearest kinsman of his clan, and he shall possess it. It shall be for the Israelites a statute and ordinance, as the Lord commanded Moses."
text.Rotherham: And, if his father hath no brethren, then shall ye give his inheritance unto his blood-relation that is near unto him of his family, and he shall possess it,—So shall it serve the sons of Israel as a regulative statute, As Yahweh commanded Moses.
text.KJV: And if his father have no brethren, then ye shall give his inheritance unto his kinsman that is next to him of his family, and he shall possess it: and it shall be unto the children of Israel a statute of judgment, as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.NABRE: if his father had no brothers, you shall give his heritage to his nearest relative in his clan, who shall then take possession of it. This will be the statutory procedure for the Israelites, as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.DRB: But if he have no such uncles by the father neither, the inheritance shall be given to them that are the next of kin: and this shall be to the children of Israel a holy ordinance by a perpetual law, as the Lord hath commanded Moyses.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Moses, "Go up this mountain of the Abarim range, and see the land that I have given to the Israelites."
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said unto Moses, Go up into this mount of Abarim,—and see, the land which I have given unto the sons of Israel;
text.KJV: And the Lord said unto Moses, Get thee up into this mount Abarim, and see the land which I have given unto the children of Israel.
text.NABRE: Joshua to Succeed Moses. The Lord said to Moses: Go up into this mountain of the Abarim range and view the land that I have given to the Israelites.
text.DRB: Our Lord also said to Moyses: Go up into this mountain Abarim, and view from thence the Land which I will give to the children of Israel:

text.NRSV: When you have seen it, you also shall be gathered to your people, as your brother Aaron was,"
text.Rotherham: And, when thou hast seen it, then shalt thou also be withdrawn unto thy kinsfolk,—as Aaron thy brother was withdrawn;
text.KJV: And when thou hast seen it, thou also shalt be gathered unto thy people, as Aaron thy brother was gathered.
text.NABRE: When you have viewed it, you will be gathered to your people, as was Aaron your brother.
text.DRB: and when thou shall have seen it, thou also shall go to thy people, as thy brother Aaron is gone:

text.NRSV: because you rebelled against my word in the wilderness of Zin during the strife of the congregation, at the waters of Meribah of Kadesh in the wilderness of Zin; you did not treat me as holy in the midst of the Israelites."
text.Rotherham: because ye resisted my bidding—in the desert of Zin when the assembly contended,—that ye should hallow me regarding the waters, before their eyes,—the same, were the waters of Meribah, of Kadesh in the desert of Zin.
text.KJV: For ye rebelled against my commandment in the desert of Zin, in the strife of the congregation, to sanctify me at the water before their eyes: that is the water of Meribah in Kadesh in the wilderness of Zin.
text.NABRE: For in the rebellion of the community in the wilderness of Zin you both rebelled against my order to acknowledge my holiness before them by means of the water. (These were the waters of Meribah of Kadesh in the wilderness of Zin.)
text.DRB: because you did offend me in the desert Sin in the contradiction of the multitude, neither would you sanctify me before them upon the waters. These are the waters of contradiction in Cades of the desert Sin.

text.NRSV: Moses spoke to the Lord, saying,
text.Rotherham: Then spake Moses unto Yahweh saying:
text.KJV: And Moses spake unto the Lord, saying,
text.NABRE: Then Moses said to the Lord,
text.DRB: To whom Moyses answered:

text.NRSV: "Let the Lord, the God of the spirits of all flesh, appoint someone over the congregation"
text.Rotherham: Let Yahweh, God of the spirits of all flesh, appoint a man over the assembly;
text.KJV: Let the Lord, the God of the spirits of all flesh, set a man over the congregation,
text.NABRE: “May the Lord, the God of the spirits of all humanity, set over the community someone
text.DRB: Our Lord, the God of the spirits of all flesh, provide a man that may be over this multitude:

text.NRSV: who shall go out before them and come in before them, who shall lead them out and bring them in, so that the congregation of the Lord may not be like sheep without a shepherd."
text.Rotherham: who may go out before them and who may come in before them, and who may take them out and who may bring them in,—that the assembly of Yahweh become not as sheep that have no shepherd.
text.KJV: Which may go out before them, and which may go in before them, and which may lead them out, and which may bring them in; that the congregation of the Lord be not as sheep which have no shepherd.
text.NABRE: who will be their leader in battle and who will lead them out and bring them in, that the Lord ’s community may not be like sheep without a shepherd.”
text.DRB: and may go out and enter in before them, and bring them out, or bring them in: lest the people of our Lord be as sheep without a pastor.

text.NRSV: So the Lord said to Moses, "Take Joshua son of Nun, a man in whom is the spirit, and lay your hand upon him;"
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said unto Moses: Take thee Joshua son of Nun, a man in whom is spirit,—then shalt thou lean thy hand upon him;
text.KJV: And the Lord said unto Moses, Take thee Joshua the son of Nun, a man in whom is the spirit, and lay thine hand upon him;
text.NABRE: And the Lord replied to Moses: Take Joshua, son of Nun, a man of spirit, and lay your hand upon him.
text.DRB: And our Lord said to him: Take Josue the son of Nun, a man in whom is the Spirit, and put thy hand upon him.

text.NRSV: have him stand before Eleazar the priest and all the congregation, and commission him in their sight.
text.Rotherham: and shalt cause him to stand before Eleazar the priest, and before all the assembly,—and shalt charge him before their eyes;
text.KJV: And set him before Eleazar the priest, and before all the congregation; and give him a charge in their sight.
text.NABRE: Have him stand before Eleazar the priest and the whole community, and commission him in their sight.
text.DRB: Who shall stand before Eleazar the Priest and all the multitude:

text.NRSV: You shall give him some of your authority, so that all the congregation of the Israelites may obey.
text.Rotherham: and shalt put some of thine honour upon him,—that all the assembly of the sons of Israel may hearken.
text.KJV: And thou shalt put some of thine honour upon him, that all the congregation of the children of Israel may be obedient.
text.NABRE: Invest him with some of your own power, that the whole Israelite community may obey him.
text.DRB: and thou shalt give him precepts in the sight of all, and part of thy glory, that all the synagogue of the children of Israel may hear him.

text.NRSV: But he shall stand before Eleazar the priest, who shall inquire for him by the decision of the Urim before the Lord; at his word they shall go out, and at his word they shall come in, both he and all the Israelites with him, the whole congregation."
text.Rotherham: And, before Eleazar the priest, shall he stand, and shall ask by him for the decision of the Lights, before Yahweh,—at the bidding thereof, shall they go out, and at the bidding thereof, shall they come in—he, and all the sons of Israel with him, even all the assembly.
text.KJV: And he shall stand before Eleazar the priest, who shall ask counsel for him after the judgment of Urim before the Lord: at his word shall they go out, and at his word they shall come in, both he, and all the children of Israel with him, even all the congregation.
text.NABRE: He shall present himself to Eleazar the priest, who will seek for him the decision of the Urim in the Lord ’s presence; and as it directs, Joshua, all the Israelites with him, and the whole community will go out for battle; and as it directs, they will come in.
text.DRB: For him, if any thing be to be done, Eleazar the Priest shall consult the Lord. At his word shall he go out and shall go in, and all the children of Israel with him, and the rest of the multitude.

text.NRSV: So Moses did as the Lord commanded him. He took Joshua and had him stand before Eleazar the priest and the whole congregation;
text.Rotherham: And Moses did as Yahweh commanded him,—and took Joshua, and caused him to stand before Eleazar the priest, and before all the assembly;
text.KJV: And Moses did as the Lord commanded him: and he took Joshua, and set him before Eleazar the priest, and before all the congregation:
text.NABRE: Moses did as the Lord had commanded him. Taking Joshua and having him stand before Eleazar the priest and the whole community,
text.DRB: Moyses did as our Lord had commanded. And when he had taken Josue, he set him before Eleazar the Priest, and all the assembly of the people.

text.NRSV: he laid his hands on him and commissioned him—as the Lord had directed through Moses.
text.Rotherham: and leaned his hands upon him and charged him,—As Yahweh spake by the hand of Moses.
text.KJV: And he laid his hands upon him, and gave him a charge, as the Lord commanded by the hand of Moses.
text.NABRE: he laid his hands on him and commissioned him, as the Lord had directed through Moses.
text.DRB: And imposing his hands on his head, he repeated all things that our Lord had commanded.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: The Lord said to Moses:
text.DRB: Our Lord also said to Moyses:

text.NRSV: Command the Israelites, and say to them: My offering, the food for my offerings by fire, my pleasing odor, you shall take care to offer to me at its appointed time.
text.Rotherham: Command the sons of Israel, and thou shalt say unto them,—My offering, my food for my altar-flames my satisfying odour, shall ye take heed to offer unto me in its season,
text.KJV: Command the children of Israel, and say unto them, My offering, and my bread for my sacrifices made by fire, for a sweet savour unto me, shall ye observe to offer unto me in their due season.
text.NABRE: Give the Israelites this commandment: At their prescribed times, you will be careful to present to me the food offerings that are due me, oblations of pleasing aroma to me.
text.DRB: Command the children of Israel, and thou shalt say to them: My oblation and breads, and burnt sacrifice of most sweet odour offer ye in their times.

text.NRSV: And you shall say to them, This is the offering by fire that you shall offer to the Lord: two male lambs a year old without blemish, daily, as a regular offering.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shalt thou say to them. This, is the altar-flame, which ye shall offer unto Yahweh,—he-lambs a year old, without defect, two daily, as a continual ascending-sacrifice.
text.KJV: And thou shalt say unto them, This is the offering made by fire which ye shall offer unto the Lord; two lambs of the first year without spot day by day, for a continual burnt offering.
text.NABRE: Each Morning and Evening. You will tell them therefore: This is the oblation which you will offer to the Lord: two unblemished yearling lambs each day as the regular burnt offering,
text.DRB: These are the sacrifices which you must offer: Two lambs of a year old without blemish daily for the everlasting holocaust:

text.NRSV: One lamb you shall offer in the morning, and the other lamb you shall offer at twilight;
text.Rotherham: The one lamb, shalt thou offer in the morning,—and the other lamb, shalt thou offer between the two evenings;
text.KJV: The one lamb shalt thou offer in the morning, and the other lamb shalt thou offer at even;
text.NABRE: offering one lamb in the morning and the other during the evening twilight,
text.DRB: one you shall offer in the morning, and the other at even:

text.NRSV: also one-tenth of an ephah of choice flour for a grain offering, mixed with one-fourth of a hin of beaten oil.
text.Rotherham: also the tenth of an ephah of fine meal for a meal-offering,—overflowed with beaten oil the fourth of a hin:
text.KJV: And a tenth part of an ephah of flour for a meat offering, mingled with the fourth part of an hin of beaten oil.
text.NABRE: each with a grain offering of one tenth of an ephah of bran flour mixed with a fourth of a hin of oil of crushed olives.
text.DRB: the tenth part of an ephi of flour, which shall be tempered with the purest oil, and shall have the fourth part of an hin.

text.NRSV: It is a regular burnt offering, ordained at Mount Sinai for a pleasing odor, an offering by fire to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: a continual ascending-sacrifice,—which was offered in Mount Sinai, as a satisfying odour an altar-flame unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: It is a continual burnt offering, which was ordained in mount Sinai for a sweet savour, a sacrifice made by fire unto the Lord.
text.NABRE: This is the regular burnt offering that was made at Mount Sinai for a pleasing aroma, an oblation to the Lord.
text.DRB: It is the continual holocaust which you offered in Mount Sinai for a most sweet odour of the burnt sacrifice of the Lord.

text.NRSV: Its drink offering shall be one-fourth of a hin for each lamb; in the sanctuary you shall pour out a drink offering of strong drink to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Also the drink-offering thereof the fourth of a hin for each lamb,—in a holy place, shall it be poured out as a libation of strong drink unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: And the drink offering thereof shall be the fourth part of an hin for the one lamb: in the holy place shalt thou cause the strong wine to be poured unto the Lord for a drink offering.
text.NABRE: And as the libation for the first lamb, you will make a libation to the Lord in the sanctuary of a fourth of a hin of strong drink.
text.DRB: and for a libament you shall offer of wine the fourth part of an hin for every lamb in the Sanctuary of the Lord.

text.NRSV: The other lamb you shall offer at twilight with a grain offering and a drink offering like the one in the morning; you shall offer it as an offering by fire, a pleasing odor to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And, the other lamb, shalt thou offer between the two evenings; like the meal-offering of the morning, and like the drink-offering thereof, shalt thou offer, an altar flame a satisfying odour unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: And the other lamb shalt thou offer at even: as the meat offering of the morning, and as the drink offering thereof, thou shalt offer it, a sacrifice made by fire, of a sweet savour unto the Lord.
text.NABRE: The other lamb you will offer during the evening twilight, making the same grain offering and the same libation as in the morning, as an oblation of pleasing aroma to the Lord.
text.DRB: And the other lamb in like manner you shall offer at even according to all the rite of the morning sacrifice, and of the libaments thereof, an oblation of most sweet odour to the Lord.

text.NRSV: On the sabbath day: two male lambs a year old without blemish, and two-tenths of an ephah of choice flour for a grain offering, mixed with oil, and its drink offering—
text.Rotherham: But, on the sabbath day, two he-lambs a year old without defect, and two-tenths of fine meal as a meal-offering, overflowed with oil and the drink-offering thereof:
text.KJV: And on the sabbath day two lambs of the first year without spot, and two tenth deals of flour for a meat offering, mingled with oil, and the drink offering thereof:
text.NABRE: On the Sabbath. On the sabbath day: two unblemished yearling lambs, with a grain offering of two tenths of an ephah of bran flour mixed with oil, and its libation.
text.DRB: And on the day of the Sabbath, you shall offer two lambs of a year old without blemish, and two tenths of flour tempered with oil in sacrifice, & the libaments,

text.NRSV: this is the burnt offering for every sabbath, in addition to the regular burnt offering and its drink offering.
text.Rotherham: the ascending-sacrifice of a sabbath, on its own sabbath,—besides the continual ascending-sacrifice and the drink-offering thereof.
text.KJV: This is the burnt offering of every sabbath, beside the continual burnt offering, and his drink offering.
text.NABRE: This is the sabbath burnt offering each sabbath, in addition to the regular burnt offering and its libation.
text.DRB: which are ritely poured every Sabbath for an everlasting holocaust.

text.NRSV: At the beginnings of your months you shall offer a burnt offering to the Lord: two young bulls, one ram, seven male lambs a year old without blemish;
text.Rotherham: And in the beginnings of your months, shall ye bring near an ascending-sacrifice unto Yahweh, two choice bullocks and one ram, seven he-lambs a year old without defect;
text.KJV: And in the beginnings of your months ye shall offer a burnt offering unto the Lord; two young bullocks, and one ram, seven lambs of the first year without spot;
text.NABRE: At the New Moon Feast. On your new moons you will offer as a burnt offering to the Lord two bulls of the herd, one ram, and seven unblemished yearling lambs,
text.DRB: And in the Calends you shall offer an holocaust to the Lord, two calves of the herd, one ram, seven lambs of a year old without blemish,

text.NRSV: also three-tenths of an ephah of choice flour for a grain offering, mixed with oil, for each bull; and two-tenths of choice flour for a grain offering, mixed with oil, for the one ram;
text.Rotherham: and three-tenths of fine meal, as a meal-offering, overflowed with oil, to each bullock,—and two-tenths of fine-meal as a meal-offering, overflowed with oil, to each ram;
text.KJV: And three tenth deals of flour for a meat offering, mingled with oil, for one bullock; and two tenth deals of flour for a meat offering, mingled with oil, for one ram;
text.NABRE: with three tenths of an ephah of bran flour mixed with oil as the grain offering for each bull, two tenths of an ephah of bran flour mixed with oil as the grain offering for the ram,
text.DRB: and three tenths of flour tempered with oil in sacrifice for every calf: and two tenths of flour tempered with oil to every ram:

text.NRSV: and one-tenth of choice flour mixed with oil as a grain offering for every lamb—a burnt offering of pleasing odor, an offering by fire to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: and a tenth, severally, of fine meal as a meal-offering, overflowed with oil, to each lamb,—an ascending-sacrifice, a satisfying odour, an altar-flame unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: And a several tenth deal of flour mingled with oil for a meat offering unto one lamb; for a burnt offering of a sweet savour, a sacrifice made by fire unto the Lord.
text.NABRE: and one tenth of an ephah of bran flour mixed with oil as the grain offering for each lamb, a burnt offering with a pleasing aroma, an oblation to the Lord.
text.DRB: and the tenth part of a tenth of flour tempered with oil in sacrifice to every lamb. It is an holocaust of most sweet odour and burnt sacrifice to the Lord.

text.NRSV: Their drink offering shall be half a hin of wine for a bull, one-third of a hin for a ram, and one-fourth of a hin for a lamb. This is the burnt offering of every month throughout the months of the year.
text.Rotherham: And as their drink-offerings, half a hin, shall be to a bullock and the third of a hin to a ram, and the fourth of a hin to a lamb, of wine,—This, is the ascending-sacrifice of a month in its month, for the months of the year.
text.KJV: And their drink offerings shall be half an hin of wine unto a bullock, and the third part of an hin unto a ram, and a fourth part of an hin unto a lamb: this is the burnt offering of every month throughout the months of the year.
text.NABRE: Their libations will consist of a half a hin of wine for each bull, a third of a hin for the ram, and a fourth of a hin for each lamb. This is the burnt offering for the new moon, for every new moon through the months of the year.
text.DRB: And the libaments of wine, that are to be poured for every victim, shall be these: The half part of an hin for every calf, the third to a ram, the fourth to a lamb. This shall be the holocaust through all months that succeed one another as the year turneth about.

text.NRSV: And there shall be one male goat for a sin offering to the Lord; it shall be offered in addition to the regular burnt offering and its drink offering.
text.Rotherham: Also one he-goat as a sin-bearer unto Yahweh,—besides the continual ascending-sacrifice, shall it be offered, with the drink-offering thereof.
text.KJV: And one kid of the goats for a sin offering unto the Lord shall be offered, beside the continual burnt offering, and his drink offering.
text.NABRE: Moreover, there will be one goat for a purification offering to the Lord; it will be offered in addition to the regular burnt offering and its libation.
text.DRB: Abuck goat also shall be offered to the Lord for sins an everlasting holocaust with his libaments.

text.NRSV: In the first month, on the fourteenth day of the month, is the passover of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And, on the first month on the fourteenth day of the month, shall be a passover unto Yahweh;
text.KJV: And in the fourteenth day of the first month is the passover of the Lord.
text.NABRE: At the Passover. The fourteenth day of the first month is the Passover of the Lord,
text.DRB: And in the first month, the fourteenth day of the month shall be the Phase of the Lord,

text.NRSV: On the fifteenth day of this month there shall be a festival; seven days shall unleavened bread be eaten.
text.Rotherham: and, on the fifteenth day of this month, a festival,—seven days, unleavened cakes, shall be eaten.
text.KJV: And in the fifteenth day of this month is the feast: seven days shall unleavened bread be eaten.
text.NABRE: and the fifteenth day of this month is the pilgrimage feast. For seven days unleavened bread is to be eaten.
text.DRB: and the fifteenth day the solemnity: seven days shall they eat azymes.

text.NRSV: On the first day there shall be a holy convocation. You shall not work at your occupations.
text.Rotherham: On the first day, a holy convocation,—no laborious work, shall ye do;
text.KJV: In the first day shall be an holy convocation; ye shall do no manner of servile work therein:
text.NABRE: On the first day you will declare a holy day, and you shall do no heavy work.
text.DRB: Of the which the first day shall be venerable & holy: no servile work shall you do therein.

text.NRSV: You shall offer an offering by fire, a burnt offering to the Lord: two young bulls, one ram, and seven male lambs a year old; see that they are without blemish.
text.Rotherham: but ye shall bring near—as an altar-flame—an ascending-sacrifice unto Yahweh,—two choice bullocks and one ram,—and seven he-lambs a year old, without defect, must they be for you;
text.KJV: But ye shall offer a sacrifice made by fire for a burnt offering unto the Lord; two young bullocks, and one ram, and seven lambs of the first year: they shall be unto you without blemish:
text.NABRE: You will offer an oblation, a burnt offering to the Lord: two bulls of the herd, one ram, and seven yearling lambs that you are sure are unblemished.
text.DRB: And you shall offer burnt sacrifice an holocaust to the Lord, two calves of the herd, one ram, seven lambs of a year old without blemish:

text.NRSV: Their grain offering shall be of choice flour mixed with oil: three-tenths of an ephah shall you offer for a bull, and two-tenths for a ram;
text.Rotherham: and, for their meal-offering, fine meal, overflowed with oil,—three-tenths for a bullock, and two-tenths for a ram, shall ye offer;
text.KJV: And their meat offering shall be of flour mingled with oil: three tenth deals shall ye offer for a bullock, and two tenth deals for a ram;
text.NABRE: Their grain offerings will be of bran flour mixed with oil; you will offer three tenths of an ephah for each bull and two tenths for the ram.
text.DRB: and the sacrifice of every one of flour which shall be tempered with oil, three tenths to every calf, & two tenths to every ram,

text.NRSV: one-tenth you shall offer for each of the seven lambs;
text.Rotherham: a tenth, severally, shalt thou offer for each lamb,—for the seven lambs,;
text.KJV: A several tenth deal shalt thou offer for every lamb, throughout the seven lambs:
text.NABRE: You will offer one tenth for each of the seven lambs;
text.DRB: and the tenth part of a tenth to every lamb, that is to say, to the seven lambs.

text.NRSV: also one male goat for a sin offering, to make atonement for you.
text.Rotherham: also one he-goat for bearing sin, to put a propitiatory-covering over you:
text.KJV: And one goat for a sin offering, to make an atonement for you.
text.NABRE: and one goat as a purification offering to make atonement for yourselves.
text.DRB: And one buck goat for sin, that expiation may be made for you,

text.NRSV: You shall offer these in addition to the burnt offering of the morning, which belongs to the regular burnt offering.
text.Rotherham: in addition to the ascending-sacrifice of the morning, which is for the continual ascending-sacrifice, shall ye offer these.
text.KJV: Ye shall offer these beside the burnt offering in the morning, which is for a continual burnt offering.
text.NABRE: These offerings you will make in addition to the morning burnt offering which is part of the regular burnt offering.
text.DRB: beside the morning holocaust which you shall always offer.

text.NRSV: In this way you shall offer daily, for seven days, the food of the offering by fire, a pleasing odor to the Lord; it shall be offered in addition to the regular burnt offering and its drink offering.
text.Rotherham: Like these, shall ye offer, daily, for seven days, as the food of the altar-flame of a satisfying odour, unto Yahweh,—besides the continual ascending-sacrifice, shall it be offered, with the drink-offering thereof.
text.KJV: After this manner ye shall offer daily, throughout the seven days, the meat of the sacrifice made by fire, of a sweet savour unto the Lord: it shall be offered beside the continual burnt offering, and his drink offering.
text.NABRE: You will make exactly the same offerings each day for seven days as food offerings, oblations of pleasing aroma to the Lord; they will be offered in addition to the regular burnt offering with its libation.
text.DRB: So shall you do every day of the seven days for a food of the fire, & for a most sweet odour to the Lord, which shall rise of the holocaust, and of the libaments of every one.

text.NRSV: And on the seventh day you shall have a holy convocation; you shall not work at your occupations.
text.Rotherham: And on the seventh day, shall ye have, a holy convocation,—no laborious work, shall ye do.
text.KJV: And on the seventh day ye shall have an holy convocation; ye shall do no servile work.
text.NABRE: On the seventh day you will declare a holy day: you shall do no heavy work.
text.DRB: The seventh day also shall be most solemn and holy unto you; no servile work shall you do therein.

text.NRSV: On the day of the first fruits, when you offer a grain offering of new grain to the Lord at your festival of weeks, you shall have a holy convocation; you shall not work at your occupations.
text.Rotherham: And on the day of firstfruits when ye bring near a new meal-offering unto Yahweh, in your weeks, a holy convocation shall there be unto you, no laborious work, shall ye do;
text.KJV: Also in the day of the firstfruits, when ye bring a new meat offering unto the Lord, after your weeks be out, ye shall have an holy convocation; ye shall do no servile work:
text.NABRE: At Pentecost. On the day of first fruits, on your feast of Weeks, when you present to the Lord an offering of new grain, you will declare a holy day: you shall do no heavy work.
text.DRB: The day also of first fruits when you shall offer new fruits to the Lord, after that the weeks be accomplished, shall be venerable and holy: no servile work shall you do therein.

text.NRSV: You shall offer a burnt offering, a pleasing odor to the Lord: two young bulls, one ram, seven male lambs a year old.
text.Rotherham: but ye shall bring near as an ascending-sacrifice, for a satisfying odour unto Yahweh, two choice bullocks one ram,—seven he-lambs a year old;
text.KJV: But ye shall offer the burnt offering for a sweet savour unto the Lord; two young bullocks, one ram, seven lambs of the first year;
text.NABRE: You will offer burnt offering for a pleasing aroma to the Lord: two bulls of the herd, one ram, and seven yearling lambs that you are sure are unblemished.
text.DRB: And you shall offer an holocaust for a most sweet odour to the Lord, two calves of the herd, one ram, and seven Lambs of a year old without blemish:

text.NRSV: Their grain offering shall be of choice flour mixed with oil, three-tenths of an ephah for each bull, two-tenths for one ram,
text.Rotherham: also their meal-offering, fine meal, overflowed with oil,—three-tenths for each bullock, two-tenths for the one ram;
text.KJV: And their meat offering of flour mingled with oil, three tenth deals unto one bullock, two tenth deals unto one ram,
text.NABRE: Their grain offerings will be of bran flour mixed with oil: three tenths of an ephah for each bull, two tenths for the ram,
text.DRB: and in the sacrifices of them three tenths of flour tempered with oil to every calf, to every ram two,

text.NRSV: one-tenth for each of the seven lambs;
text.Rotherham: a tenth severally, for each lamb,—of the seven lambs;
text.KJV: A several tenth deal unto one lamb, throughout the seven lambs;
text.NABRE: and one tenth for each of the seven lambs.
text.DRB: every lamb the tenth part of a tenth, which together are seven lambs. A goat also

text.NRSV: with one male goat, to make atonement for you.
text.Rotherham: one young he-goat,—for putting a propitiatory-covering over you:
text.KJV: And one kid of the goats, to make an atonement for you.
text.NABRE: One goat will be for a purification offering to make atonement for yourselves.
text.DRB: which is slain for expiation: beside the everlasting holocaust & the libaments thereof.

text.NRSV: In addition to the regular burnt offering and its grain offering, you shall offer them and their drink offering. They shall be without blemish.
text.Rotherham: in addition to, the continual ascending-sacrifice, with the meal-offering thereof, shall ye offer them,—without defect, shall they be for you, with their drink-offerings.
text.KJV: Ye shall offer them beside the continual burnt offering, and his meat offering, (they shall be unto you without blemish) and their drink offerings.
text.NABRE: You will make these offerings, together with their libations, in addition to the regular burnt offering with its grain offering.
text.DRB: All shall you offer without blemish with their libaments.

text.NRSV: On the first day of the seventh month you shall have a holy convocation; you shall not work at your occupations. It is a day for you to blow the trumpets.
text.Rotherham: And in the seventh month, on the first of the month, a holy convocation, shall there be unto you, no laborious work, shall ye do,—a day of loud acclamation, shall it be unto you.
text.KJV: And in the seventh month, on the first day of the month, ye shall have an holy convocation; ye shall do no servile work: it is a day of blowing the trumpets unto you.
text.NABRE: In the seventh month on the first day you will declare a holy day, and do no heavy work; it shall be a day on which you sound the trumpet.
text.DRB: The first day also of the seventh month shall be venerable and holy unto you, no servile work shall you do therein, because it is the day of sounding and of trumpets.

text.NRSV: You shall offer a burnt offering as a pleasing odor to the Lord: one young bull, one ram, seven male lambs a year old without blemish;
text.Rotherham: Therefore shall ye offer, as an ascending-sacrifice for a satisfying odour unto Yahweh, one choice bullock one ram,—seven he-lambs a year old without defect;
text.KJV: And ye shall offer a burnt offering for a sweet savour unto the Lord; one young bullock, one ram, and seven lambs of the first year without blemish:
text.NABRE: You will offer a burnt offering for a pleasing aroma to the Lord: one bull of the herd, one ram, and seven unblemished yearling lambs.
text.DRB: And you shall offer an holocaust for a most sweet odour to the Lord, one calf of the herd, one ram, and seven lambs of a year old without blemish:

text.NRSV: their grain offering of choice flour mixed with oil: three-tenths of one ephah for the bull, two-tenths for the ram,
text.Rotherham: and as their meal-offering, fine meal, overflowed with oil,—three-tenths to a bullock two-tenths to a ram;
text.KJV: And their meat offering shall be of flour mingled with oil, three tenth deals for a bullock, and two tenth deals for a ram,
text.NABRE: Their grain offerings will be of bran flour mixed with oil: three tenths of an ephah for the bull, two tenths for the ram,
text.DRB: and in their sacrifices of flour tempered with oil three tenths to every calf, two tenths to a ram,

text.NRSV: and one-tenth for each of the seven lambs;
text.Rotherham: and one-tenth to each lamb,—of the seven lambs;
text.KJV: And one tenth deal for one lamb, throughout the seven lambs:
text.NABRE: and one tenth for each of the seven lambs.
text.DRB: one tenth to a lamb, which together are seven lambs:

text.NRSV: with one male goat for a sin offering, to make atonement for you.
text.Rotherham: and one young he-goat as a sin-bearer,—for putting a propitiatory-covering over you:
text.KJV: And one kid of the goats for a sin offering, to make an atonement for you:
text.NABRE: One goat will be a purification offering to make atonement for yourselves.
text.DRB: and a buck goat for sin, which is offered for the expiation of the people,

text.NRSV: These are in addition to the burnt offering of the new moon and its grain offering, and the regular burnt offering and its grain offering, and their drink offerings, according to the ordinance for them, a pleasing odor, an offering by fire to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: in addition to the monthly ascending-sacrifice with the meal-offering thereof and the continual ascending sacrifice, with the meal-offering thereof and the drink-offering thereof according to their regulation,—for a satisfying odour, an altar-flame, unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: Beside the burnt offering of the month, and his meat offering, and the daily burnt offering, and his meat offering, and their drink offerings, according unto their manner, for a sweet savour, a sacrifice made by fire unto the Lord.
text.NABRE: These are in addition to the burnt offering for the new moon with its grain offering, and in addition to the regular burnt offering with its grain offering, together with the libations prescribed for them, for a pleasing aroma, an oblation to the Lord.
text.DRB: besides the holocaust of the Calends with the sacrifices thereof, and the everlasting holocaust with the accustomed libaments. With the same ceremonies shall you offer burnt sacrifice for a most sweet odour to the Lord.

text.NRSV: On the tenth day of this seventh month you shall have a holy convocation, and deny yourselves; you shall do no work.
text.Rotherham: And on the tenth of this seventh month, a holy convocation, shall there be unto you,—when ye shall humble your souls,—no work, shall ye do;
text.KJV: And ye shall have on the tenth day of this seventh month an holy convocation; and ye shall afflict your souls: ye shall not do any work therein:
text.NABRE: On the Day of Atonement. On the tenth day of this seventh month you will declare a holy day, humble yourselves, and do no sort of work.
text.DRB: The tenth day also of this seventh month shall be holy and venerable unto you, & you shall afflict your souls: no servile work shall you do therein.

text.NRSV: You shall offer a burnt offering to the Lord, a pleasing odor: one young bull, one ram, seven male lambs a year old. They shall be without blemish.
text.Rotherham: but ye shall bring near, as an ascending-sacrifice unto Yahweh a satisfying odour, one choice bullock one ram,—seven he-lambs a year old, without defect, shall they be for you;
text.KJV: But ye shall offer a burnt offering unto the Lord for a sweet savour; one young bullock, one ram, and seven lambs of the first year; they shall be unto you without blemish:
text.NABRE: You will offer a burnt offering to the Lord, a pleasing aroma: one bull of the herd, one ram, and seven yearling lambs that you are sure are unblemished.
text.DRB: And you shall offer an holocaust to the Lord for a most sweet odour, one calf of the herd, one ram, seven Lambs of a year old without blemish:

text.NRSV: Their grain offering shall be of choice flour mixed with oil, three-tenths of an ephah for the bull, two-tenths for the one ram,
text.Rotherham: and, as their meal-offering, fine meal overflowed with oil,—three-tenths to a bullock, two-tenths to the one ram;
text.KJV: And their meat offering shall be of flour mingled with oil, three tenth deals to a bullock, and two tenth deals to one ram,
text.NABRE: Their grain offerings of bran flour mixed with oil: three tenths of an ephah for the bull, two tenths for the one ram,
text.DRB: and in their sacrifices of flour tempered with oil three tenths to every calf, two tenths to the ram,

text.NRSV: one-tenth for each of the seven lambs;
text.Rotherham: a tenth severally to each lamb,—of the seven lambs;
text.KJV: A several tenth deal for one lamb, throughout the seven lambs:
text.NABRE: and one tenth for each of the seven lambs.
text.DRB: the tenth part of a tenth to every lamb, which are in all seven lambs:

text.NRSV: with one male goat for a sin offering—in addition to the sin offering of atonement, and the regular burnt offering and its grain offering, and their drink offerings.
text.Rotherham: one young he-goat as a sin-bearer,—in addition to, the propitiatory sin-bearer, and the continual ascending-sacrifice, with its meal-offering and their drink-offerings.
text.KJV: One kid of the goats for a sin offering; beside the sin offering of atonement, and the continual burnt offering, and the meat offering of it, and their drink offerings.
text.NABRE: One goat will be a purification offering. These are in addition to the purification offering for purging, the regular burnt offering with its grain offering, and their libations.
text.DRB: and a buck goat for sin, besides these things that are wont to be offered for offence unto expiation, and for the everlasting holocaust with their sacrifice and libaments.

text.NRSV: On the fifteenth day of the seventh month you shall have a holy convocation; you shall not work at your occupations. You shall celebrate a festival to the Lord seven days.
text.Rotherham: And on the fifteenth day of the seventh month, a holy convocation shall there be unto you, no laborious work, shall ye do, but ye shall celebrate a festival unto Yahweh, seven days.
text.KJV: And on the fifteenth day of the seventh month ye shall have an holy convocation; ye shall do no servile work, and ye shall keep a feast unto the Lord seven days:
text.NABRE: On the Feast of Booths. On the fifteenth day of the seventh month you will declare a holy day: you shall do no heavy work. For the following seven days you will celebrate a pilgrimage feast to the Lord.
text.DRB: But the fifteenth day of the seventh month, which shall be unto you holy and venerable, no servile work shall you do therein, but you shall celebrate the solemnity to the Lord seven days.

text.NRSV: You shall offer a burnt offering, an offering by fire, a pleasing odor to the Lord: thirteen young bulls, two rams, fourteen male lambs a year old. They shall be without blemish.
text.Rotherham: Then shall ye bring near as an ascending-sacrifice—an altar-flame of a satisfying odour unto Yahweh,—thirteen choice bullocks two rams,—fourteen he-lambs a year old without defect, shall they be;
text.KJV: And ye shall offer a burnt offering, a sacrifice made by fire, of a sweet savour unto the Lord; thirteen young bullocks, two rams, and fourteen lambs of the first year; they shall be without blemish:
text.NABRE: You will offer a burnt offering, an oblation of pleasing aroma to the Lord: thirteen bulls of the herd, two rams, and fourteen yearling lambs that are unblemished.
text.DRB: and you shall offer an holocaust for a most sweet odour to the Lord, calves of the herd thirteen, rams two, lambs of a year old without blemish fourteen:

text.NRSV: Their grain offering shall be of choice flour mixed with oil, three-tenths of an ephah for each of the thirteen bulls, two-tenths for each of the two rams,
text.Rotherham: and, as their meal-offering, fine-meal overflowed with oil, three-tenths to each bullock of the thirteen bullocks, two-tenths to each ram, of the two rams;
text.KJV: And their meat offering shall be of flour mingled with oil, three tenth deals unto every bullock of the thirteen bullocks, two tenth deals to each ram of the two rams,
text.NABRE: Their grain offerings will be of bran flour mixed with oil: three tenths of an ephah for each of the thirteen bulls, two tenths for each of the two rams,
text.DRB: & in their libaments of flour tempered with oil three tenths to every calf, which are together thirteen calves: & two tenths to every ram, that is, together to two rams,

text.NRSV: and one-tenth for each of the fourteen lambs;
text.Rotherham: and a tenth severally, to each lamb,—of the fourteen lambs;
text.KJV: And a several tenth deal to each lamb of the fourteen lambs:
text.NABRE: and one tenth for each of the fourteen lambs.
text.DRB: And the tenth part of a tenth to every lamb, which are together fourteen lambs:

text.NRSV: also one male goat for a sin offering, in addition to the regular burnt offering, its grain offering and its drink offering.
text.Rotherham: also one young he-goat, as a sin-bearer,—in addition to the continual ascending-sacrifice, the meal-offering thereof and the drink-offering thereof.
text.KJV: And one kid of the goats for a sin offering; beside the continual burnt offering, his meat offering, and his drink offering.
text.NABRE: One goat will be a purification offering. These are in addition to the regular burnt offering with its grain offering and libation.
text.DRB: and a buck goat for sin, beside the everlasting holocaust, & the sacrifice & the libament thereof.

text.NRSV: On the second day: twelve young bulls, two rams, fourteen male lambs a year old without blemish,
text.Rotherham: And on the second day, twelve choice bullocks two rams,—fourteen he-lambs a year old without defect;
text.KJV: And on the second day ye shall offer twelve young bullocks, two rams, fourteen lambs of the first year without spot:
text.NABRE: On the second day: twelve bulls of the herd, two rams, and fourteen unblemished yearling lambs,
text.DRB: The next day you shall offer calves of the herd twelve, two rams, lambs a year old without blemish fourteen:

text.NRSV: with the grain offering and the drink offerings for the bulls, for the rams, and for the lambs, as prescribed in proportion to their number;
text.Rotherham: with their meal-offerings and their drink-offerings—to the bullocks, to the rams, and to the lambs, by their number according to the regulation;
text.KJV: And their meat offering and their drink offerings for the bullocks, for the rams, and for the lambs, shall be according to their number, after the manner:
text.NABRE: with the grain offerings and libations for the bulls, rams and lambs in their prescribed number,
text.DRB: and the sacrifices and libaments of every one to the calves and the rams and the lambs you shall ritely celebrate:

text.NRSV: also one male goat for a sin offering, in addition to the regular burnt offering and its grain offering, and their drink offerings.
text.Rotherham: also one young he-goat as a sin-bearer,—in addition to the continual ascending-sacrifice, with its meal-offering, and their drink-offerings.
text.KJV: And one kid of the goats for a sin offering; beside the continual burnt offering, and the meat offering thereof, and their drink offerings.
text.NABRE: as well as one goat as a purification offering, besides the regular burnt offering with its grain offering and libation.
text.DRB: and a buck goat for sin, beside the everlasting holocaust, and the sacrifice and libament thereof.

text.NRSV: On the third day: eleven bulls, two rams, fourteen male lambs a year old without blemish,
text.Rotherham: And on the third day, eleven bullocks two rams,—and fourteen he-lambs a year old without defect;
text.KJV: And on the third day eleven bullocks, two rams, fourteen lambs of the first year without blemish;
text.NABRE: On the third day: eleven bulls, two rams, and fourteen unblemished yearling lambs,
text.DRB: The third day you shall offer eleven calves, two rams, lambs of a year old without blemish fourteen:

text.NRSV: with the grain offering and the drink offerings for the bulls, for the rams, and for the lambs, as prescribed in proportion to their number;
text.Rotherham: with their meal-offering and their drink-offerings, to the bullocks, to the rams, and to the lambs, by their number, according to the regulation;
text.KJV: And their meat offering and their drink offerings for the bullocks, for the rams, and for the lambs, shall be according to their number, after the manner:
text.NABRE: with the grain offerings and libations for the bulls, rams and lambs in their prescribed number,
text.DRB: and the sacrifices and the libaments of every one to the calves & the rams and the lambs you shall ritely celebrate:

text.NRSV: also one male goat for a sin offering, in addition to the regular burnt offering and its grain offering and its drink offering.
text.Rotherham: also one young he-goat as a sin-bearer, in addition to the continual ascending-sacrifice, with the meal-offering thereof and the drink-offering thereof.
text.KJV: And one goat for a sin offering; beside the continual burnt offering, and his meat offering, and his drink offering.
text.NABRE: as well as one goat for a purification offering, besides the regular burnt offering with its grain offering and libation.
text.DRB: and a buck-goat for sin, beside the everlasting holocaust, and the sacrifice, and libament thereof.

text.NRSV: On the fourth day: ten bulls, two rams, fourteen male lambs a year old without blemish,
text.Rotherham: And on the fourth day, ten bullocks, two rams,—fourteen he-lambs a year old without defect;
text.KJV: And on the fourth day ten bullocks, two rams, and fourteen lambs of the first year without blemish:
text.NABRE: On the fourth day: ten bulls, two rams, and fourteen unblemished yearling lambs,
text.DRB: The fourth day you shall offer ten calves, two rams, lambs a year old without blemish fourteen:

text.NRSV: with the grain offering and the drink offerings for the bulls, for the rams, and for the lambs, as prescribed in proportion to their number;
text.Rotherham: their meal-offering, and their drink-offerings, to the bullocks, to the rams, and to the lambs, by their number, according to the regulation;
text.KJV: Their meat offering and their drink offerings for the bullocks, for the rams, and for the lambs, shall be according to their number, after the manner:
text.NABRE: the grain offerings and libations for the bulls, rams and lambs in their prescribed number,
text.DRB: and the sacrifices and the libaments of every one to the calves and the rams and the lambs you shall ritely celebrate:

text.NRSV: also one male goat for a sin offering, in addition to the regular burnt offering, its grain offering and its drink offering.
text.Rotherham: also one he-goat as a sin-bearer,—in addition to the continual ascending-sacrifice, the meal-offering thereof and the drink-offering thereof.
text.KJV: And one kid of the goats for a sin offering; beside the continual burnt offering, his meat offering, and his drink offering.
text.NABRE: as well as one goat as a purification offering, besides the regular burnt offering, its grain offering and libation.
text.DRB: and a buck-goat for sin, beside the everlasting holocaust, and the sacrifice thereof and libament.

text.NRSV: On the fifth day: nine bulls, two rams, fourteen male lambs a year old without blemish,
text.Rotherham: And on the fifth day, nine bullocks two rams,—fourteen he-lambs, a year old without defect;
text.KJV: And on the fifth day nine bullocks, two rams, and fourteen lambs of the first year without spot:
text.NABRE: On the fifth day: nine bulls, two rams, and fourteen unblemished yearling lambs,
text.DRB: The fifth day you shall offer nine calves, two rams, lambs of a year old without blemish fourteen:

text.NRSV: with the grain offering and the drink offerings for the bulls, for the rams, and for the lambs, as prescribed in proportion to their number;
text.Rotherham: with their meal-offering and their drink-offerings,—to the bullocks to the rams, and to the lambs, by their number, according to the regulation:
text.KJV: And their meat offering and their drink offerings for the bullocks, for the rams, and for the lambs, shall be according to their number, after the manner:
text.NABRE: with the grain offerings and libations for the bulls, rams and lambs in their prescribed number,
text.DRB: and the sacrifices and the libaments of every one to the calves and the rams and the lambs you shall ritely celebrate:

text.NRSV: also one male goat for a sin offering, in addition to the regular burnt offering and its grain offering and its drink offering.
text.Rotherham: also one young he-goat as a sin-bearer,—in addition to the continual ascending-sacrifice, with the meal-offering thereof and the drink-offering thereof.
text.KJV: And one goat for a sin offering; beside the continual burnt offering, and his meat offering, and his drink offering.
text.NABRE: as well as one goat as a purification offering, besides the regular burnt offering with its grain offering and libation.
text.DRB: and a buck-goat for sin, beside the everlasting holocaust, and the sacrifices thereof and libament.

text.NRSV: On the sixth day: eight bulls, two rams, fourteen male lambs a year old without blemish,
text.Rotherham: And on the sixth day, eight bullocks, two rams, fourteen he-lambs a year old without defect;
text.KJV: And on the sixth day eight bullocks, two rams, and fourteen lambs of the first year without blemish:
text.NABRE: On the sixth day: eight bulls, two rams, and fourteen unblemished yearling lambs,
text.DRB: The sixth day you shall offer eight calves, two rams, lambs of a year old without blemish fourteen:

text.NRSV: with the grain offering and the drink offerings for the bulls, for the rams, and for the lambs, as prescribed in proportion to their number;
text.Rotherham: with their meal-offering and their drink-offerings to the bullocks, to the rams, and to the lambs, by their number, according to the regulation;
text.KJV: And their meat offering and their drink offerings for the bullocks, for the rams, and for the lambs, shall be according to their number, after the manner:
text.NABRE: with the grain offerings and libations for the bulls, rams and lambs in their prescribed number,
text.DRB: & the sacrifices and the libaments of every one to the calves and the rams & the lambs you shall ritely celebrate:

text.NRSV: also one male goat for a sin offering, in addition to the regular burnt offering, its grain offering, and its drink offerings.
text.Rotherham: also one young he-goat as a sin-bearer,—in addition to the continual ascending-sacrifice, the meal-offering thereof and the drink-offerings thereof,
text.KJV: And one goat for a sin offering; beside the continual burnt offering, his meat offering, and his drink offering.
text.NABRE: as well as one goat as a purification offering, besides the regular burnt offering, its grain offering and libation.
text.DRB: and a buck-goat for sin, beside the everlasting holocaust, and the sacrifice thereof and libament.

text.NRSV: On the seventh day: seven bulls, two rams, fourteen male lambs a year old without blemish,
text.Rotherham: And on the seventh day, seven bullocks, two rams, fourteen he-lambs a year old, with-out defect:
text.KJV: And on the seventh day seven bullocks, two rams, and fourteen lambs of the first year without blemish:
text.NABRE: On the seventh day: seven bulls, two rams, and fourteen unblemished yearling lambs,
text.DRB: The seventh day you shall offer seven calves, and two rams, lambs of a year old without blemish fourteen:

text.NRSV: with the grain offering and the drink offerings for the bulls, for the rams, and for the lambs, as prescribed in proportion to their number;
text.Rotherham: with their meal-offering and their drink-offering, to the bullock, to the rams, and to the lambs by their number, according to their regulation;
text.KJV: And their meat offering and their drink offerings for the bullocks, for the rams, and for the lambs, shall be according to their number, after the manner:
text.NABRE: with the grain offerings and libations for the bulls, rams and lambs in their prescribed number,
text.DRB: and the sacrifices and the libaments of every one to the calves and the rams and the lambs you shall ritely celebrate:

text.NRSV: also one male goat for a sin offering, in addition to the regular burnt offering, its grain offering, and its drink offering.
text.Rotherham: also one young he-goat as a sin-bearer, in addition to the continual ascending-sacrifice, the meal-offering thereof and the drink-offering thereof
text.KJV: And one goat for a sin offering; beside the continual burnt offering, his meat offering, and his drink offering.
text.NABRE: as well as one goat as a purification offering, besides the regular burnt offering, its grain offering and libation.
text.DRB: and a buck-goat for sin, beside the everlasting holocaust and the sacrifice thereof and libament.

text.NRSV: On the eighth day you shall have a solemn assembly; you shall not work at your occupations.
text.Rotherham: [And] on the eighth day, a closing feast shall there be unto you, no laborious work, shall ye do;
text.KJV: On the eighth day ye shall have a solemn assembly: ye shall do no servile work therein:
text.NABRE: On the eighth day you will hold a public assembly: you shall do no heavy work.
text.DRB: The eighth day, which is most solemn, no servile work you do therein,

text.NRSV: You shall offer a burnt offering, an offering by fire, a pleasing odor to the Lord: one bull, one ram, seven male lambs a year old without blemish,
text.Rotherham: but ye shall bring near—as an ascending-sacrifice, an altar-flame of a satisfying odour, unto Yahweh—one bullock, one ram,—seven he-lambs a year old, without defect:
text.KJV: But ye shall offer a burnt offering, a sacrifice made by fire, of a sweet savour unto the Lord: one bullock, one ram, seven lambs of the first year without blemish:
text.NABRE: You will offer a burnt offering, an oblation of pleasing aroma to the Lord: one bull, one ram, and seven unblemished yearling lambs,
text.DRB: offering an holocaust for a most sweet odour to the Lord: one calf, one ram, lambs of a year old with out blemish seven.

text.NRSV: and the grain offering and the drink offerings for the bull, for the ram, and for the lambs, as prescribed in proportion to their number;
text.Rotherham: [with] their meal-offering and their drink-offerings—to the bullock to the ram, and to the lambs—by their number, according to the regulation;
text.KJV: Their meat offering and their drink offerings for the bullock, for the ram, and for the lambs, shall be according to their number, after the manner:
text.NABRE: with the grain offerings and libations for the bulls, rams and lambs in their prescribed number,
text.DRB: And the sacrifices and the libaments of every one to the calves and the rams and the lambs you shall ritely celebrate:

text.NRSV: also one male goat for a sin offering, in addition to the regular burnt offering and its grain offering and its drink offering.
text.Rotherham: also one young he-goat as a sin-bearer,—in addition to the continual ascending-sacrifice, with the meal-offering thereof, and the drink-offering thereof.
text.KJV: And one goat for a sin offering; beside the continual burnt offering, and his meat offering, and his drink offering.
text.NABRE: as well as one goat as a purification offering, besides the regular burnt offering with its grain offering and libation.
text.DRB: and a buck-goat for sin, besides the everlasting holocaust, & the sacrifice thereof and libament.

text.NRSV: These you shall offer to the Lord at your appointed festivals, in addition to your votive offerings and your freewill offerings, as your burnt offerings, your grain offerings, your drink offerings, and your offerings of well-being.
text.Rotherham: These, shall ye offer unto Yahweh in your appointed seasons,—besides your vow-offerings and your freewill offerings—as your ascending-sacrifices, and as your meal-offerings, and as your drink-offerings and as your peace-offerings.
text.KJV: These things ye shall do unto the Lord in your set feasts, beside your vows, and your freewill offerings, for your burnt offerings, and for your meat offerings, and for your drink offerings, and for your peace offerings.
text.NABRE: These are the offerings you will make to the Lord on your festivals, besides your votive or voluntary offerings of burnt offerings, grain offerings, libations, and communion offerings.
text.DRB: These things shall you offer to the Lord in your solemnities: besides your vows and voluntary oblations in holocaust, in sacrifice, in libament, and in pacific hosts.

text.NRSV: So Moses told the Israelites everything just as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: So Moses told the sons of Israel,—According to all that Yahweh commanded Moses.
text.KJV: And Moses told the children of Israel according to all that the Lord commanded Moses.

text.NRSV: Then Moses said to the heads of the tribes of the Israelites: This is what the Lord has commanded.
text.Rotherham: And Moses spake unto the heads of the tribes of the sons of Israel saying: This, is the thing which Yahweh hath commanded-
text.KJV: And Moses spake unto the heads of the tribes concerning the children of Israel, saying, This is the thing which the Lord hath commanded.
text.NABRE: So Moses instructed the Israelites exactly as the Lord had commanded him.
text.DRB: And Moyses told the children of Israel all things that our Lord had commanded him:

text.NRSV: When a man makes a vow to the Lord, or swears an oath to bind himself by a pledge, he shall not break his word; he shall do according to all that proceeds out of his mouth.
text.Rotherham: When, a man, shall vow a vow unto Yahweh, or swear an oath to bind a bond upon his soul, he shall not break his word,—according to all that proceedeth out of his mouth, shall he do.
text.KJV: If a man vow a vow unto the Lord, or swear an oath to bind his soul with a bond; he shall not break his word, he shall do according to all that proceedeth out of his mouth.
text.NABRE: Validity and Annulment of Vows. Moses said to the heads of the Israelite tribes, “This is what the Lord has commanded:
text.DRB: and he spake to the Princes of the tribes of the children of Israel: This is the word that our Lord hath commanded:

text.NRSV: When a woman makes a vow to the Lord, or binds herself by a pledge, while within her father's house, in her youth,"
text.Rotherham: And, when, a woman, shall vow a vow unto Yahweh, and bind a bond, in the house of her father, in her youth;
text.KJV: If a woman also vow a vow unto the Lord, and bind herself by a bond, being in her father’s house in her youth;
text.NABRE: When a man makes a vow to the Lord or binds himself under oath to a pledge, he shall not violate his word, but must fulfill exactly the promise he has uttered.
text.DRB: If any man make a vow to our Lord, or bind himself by an oath: he shall not make his word frustrate, but all that he promised he shall fulfill.

text.NRSV: and her father hears of her vow or her pledge by which she has bound herself, and says nothing to her; then all her vows shall stand, and any pledge by which she has bound herself shall stand."
text.Rotherham: and her father shall hear her vow or her bond which she bindeth upon her soul, and her father shall hold his peace at her, then shall all her vows stand, and every bond which she hath bound upon her soul shall stand.
text.KJV: And her father hear her vow, and her bond wherewith she hath bound her soul, and her father shall hold his peace at her: then all her vows shall stand, and every bond wherewith she hath bound her soul shall stand.
text.NABRE: “When a woman makes a vow to the Lord, or binds herself to a pledge, while still in her father’s house in her youth,
text.DRB: If a woman vow any thing, and bind herself with an oath, she that is in her father's house, & as yet in maiden's age: if her father know the vow that she promised, and the oath wherewith she bound her soul, and hold his peace, she shall be bound to the vow:

text.NRSV: But if her father expresses disapproval to her at the time that he hears of it, no vow of hers, and no pledge by which she has bound herself, shall stand; and the Lord will forgive her, because her father had expressed to her his disapproval."
text.Rotherham: But, if her father forbade her, in the day when he heard, none of her vows or her bonds which she hath bound upon her soul, shall stand,—and, Yahweh, will pardon her, because her father forbade her.
text.KJV: But if her father disallow her in the day that he heareth; not any of her vows, or of her bonds wherewith she hath bound her soul, shall stand: and the Lord shall forgive her, because her father disallowed her.
text.NABRE: and her father learns of her vow or the pledge to which she bound herself and says nothing to her about it, then any vow or any pledge to which she bound herself remains valid.
text.DRB: Whatsoever she promised and sware, she shall fulfill in deed.

text.NRSV: If she marries, while obligated by her vows or any thoughtless utterance of her lips by which she has bound herself,"
text.Rotherham: But, if she, belonged to a husband, when her vows were taken upon her,—or a rash utterance fell from her lips, wherewith she put a bond upon her soul;
text.KJV: And if she had at all an husband, when she vowed, or uttered ought out of her lips, wherewith she bound her soul;
text.NABRE: But if on the day he learns of it her father opposes her, then any vow or any pledge to which she bound herself becomes invalid; and the Lord will release her from it, since her father opposed her.
text.DRB: But if immediately as he heareth it, her father do gainsay it, both her vows & her oaths shall be frustrate, neither shall she be bound to the promise, for that her father hath gainsaid it.

text.NRSV: and her husband hears of it and says nothing to her at the time that he hears, then her vows shall stand, and her pledges by which she has bound herself shall stand."
text.Rotherham: and her husband heard it, and on the day that he heard, he held his peace at her, then shall her vows stand and her bonds which she hath bound upon her soul, shall stand,
text.KJV: And her husband heard it, and held his peace at her in the day that he heard it: then her vows shall stand, and her bonds wherewith she bound her soul shall stand.
text.NABRE: “If she marries while under a vow or under a rash pledge to which she bound herself,
text.DRB: If she have a husband, and vow any thing, and the word once going out of her mouth bind her soul by an oath,

text.NRSV: But if, at the time that her husband hears of it, he expresses disapproval to her, then he shall nullify the vow by which she was obligated, or the thoughtless utterance of her lips, by which she bound herself; and the Lord will forgive her."
text.Rotherham: But if, on the day her husband heard, he forbade her, then shall he have made of none effect her vow that is upon her, or the rash utterance of her lips, wherewith she put a bond upon her soul,—and, Yahweh, will pardon her.
text.KJV: But if her husband disallowed her on the day that he heard it; then he shall make her vow which she vowed, and that which she uttered with her lips, wherewith she bound her soul, of none effect: and the Lord shall forgive her.
text.NABRE: and her husband learns of it, yet says nothing to her on the day he learns it, then the vows or the pledges to which she bound herself remain valid.
text.DRB: the day that her husband heareth it, and doth not gainsay it, she shall be bound to the vow, and shall render whatsoever she promised.

text.NRSV: (But every vow of a widow or of a divorced woman, by which she has bound herself, shall be binding upon her.)"
text.Rotherham: But as for the vow of a widow, or of a woman divorced, whatsoever she hath bound on her soul, shall stand against her.
text.KJV: But every vow of a widow, and of her that is divorced, wherewith they have bound their souls, shall stand against her.
text.NABRE: But if on the day her husband learns of it he opposes her, he thereby annuls the vow she had made or the rash pledge to which she had bound herself, and the Lord will release her from it.
text.DRB: But if as soon as he heareth he gainsay it, and make her promises frustrate, and the words wherewith she had bound her soul, our Lord will be propitious to her.

text.NRSV: If she made a vow in her husband's house, or bound herself by a pledge with an oath,"
text.Rotherham: But, if in the house of her husband, she vowed, or bound a bond upon her soul. with an oath;
text.KJV: And if she vowed in her husband’s house, or bound her soul by a bond with an oath;
text.NABRE: (The vow of a widow or of a divorced woman, however, any pledge to which such a woman binds herself, is valid.)
text.DRB: The widow, and she that is divorced, whatsoever they vow they shall render.

text.NRSV: and her husband heard it and said nothing to her, and did not express disapproval to her, then all her vows shall stand, and any pledge by which she bound herself shall stand."
text.Rotherham: and her husband heard, and held his peace at her, did not forbid her, then shall all her vows stand, and every bond which she hath bound upon her soul, shall stand.
text.KJV: And her husband heard it, and held his peace at her, and disallowed her not: then all her vows shall stand, and every bond wherewith she bound her soul shall stand.
text.NABRE: “If it is in her husband’s house that she makes a vow or binds herself under oath to a pledge,
text.DRB: The wife in the house of her husband, when she hath bound herself by vow and by oath,

text.NRSV: But if her husband nullifies them at the time that he hears them, then whatever proceeds out of her lips concerning her vows, or concerning her pledge of herself, shall not stand. Her husband has nullified them, and the Lord will forgive her."
text.Rotherham: But if her husband, did make, them, of none effect, on the day he heard, nothing which came forth out of her lips—of her vows, or of the bond of her soul, shall stand,—her husband, made it of none effect, and Yahweh, will pardon her:
text.KJV: But if her husband hath utterly made them void on the day he heard them; then whatsoever proceeded out of her lips concerning her vows, or concerning the bond of her soul, shall not stand: her husband hath made them void; and the Lord shall forgive her.
text.NABRE: and her husband learns of it yet says nothing to her to oppose her, then all her vows remain valid or any pledge to which she has bound herself.
text.DRB: if her husband hear, and hold his peace, neither do gainsay the promise, she shall render whatsoever she had promised.

text.NRSV: Any vow or any binding oath to deny herself, her husband may allow to stand, or her husband may nullify."
text.Rotherham: As for any vow, or any oath of binding, to humble one’s soul, her husband, may make it stand, or, her husband, may make it of none effect.
text.KJV: Every vow, and every binding oath to afflict the soul, her husband may establish it, or her husband may make it void.
text.NABRE: But if on the day he learns of them her husband annuls them, then whatever she has expressly promised in her vows or in her pledge becomes invalid; since her husband has annulled them, the Lord will release her from them.
text.DRB: But if forthwith he gainsay it, she shall not be holden bound to the promise: because her husband gainsaid it, and our Lord will be propitious to her.

text.NRSV: But if her husband says nothing to her from day to day, then he validates all her vows, or all her pledges, that are upon her; he has validated them, because he said nothing to her at the time that he heard of them."
text.Rotherham: But if her husband do hold his peace, at her, from day to day, then shall he cause all her vows to stand, or all her bonds which are upon her,—cause them to stand, because he held his peace at her, on the day when he heard.
text.KJV: But if her husband altogether hold his peace at her from day to day; then he establisheth all her vows, or all her bonds, which are upon her: he confirmeth them, because he held his peace at her in the day that he heard them.
text.NABRE: “Any vow or any pledge that she makes under oath to humble herself, her husband may either confirm or annul.
text.DRB: If she vow and bind herself by oath, to afflict her soul by fasting, or abstinence from other things, it shall be in the arbitrement of her husband, whether she shall do it, or not do it.

text.NRSV: But if he nullifies them some time after he has heard of them, then he shall bear her guilt."
text.Rotherham: And, if he, do make them of none effect, after that he hath heard them, then shall he bear her iniquity.
text.KJV: But if he shall any ways make them void after that he hath heard them; then he shall bear her iniquity.
text.NABRE: But if her husband, day after day, says nothing at all to her, he thereby confirms all her vows or all the pledges incumbent upon her; he has confirmed them, because on the day he learned of them he said nothing to her.
text.DRB: And if the husband hearing it hold his peace, and defer sentence till another day: whatsoever she had vowed and promised, she shall render: because immediately as he heard it, he held his peace,

text.NRSV: These are the statutes that the Lord commanded Moses concerning a husband and his wife, and a father and his daughter while she is still young and in her father's house.
text.Rotherham: These are the statutes which Yahweh commanded Moses, between a man and his wife, between a father and his daughter, in her youth in the house of her father.
text.KJV: These are the statutes, which the Lord commanded Moses, between a man and his wife, between the father and his daughter, being yet in her youth in her father’s house.
text.NABRE: If, however, he annuls them some time after he first learned of them, he will be responsible for her guilt.”
text.DRB: but if he gainsay it after that he knew it, himself shall bear her iniquity.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying,
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: The Lord said to Moses:
text.DRB: And our Lord spake to Moyses, saying:

text.NRSV: "Avenge the Israelites on the Midianites; afterward you shall be gathered to your people."
