text.Rotherham: Exact thou the avenging of the sons of Israel, from the Midianites,—and, afterwards, shalt thou be withdrawn unto thy kinsfolk.
text.KJV: Avenge the children of Israel of the Midianites: afterward shalt thou be gathered unto thy people.
text.NABRE: Avenge the Israelites on the Midianites, and then you will be gathered to your people.
text.DRB: Revenge first the children of Israel on the Madianites, and so thou shalt be gathered to thy people.

text.NRSV: So Moses said to the people, "Arm some of your number for the war, so that they may go against Midian, to execute the Lord's vengeance on Midian."
text.Rotherham: So Moses spake unto the people saying, Arm ye from among you men, for the war, and let them go against Midian, to render the avenging of Yahweh upon Midian.
text.KJV: And Moses spake unto the people, saying, Arm some of yourselves unto the war, and let them go against the Midianites, and avenge the Lord of Midian.
text.NABRE: So Moses told the people, “Arm some men among you for the campaign, to attack Midian and to execute the Lord ’s vengeance on Midian.
text.DRB: And Moyses forthwith said: Arm of you men to fight, which may take the revenge of our Lord on the Madianites.

text.NRSV: You shall send a thousand from each of the tribes of Israel to the war."
text.Rotherham: A thousand from each tribe,—of all the tribes of Israel, shall ye send forth unto the war.
text.KJV: Of every tribe a thousand, throughout all the tribes of Israel, shall ye send to the war.
text.NABRE: From each of the tribes of Israel you will send a thousand men to the campaign.”
text.DRB: Let a thousand men of every tribe be chosen of Israel to be sent to the war.

text.NRSV: So out of the thousands of Israel, a thousand from each tribe were conscripted, twelve thousand armed for battle.
text.Rotherham: And there volunteered out of the thousands of Israel, a thousand of each tribe,—twelve thousand, armed for war.
text.KJV: So there were delivered out of the thousands of Israel, a thousand of every tribe, twelve thousand armed for war.
text.NABRE: From the contingents of Israel, therefore, a thousand men of each tribe were levied, so that there were twelve thousand men armed for war.
text.DRB: And they gave a thousand of every tribe, that is to say, twelve thousand well appointed to fight:

text.NRSV: Moses sent them to the war, a thousand from each tribe, along with Phinehas son of Eleazar the priest, with the vessels of the sanctuary and the trumpets for sounding the alarm in his hand.
text.Rotherham: And Moses sent them a thousand of each tribe to the war,—them, and Phinehas son of Eleazar the priest, to the war, with the vessels of the sanctuary, and the alarm trumpets, in his hand.
text.KJV: And Moses sent them to the war, a thousand of every tribe, them and Phinehas the son of Eleazar the priest, to the war, with the holy instruments, and the trumpets to blow in his hand.
text.NABRE: Moses sent them out on the campaign, a thousand from each tribe, with Phinehas, son of Eleazar, the priest for the campaign, who had with him the sacred vessels and the trumpets for sounding the alarm.
text.DRB: whom Moyses sent with Phinees the son of Eleazar the Priest, and the holy vessels, and the trumpets to sound he delivered to him.

text.NRSV: They did battle against Midian, as the Lord had commanded Moses, and killed every male.
text.Rotherham: So they made war upon Midian, as Yahweh had commanded Moses, and slew every male:
text.KJV: And they warred against the Midianites, as the Lord commanded Moses; and they slew all the males.
text.NABRE: They waged war against the Midianites, as the Lord had commanded Moses, and killed every male.
text.DRB: And when they had fought against the Madianites and had overcome, all the men they slew,

text.NRSV: They killed the kings of Midian: Evi, Rekem, Zur, Hur, and Reba, the five kings of Midian, in addition to others who were slain by them; and they also killed Balaam son of Beor with the sword.
text.Rotherham: and the kings of Midian, slew they besides their other slain namely, Evi, and Rekem, and Zur, and Hur, and Reba, five kings of Midian,—Balaam also, son of Beor, slew they with the sword.
text.KJV: And they slew the kings of Midian, beside the rest of them that were slain; namely, Evi, and Rekem, and Zur, and Hur, and Reba, five kings of Midian: Balaam also the son of Beor they slew with the sword.
text.NABRE: Besides those slain in battle, they killed the kings of Midian: Evi, Rekem, Zur, Hur and Reba, the five kings of Midian; and they also killed Balaam, son of Beor, with the sword.
text.DRB: and their kings Evi, and Recem, and Sur, and Hur, and Rebe, five Princes of the Nation: Balaam also the son of Beor they killed with the sword.

text.NRSV: The Israelites took the women of Midian and their little ones captive; and they took all their cattle, their flocks, and all their goods as booty.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Israel took captive the women of Midian and their little ones,—all their cattle also and all their flocks and all their substance, carried they off as a prey;
text.KJV: And the children of Israel took all the women of Midian captives, and their little ones, and took the spoil of all their cattle, and all their flocks, and all their goods.
text.NABRE: But the Israelites took captive the women of the Midianites with their children, and all their herds and flocks and wealth as loot,
text.DRB: And they took their wives, and little ones, and all their goods: whatsoever they had been able to make, they spoiled:

text.NRSV: All their towns where they had settled, and all their encampments, they burned,
text.Rotherham: all their cities also in their sites, and all their encampments, burned they up with fire.
text.KJV: And they burnt all their cities wherein they dwelt, and all their goodly castles, with fire.
text.NABRE: while they set on fire all the towns where they had settled and all their encampments.
text.DRB: as well cities as towns and castles the fire did consume.

text.NRSV: but they took all the spoil and all the booty, both people and animals.
text.Rotherham: Then took they all the spoil, and all the booty,—both of man and beast;
text.KJV: And they took all the spoil, and all the prey, both of men and of beasts.
text.NABRE: Then they took all the plunder, with the people and animals they had captured, and brought the captives, together with the spoils and plunder,
text.DRB: And they took the prey, and all things that they had taken as well of men as of beasts,

text.NRSV: Then they brought the captives and the booty and the spoil to Moses, to Eleazar the priest, and to the congregation of the Israelites, at the camp on the plains of Moab by the Jordan at Jericho.
text.Rotherham: and brought in unto Moses and unto Eleazar the priest and unto the assembly of the sons of Israel—the captives and the booty, and the spoil unto the camp,—unto the waste plains of Moab, which are by Jordan, near Jericho.
text.KJV: And they brought the captives, and the prey, and the spoil, unto Moses, and Eleazar the priest, and unto the congregation of the children of Israel, unto the camp at the plains of Moab, which are by Jordan near Jericho.
text.NABRE: to Moses and Eleazar the priest and to the Israelite community at their camp on the plains of Moab by the Jordan opposite Jericho.
text.DRB: and they brought them to Moyses, and Eleazar the Priest, and to all the multitude of the children of Israel. But the rest of the implements they carried to the camp in the champion countries of Moab, beside Jordan against Jericho.

text.NRSV: Moses, Eleazar the priest, and all the leaders of the congregation went to meet them outside the camp.
text.Rotherham: And Moses, and Eleazar the priest and all the princes of the assembly, went forth to meet them,—unto the outside of the camp.
text.KJV: And Moses, and Eleazar the priest, and all the princes of the congregation, went forth to meet them without the camp.
text.NABRE: Treatment of the Captives. When Moses and Eleazar the priest, with all the leaders of the community, went outside the camp to meet them,
text.DRB: And Moyses and Eleazar the Priest went forth, & all the Princes of the synagogue to meet them without the camp.

text.NRSV: Moses became angry with the officers of the army, the commanders of thousands and the commanders of hundreds, who had come from service in the war.
text.Rotherham: Then was Moses sore displeased with the officers of the force,—the princes of thousands, and the princes of hundreds who were coming in from the warring host.
text.KJV: And Moses was wroth with the officers of the host, with the captains over thousands, and captains over hundreds, which came from the battle.
text.NABRE: Moses became angry with the officers of the army, the commanders of thousands and the commanders of hundreds, who were returning from the military campaign.
text.DRB: And Moyses being angry with the Princes of the host, the tribunes, and the centurions that were come from the battle,

text.NRSV: Moses said to them, "Have you allowed all the women to live?"
text.Rotherham: And Moses said unto them,—Have ye saved alive every female?
text.KJV: And Moses said unto them, Have ye saved all the women alive?
text.NABRE: “So you have spared all the women!” he exclaimed.
text.DRB: said: Why have you reserved the women?

text.NRSV: These women here, on Balaam's advice, made the Israelites act treacherously against the Lord in the affair of Peor, so that the plague came among the congregation of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Lo! they, became unto the sons of Israel, by the advice of Balaam, the cause of daring acts of treachery against Yahweh, over the affair of Peor,—and then came the plague against the assembly of Yahweh!
text.KJV: Behold, these caused the children of Israel, through the counsel of Balaam, to commit trespass against the Lord in the matter of Peor, and there was a plague among the congregation of the Lord.
text.NABRE: “These are the very ones who on Balaam’s advice were behind the Israelites’ unfaithfulness to the Lord in the affair at Peor, so that plague struck the Lord ’s community.
text.DRB: Are not these they, that deceived the children of Israel at the suggestion of Balaam, and made you prevaricate against our Lord upon the sin of Phogor, whereupon also the people was strucken?

text.NRSV: Now therefore, kill every male among the little ones, and kill every woman who has known a man by sleeping with him.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, slay ye every male among the young,—every woman also that hath cohabited with man, slay ye.
text.KJV: Now therefore kill every male among the little ones, and kill every woman that hath known man by lying with him.
text.NABRE: Now kill, therefore, every male among the children and kill every woman who has had sexual relations with a man.
text.DRB: Therefore kill all whatsoever is of the male sex, among the little ones also: & the women, that have known men in carnal copulation, slay ye:

text.NRSV: But all the young girls who have not known a man by sleeping with him, keep alive for yourselves.
text.Rotherham: But all the young of womankind that have, not cohabited with man, preserve alive for yourselves.
text.KJV: But all the women children, that have not known a man by lying with him, keep alive for yourselves.
text.NABRE: But you may spare for yourselves all the girls who have not had sexual relations.
text.DRB: but the girls and all the women that are virgins reserve to you:

text.NRSV: Camp outside the camp seven days; whoever of you has killed any person, or touched a corpse, purify yourselves and your captives on the third and on the seventh day.
text.Rotherham: Ye, then, pitch outside the camp, for seven days,—whosoever hath killed a person and whoever hath touched the slain, cleanse yourselves (from sin) on the third day and on the seventh day ye and your captives,.
text.KJV: And do ye abide without the camp seven days: whosoever hath killed any person, and whosoever hath touched any slain, purify both yourselves and your captives on the third day, and on the seventh day.
text.NABRE: Purification After Combat. “Moreover, remain outside the camp for seven days; every one of you who has killed anyone or touched someone killed will purify yourselves on the third and on the seventh day—both you and your captives.
text.DRB: and tarry without the camp seven days. He that hath killed a man, or touched him that is killed, shall be expiated the third day and the seventh.

text.NRSV: You shall also purify every garment, every article of skin, everything made of goats' hair, and every article of wood.
text.Rotherham: Every garment also, and every article of skin and every thing made of goat’s-hair, and every article of wood, shall ye cleanse (from sin).
text.KJV: And purify all your raiment, and all that is made of skins, and all work of goats’ hair, and all things made of wood.
text.NABRE: You will also purify every garment, every article of leather, everything made of goats’ hair, and every article of wood.”
text.DRB: And of all the prey, whether it be garment, or vessel, and something prepared for implements, of goats' skins, and hair, and wood shall be expiated.

text.NRSV: Eleazar the priest said to the troops who had gone to battle: "This is the statute of the law that the Lord has commanded Moses:"
text.Rotherham: Then said Eleazar the priest unto the men of the host, who had been to the war: This, is the statute of the law, which Yahweh hath commanded Moses:
text.KJV: And Eleazar the priest said unto the men of war which went to the battle, This is the ordinance of the law which the Lord commanded Moses;
text.NABRE: Eleazar the priest told the soldiers who had taken part in the battle: “This is the prescribed ritual which the Lord has commanded Moses:
text.DRB: Eleazar also the Priest spake to the host, that had fought in this manner: This is the precept of the law, which our Lord hath commanded Moyses:

text.NRSV: gold, silver, bronze, iron, tin, and lead—"
text.Rotherham: Surely the gold and the silver,—the bronze the iron, the tin and the lead,
text.KJV: Only the gold, and the silver, the brass, the iron, the tin, and the lead,
text.NABRE: gold, silver, bronze, iron, tin and lead—
text.DRB: Gold, and silver, and brass, and iron, and lead, and tin,

text.NRSV: everything that can withstand fire, you shall pass through fire, and it shall be clean. Nevertheless it shall also be purified with the water for purification; and whatever cannot withstand fire, you shall pass through the water."
text.Rotherham: whatsoever thing can go into fire, ye shall pass through fire and it shall be clean, only with the water of separation, shall ye cleanse it (from sin). But whatsoever cannot go into fire, ye shall pass through water.
text.KJV: Every thing that may abide the fire, ye shall make it go through the fire, and it shall be clean: nevertheless it shall be purified with the water of separation: and all that abideth not the fire ye shall make go through the water.
text.NABRE: whatever can stand fire—you shall put into the fire, that it may become clean; however, it must also be purified with water of purification. But whatever cannot stand fire you must put into the water.
text.DRB: and all that may pass through the fire, shall be purged by fire, but whatsoever cannot abide the fire, shall be sanctified with the water of expiation:

text.NRSV: You must wash your clothes on the seventh day, and you shall be clean; afterward you may come into the camp.
text.Rotherham: And ye shall wash your clothes on the seventh day, and be clean,—and after-wards, shall ye come into the camp.
text.KJV: And ye shall wash your clothes on the seventh day, and ye shall be clean, and afterward ye shall come into the camp.
text.NABRE: On the seventh day you will wash your garments, and then you will again be clean. After that you may enter the camp.”
text.DRB: and you shall wash your garments the seventh day, and being purified, afterward you shall enter into the camp.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying,
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: Division of the Spoils. The Lord said to Moses:
text.DRB: And our Lord said to Moyses:

text.NRSV: "You and Eleazar the priest and the heads of the ancestral houses of the congregation make an inventory of the booty captured, both human and animal."
text.Rotherham: Reckon thou up the sum of the booty that was captured, both of man and of beast,—thou and Eleazar the priest, and the ancestral heads of the assembly;
text.KJV: Take the sum of the prey that was taken, both of man and of beast, thou, and Eleazar the priest, and the chief fathers of the congregation:
text.NABRE: With the help of Eleazar the priest and of the heads of the ancestral houses of the community, inventory all the spoils captured, human being and beast alike;
text.DRB: Take the sum of those things that were taken, from man unto beast, thou and Eleazar the Priest and the Princes of the multitude:

text.NRSV: Divide the booty into two parts, between the warriors who went out to battle and all the congregation.
text.Rotherham: and divide the booty into two parts, between them who took upon them the war, who went out in the host,—and all the [rest of the] assembly.
text.KJV: And divide the prey into two parts; between them that took the war upon them, who went out to battle, and between all the congregation:
text.NABRE: then divide the spoils between the warriors who went on the campaign and the whole community.
text.DRB: and thou shalt divide the prey equally between them, that fought & went forth to the war, and between the rest of the multitude:

text.NRSV: From the share of the warriors who went out to battle, set aside as tribute for the Lord, one item out of every five hundred, whether persons, oxen, donkeys, sheep, or goats.
text.Rotherham: Then shalt thou levy a tribute unto Yahweh—from the men of war, who went forth in the host, one living thing, out of five hundred,—of the human beings, and of the herd, and of the asses and of the flock:
text.KJV: And levy a tribute unto the Lord of the men of war which went out to battle: one soul of five hundred, both of the persons, and of the beeves, and of the asses, and of the sheep:
text.NABRE: You will levy a tax for the Lord on the soldiers who went on the campaign: one out of every five hundred persons, oxen, donkeys, and sheep.
text.DRB: and thou shalt separate a portion to the Lord from them that fought and were in battle, one soul of five hundred as well of mankind as of oxen and asses and sheep,

text.NRSV: Take it from their half and give it to Eleazar the priest as an offering to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: out of their half, shall ye take [them],—and thou shall give [them] unto Eleazar the priest as a heave-offering unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: Take it of their half, and give it unto Eleazar the priest, for an heave offering of the Lord.
text.NABRE: From their half you will take it and give it to Eleazar the priest as a contribution to the Lord.
text.DRB: and thou shalt give it to Eleazar the Priest, because they are the first fruits of the Lord.

text.NRSV: But from the Israelites' half you shall take one out of every fifty, whether persons, oxen, donkeys, sheep, or goats—all the animals—and give them to the Levites who have charge of the tabernacle of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And out of the half allotted to the sons of Israel, shalt thou take one allotted portion out of fifty of the human beings, of the herd, of the asses and of the flock of all the cattle,—and shalt give them unto the Levites, who keep the charge of the habitation of Yahweh.
text.KJV: And of the children of Israel’s half, thou shalt take one portion of fifty, of the persons, of the beeves, of the asses, and of the flocks, of all manner of beasts, and give them unto the Levites, which keep the charge of the tabernacle of the Lord.
text.NABRE: From the Israelites’ half you will take one captive from every fifty human beings, oxen, donkeys, and sheep—all the animals—and give them to the Levites, who perform the duties of the Lord ’s tabernacle.
text.DRB: Out of the half part also of the children of Israel thou shalt take the fiftieth head of mankind, and of oxen, and asses, and sheep, and of all beasts, and thou shalt give them to the Levites, that watch in the custodies of the tabernacle of the Lord.

text.NRSV: Then Moses and Eleazar the priest did as the Lord had commanded Moses:
text.Rotherham: And Moses and Eleazar the priest did,—As Yahweh commanded Moses.
text.KJV: And Moses and Eleazar the priest did as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.NABRE: So Moses and Eleazar the priest did this, as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.DRB: And Moyses and Eleazar did, as our Lord commanded.

text.NRSV: The booty remaining from the spoil that the troops had taken totaled six hundred seventy-five thousand sheep,
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass that the booty, over and above the prey which the people of the host had seized, was,—of the flock, six hundred and seventy-five thousand;
text.KJV: And the booty, being the rest of the prey which the men of war had caught, was six hundred thousand and seventy thousand and five thousand sheep,
text.NABRE: Amount of the Plunder. This plunder, what was left of the loot which the troops had taken, amounted to six hundred and seventy-five thousand sheep,
text.DRB: And the prey which the host had taken, was of sheep six hundred seventy five thousand,

text.NRSV: seventy-two thousand oxen,
text.Rotherham: and, of the herd, seventy-two thousand;
text.KJV: And threescore and twelve thousand beeves,
text.NABRE: seventy-two thousand oxen,
text.DRB: of oxen seventy two thousand,

text.NRSV: sixty-one thousand donkeys,
text.Rotherham: and, of the asses, sixty-one thousand;
text.KJV: And threescore and one thousand asses,
text.NABRE: sixty-one thousand donkeys,
text.DRB: of asses sixty one thousand.

text.NRSV: and thirty-two thousand persons in all, women who had not known a man by sleeping with him.
text.Rotherham: and, of the human persons, even of the woman-kind who had not cohabited with man,—all the persons, thirty-two thousand.
text.KJV: And thirty and two thousand persons in all, of women that had not known man by lying with him.
text.NABRE: and thirty-two thousand women who had not had sexual relations.
text.DRB: The souls of the folks of the female sex, that had not known men, thirty two thousand.

text.NRSV: The half-share, the portion of those who had gone out to war, was in number three hundred thirty-seven thousand five hundred sheep and goats,
text.Rotherham: And the half, the share of them who had gone forth in the host, was,—the number of the flock—three hundred and thirty-seven thousand and five hundred;
text.KJV: And the half, which was the portion of them that went out to war, was in number three hundred thousand and seven and thirty thousand and five hundred sheep:
text.NABRE: The half-share that fell to those who had gone out on the campaign was in number: three hundred and thirty-seven thousand five hundred sheep,
text.DRB: And the half part was given to them, that had been in the battle, of sheep three hundred thirty seven thousand five hundred:

text.NRSV: and the Lord's tribute of sheep and goats was six hundred seventy-five.
text.Rotherham: and so, the tribute unto Yahweh, out of the flock, was—six hundred and seventy-five;
text.KJV: And the Lord’s tribute of the sheep was six hundred and threescore and fifteen.
text.NABRE: of which six hundred and seventy-five fell as tax to the Lord;
text.DRB: of the which for the portion of our Lord where reckoned sheep six hundred seventy five.

text.NRSV: The oxen were thirty-six thousand, of which the Lord's tribute was seventy-two.
text.Rotherham: and the herd, thirty-six thousand,—and the tribute of them unto Yahweh—seventy-two;
text.KJV: And the beeves were thirty and six thousand; of which the Lord’s tribute was threescore and twelve.
text.NABRE: thirty-six thousand oxen, of which seventy-two fell as tax to the Lord;
text.DRB: And of the thirty six thousand oxen, oxen seventy and two:

text.NRSV: The donkeys were thirty thousand five hundred, of which the Lord's tribute was sixty-one.
text.Rotherham: and the asses, thirty thousand and five hundred,—and the tribute of them unto Yahweh, sixty-one;
text.KJV: And the asses were thirty thousand and five hundred; of which the Lord’s tribute was threescore and one.
text.NABRE: thirty thousand five hundred donkeys, of which sixty-one fell as tax to the Lord;
text.DRB: of the thirty thousand five hundred asses, asses sixty one:

text.NRSV: The persons were sixteen thousand, of which the Lord's tribute was thirty-two persons.
text.Rotherham: and the human persons, sixteen thousand,—and the tribute of them unto Yahweh, thirty-two persons.
text.KJV: And the persons were sixteen thousand; of which the Lord’s tribute was thirty and two persons.
text.NABRE: and sixteen thousand persons, of whom thirty-two persons fell as tax to the Lord.
text.DRB: of the souls of mankind sixteen thousand, there fell to the portion of our Lord thirty two souls.

text.NRSV: Moses gave the tribute, the offering for the Lord, to Eleazar the priest, as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: And Moses gave the tribute—the heave-offering of Yahweh, unto Eleazar the priest,—As Yahweh commanded Moses.
text.KJV: And Moses gave the tribute, which was the Lord’s heave offering, unto Eleazar the priest, as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.NABRE: Moses gave the taxes contributed to the Lord to Eleazar the priest, exactly as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.DRB: And Moyses delivered the number of the first fruits of our Lord to Eleazar the Priest, as it had been commanded him,

text.NRSV: As for the Israelites' half, which Moses separated from that of the troops,
text.Rotherham: And, of the half, allotted unto the sons of Israel,—which Moses halved away from the men who had gone out in the host,
text.KJV: And of the children of Israel’s half, which Moses divided from the men that warred,
text.NABRE: As for the Israelites’ half, which Moses had taken from the men who had fought—
text.DRB: of the half part of the children of Israel, which he had separated to them, that had been in the battle.

text.NRSV: the congregation's half was three hundred thirty-seven thousand five hundred sheep and goats,
text.Rotherham: the half allotted unto the assembly, was—of the flock, three hundred and thirty-seven thousand, and five hundred;
text.KJV: (Now the half that pertained unto the congregation was three hundred thousand and thirty thousand and seven thousand and five hundred sheep,
text.NABRE: the community’s half was three hundred and thirty-seven thousand five hundred sheep,
text.DRB: But of the half part that had chanced to the rest of the multitude, that is to say, of the three hundred thirty seven thousand five hundred sheep,

text.NRSV: thirty-six thousand oxen,
text.Rotherham: and, of the herd, six and thirty thousand;
text.KJV: And thirty and six thousand beeves,
text.NABRE: thirty-six thousand oxen,
text.DRB: and of the thirty six thousand oxen,

text.NRSV: thirty thousand five hundred donkeys,
text.Rotherham: and, of asses, thirty thousand and five hundred;
text.KJV: And thirty thousand asses and five hundred,
text.NABRE: thirty thousand five hundred donkeys,
text.DRB: and of the thirty thousand five hundred asses,

text.NRSV: and sixteen thousand persons.
text.Rotherham: and, human persons, sixteen thousand.
text.KJV: And sixteen thousand persons;)
text.NABRE: and sixteen thousand persons.
text.DRB: and of the sixteen thousand of mankind,

text.NRSV: From the Israelites' half Moses took one of every fifty, both of persons and of animals, and gave them to the Levites, who had charge of the tabernacle of the Lord; as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: So then Moses took—out of the half belonging to the sons of Israel the allotted portion one out of fifty, of the human beings and of the beasts,—and gave them unto the Levites, the keepers of the charge of the habitation of Yahweh, As Yahweh commanded Moses.
text.KJV: Even of the children of Israel’s half, Moses took one portion of fifty, both of man and of beast, and gave them unto the Levites, which kept the charge of the tabernacle of the Lord; as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.NABRE: From the Israelites’ half, Moses took one captive from every fifty, from human being and beast alike, and gave them to the Levites, who performed the duties of the Lord ’s tabernacle, exactly as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.DRB: Moyses took the fiftieth head, and gave it to the Levites, that watched in the tabernacle of our Lord, as our Lord had commanded.

text.NRSV: Then the officers who were over the thousands of the army, the commanders of thousands and the commanders of hundreds, approached Moses,
text.Rotherham: Then came near unto Moses the officers who belonged unto the thousands of the host,—the princes of thousands and the princes of hundreds;
text.KJV: And the officers which were over thousands of the host, the captains of thousands, and captains of hundreds, came near unto Moses:
text.NABRE: Gifts of the Officers. Then those who were officers over the contingents of the army, commanders of thousands and commanders of hundreds, came up to Moses
text.DRB: And when the Princes of the host were come to Moyses, and the tribunes, and centurions, they said:

text.NRSV: and said to Moses, "Your servants have counted the warriors who are under our command, and not one of us is missing."
text.Rotherham: and said unto Moses, Thy servants have reckoned up the sum of the men of war who are in our hand,—and there is not missed from among us, a man!
text.KJV: And they said unto Moses, Thy servants have taken the sum of the men of war which are under our charge, and there lacketh not one man of us.
text.NABRE: and said to him, “Your servants have counted the soldiers under our command, and not one of us is missing.
text.DRB: We thy servants have reckoned the number of the warriors, which we had under our hand: and not so much as one verily was lacking.

text.NRSV: So we have brought as an offering to the Lord what each of us found, articles of gold, armlets and bracelets, signet rings, earrings, and pendants, to make atonement for ourselves before the Lord."
text.Rotherham: Therefore have we brought near an offering unto Yahweh, what, each man, hath found—articles of gold, ankle chains and bracelets, rings earrings and buckles,—to put a propitiatory-covering over our souls before Yahweh.
text.KJV: We have therefore brought an oblation for the Lord, what every man hath gotten, of jewels of gold, chains, and bracelets, rings, earrings, and tablets, to make an atonement for our souls before the Lord.
text.NABRE: So, we have brought as an offering to the Lord articles of gold that each of us has picked up—anklets, bracelets, rings, earrings, or pendants—to make atonement for ourselves before the Lord.”
text.DRB: For this cause we offer in the donaries of our Lord every one that which we could find in the prey of gold, garters and tablets, rings and bracelets, and chains, that thou mayest pray to our Lord for us.

text.NRSV: Moses and Eleazar the priest received the gold from them, all in the form of crafted articles.
text.Rotherham: So Moses, and Eleazar the priest took the gold of them,—all the wrought articles.
text.KJV: And Moses and Eleazar the priest took the gold of them, even all wrought jewels.
text.NABRE: Moses and Eleazar the priest accepted the gold from them, all fashioned pieces.
text.DRB: And Moyses and Eleazar the Priest received all the gold in diverse forms

text.NRSV: And all the gold of the offering that they offered to the Lord, from the commanders of thousands and the commanders of hundreds, was sixteen thousand seven hundred fifty shekels.
text.Rotherham: And all the gold of the heave-offering which they offered up unto Yahweh, was—sixteen thousand seven hundred and fifty shekels,—of the princes of thousands, and of the princes of hundreds.
text.KJV: And all the gold of the offering that they offered up to the Lord, of the captains of thousands, and of the captains of hundreds, was sixteen thousand seven hundred and fifty shekels.
text.NABRE: The gold that was given as a contribution to the Lord —from the commanders of thousands and the commanders of hundreds—amounted in all to sixteen thousand seven hundred and fifty shekels.
text.DRB: in weight sixteen thousand seven hundred fifty sicles of the tribunes and of the centurions.

text.NRSV: (The troops had all taken booty for themselves.)"
text.Rotherham: The men of the host, had taken prey, each man for himself.
text.KJV: (For the men of war had taken spoil, every man for himself.)
text.NABRE: What the common soldiers had looted each one kept for himself.
text.DRB: For that which every one had spoiled in the prey, was his own.

text.NRSV: So Moses and Eleazar the priest received the gold from the commanders of thousands and of hundreds, and brought it into the tent of meeting as a memorial for the Israelites before the Lord.
text.Rotherham: So then Moses and Eleazar the priest took the gold of the princes of thousands, and hundreds,—and brought it into the tent of meeting, as a memorial for the sons of Israel before Yahweh.
text.KJV: And Moses and Eleazar the priest took the gold of the captains of thousands and of hundreds, and brought it into the tabernacle of the congregation, for a memorial for the children of Israel before the Lord.
text.NABRE: So Moses and Eleazar the priest accepted the gold from the commanders of thousands and of hundreds, and put it in the tent of meeting as a reminder on behalf of the Israelites before the Lord.
text.DRB: And that which was received they brought into the tabernacle of testimony, for a monument of the children of Israel before our Lord.

text.NRSV: Now the Reubenites and the Gadites owned a very great number of cattle. When they saw that the land of Jazer and the land of Gilead was a good place for cattle,
text.Rotherham: Now, much cattle, had come into the possession of the sons of Reuben and of the sons of Gad, an exceeding mighty number. And when they saw the land of Jazer and the land of Gilead, lo! the place, was a place for cattle.
text.KJV: Now the children of Reuben and the children of Gad had a very great multitude of cattle: and when they saw the land of Jazer, and the land of Gilead, that, behold, the place was a place for cattle;
text.NABRE: Now the Reubenites and Gadites had a very large number of livestock. Noticing that the land of Jazer and of Gilead was a place suited to livestock,
text.DRB: And the children of Ruben and Gad had much cattle, and they had in beasts an infinite substance. And when they had seen Jazer and Galaad fit countries for to feed cattle,

text.NRSV: the Gadites and the Reubenites came and spoke to Moses, to Eleazar the priest, and to the leaders of the congregation, saying,"
text.Rotherham: So the sons of Gad and the sons of Reuben came in,—and spake unto Moses and unto Eleazar the priest, and unto the princes of assembly, saying,
text.KJV: The children of Gad and the children of Reuben came and spake unto Moses, and to Eleazar the priest, and unto the princes of the congregation, saying,
text.NABRE: the Gadites and Reubenites came to Moses and Eleazar the priest and to the leaders of the community and said,
text.DRB: they came to Moyses and Eleazar the Priest, and the Princes of the multitude, and said:

text.NRSV: Ataroth, Dibon, Jazer, Nimrah, Heshbon, Elealeh, Sebam, Nebo, and Beon—"
text.Rotherham: As touching Ataroth and Dibon, and Jazer and Nimrah, and Heshbon, and Elealeh,—and Sebam and Nebo, and Beon-
text.KJV: Ataroth, and Dibon, and Jazer, and Nimrah, and Heshbon, and Elealeh, and Shebam, and Nebo, and Beon,
text.NABRE: “The region of Ataroth, Dibon, Jazer, Nimrah, Heshbon, Elealeh, Sebam, Nebo and Baal-meon—
text.DRB: Ataroth, and Dibon, and Jazer, and Nemra, Hesebon, and Eleale, and Sabam, and Nebo, and Beon,

text.NRSV: the land that the Lord subdued before the congregation of Israel—is a land for cattle; and your servants have cattle."
text.Rotherham: the land which Yahweh smote before the assembly of Israel, a land for cattle, it is,—and, thy servants, have cattle.
text.KJV: Even the country which the Lord smote before the congregation of Israel, is a land for cattle, and thy servants have cattle:
text.NABRE: the land which the Lord has laid low before the community of Israel, is a land for livestock, and your servants have livestock.”
text.DRB: the land, which our Lord smote in the sight of the children of Israel, is of a very plentiful soil for the feeding of beasts: and we thy servants have very much cattle:

text.NRSV: They continued, "If we have found favor in your sight, let this land be given to your servants for a possession; do not make us cross the Jordan."
text.Rotherham: And they said—If we have found favour in thine eyes, let this land be given unto thy servants, for a possession,—do not take us over the Jordan.
text.KJV: Wherefore, said they, if we have found grace in thy sight, let this land be given unto thy servants for a possession, and bring us not over Jordan.
text.NABRE: They continued, “If we find favor with you, let this land be given to your servants as their possession. Do not make us cross the Jordan.”
text.DRB: and we pray thee if we have found grace before thee, that thou give us thy servants the same in possession, and make us not pass over Jordan.

text.NRSV: But Moses said to the Gadites and to the Reubenites, "Shall your brothers go to war while you sit here?"
text.Rotherham: Then said Moses unto the sons of Gad and unto the sons of Reuben,—Shall, your brethren, go into the war, and ye, settle down here?
text.KJV: And Moses said unto the children of Gad and to the children of Reuben, Shall your brethren go to war, and shall ye sit here?
text.NABRE: Moses’ Rebuke. But Moses answered the Gadites and Reubenites: “Are your kindred, then, to go to war, while you remain here?
text.DRB: To whom Moyses answered: Why shall your brethren go to fight, and will you sit here?

text.NRSV: Why will you discourage the hearts of the Israelites from going over into the land that the Lord has given them?"
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, then, should ye dissuade the heart of the sons of Israel, from passing over into the land which Yahweh hath given to them?
text.KJV: And wherefore discourage ye the heart of the children of Israel from going over into the land which the Lord hath given them?
text.NABRE: Why do you wish to discourage the Israelites from crossing to the land the Lord has given them?
text.DRB: Why subvert you the minds of the children of Israel, that they may not be bold to pass into the place, which our Lord will give them?

text.NRSV: Your fathers did thus when I sent them from Kadesh-barnea to see the land."
text.Rotherham: Thus, did your fathers,—when I sent them from Kadesh-barnea, to view the land,
text.KJV: Thus did your fathers, when I sent them from Kadesh–barnea to see the land.
text.NABRE: That is just what your ancestors did when I sent them from Kadesh-barnea to reconnoiter the land.
text.DRB: Did not your fathers so, when I sent from Cadesbarne to view the Land?

text.NRSV: When they went up to the Wadi Eshcol and saw the land, they discouraged the hearts of the Israelites from going into the land that the Lord had given them."
text.Rotherham: and they went up as far as the ravine of Eschol, and viewed the land, and then dissuaded the heart of the sons of Israel from entering into the land, which Yahweh had given them.
text.KJV: For when they went up unto the valley of Eshcol, and saw the land, they discouraged the heart of the children of Israel, that they should not go into the land which the Lord had given them.
text.NABRE: They went up to the Wadi Eshcol and reconnoitered the land, then so discouraged the Israelites that they would not enter the land the Lord had given them.
text.DRB: And when they were come as far as the Valley of cluster, having viewed all the country, they subverted the heart of the children of Israel, that they should not enter the coasts, which our Lord gave them.

text.NRSV: The Lord's anger was kindled on that day and he swore, saying,"
text.Rotherham: Then was the anger of Yahweh kindled on that day,—and he sware, saying:
text.KJV: And the Lord’s anger was kindled the same time, and he sware, saying,
text.NABRE: At that time the anger of the Lord flared up, and he swore:
text.DRB: Who being wrath sware, saying:

text.NRSV: 'Surely none of the people who came up out of Egypt, from twenty years old and upward, shall see the land that I swore to give to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob, because they have not unreservedly followed me—"
text.Rotherham: Surely none of the men that came up out of Egypt from twenty years old and upwards, shall see the soil, which I sware unto Abraham, unto Isaac and unto Jacob, because they have not followed after me fully;
text.KJV: Surely none of the men that came up out of Egypt, from twenty years old and upward, shall see the land which I sware unto Abraham, unto Isaac, and unto Jacob; because they have not wholly followed me:
text.NABRE: None of the men twenty years old or more who have come up from Egypt will see the land I promised under oath to Abraham and Isaac and Jacob, because they have not followed me unreservedly—
text.DRB: If these men, that came up out of Ægypt, from twenty years and upward, shall see the land, which under oath I promised to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob: and they would not follow me,

text.NRSV: none except Caleb son of Jephunneh the Kenizzite and Joshua son of Nun, for they have unreservedly followed the Lord.'"
text.Rotherham: save Caleb son of Jephunneh, the Kenezite, and Joshua, son of Nun,—for they followed after Yahweh, fully.
text.KJV: Save Caleb the son of Jephunneh the Kenezite, and Joshua the son of Nun: for they have wholly followed the Lord.
text.NABRE: except the Kenizzite Caleb, son of Jephunneh, the Kenizzite, and Joshua, son of Nun, since they have followed the Lord unreservedly.
text.DRB: except Caleb the son of Jephone the Cenezeite, and Josue the son of Nun: these have fulfilled my will.

text.NRSV: And the Lord's anger was kindled against Israel, and he made them wander in the wilderness for forty years, until all the generation that had done evil in the sight of the Lord had disappeared."
text.Rotherham: So the anger of Yahweh kindled upon Israel, and he made them wander in the desert, forty years,—until all the generation who had done the evil in the eyes of Yahweh, had wasted away!
text.KJV: And the Lord’s anger was kindled against Israel, and he made them wander in the wilderness forty years, until all the generation, that had done evil in the sight of the Lord, was consumed.
text.NABRE: So the anger of the Lord flared up against the Israelites and he made them wander in the wilderness forty years, until the whole generation that had done evil in the sight of the Lord had disappeared.
text.DRB: And our Lord being wrath against Israel, led him about through the desert forty years, until the whole generation was consumed, that had done evil in his sight.

text.NRSV: And now you, a brood of sinners, have risen in place of your fathers, to increase the Lord's fierce anger against Israel!"
text.Rotherham: Lo! therefore, ye have arisen in the room of your fathers, a brood of sinful men,—to heap up yet more upon the heat of the anger of Yahweh, towards Israel.
text.KJV: And, behold, ye are risen up in your fathers’ stead, an increase of sinful men, to augment yet the fierce anger of the Lord toward Israel.
text.NABRE: And now here you are, offspring of sinful stock, rising up in your ancestors’ place to add still more to the Lord ’s blazing anger against the Israelites.
text.DRB: And behold, quoth he, you are risen up in stead of your fathers, the offspring and disciples of sinful men, to augment the fury of our Lord against Israel.

text.NRSV: If you turn away from following him, he will again abandon them in the wilderness; and you will destroy all this people."
text.Rotherham: If ye do turn away from following him, then will he yet again leave them behind in the desert,—so shall ye bring destruction upon all this people.
text.KJV: For if ye turn away from after him, he will yet again leave them in the wilderness; and ye shall destroy all this people.
text.NABRE: If you turn away from following him, he will again abandon them in the wilderness, and you will bring about the ruin of this entire people.”
text.DRB: But if you will not follow him, he will leave the people in the wilderness, and you shall be the cause of the death of all.

text.NRSV: Then they came up to him and said, "We will build sheepfolds here for our flocks, and towns for our little ones,"
text.Rotherham: Then came they near unto him, and said, Folds, for flocks, would we build for our cattle, here,—and cities for our little ones;
text.KJV: And they came near unto him, and said, We will build sheepfolds here for our cattle, and cities for our little ones:
text.NABRE: Counter Proposal. But they approached him and said: “We will only build sheepfolds here for our flocks and towns for our families;
text.DRB: But they coming near, said: We will make sheepcotes, and stalls for our cattle, for our little ones also fenced cities:

text.NRSV: but we will take up arms as a vanguard before the Israelites, until we have brought them to their place. Meanwhile our little ones will stay in the fortified towns because of the inhabitants of the land."
text.Rotherham: but, we, would arm ourselves promptly, before the sons of Israel, until that we have brought them into their place,—so should our little ones remain in the fortified cities, because of the inhabitants of the land.
text.KJV: But we ourselves will go ready armed before the children of Israel, until we have brought them unto their place: and our little ones shall dwell in the fenced cities because of the inhabitants of the land.
text.NABRE: but we ourselves will march as troops in the vanguard before the Israelites, until we have led them to their destination. Meanwhile our families will remain in the fortified towns because of the land’s inhabitants.
text.DRB: and we ourselves armed and girded will march on to battle before the children of Israel, until we bring them in unto their places. Our little ones and whatsoever we can have, shall be in walled cities, because of the lying of wait of the inhabitants.

text.NRSV: We will not return to our homes until all the Israelites have obtained their inheritance."
text.Rotherham: We would not return unto our houses,—until the sons of Israel have possessed themselves each man, of his inheritance.
text.KJV: We will not return unto our houses, until the children of Israel have inherited every man his inheritance.
text.NABRE: We will not return to our homes until all the Israelites have taken possession of their heritage.
text.DRB: We will not return into our houses, until the children of Israel possess their inheritance:

text.NRSV: We will not inherit with them on the other side of the Jordan and beyond, because our inheritance has come to us on this side of the Jordan to the east."
text.Rotherham: For we would not inherit with them, over the Jordan, and onwards,—because our inheritance hath come unto us on this side the Jordan towards sunrise.
text.KJV: For we will not inherit with them on yonder side Jordan, or forward; because our inheritance is fallen to us on this side Jordan eastward.
text.NABRE: But we will not claim any heritage with them across the Jordan and beyond, because we have received a heritage for ourselves on the eastern side of the Jordan.”
text.DRB: neither will we seek any thing beyond Jordan, because already we have our possession on the east side thereof.

text.NRSV: Moses said to them: "If you do this—if you take up arms to go before the Lord for the war,"
text.Rotherham: And Moses said unto them, If ye will indeed do this thing,—if ye will arm yourselves, before Yahweh, for the war;
text.KJV: And Moses said unto them, If ye will do this thing, if ye will go armed before the Lord to war,
text.NABRE: Agreement Reached. Moses said to them in reply: “If you do this—if you march as troops before the Lord into battle
text.DRB: To whom Moyses said: If you do that you promise, march on well appointed before our Lord to fight:

text.NRSV: and all those of you who bear arms cross the Jordan before the Lord, until he has driven out his enemies from before him"
text.Rotherham: and every armed man of you pass over the Jordan before Yahweh,—until he hath dispossessed his enemies, from before him;
text.KJV: And will go all of you armed over Jordan before the Lord, until he hath driven out his enemies from before him,
text.NABRE: and cross the Jordan in full force before the Lord until he has driven his enemies out of his way
text.DRB: and let every man of war pass over Jordan, until our Lord subvert his enemies,

text.NRSV: and the land is subdued before the Lord—then after that you may return and be free of obligation to the Lord and to Israel, and this land shall be your possession before the Lord."
text.Rotherham: and the land be subdued before Yahweh then afterwards, shall ye return, and shall be acquitted by Yahweh and by Israel,—and this land shall be yours for a possession before Yahweh.
text.KJV: And the land be subdued before the Lord: then afterward ye shall return, and be guiltless before the Lord, and before Israel; and this land shall be your possession before the Lord.
text.NABRE: and the land is subdued before the Lord, then you may return here, free from every obligation to the Lord and to Israel, and this land will be your possession before the Lord.
text.DRB: and all the Land be subdued to him: then shall you be blameless before our Lord & before Israel, & you shall obtain the countries that you would in the sight of our Lord.

text.NRSV: But if you do not do so, you have sinned against the Lord; and be sure your sin will find you out."
text.Rotherham: But, if ye shall not do this, lo! ye will have sinned against Yahweh,—and take note of your sin, that it will find you out.
text.KJV: But if ye will not do so, behold, ye have sinned against the Lord: and be sure your sin will find you out.
text.NABRE: But if you do not do this, you will have sinned against the Lord, and you can be sure that the consequences of your sin will overtake you.
text.DRB: But if you do not that which you say, no man can doubt but you sin against God: and know ye that your sin shall apprehend you.

text.NRSV: Build towns for your little ones, and folds for your flocks; but do what you have promised."
text.Rotherham: Build you cities for your little ones, and folds for your flocks,—and then that which hath gone forth from your mouth, ye shall do.
text.KJV: Build you cities for your little ones, and folds for your sheep; and do that which hath proceeded out of your mouth.
text.NABRE: Build the towns, then, for your families, and the folds for your flocks, but fulfill what you have promised.”
text.DRB: Build therefore cities for your little ones, and sheepcotes and stalls for your sheep and cattle: and accomplish that which you have promised.

text.NRSV: Then the Gadites and the Reubenites said to Moses, "Your servants will do as my lord commands."
text.Rotherham: Then spake the sons of Gad, and the sons of Reuben unto Moses, saying,—thy servants, will do, as, my lord, is giving command.
text.KJV: And the children of Gad and the children of Reuben spake unto Moses, saying, Thy servants will do as my lord commandeth.
text.NABRE: The Gadites and Reubenites answered Moses, “Your servants will do as my lord commands.
text.DRB: And the children of Gad and Ruben said to Moyses: We are thy servants, we will do that which our Lord commandeth.

text.NRSV: Our little ones, our wives, our flocks, and all our livestock shall remain there in the towns of Gilead;"
text.Rotherham: Our little ones, our wives, our cattle, and all our beasts, shall remain there in the cities of Gilead;
text.KJV: Our little ones, our wives, our flocks, and all our cattle, shall be there in the cities of Gilead:
text.NABRE: While our wives and children, our livestock and other animals remain there in the towns of Gilead,
text.DRB: We will leave our little ones, and our wives, and sheep, and cattle in the cities of Galaad:

text.NRSV: but your servants will cross over, everyone armed for war, to do battle for the Lord, just as my lord orders."
text.Rotherham: and thy servants, will pass over every one armed for war, before Yahweh to battle,—as, my lord is speaking.
text.KJV: But thy servants will pass over, every man armed for war, before the Lord to battle, as my lord saith.
text.NABRE: all your servants will go across as armed troops before the Lord to battle, just as my lord says.”
text.DRB: and we thy servants all well appointed will march on to the war, as thou my Lord speakest.

text.NRSV: So Moses gave command concerning them to Eleazar the priest, to Joshua son of Nun, and to the family heads of the tribes of the Israelites."
text.Rotherham: So Moses gave command concerning them, unto Eleazar the priest and unto Joshua son of Nun, and unto the heads of the fathers of the tribes, of the sons of Israel;
text.KJV: So concerning them Moses commanded Eleazar the priest, and Joshua the son of Nun, and the chief fathers of the tribes of the children of Israel:
text.NABRE: So Moses gave this command in their regard to Eleazar the priest, to Joshua, son of Nun, and to the heads of the ancestral houses of the Israelite tribes:
text.DRB: Moyses therefore commanded Eleazar the Priest, & Josue the son of Nun, and the Princes of the families by the tribes of Israel, and he said to them:

text.NRSV: And Moses said to them, "If the Gadites and the Reubenites, everyone armed for battle before the Lord, will cross over the Jordan with you and the land shall be subdued before you, then you shall give them the land of Gilead for a possession;"
text.Rotherham: and Moses said unto them—If the sons of Gad and the son of Reuben will pass with you over the Jordan, every one armed for the war before Yahweh, and the land be subdued before you, then shall ye give unto them the land of Gilead, for a possession.
text.KJV: And Moses said unto them, If the children of Gad and the children of Reuben will pass with you over Jordan, every man armed to battle, before the Lord, and the land shall be subdued before you; then ye shall give them the land of Gilead for a possession:
text.NABRE: He said to them, “If all the Gadites and Reubenites cross the Jordan with you in full force before the Lord into battle, the land will be subdued before you, and you will give them Gilead as a possession.
text.DRB: If the children of Gad, and the children of Ruben pass with you over Jordan all armed to the war before our Lord, and the Land be subdued to you: give them Galaad in possession.

text.NRSV: but if they will not cross over with you armed, they shall have possessions among you in the land of Canaan."
text.Rotherham: But, if they will not pass over armed, with you, then shall they accept a possession in your midst, in the land of Canaan.
text.KJV: But if they will not pass over with you armed, they shall have possessions among you in the land of Canaan.
text.NABRE: But if they will not go across in force with you before the Lord, you will bring their wives and children and livestock across before you into Canaan, and they will possess a holding among you in the land of Canaan.”
text.DRB: But if they will not pass armed with you into the Land of Chanaan, let them take places to dwell in among you.

text.NRSV: The Gadites and the Reubenites answered, saying, "As the Lord has spoken to your servants, so we will do."
text.Rotherham: Then responded the sons of Gad and the sons of Reuben, saying,—That which Yahweh had spoken unto thy servants, so, will we do.
text.KJV: And the children of Gad and the children of Reuben answered, saying, As the Lord hath said unto thy servants, so will we do.
text.NABRE: To this the Gadites and Reubenites replied, “We will do what the Lord has ordered your servants.
text.DRB: And the children of Gad, and the children of Ruben answered: As our lord hath spoken to his servants, so will we do:

text.NRSV: We will cross over armed before the Lord into the land of Canaan, but the possession of our inheritance shall remain with us on this side of the Jordan."
text.Rotherham: We, will pass over armed, before Yahweh into the land of Canaan,—then shall be ours, the possession of our inheritance across the Jordan.
text.KJV: We will pass over armed before the Lord into the land of Canaan, that the possession of our inheritance on this side Jordan may be ours.
text.NABRE: We ourselves will go across in force before the Lord into the land of Canaan, but we will retain our hereditary property on this side of the Jordan.”
text.DRB: ourselves armed will march before our Lord into the Land of Chanaan, and we confess that we have already received our possession beyond Jordan.

text.NRSV: Moses gave to them—to the Gadites and to the Reubenites and to the half-tribe of Manasseh son of Joseph—the kingdom of King Sihon of the Amorites and the kingdom of King Og of Bashan, the land and its towns, with the territories of the surrounding towns."
text.Rotherham: So then Moses gave unto them even unto the sons of Gad and unto the sons of Reuben and unto the half tribe of Manasseh son of Joseph the kingdom of sihon, king of the Amorites, and the kingdom of Og, the king of Bashan, the land by her cities with boundaries, even the cities of the land round about.
text.KJV: And Moses gave unto them, even to the children of Gad, and to the children of Reuben, and unto half the tribe of Manasseh the son of Joseph, the kingdom of Sihon king of the Amorites, and the kingdom of Og king of Bashan, the land, with the cities thereof in the coasts, even the cities of the country round about.
text.NABRE: So Moses gave them— the Gadites and Reubenites, as well as half the tribe of Manasseh, son of Joseph—the kingdom of Sihon, king of the Amorites, and the kingdom of Og, king of Bashan, the land with its towns, and the districts of the surrounding towns.
text.DRB: Moyses therefore gave to the children of Gad and Ruben, and to the half tribe of Manasses the son of Joseph, the kingdom of Sehon king of the Amorrhite, and the Kingdom of Og king of Basan, and their land and the cities thereof round about.

text.NRSV: The Gadites rebuilt Dibon, Ataroth, Aroer,"
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Gad built Dibon, and Ataroth,—and Aroer;
text.KJV: And the children of Gad built Dibon, and Ataroth, and Aroer,
text.NABRE: The Gadites rebuilt the cities of Dibon, Ataroth, Aroer,
text.DRB: Therefore the sons of Gad built Dibon, and Ataroth, and Aroer,

text.NRSV: Atroth-shophan, Jazer, Jogbehah,"
text.Rotherham: and Atroth-shophan and Jazer, and Jogbehah;
text.KJV: And Atroth, Shophan, and Jaazer, and Jogbehah,
text.NABRE: Atroth-shophan, Jazer, Jogbehah,
text.DRB: and Etroth, and Sophan, and Iazar, and Iegbaa,

text.NRSV: Beth-nimrah, and Beth-haran, fortified cities, and folds for sheep."
text.Rotherham: and Beth-nimrah, and Beth-haran,—fortified cities and folds for flocks.
text.KJV: And Beth–nimrah, and Beth–haran, fenced cities: and folds for sheep.
text.NABRE: Beth-nimrah and Beth-haran—fortified cities—and sheepfolds.
text.DRB: and Bethnemra, and Betharan, cities fenced, and sheepcotes for their sheep.

text.NRSV: The Reubenites rebuilt Heshbon, Elealeh, Kiriathaim,"
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Reuben built Heshbon, and Elealeh,—and Kiriathaim,
text.KJV: And the children of Reuben built Heshbon, and Elealeh, and Kirjathaim,
text.NABRE: The Reubenites rebuilt Heshbon, Elealeh, Kiriathaim,
text.DRB: But the children of Ruben builded Hesebon, and Eleale, & Cariathaim,

text.NRSV: Nebo, and Baal-meon (some names being changed), and Sibmah; and they gave names to the towns that they rebuilt."
text.Rotherham: and Nebo and Baal-meon—their names being changed, and Sibmah,—and they gave names to the cities which they built.
text.KJV: And Nebo, and Baal–meon, (their names being changed,) and Shibmah: and gave other names unto the cities which they builded.
text.NABRE: Nebo, Baal-meon (names to be changed!), and Sibmah. These towns, which they rebuilt, they called by their old names.
text.DRB: and Nabo, and Baalmeon changing their names, Sabama also: giving names to the cities, which they had built.

text.NRSV: The descendants of Machir son of Manasseh went to Gilead, captured it, and dispossessed the Amorites who were there."
text.Rotherham: Then went the sons of Machir son of Manasseh to Gilead, and captured it, and dispossessed the Amorites who were therein,
text.KJV: And the children of Machir the son of Manasseh went to Gilead, and took it, and dispossessed the Amorite which was in it.
text.NABRE: Other Conquests. The descendants of Machir, son of Manasseh, went to Gilead and captured it, dispossessing the Amorites who were there.
text.DRB: Moreover the children of Machir, the son of Manasses, went into Galaad, and wasted it killing the Amorrhite the inhabiter thereof.

text.NRSV: So Moses gave Gilead to Machir son of Manasseh, and he settled there."
text.Rotherham: So Moses gave Gilead unto Machir son of Manasseh, and he dwelt therein.
text.KJV: And Moses gave Gilead unto Machir the son of Manasseh; and he dwelt therein.
text.NABRE: (Moses gave Gilead to Machir, son of Manasseh, and he settled there.)
text.DRB: Moyses therefore gave the land of Galaad to Machir the son of Manasses, who dwelt in it.

text.NRSV: Jair son of Manasseh went and captured their villages, and renamed them Havvoth-jair."
text.Rotherham: And Jair, son of Manasseh, went, and captured their encampments,—and called them Havvoth-jair [="The encampments of Jair"].
text.KJV: And Jair the son of Manasseh went and took the small towns thereof, and called them Havoth–jair.
text.NABRE: Jair, a descendant of Manasseh, went and captured their tent villages, and called them Havvoth-jair.
text.DRB: And Jair the son of Manasses went, and took the villages thereof, which he called Havoth Jair, that is to say, the Villages of Jair.

text.NRSV: And Nobah went and captured Kenath and its villages, and renamed it Nobah after himself.
text.Rotherham: And, Nobah, went, and captured Kenath, with the villages thereof,—and called it Nobah, after his own name.
text.KJV: And Nobah went and took Kenath, and the villages thereof, and called it Nobah, after his own name.
text.NABRE: Nobah went and captured Kenath with its dependencies and called it Nobah after his own name.
text.DRB: Nobe also went, and took Canath with the villages thereof: and he called it by his own name Nobe.

text.NRSV: These are the stages by which the Israelites went out of the land of Egypt in military formation under the leadership of Moses and Aaron."
text.Rotherham: These, are the departures of the sons of Israel whereby they came forth out of the land of Egypt by their hosts,—in the hand of Moses and Aaron.
text.KJV: These are the journeys of the children of Israel, which went forth out of the land of Egypt with their armies under the hand of Moses and Aaron.
text.NABRE: The following are the stages by which the Israelites went out by companies from the land of Egypt under the guidance of Moses and Aaron.
text.DRB: These are the mansions of the children of Israel, that went out of Ægypt by their troops in the hand of Moyses and Aaron,

text.NRSV: Moses wrote down their starting points, stage by stage, by command of the Lord; and these are their stages according to their starting places."
text.Rotherham: And Moses wrote their comings forth by their departures, at the bidding of Yahweh,—and these, are their departures by their comings forth.
text.KJV: And Moses wrote their goings out according to their journeys by the commandment of the Lord: and these are their journeys according to their goings out.
text.NABRE: Moses recorded the starting points of the various stages at the direction of the Lord. These are the stages according to their starting points:
text.DRB: which Moyses described according to the places of the camp, which by our Lord's commandment they changed.

text.NRSV: They set out from Rameses in the first month, on the fifteenth day of the first month; on the day after the passover the Israelites went out boldly in the sight of all the Egyptians,"
text.Rotherham: So then they brake up from Rameses in the first month, on the fifteenth day of the first month, on the morrow of the passover, came forth the sons of Israel with an uplifted hand, in the sight of all the Egyptians;
text.KJV: And they departed from Rameses in the first month, on the fifteenth day of the first month; on the morrow after the passover the children of Israel went out with an high hand in the sight of all the Egyptians.
text.NABRE: They set out from Rameses in the first month, on the fifteenth day of the first month. On the day after the Passover the Israelites went forth in triumph, in view of all Egypt,
text.DRB: The children of Israel therefore departing from Ramesses the first month, the fifteenth day of the first month, the morrow after they made the Phase, in a mighty hand, all the Ægyptians seeing them,

text.NRSV: while the Egyptians were burying all their firstborn, whom the Lord had struck down among them. The Lord executed judgments even against their gods."
text.Rotherham: when the Egyptians, were burying them whom Yahweh had smitten among them, every firstborn,—when, upon their gods, Yahweh had executed judgments,
text.KJV: For the Egyptians buried all their firstborn, which the Lord had smitten among them: upon their gods also the Lord executed judgments.
text.NABRE: while the Egyptians buried those whom the Lord had struck down, every firstborn; on their gods, too, the Lord executed judgments.
text.DRB: & burying their first born, which our Lord had strucken (yea and on their gods also he had exercised vengeance)

text.NRSV: So the Israelites set out from Rameses, and camped at Succoth."
text.Rotherham: Thus then the sons of Israel brake up from Rameses,—and encamped in Succoth.
text.KJV: And the children of Israel removed from Rameses, and pitched in Succoth.
text.NABRE: From Egypt to Sinai. Setting out from Rameses, the Israelites camped at Succoth.
text.DRB: they camped in Soccoth.

text.NRSV: They set out from Succoth, and camped at Etham, which is on the edge of the wilderness."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Succoth,—and encamped in Etham, which is at the edge of the desert.
text.KJV: And they departed from Succoth, and pitched in Etham, which is in the edge of the wilderness.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Succoth, they camped at Etham near the edge of the wilderness.
text.DRB: And from Soccoth they came into Etham, which is in the uttermost borders of the wilderness.

text.NRSV: They set out from Etham, and turned back to Pi-hahiroth, which faces Baal-zephon; and they camped before Migdol."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Etham, and turned upon Pi-hahiroth, which is over against Baal-zephon,—and encamped before Migdol.
text.KJV: And they removed from Etham, and turned again unto Pi–hahiroth, which is before Baal–zephon: and they pitched before Migdol.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Etham, they turned back to Pi-hahiroth, which is opposite Baal-zephon, and they camped opposite Migdol.
text.DRB: Departing thence they came over against Phihahiroth, which looketh toward Beelsephon, and camped before Magdal.

text.NRSV: They set out from Pi-hahiroth, passed through the sea into the wilderness, went a three days' journey in the wilderness of Etham, and camped at Marah."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from before Hahiroth, and passed through the midst of the sea towards the desert,—and went their way a journey of three days in the desert of Etham, and encamped in Marah.
text.KJV: And they departed from before Pi–hahiroth, and passed through the midst of the sea into the wilderness, and went three days’ journey in the wilderness of Etham, and pitched in Marah.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Pi-hahiroth, they crossed over through the sea into the wilderness, and after they traveled a three days’ journey in the wilderness of Etham, they camped at Marah.
text.DRB: And departing from Phihahiroth, they passed through the midst of the sea into the wilderness: and walking three days through the desert Etham, they camped in Mara.

text.NRSV: They set out from Marah and came to Elim; at Elim there were twelve springs of water and seventy palm trees, and they camped there."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Marah, and came in towards Elim; there being, in Elim, twelve fountains of water, and seventy palm-trees, so they encamped there.
text.KJV: And they removed from Marah, and came unto Elim: and in Elim were twelve fountains of water, and threescore and ten palm trees; and they pitched there.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Marah, they came to Elim; at Elim there were twelve springs of water and seventy palm trees, and they camped there.
text.DRB: And departing from Mara, they came into Elim, where there were the twelve fountains of waters, and the seventy palm trees: and there they camped.

text.NRSV: They set out from Elim and camped by the Red Sea."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Elim,—and encamped by the Red Sea.
text.KJV: And they removed from Elim, and encamped by the Red sea.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Elim, they camped beside the Red Sea.
text.DRB: But departing thence also, they pitched their tents upon the Red sea. And departing from the Red sea,

text.NRSV: They set out from the Red Sea and camped in the wilderness of Sin."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from the Red Sea,—and encamped in the desert of Sin.
text.KJV: And they removed from the Red sea, and encamped in the wilderness of Sin.
text.NABRE: Setting out from the Red Sea, they camped in the wilderness of Sin.
text.DRB: they camped in the desert Sin.

text.NRSV: They set out from the wilderness of Sin and camped at Dophkah."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from the desert of Sin,—and encamped in Dophkah.
text.KJV: And they took their journey out of the wilderness of Sin, and encamped in Dophkah.
text.NABRE: Setting out from the wilderness of Sin, they camped at Dophkah.
text.DRB: From whence departing, they came into Daphca.

text.NRSV: They set out from Dophkah and camped at Alush."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Dophkah,—and encamped in Alush.
text.KJV: And they departed from Dophkah, and encamped in Alush.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Dophkah, they camped at Alush.
text.DRB: And departing from Daphca, they camped in Alus.

text.NRSV: They set out from Alush and camped at Rephidim, where there was no water for the people to drink."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Alush,—and encamped in Rephidim, where was no water for the people to drink.
text.KJV: And they removed from Alush, and encamped at Rephidim, where was no water for the people to drink.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Alush, they camped at Rephidim, where there was no water for the people to drink.
text.DRB: And departing from Alus, they pitched their tents in Raphidim, where the people wanted water to drink.

text.NRSV: They set out from Rephidim and camped in the wilderness of Sinai."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Rephidim,—and encamped in the desert of Sinai.
text.KJV: And they departed from Rephidim, and pitched in the wilderness of Sinai.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Rephidim, they camped in the wilderness of Sinai.
text.DRB: And departing from Raphidim, they camped in the desert of Sinai.

text.NRSV: They set out from the wilderness of Sinai and camped at Kibroth-hattaavah."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from the desert of Sinai,—and encamped in Kibroth-hattaavah.
text.KJV: And they removed from the desert of Sinai, and pitched at Kibroth–hattaavah.
text.NABRE: From Sinai to Kadesh. Setting out from the wilderness of Sinai, they camped at Kibroth-hattaavah.
text.DRB: But departing also from the desert Sinai, they came to the Sepulchres of concupiscence.

text.NRSV: They set out from Kibroth-hattaavah and camped at Hazeroth."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Kibroth-hattaavah,—and encamped in Hazeroth.
text.KJV: And they departed from Kibroth–hattaavah, and encamped at Hazeroth.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Kibroth-hattaavah, they camped at Hazeroth.
text.DRB: And departing from the Sepulchres of concupiscence, they camped in Haseroth.

text.NRSV: They set out from Hazeroth and camped at Rithmah."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Hazeroth,—and encamped in Rithmah.
text.KJV: And they departed from Hazeroth, and pitched in Rithmah.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Hazeroth, they camped at Rithmah.
text.DRB: And from Haseroth they came into Rethma.

text.NRSV: They set out from Rithmah and camped at Rimmon-perez."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Rithmah,—and encamped in Rimmon-perez.
text.KJV: And they departed from Rithmah, and pitched at Rimmon–parez.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Rithmah, they camped at Rimmon-perez.
text.DRB: And departing from Rethma, they camped in Remmonphares.

text.NRSV: They set out from Rimmon-perez and camped at Libnah."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Rimmon-perez,—and encamped in Libnah.
text.KJV: And they departed from Rimmon–parez, and pitched in Libnah.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Rimmon-perez, they camped at Libnah.
text.DRB: Whence departing they came into Lebna.

text.NRSV: They set out from Libnah and camped at Rissah."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Libnah,—and encamped in Rissah.
text.KJV: And they removed from Libnah, and pitched at Rissah.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Libnah, they camped at Rissah.
text.DRB: From Lebna they camped in Ressa.

text.NRSV: They set out from Rissah and camped at Kehelathah."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Rissah,—and encamped in Kehelathah.
text.KJV: And they journeyed from Rissah, and pitched in Kehelathah.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Rissah, they camped at Kehelathah.
text.DRB: And departing from Ressa, they came into Ceelatha.

text.NRSV: They set out from Kehelathah and camped at Mount Shepher."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Kehelathah,—and encamped in Mount Shepher.
text.KJV: And they went from Kehelathah, and pitched in mount Shapher.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Kehelathah, they camped at Mount Shepher.
text.DRB: Whence departing, they camped in the mountain Sepher.

text.NRSV: They set out from Mount Shepher and camped at Haradah."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Mount Shepher,—and encamped in Haradah.
text.KJV: And they removed from mount Shapher, and encamped in Haradah.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Mount Shepher, they camped at Haradah.
text.DRB: Departing from the mountain Sepher, they came into Harada.

text.NRSV: They set out from Haradah and camped at Makheloth."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Haradah,—and encamped in Makheloth.
text.KJV: And they removed from Haradah, and pitched in Makheloth.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Haradah, they camped at Makheloth.
text.DRB: Thence departing, they camped in Maceloth.

text.NRSV: They set out from Makheloth and camped at Tahath."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Makheloth,—and encamped in Tahath.
text.KJV: And they removed from Makheloth, and encamped at Tahath.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Makheloth, they camped at Tahath.
text.DRB: And departing from Maceloth, they came into Thahath.

text.NRSV: They set out from Tahath and camped at Terah."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Tahath,—and encamped in Terah.
text.KJV: And they departed from Tahath, and pitched at Tarah.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Tahath, they camped at Terah.
text.DRB: From Thahath they camped in Thare.

text.NRSV: They set out from Terah and camped at Mithkah."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Terah,—and encamped in Mithkah.
text.KJV: And they removed from Tarah, and pitched in Mithcah.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Terah, they camped at Mithkah.
text.DRB: Whence departing, they pitched their tents in Methca.

text.NRSV: They set out from Mithkah and camped at Hashmonah."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Mithkah,—and encamped in Hashmonah.
text.KJV: And they went from Mithcah, and pitched in Hashmonah.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Mithkah, they camped at Hashmonah.
text.DRB: And from Methca they camped in Hesmona.

text.NRSV: They set out from Hashmonah and camped at Moseroth."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Hashmonah,—and encamped in Moseroth.
text.KJV: And they departed from Hashmonah, and encamped at Moseroth.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Hashmonah, they camped at Moseroth.
text.DRB: And departing from Hesmona, they came into Moseroth.

text.NRSV: They set out from Moseroth and camped at Bene-jaakan."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Moseroth, and encamped in Bene-jaakan.
text.KJV: And they departed from Moseroth, and pitched in Bene–jaakan.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Moseroth, they camped at Bene-jaakan.
text.DRB: And from Moseroth they camped in Benejaacan.

text.NRSV: They set out from Bene-jaakan and camped at Hor-haggidgad."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Bene-jaakan, and encamped in Hor-haggidgad.
text.KJV: And they removed from Bene–jaakan, and encamped at Hor–hagidgad.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Bene-jaakan, they camped at Mount Gidgad.
text.DRB: And departing from Benejaacan, they came into the mount Gadgad.

text.NRSV: They set out from Hor-haggidgad and camped at Jotbathah."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Hor-haggidgad,—and encamped in Jotbathah,—
text.KJV: And they went from Hor–hagidgad, and pitched in Jotbathah.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Mount Gidgad, they camped at Jotbathah.
text.DRB: Whence departing, they camped in Jetebatha.

text.NRSV: They set out from Jotbathah and camped at Abronah."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Jotbathah,—and encamped in Abronah.
text.KJV: And they removed from Jotbathah, and encamped at Ebronah.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Jotbathah, they camped at Abronah.
text.DRB: And from Jetebatha they came into Hebrona.

text.NRSV: They set out from Abronah and camped at Ezion-geber."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Abronah,—and encamped in Eziongeber.
text.KJV: And they departed from Ebronah, and encamped at Ezion–gaber.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Abronah, they camped at Ezion-geber.
text.DRB: And departing from Hebrona, they camped in Asiongaber.

text.NRSV: They set out from Ezion-geber and camped in the wilderness of Zin (that is, Kadesh)."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Eziongeber,—and encamped in the desert of Zin, the same, is Kadesh.
text.KJV: And they removed from Ezion–gaber, and pitched in the wilderness of Zin, which is Kadesh.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Ezion-geber, they camped in the wilderness of Zin, that is, Kadesh.
text.DRB: Thence departing, they came into the desert Sin, this is Cades.

text.NRSV: They set out from Kadesh and camped at Mount Hor, on the edge of the land of Edom."
text.Rotherham: And they brake, up from Kadesh,—and encamped in Mount Hor, on the outskirts of the land of Edom;
text.KJV: And they removed from Kadesh, and pitched in mount Hor, in the edge of the land of Edom.
text.NABRE: From Kadesh to the Plains of Moab. Setting out from Kadesh, they camped at Mount Hor on the border of the land of Edom.
text.DRB: And departing from Cades, they camped in the mount Hor, in the uttermost borders of the Land of Edom.
