text.NRSV: Aaron the priest went up Mount Hor at the command of the Lord and died there in the fortieth year after the Israelites had come out of the land of Egypt, on the first day of the fifth month."
text.Rotherham: and Aaron the priest went up into Mount Hor, at the bidding of Yahweh, and died there, in the fortieth year, by the coming forth of the sons of Israel out of the land of Egypt, in the fifth month, on the first of the month.
text.KJV: And Aaron the priest went up into mount Hor at the commandment of the Lord, and died there, in the fortieth year after the children of Israel were come out of the land of Egypt, in the first day of the fifth month.
text.NABRE: Aaron the priest ascended Mount Hor at the Lord ’s direction, and there he died in the fortieth year after the departure of the Israelites from the land of Egypt, on the first day of the fifth month.
text.DRB: And Aaron the Priest went up into the mountain Hor at the commandment of our Lord: and there he died the fortieth year of the coming forth of the children of Israel out of Ægypt, the fifth month, the first day of the month,

text.NRSV: Aaron was one hundred twenty-three years old when he died on Mount Hor."
text.Rotherham: Now, Aaron, was a hundred and twenty-three years old, when he died in Mount Hor.
text.KJV: And Aaron was an hundred and twenty and three years old when he died in mount Hor.
text.NABRE: Aaron was a hundred and twenty-three years old when he died on Mount Hor.
text.DRB: when he was an hundred three and twenty years old.

text.NRSV: The Canaanite, the king of Arad, who lived in the Negeb in the land of Canaan, heard of the coming of the Israelites."
text.Rotherham: And a Canaanite king of Arad, who was dwelling in the South, in the land of Canaan, heard of the coming in of the sons of Israel.
text.KJV: And king Arad the Canaanite, which dwelt in the south in the land of Canaan, heard of the coming of the children of Israel.
text.NABRE: When the Canaanite, the king of Arad, who ruled over the Negeb in the land of Canaan, heard that the Israelites were coming….
text.DRB: And the Chananite king of Arad, who dwelt toward the south, heard that the children of Israel were come into the Land of Chanaan.

text.NRSV: They set out from Mount Hor and camped at Zalmonah."
text.Rotherham: And, they brake up from Mount Hor,—and encamped in Zalmonah.
text.KJV: And they departed from mount Hor, and pitched in Zalmonah.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Mount Hor, they camped at Zalmonah.
text.DRB: And departing from the mountain Hor, they camped in Salmona.

text.NRSV: They set out from Zalmonah and camped at Punon."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Zalmonah,—and encamped in Punon.
text.KJV: And they departed from Zalmonah, and pitched in Punon.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Zalmonah, they camped at Punon.
text.DRB: Whence departing, they came into Phunon.

text.NRSV: They set out from Punon and camped at Oboth."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Punon, and encamped in Oboth.
text.KJV: And they departed from Punon, and pitched in Oboth.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Punon, they camped at Oboth.
text.DRB: And departing from Phunon, they camped in Oboth.

text.NRSV: They set out from Oboth and camped at Iye-abarim, in the territory of Moab."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Oboth,—and encamped in Iye-abarim, within the bounds of Moab.
text.KJV: And they departed from Oboth, and pitched in Ije–abarim, in the border of Moab.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Oboth, they camped at Iye-abarim on the border of Moab.
text.DRB: And from Oboth they came into Ijeabarim, which is in the borders of the Moabites.

text.NRSV: They set out from Iyim and camped at Dibon-gad."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Iyim,—and encamped in Dibon-gad.
text.KJV: And they departed from Iim, and pitched in Dibon–gad.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Iye-abarim, they camped at Dibon-gad.
text.DRB: And departing from Ijeabarim they pitched their tents in Dibongad.

text.NRSV: They set out from Dibon-gad and camped at Almon-diblathaim."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Dibon-gad, and encamped in Almon-diblathaim.
text.KJV: And they removed from Dibon–gad, and encamped in Almon–diblathaim.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Dibon-gad, they camped at Almon-diblathaim.
text.DRB: Whence departing, they camped in Helmondeblathaim.

text.NRSV: They set out from Almon-diblathaim and camped in the mountains of Abarim, before Nebo."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Almon-diblathaim,—and encamped among the mountains of Abarim, before Nebo.
text.KJV: And they removed from Almon–diblathaim, and pitched in the mountains of Abarim, before Nebo.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Almon-diblathaim, they camped in the Abarim range opposite Nebo.
text.DRB: And departing from Helmondeblathaim, they came to the mountains Abarim against Nabo.

text.NRSV: They set out from the mountains of Abarim and camped in the plains of Moab by the Jordan at Jericho;"
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from the mountains of Abarim,—and encamped in the waste plains of Moab, by Jordan near Jericho.
text.KJV: And they departed from the mountains of Abarim, and pitched in the plains of Moab by Jordan near Jericho.
text.NABRE: Setting out from the Abarim range, they camped on the plains of Moab by the Jordan opposite Jericho.
text.DRB: And departing from the mountains Abarim, they passed to the champion countries of Moab, upon Jordan, against Jericho.

text.NRSV: they camped by the Jordan from Beth-jeshimoth as far as Abel-shittim in the plains of Moab."
text.Rotherham: Thus did they encamp by the Jordan from Beth-jeshimoth unto Abel-Shittim [that is, "The acacia—meadows"] —in the waste plains of Moab.
text.KJV: And they pitched by Jordan, from Beth–jesimoth even unto Abel–shittim in the plains of Moab.
text.NABRE: They camped by the Jordan on the plains of Moab extended from Beth-jeshimoth to Abel-shittim.
text.DRB: And there they camped from Bethsimoth unto Abelsatim in the plainer places of the Moabites,

text.NRSV: In the plains of Moab by the Jordan at Jericho, the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:"
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, in the waste plains of Moab, by Jordan near Jericho saying:
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses in the plains of Moab by Jordan near Jericho, saying,
text.NABRE: Conquest and Division of Canaan. The Lord spoke to Moses on the plains of Moab by the Jordan opposite Jericho:
text.DRB: where our Lord spake to Moyses:

text.NRSV: Speak to the Israelites, and say to them: When you cross over the Jordan into the land of Canaan,"
text.Rotherham: Speak unto the sons of Israel, and thou shalt say unto them,—When ye do pass over the Jordan into the land of Canaan,
text.KJV: Speak unto the children of Israel, and say unto them, When ye are passed over Jordan into the land of Canaan;
text.NABRE: Speak to the Israelites and say to them: When you go across the Jordan into the land of Canaan,
text.DRB: Command the children of Israel, and say to them: When you shall have passed Jordan, entering the Land of Chanaan,

text.NRSV: you shall drive out all the inhabitants of the land from before you, destroy all their figured stones, destroy all their cast images, and demolish all their high places."
text.Rotherham: then shall ye dispossess all the inhabitants of the land from before you, and shall destroy all their figured stones,—all their molten images, also shall ye destroy, and all their high places, shall ye lay waste.
text.KJV: Then ye shall drive out all the inhabitants of the land from before you, and destroy all their pictures, and destroy all their molten images, and quite pluck down all their high places:
text.NABRE: dispossess all the inhabitants of the land before you; destroy all their stone figures, destroy all their molten images, and demolish all their high places.
text.DRB: destroy all the inhabitants of that Land: break their titles, and burst to fitters their statues, and waste all their excelses,

text.NRSV: You shall take possession of the land and settle in it, for I have given you the land to possess."
text.Rotherham: So shall ye possess the land, and settle down therein,—for unto you, have I given the land to possess it.
text.KJV: And ye shall dispossess the inhabitants of the land, and dwell therein: for I have given you the land to possess it.
text.NABRE: You will take possession of the land and settle in it, for I have given you the land to possess.
text.DRB: cleansing the Land, and dwelling in it. For I have given it you in possession,

text.NRSV: You shall apportion the land by lot according to your clans; to a large one you shall give a large inheritance, and to a small one you shall give a small inheritance; the inheritance shall belong to the person on whom the lot falls; according to your ancestral tribes you shall inherit."
text.Rotherham: And ye shall take your inheritance in the land by lot by your families—for the large one, ye shall make large his inheritance, and for the small one, make small his inheritance, whithersoever the lot cometh out to him, his, shall, it be, by the tribes of your fathers, shall ye take your inheritance.
text.KJV: And ye shall divide the land by lot for an inheritance among your families: and to the more ye shall give the more inheritance, and to the fewer ye shall give the less inheritance: every man’s inheritance shall be in the place where his lot falleth; according to the tribes of your fathers ye shall inherit.
text.NABRE: You will apportion the land among yourselves by lot, clan by clan, assigning a large heritage to a large clan and a small heritage to a small clan. Wherever anyone’s lot falls, there will his possession be; you will apportion these shares within your ancestral tribe.
text.DRB: which you shall divide among you by lot. To the more you shall give the larger, and to the fewer the straiter. To every one as the lot shall fall, so shall the inheritance be given. By the tribes and the families the possession shall be divided.

text.NRSV: But if you do not drive out the inhabitants of the land from before you, then those whom you let remain shall be as barbs in your eyes and thorns in your sides; they shall trouble you in the land where you are settling."
text.Rotherham: But if ye do not dispossess the inhabitants of the land from before you, then shall it be. that they whom ye leave remaining of them will become pricks in your eyes, and thorns in your sides, and will harass you, concerning the land, wherein, ye are settling down.
text.KJV: But if ye will not drive out the inhabitants of the land from before you; then it shall come to pass, that those which ye let remain of them shall be pricks in your eyes, and thorns in your sides, and shall vex you in the land wherein ye dwell.
text.NABRE: But if you do not dispossess the inhabitants of the land before you, those whom you allow to remain will become barbs in your eyes and thorns in your sides, and they will harass you in the land where you live,
text.DRB: But if you will not kill the inhabitants of the Land: they that remain, shall be unto you as it were nails in your eyes, and spears in your sides, and they shall be your adversaries in the Land of your habitation:

text.NRSV: And I will do to you as I thought to do to them.
text.Rotherham: And it shall be, that as I thought to do unto them, I will do unto you.
text.KJV: Moreover it shall come to pass, that I shall do unto you, as I thought to do unto them.
text.NABRE: and I will treat you as I had intended to treat them.
text.DRB: and whatsoever I had thought to do to them, I will do to you.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: The Lord spoke to Moses:
text.DRB: And our Lord spake to Moyses, saying:

text.NRSV: Command the Israelites, and say to them: When you enter the land of Canaan (this is the land that shall fall to you for an inheritance, the land of Canaan defined by its boundaries),"
text.Rotherham: Command the sons of Israel, and thou shalt say unto them, When, ye, are coming into the land of Canaan, this is the land which shall fall unto you, as an inheritance, even the land of Canaan, by the boundaries thereof.
text.KJV: Command the children of Israel, and say unto them, When ye come into the land of Canaan; (this is the land that shall fall unto you for an inheritance, even the land of Canaan with the coasts thereof:)
text.NABRE: Give the Israelites this order: When you enter the land of Canaan, this is the territory that shall fall to you as your heritage—the land of Canaan with its boundaries:
text.DRB: Command the children of Israel, and thou shalt say to them: When you are entered into the Land of Chanaan, and it shall be fallen unto you by lot in possession, it shall be limited by these borders:

text.NRSV: your south sector shall extend from the wilderness of Zin along the side of Edom. Your southern boundary shall begin from the end of the Dead Sea on the east;"
text.Rotherham: Ye shall therefore have a south corner, from the desert of Zin, on the side of Edom,—so shall ye have a south boundary, from the end of the salt sea, eastward:
text.KJV: Then your south quarter shall be from the wilderness of Zin along by the coast of Edom, and your south border shall be the outmost coast of the salt sea eastward:
text.NABRE: Your southern boundary will be at the wilderness of Zin along the border of Edom; on the east your southern boundary will begin at the end of the Salt Sea.
text.DRB: The southside shall begin from the wilderness Sin, which is beside Edom: and it shall have toward the East the most salt sea for the limits.

text.NRSV: your boundary shall turn south of the ascent of Akrabbim, and cross to Zin, and its outer limit shall be south of Kadesh-barnea; then it shall go on to Hazar-addar, and cross to Azmon;"
text.Rotherham: and the boundary shall go round for you from the south towards the cliffs of Akrabbim, then cross over towards Zin, and the extension thereof shall be from the south to Kadesh-barnea, then shall it reach out to Hazar-addar, and cross over towards Azmon;
text.KJV: And your border shall turn from the south to the ascent of Akrabbim, and pass on to Zin: and the going forth thereof shall be from the south to Kadesh–barnea, and shall go on to Hazar–addar, and pass on to Azmon:
text.NABRE: Then your boundary will turn south of the Akrabbim Pass and cross Zin. Terminating south of Kadesh-barnea, it extends to Hazar-addar and crosses to Azmon.
text.DRB: The which shall compass the south side by the ascending of the Scorpion, so that they shall pass into Senna, and reach toward the South as far as Cadesbarne, from whence the frontiers shall go forth to the town named Adar, and shall reach as far as Asemona.

text.NRSV: the boundary shall turn from Azmon to the Wadi of Egypt, and its termination shall be at the Sea."
text.Rotherham: then shall the boundary turn round from Azmon towards the ravine of Egypt,—and the extension thereof shall be towards the sea.
text.KJV: And the border shall fetch a compass from Azmon unto the river of Egypt, and the goings out of it shall be at the sea.
text.NABRE: Then the boundary will turn from Azmon to the Wadi of Egypt and terminate at the Sea.
text.DRB: And the border shall go round about from Asemona to the Torrent of Ægypt, and shall end in the shore of the great sea.

text.NRSV: For the western boundary, you shall have the Great Sea and its coast; this shall be your western boundary."
text.Rotherham: And, for a west boundary,—ye shall have the great sea, even a boundary, this, shall serve you as west boundary.
text.KJV: And as for the western border, ye shall even have the great sea for a border: this shall be your west border.
text.NABRE: For your western boundary you will have the Great Sea with its coast; this will be your western boundary.
text.DRB: And the west side shall begin from the great sea, and the same shall be the limit thereof.

text.NRSV: This shall be your northern boundary: from the Great Sea you shall mark out your line to Mount Hor;"
text.Rotherham: And, this, shall serve you as a north boundary,—From the great sea, ye shall draw a line for you to Mount Hor:
text.KJV: And this shall be your north border: from the great sea ye shall point out for you mount Hor:
text.NABRE: This will be your boundary on the north: from the Great Sea you will draw a line to Mount Hor,
text.DRB: Moreover toward the North part the borders shall begin from the great sea, reaching unto the most high mountain,

text.NRSV: from Mount Hor you shall mark it out to Lebo-hamath, and the outer limit of the boundary shall be at Zedad;"
text.Rotherham: From Mount Hor, ye shall draw a line to the entering in of Hamath,—and the extension of the boundary shall be towards Zedad:
text.KJV: From mount Hor ye shall point out your border unto the entrance of Hamath; and the goings forth of the border shall be to Zedad:
text.NABRE: and draw it from Mount Hor to Lebo-hamath, with the boundary terminating at Zedad.
text.DRB: from the which they shall come unto Emath as far as the borders of Sedada:

text.NRSV: then the boundary shall extend to Ziphron, and its end shall be at Hazar-enan; this shall be your northern boundary."
text.Rotherham: then shall the boundary reach out towards Ziphron, and the extension thereof, be to Hazar-enan. This, shall serve you as a north, boundary.
text.KJV: And the border shall go on to Ziphron, and the goings out of it shall be at Hazar–enan: this shall be your north border.
text.NABRE: Then the boundary extends to Ziphron and terminates at Hazar-enan. This will be your northern boundary.
text.DRB: and the frontiers shall go as far as Sephorna, and the town Enan. These shall be the borders in the North part.

text.NRSV: You shall mark out your eastern boundary from Hazar-enan to Shepham;"
text.Rotherham: Then shall ye draw for yourselves a line, for an east boundary,—from Hazar-enan towards Shepham;
text.KJV: And ye shall point out your east border from Hazar–enan to Shepham:
text.NABRE: For your eastern boundary you will draw a line from Hazar-enan to Shepham.
text.DRB: From thence they shall mark out the bounds toward the East side from the Village Enan unto Sephama,

text.NRSV: and the boundary shall continue down from Shepham to Riblah on the east side of Ain; and the boundary shall go down, and reach the eastern slope of the sea of Chinnereth;"
text.Rotherham: and the boundary shall go down from Shepham towards Riblah, on the east of Ain—then shall the boundary go down and strike on the side of the Sea of Chinnereth, eastward:
text.KJV: And the coast shall go down from Shepham to Riblah, on the east side of Ain; and the border shall descend, and shall reach unto the side of the sea of Chinnereth eastward:
text.NABRE: From Shepham the boundary will go down to Riblah, east of Ain, and descending further, the boundary will strike the ridge on the east side of the Sea of Chinnereth;
text.DRB: and from Sephama the bounds shall go down unto Rebla against the fountain Daphnim: from thence they shall come eastward to the sea Cenereth,

text.NRSV: and the boundary shall go down to the Jordan, and its end shall be at the Dead Sea. This shall be your land with its boundaries all around."
text.Rotherham: then shall the boundary go down towards the Jordan, and the extension thereof be to the salt sea. This shall be your land by the boundaries thereof round about.
text.KJV: And the border shall go down to Jordan, and the goings out of it shall be at the salt sea: this shall be your land with the coasts thereof round about.
text.NABRE: then the boundary will descend along the Jordan and terminate with the Salt Sea. This will be your land, with the boundaries that surround it.
text.DRB: and shall reach as far as Jordan, and at the last shall be closed in by the most salt sea. This you shall have for your Land by the bounds thereof round about.

text.NRSV: Moses commanded the Israelites, saying: This is the land that you shall inherit by lot, which the Lord has commanded to give to the nine tribes and to the half-tribe;"
text.Rotherham: And Moses commanded the sons of Israel saying,—This, is the land, which ye shall inherit by lot, which Yahweh had commanded to be given to the nine tribes and the half tribe.
text.KJV: And Moses commanded the children of Israel, saying, This is the land which ye shall inherit by lot, which the Lord commanded to give unto the nine tribes, and to the half tribe:
text.NABRE: Moses also gave this order to the Israelites: “This is the land, to be apportioned among you by lot, which the Lord has commanded to be given to the nine and a half tribes.
text.DRB: And Moyses commanded the children of Israel, saying: This shall be the Land, which you shall possess by lot, and which our Lord hath commanded to be given to the nine tribes, and to the half tribe.

text.NRSV: for the tribe of the Reubenites by their ancestral houses and the tribe of the Gadites by their ancestral houses have taken their inheritance, and also the half-tribe of Manasseh;"
text.Rotherham: For the tribe of the sons of the Reubenites by their ancestral house, and the tribe of the sons of Gad, by their ancestral house, have received, and, the half tribe of Manasseh have received, their inheritance:
text.KJV: For the tribe of the children of Reuben according to the house of their fathers, and the tribe of the children of Gad according to the house of their fathers, have received their inheritance; and half the tribe of Manasseh have received their inheritance:
text.NABRE: For the tribe of the Reubenites according to their ancestral houses, and the tribe of the Gadites according to their ancestral houses, as well as half of the tribe of Manasseh, have already received their heritage;
text.DRB: For the tribe of the children of Ruben by their families, and the tribe of the children of Gad according to the number of their kindreds, the half also of the tribe of Manasses,

text.NRSV: the two tribes and the half-tribe have taken their inheritance beyond the Jordan at Jericho eastward, toward the sunrise."
text.Rotherham: the two tribes, and the half tribe, have received their inheritance—on this side Jordan near Jericho, eastwards, towards sunrise.
text.KJV: The two tribes and the half tribe have received their inheritance on this side Jordan near Jericho eastward, toward the sunrising.
text.NABRE: these two and a half tribes have received their heritage across the Jordan opposite Jericho, in the east, toward the sunrise.”
text.DRB: that is, two tribes and an half, have taken their portion beyond Jordan against Jericho at the east side.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: Supervisors of the Allotment. The Lord spoke to Moses:
text.DRB: And our Lord said to Moyses:

text.NRSV: These are the names of the men who shall apportion the land to you for inheritance: the priest Eleazar and Joshua son of Nun."
text.Rotherham: These, are the names of the men who shall receive for you the land, as an inheritance, Eleazar, the priest, and Joshua, son of Nun.
text.KJV: These are the names of the men which shall divide the land unto you: Eleazar the priest, and Joshua the son of Nun.
text.NABRE: These are the names of the men who shall apportion the land among you: Eleazar the priest, and Joshua, son of Nun;
text.DRB: These are the names of the men, that shall divide the Land unto you: Eleazar the Priest, and Josue the son of Nun,

text.NRSV: You shall take one leader of every tribe to apportion the land for inheritance."
text.Rotherham: Also, one prince from each tribe, shall ye take to receive the land, as an inheritance.
text.KJV: And ye shall take one prince of every tribe, to divide the land by inheritance.
text.NABRE: and you will designate one leader from each of the tribes to apportion the land.
text.DRB: and one Prince of every tribe,

text.NRSV: These are the names of the men: Of the tribe of Judah, Caleb son of Jephunneh."
text.Rotherham: These, therefore, are the names of the men,—For the tribe of Judah, Caleb son of Jephunneh;
text.KJV: And the names of the men are these: Of the tribe of Judah, Caleb the son of Jephunneh.
text.NABRE: These are the names of the men: from the tribe of Judah: Caleb, son of Jephunneh,
text.DRB: whose names are these: Of the tribe of Juda, Caleb son of Jephone.

text.NRSV: Of the tribe of the Simeonites, Shemuel son of Ammihud."
text.Rotherham: And, for the tribe of the sons of Simeon, Shemuel son of Ammihud:
text.KJV: And of the tribe of the children of Simeon, Shemuel the son of Ammihud.
text.NABRE: from the tribe of the Simeonites: Samuel, son of Ammihud;
text.DRB: Of the tribe of Simeon, Samuel the son of Ammiud.

text.NRSV: Of the tribe of Benjamin, Elidad son of Chislon."
text.Rotherham: For the tribe of Benjamin, Elidad son of Chislon;
text.KJV: Of the tribe of Benjamin, Elidad the son of Chislon.
text.NABRE: from the tribe of Benjamin: Elidad, son of Chislon;
text.DRB: Of the tribe of Benjamin Elidad the son of Chaselon.

text.NRSV: Of the tribe of the Danites a leader, Bukki son of Jogli."
text.Rotherham: And, for the tribe of the sons of Dan, a prince—Bukki, son of Jogli:
text.KJV: And the prince of the tribe of the children of Dan, Bukki the son of Jogli.
text.NABRE: from the tribe of the Danites: a leader, Bukki, son of Jogli;
text.DRB: Of the tribe of the children of Dan, Bocci the son of Iogli.

text.NRSV: Of the Josephites: of the tribe of the Manassites a leader, Hanniel son of Ephod,"
text.Rotherham: For the sons of Joseph, For the tribe of the sons of Manasseh, a prince,—Hanniel son of Ephod;
text.KJV: The prince of the children of Joseph, for the tribe of the children of Manasseh, Hanniel the son of Ephod.
text.NABRE: for the descendants of Joseph: from the tribe of the Manassites: a leader, Hanniel, son of Ephod; and
text.DRB: Of the children of Joseph of the tribe of Manasses, Hanniel the son of Ephod.

text.NRSV: and of the tribe of the Ephraimites a leader, Kemuel son of Shiphtan."
text.Rotherham: And, for the tribe of the sons of Ephraim, a prince,—Kemuel, son of Shiphtan;
text.KJV: And the prince of the tribe of the children of Ephraim, Kemuel the son of Shiphtan.
text.NABRE: from the tribe of the Ephraimites: a leader, Kemuel, son of Shiphtan;
text.DRB: Of the tribe of Ephraim, Camuel the son of Sephthan.

text.NRSV: Of the tribe of the Zebulunites a leader, Eli-zaphan son of Parnach."
text.Rotherham: And, for the tribe of the sons of Zebulun, a prince,—Elizaphan, son of Parnach;
text.KJV: And the prince of the tribe of the children of Zebulun, Elizaphan the son of Parnach.
text.NABRE: from the tribe of the Zebulunites: a leader, Elizaphan, son of Parnach;
text.DRB: Of the tribe of Zabulon, Elisaphan the son of Pharnach.

text.NRSV: Of the tribe of the Issacharites a leader, Paltiel son of Azzan."
text.Rotherham: And, for the tribe of the sons of Issachar, a prince,—Paltiel son of Azzan;
text.KJV: And the prince of the tribe of the children of Issachar, Paltiel the son of Azzan.
text.NABRE: from the tribe of the Issacharites: a leader, Paltiel, son of Azzan;
text.DRB: Of the tribe of Issachar, Duke Phaltiel the son of Ozan.

text.NRSV: Of the tribe of the Asherites a leader, Ahihud son of Shelomi."
text.Rotherham: And, for the tribe of the sons of Asher, a prince,—Ahihud son of Shelomi:
text.KJV: And the prince of the tribe of the children of Asher, Ahihud the son of Shelomi.
text.NABRE: from the tribe of the Asherites: a leader, Ahihud, son of Shelomi;
text.DRB: Of the tribe of Aser, Ahiud the son of Salomi.

text.NRSV: Of the tribe of the Naphtalites a leader, Pedahel son of Ammihud."
text.Rotherham: And, for the tribe of the sons of Naphtali, a prince,—Pedahel, son of Ammihud.
text.KJV: And the prince of the tribe of the children of Naphtali, Pedahel the son of Ammihud.
text.NABRE: from the tribe of the Naphtalites: a leader, Pedahel, son of Ammihud.
text.DRB: Of the tribe of Nephthali: Phedael the son of Ammiud.

text.NRSV: These were the ones whom the Lord commanded to apportion the inheritance for the Israelites in the land of Canaan.
text.Rotherham: These, are they whom Yahweh hath commanded to receive for the sons of Israel their inheritance, in the land of Canaan.
text.KJV: These are they whom the Lord commanded to divide the inheritance unto the children of Israel in the land of Canaan.
text.NABRE: These are the ones whom the Lord commanded to apportion to the Israelites their heritage in the land of Canaan.
text.DRB: These are they whom our Lord commanded to divide the Land of Chanaan to the children of Israel.

text.NRSV: In the plains of Moab by the Jordan at Jericho, the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:"
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses in the waste plains of Moab,—by Jordan, near Jericho, saying:
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses in the plains of Moab by Jordan near Jericho, saying,
text.NABRE: The Lord spoke to Moses on the plains of Moab by the Jordan opposite Jericho:
text.DRB: These things also spake our Lord to Moyses in the champion countries of Moab upon Jordan, against Jericho:

text.NRSV: Command the Israelites to give, from the inheritance that they possess, towns for the Levites to live in; you shall also give to the Levites pasture lands surrounding the towns."
text.Rotherham: Command the sons of Israel, that they give unto the Levites—out of the inheritance which they possess—cities to dwell in,—pasture land also unto the cities, round about them, shall ye give unto the Levites.
text.KJV: Command the children of Israel, that they give unto the Levites of the inheritance of their possession cities to dwell in; and ye shall give also unto the Levites suburbs for the cities round about them.
text.NABRE: Command the Israelites out of the heritage they possess to give the Levites cities to dwell in; you will also give the Levites the pasture lands around the cities.
text.DRB: Command the children of Israel that they give unto the Levites of their possessions

text.NRSV: The towns shall be theirs to live in, and their pasture lands shall be for their cattle and for their livestock and for all their animals."
text.Rotherham: So shall the cities be theirs, to dwell in,—and their pasture lands, shall, be for their cattle and for their goods and for all their beasts.
text.KJV: And the cities shall they have to dwell in; and the suburbs of them shall be for their cattle, and for their goods, and for all their beasts.
text.NABRE: The cities will be for them to dwell in, and the pasture lands will be for their cattle, their flocks, and all their other animals.
text.DRB: cities to dwell in, and their suburbs round about: that themselves may abide in the towns, and the suburbs may be for their cattle and beasts.

text.NRSV: The pasture lands of the towns, which you shall give to the Levites, shall reach from the wall of the town outward a thousand cubits all around."
text.Rotherham: And, the pasture lands of the cities which ye shall give unto the Levite, shall be,—from the wall of the city, and outwards, a thousand cubits round about
text.KJV: And the suburbs of the cities, which ye shall give unto the Levites, shall reach from the wall of the city and outward a thousand cubits round about.
text.NABRE: The pasture lands of the cities to be assigned the Levites shall extend a thousand cubits out from the city walls in every direction.
text.DRB: Which from the walls of the cities outward, round about, shall reach the space of a thousand paces.

text.NRSV: You shall measure, outside the town, for the east side two thousand cubits, for the south side two thousand cubits, for the west side two thousand cubits, and for the north side two thousand cubits, with the town in the middle. This shall belong to them as pasture land for their towns."
text.Rotherham: So ye shall measure—on the outside of the city—the eastward quarter two thousand by the cubit, and the south quarter two thousand by the cubit, and the west quarter two thousand by the cubit and the north quarter two thousand by the cubit, with, the city, in the midst. This, shall be unto them the pasture lands of the cities,
text.KJV: And ye shall measure from without the city on the east side two thousand cubits, and on the south side two thousand cubits, and on the west side two thousand cubits, and on the north side two thousand cubits; and the city shall be in the midst: this shall be to them the suburbs of the cities.
text.NABRE: You will measure out two thousand cubits outside the city along the east side, two thousand cubits along the south side, two thousand cubits along the west side, and two thousand cubits along the north side, with the city lying in the center. These will be the pasture lands of their cities.
text.DRB: Toward the East shall be two thousand cubits: and toward the South in like manner shall be two thousand: toward the sea also, which looketh to the West, shall be the same measure: and the North part shall be limited with equal bounds. And the cities shall be in the midst, and the suburbs without.

text.NRSV: The towns that you give to the Levites shall include the six cities of refuge, where you shall permit a slayer to flee, and in addition to them you shall give forty-two towns."
text.Rotherham: And, among the cities which ye shall give unto the Levites, shall be the six cities of refuge, which ye shall give that the manslayer may flee thither,—and besides them, ye shall give, forty-two cities,
text.KJV: And among the cities which ye shall give unto the Levites there shall be six cities for refuge, which ye shall appoint for the manslayer, that he may flee thither: and to them ye shall add forty and two cities.
text.NABRE: Now these are the cities you will give to the Levites: the six cities of asylum which you must establish for the homicide to run to, and in addition forty-two other cities—
text.DRB: And of the same towns, which you shall give to the Levites, six shall be separated for the aid of fugitives, that he may fly to them which hath shed blood: and beside these other forty two towns,

text.NRSV: The towns that you thus give shall be forty-eight in all, together with their pasture lands."
text.Rotherham: All the cities which ye shall give unto the Levites, shall be forty-eight cities,—them, and their pasture lands,
text.KJV: So all the cities which ye shall give to the Levites shall be forty and eight cities: them shall ye give with their suburbs.
text.NABRE: a total of forty-eight cities with their pasture lands which you will assign to the Levites.
text.DRB: that is, in all forty eight with their suburbs.

text.NRSV: And as for the towns that you shall give from the possession of the Israelites, from the larger tribes you shall take many, and from the smaller tribes you shall take few; each, in proportion to the inheritance that it obtains, shall give of its towns to the Levites."
text.Rotherham: And as touching the cities which ye shall give out of the possession of the sons of Israel from the many, shall ye take many, and from the few, shall ye take few,—each, according to his inheritance, which they shall inherit, shall give of his cities unto the Levites.
text.KJV: And the cities which ye shall give shall be of the possession of the children of Israel: from them that have many ye shall give many; but from them that have few ye shall give few: every one shall give of his cities unto the Levites according to his inheritance which he inheriteth.
text.NABRE: In assigning the cities from what the Israelites possess, take more from a larger group and fewer from a smaller one, so that each will cede cities to the Levites in proportion to the heritage which it receives.
text.DRB: And those cities, that shall be given of the possessions of the children of Israel, from them that have more, more shall be taken away: and that have less, fewer. Each shall give towns to the Levites according to the measure of their inheritance.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:"
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: Cities of Asylum. The Lord spoke to Moses:
text.DRB: Our Lord said to Moyses:

text.NRSV: Speak to the Israelites, and say to them: When you cross the Jordan into the land of Canaan,"
text.Rotherham: Speak unto the sons of Israel, and thou shalt say unto them,—When ye are passing over the Jordan unto the land of Canaan,
text.KJV: Speak unto the children of Israel, and say unto them, When ye be come over Jordan into the land of Canaan;
text.NABRE: Speak to the Israelites and say to them: When you go across the Jordan into the land of Canaan,
text.DRB: Speak to the children of Israel, and thou shalt say to them: When you shall have passed over Jordan into the Land of Chanaan,

text.NRSV: then you shall select cities to be cities of refuge for you, so that a slayer who kills a person without intent may flee there."
text.Rotherham: then shall ye find for you, convenient cities, cities of refuge, shall they be unto you,—and the manslayer, who hath slain a person, by mistake, shall flee thither.
text.KJV: Then ye shall appoint you cities to be cities of refuge for you; that the slayer may flee thither, which killeth any person at unawares.
text.NABRE: select for yourselves cities to serve as cities of asylum, where a homicide who has killed someone inadvertently may flee.
text.DRB: determine what cities shall be for the succour of fugitives, that have not voluntarily shed blood:

text.NRSV: The cities shall be for you a refuge from the avenger, so that the slayer may not die until there is a trial before the congregation."
text.Rotherham: So shall the cities serve you for places of refuge from the redeemer,—that the manslayer may not die, until he has stood before the assembly, for judgment,
text.KJV: And they shall be unto you cities for refuge from the avenger; that the manslayer die not, until he stand before the congregation in judgment.
text.NABRE: These cities will serve you as places of asylum from the avenger of blood, so that a homicide will not be put to death until tried before the community.
text.DRB: in which when the fugitive shall be, the kinsman of him that is killed cannot kill him, until he stand in the sight of the multitude, and his cause be judged.

text.NRSV: The cities that you designate shall be six cities of refuge for you:"
text.Rotherham: And as touching the cities which ye shall give six cities of refuge, shall there be unto you.
text.KJV: And of these cities which ye shall give six cities shall ye have for refuge.
text.NABRE: As for the cities you assign, you will have six cities of asylum:
text.DRB: And of those cities, that are separated for refuge of the fugitives,

text.NRSV: you shall designate three cities beyond the Jordan, and three cities in the land of Canaan, to be cities of refuge."
text.Rotherham: Three of the cities, shall ye give, on this side the Jordan, and three of the cities, shall ye give in the land of Canaan,—cities of refuge, shall they be.
text.KJV: Ye shall give three cities on this side Jordan, and three cities shall ye give in the land of Canaan, which shall be cities of refuge.
text.NABRE: you will designate three cities beyond the Jordan, and you will designate three cities in the land of Canaan. These will be cities of asylum.
text.DRB: three shall be beyond Jordan, and three in the Land of Chanaan,

text.NRSV: These six cities shall serve as refuge for the Israelites, for the resident or transient alien among them, so that anyone who kills a person without intent may flee there."
text.Rotherham: For the sons of Israel and for the sojourner. and for the settler in your midst, shall these six cities serve, as places of refuge,—that any one may flee thither who hath slain a person, by mistake.
text.KJV: These six cities shall be a refuge, both for the children of Israel, and for the stranger, and for the sojourner among them: that every one that killeth any person unawares may flee thither.
text.NABRE: These six cities will serve as places of asylum for the Israelites, and for the resident or transient aliens among them, so that anyone who has killed a person inadvertently may flee there.
text.DRB: as well for the children of Israel as for strangers and sojourners, that he may fly to them, which hath not voluntarily shed blood.

text.NRSV: But anyone who strikes another with an iron object, and death ensues, is a murderer; the murderer shall be put to death."
text.Rotherham: If, then, with an instrument of iron, he smote him, and he died, a manslayer, he is,—a manslayer, shall surely die.
text.KJV: And if he smite him with an instrument of iron, so that he die, he is a murderer: the murderer shall surely be put to death.
text.NABRE: Murder and Manslaughter. If someone strikes another with an iron instrument and causes death, that person is a murderer, and the murderer must be put to death.
text.DRB: If any man strike with iron, and he die that was strucken, he shall be guilty of murder, and himself shall die.

text.NRSV: Or anyone who strikes another with a stone in hand that could cause death, and death ensues, is a murderer; the murderer shall be put to death."
text.Rotherham: Or if with a stone thrown by hand, where-by one might die, he smote him and he died, a manslayer, he is,—the man-slayer, shall surely die,
text.KJV: And if he smite him with throwing a stone, wherewith he may die, and he die, he is a murderer: the murderer shall surely be put to death.
text.NABRE: If someone strikes another with a death-dealing stone in the hand and death results, that person is a murderer, and the murderer must be put to death.
text.DRB: If he cast a stone, and he that is strucken die: he shall be punished in like manner.

text.NRSV: Or anyone who strikes another with a weapon of wood in hand that could cause death, and death ensues, is a murderer; the murderer shall be put to death."
text.Rotherham: Or, if with hand-weapon of wood, wherewith one might die, he smote him and he died a manslayer, he is,—the manslayer shall surely die.
text.KJV: Or if he smite him with an hand weapon of wood, wherewith he may die, and he die, he is a murderer: the murderer shall surely be put to death.
text.NABRE: Or if someone strikes another with a death-dealing club in the hand and death results, that person is a murderer, and the murderer must be put to death.
text.DRB: If he that is strucken with wood die, he shall be revenged by the striker's blood.

text.NRSV: The avenger of blood is the one who shall put the murderer to death; when they meet, the avenger of blood shall execute the sentence."
text.Rotherham: The blood-redeemer, he, shall put to death the man-slayer,—when he lighteth upon him, he, shall put him to death.
text.KJV: The revenger of blood himself shall slay the murderer: when he meeteth him, he shall slay him.
text.NABRE: The avenger of blood is the one who will kill the murderer, putting the individual to death on sight.
text.DRB: The kinsman of him that is slain, shall kill the murderer, as soon as he apprehendeth him, he shall kill him.

text.NRSV: Likewise, if someone pushes another from hatred, or hurls something at another, lying in wait, and death ensues,"
text.Rotherham: Or if with hate, he shall thrust at him, or hurl at him designedly, and he have died,
text.KJV: But if he thrust him of hatred, or hurl at him by laying of wait, that he die;
text.NABRE: If someone pushes another out of hatred, or throws something from an ambush, and death results,
text.DRB: If through hatred one push a man, or cast any thing at him by stratagem,

text.NRSV: or in enmity strikes another with the hand, and death ensues, then the one who struck the blow shall be put to death; that person is a murderer; the avenger of blood shall put the murderer to death, when they meet."
text.Rotherham: or if with enmity, he hath smitten him with his hand and he is dead, then shall he that dealt the blow, be surely put to death. a manslayer, he is—the blood-redeemer shall put to death the man-slayer, when he lighteth upon him.
text.KJV: Or in enmity smite him with his hand, that he die: he that smote him shall surely be put to death; for he is a murderer: the revenger of blood shall slay the murderer, when he meeteth him.
text.NABRE: or strikes another with the hand out of enmity and death results, the assailant must be put to death as a murderer. The avenger of blood will kill the murderer on sight.
text.DRB: or being his enemy, strike him with his hand, and he die: the striker shall be guilty of murder. The kinsman of him that is slain as soon as he findeth him, shall kill him.

text.NRSV: But if someone pushes another suddenly without enmity, or hurls any object without lying in wait,"
text.Rotherham: But if suddenly, without enmity, he shall thrust him, or hurl upon him any missile, without design;
text.KJV: But if he thrust him suddenly without enmity, or have cast upon him any thing without laying of wait,
text.NABRE: However, if someone pushes another without malice aforethought, or without lying in ambush throws some object at another,
text.DRB: But if by chance-medley, and without hatred,

text.NRSV: or, while handling any stone that could cause death, unintentionally drops it on another and death ensues, though they were not enemies, and no harm was intended,"
text.Rotherham: Or with any stone whereby one might die without seeing him, and it fall upon him, and he dieth; he, not being at enmity with him, nor seeking to harm him,
text.KJV: Or with any stone, wherewith a man may die, seeing him not, and cast it upon him, that he die, and was not his enemy, neither sought his harm:
text.NABRE: or without seeing drops upon another some death-dealing stone and death results, although there was neither enmity nor malice—
text.DRB: and enmity, he do any of these things,

text.NRSV: then the congregation shall judge between the slayer and the avenger of blood, in accordance with these ordinances;"
text.Rotherham: then shall the assembly judge, between him that smote and the blood-redeemer,—according to these regulations;
text.KJV: Then the congregation shall judge between the slayer and the revenger of blood according to these judgments:
text.NABRE: then the community will judge between the assailant and the avenger of blood in accordance with these norms.
text.DRB: and this be proved in the hearing of the people, and the question debated between him that struck, and the next of blood:

text.NRSV: and the congregation shall rescue the slayer from the avenger of blood. Then the congregation shall send the slayer back to the original city of refuge. The slayer shall live in it until the death of the high priest who was anointed with the holy oil."
text.Rotherham: and the assembly shall rescue the manslayer out of the hand of the blood-redeemer, and the assembly shall restore him unto his city of refuge whither he had fled,—and he shall dwell therein until the death of the high priest, who hath been anointed with the hallowing oil.
text.KJV: And the congregation shall deliver the slayer out of the hand of the revenger of blood, and the congregation shall restore him to the city of his refuge, whither he was fled: and he shall abide in it unto the death of the high priest, which was anointed with the holy oil.
text.NABRE: The community will deliver the homicide from the avenger of blood and the community will return the homicide to the city of asylum where the latter had fled; and the individual will stay there until the death of the high priest who has been anointed with sacred oil.
text.DRB: the innocent shall be delivered from the hand of the revenger, & by sentence shall be brought back into the city, to which he had fled, and he shall tarry there, until the High-priest, that is anointed with the holy oil, do die.

text.NRSV: But if the slayer shall at any time go outside the bounds of the original city of refuge,"
text.Rotherham: But if the man-slayer, go out, of the bounds of his city of refuge, whereinto he hath fled;
text.KJV: But if the slayer shall at any time come without the border of the city of his refuge, whither he was fled;
text.NABRE: If the homicide leaves at all the bounds of the city of asylum to which flight had been made,
text.DRB: If the murderer be found without the limits of the cities, that are deputed to the banished,

text.NRSV: and is found by the avenger of blood outside the bounds of the city of refuge, and is killed by the avenger, no bloodguilt shall be incurred."
text.Rotherham: and the blood-redeemer, find him, outside the bounds of his city of refuge, then may the blood-redeemer slay the slayer, without being guilty of blood;
text.KJV: And the revenger of blood find him without the borders of the city of his refuge, and the revenger of blood kill the slayer; he shall not be guilty of blood:
text.NABRE: and is found by the avenger of blood beyond the bounds of the city of asylum, and the avenger of blood kills the homicide, the avenger incurs no bloodguilt;
text.DRB: and be strucken of him that is the revenger of blood: he shall be guiltless that killed him.

text.NRSV: For the slayer must remain in the city of refuge until the death of the high priest; but after the death of the high priest the slayer may return home."
text.Rotherham: for in his city of refuge, should he have remained, until the death of the high priest,—and, after the death of the high priest, might he have returned into the land which he doth possess.
text.KJV: Because he should have remained in the city of his refuge until the death of the high priest: but after the death of the high priest the slayer shall return into the land of his possession.
text.NABRE: for the homicide was required to stay in the city of asylum until the death of the high priest. Only after the death of the high priest may the homicide return to the land of the homicide’s possession.
text.DRB: For the fugitive ought to have stayed in the city until the death of the High-priest. And after he is dead, then shall the murderer return into his country.

text.NRSV: These things shall be a statute and ordinance for you throughout your generations wherever you live."
text.Rotherham: So shall these serve you as a regulative statute unto your generations,—wheresoever ye may dwell.
text.KJV: So these things shall be for a statute of judgment unto you throughout your generations in all your dwellings.
text.NABRE: This is the statute for you throughout all your generations, wherever you live, for rendering judgment.
text.DRB: These things shall be perpetual, and for an ordinance in all your habitations.

text.NRSV: If anyone kills another, the murderer shall be put to death on the evidence of witnesses; but no one shall be put to death on the testimony of a single witness."
text.Rotherham: Whosoever taketh away life, at the mouth of witnesses, shall, the slayer be slain,—but, one witness, shall not testify against a person, to put him to death.
text.KJV: Whoso killeth any person, the murderer shall be put to death by the mouth of witnesses: but one witness shall not testify against any person to cause him to die.
text.NABRE: Judgment. Whenever someone kills another, the evidence of witnesses is required to kill the murderer. A single witness does not suffice for putting a person to death.
text.DRB: The murderer shall be punished by witnesses: none shall be condemned, at the testimony of one man.

text.NRSV: Moreover you shall accept no ransom for the life of a murderer who is subject to the death penalty; a murderer must be put to death."
text.Rotherham: And ye shall accept no ransom for the life of him that slayeth, if he have unlawfully caused death,—but he, must surely be put to death.
text.KJV: Moreover ye shall take no satisfaction for the life of a murderer, which is guilty of death: but he shall be surely put to death.
text.NABRE: No Indemnity. You will not accept compensation in place of the life of a murderer who deserves to die, but that person must be put to death.
text.DRB: You shall not take a price of him, that is guilty of blood, he also shall die forthwith.

text.NRSV: Nor shall you accept ransom for one who has fled to a city of refuge, allowing the fugitive to return to live in the land before the death of the high priest."
text.Rotherham: And ye shall accept no ransom for him that hath fled to his city of refuge, if he should return to dwell in the land before the death of the priest.
text.KJV: And ye shall take no satisfaction for him that is fled to the city of his refuge, that he should come again to dwell in the land, until the death of the priest.
text.NABRE: Nor will you accept compensation to allow one who has fled to a city of asylum to return to live in the land before the death of the high priest.
text.DRB: The banished and fugitives before the death of the High-Priest may by no means return into their own cities.

text.NRSV: You shall not pollute the land in which you live; for blood pollutes the land, and no expiation can be made for the land, for the blood that is shed in it, except by the blood of the one who shed it."
text.Rotherham: So shall ye not pollute the land wherein ye are, for blood, doth pollute the land, and for the land, no propitiatory-covering can be made, as touching blood, that is shed therein, save with the blood of him that shed it;
text.KJV: So ye shall not pollute the land wherein ye are: for blood it defileth the land: and the land cannot be cleansed of the blood that is shed therein, but by the blood of him that shed it.
text.NABRE: You will not pollute the land where you live. For bloodshed pollutes the land, and the land can have no expiation for the blood shed on it except through the blood of the one who shed it.
text.DRB: Pollute not the land of your habitation, which is polluted with the blood of innocents: neither can it otherwise be expiated, but by his blood, that sheddeth another man's blood.

text.NRSV: You shall not defile the land in which you live, in which I also dwell; for I the Lord dwell among the Israelites.
text.Rotherham: thou must not then make unclean the land wherein, ye, are dwelling, in the midst of which, I, have my habitation; for, I,—Yahweh, am making my habitation in the midst of the sons of Israel.
text.KJV: Defile not therefore the land which ye shall inhabit, wherein I dwell: for I the Lord dwell among the children of Israel.
text.NABRE: Do not defile the land in which you live and in the midst of which I dwell; for I the Lord dwell in the midst of the Israelites.
text.DRB: And so shall your possession be cleansed myself abiding with you. For I am the Lord that dwell among the children of Israel.

text.NRSV: The heads of the ancestral houses of the clans of the descendants of Gilead son of Machir son of Manasseh, of the Josephite clans, came forward and spoke in the presence of Moses and the leaders, the heads of the ancestral houses of the Israelites;"
text.Rotherham: Then came near the heads of fathers, belonging to the family of the sons of Gilead son of Machir son of Manasseh, out of the families of the sons of Joseph,—and spake before Moses, and before the princes, heads of fathers belonging to the sons of Israel;
text.KJV: And the chief fathers of the families of the children of Gilead, the son of Machir, the son of Manasseh, of the families of the sons of Joseph, came near, and spake before Moses, and before the princes, the chief fathers of the children of Israel:
text.NABRE: The heads of the ancestral houses in a clan of the descendants of Gilead, son of Machir, son of Manasseh—one of the Josephite clans—came up and spoke before Moses and Eleazar the priest and before the leaders who were the heads of the ancestral houses of the Israelites.
text.DRB: And the Princes also of the families of Galaad, the son of Machir, the son of Manasses of the stock of the children of Joseph came, and spake to Moyses before the Princes of Israel, and said:

text.NRSV: they said, "The Lord commanded my lord to give the land for inheritance by lot to the Israelites; and my lord was commanded by the Lord to give the inheritance of our brother Zelophehad to his daughters."
text.Rotherham: and they said—Unto my lord, did Yahweh give command, to bestow the land as an inheritance, by lot, unto the sons of Israel,—my lord, therefore, was commanded by Yahweh, to give the inheritance of Zelophehad our brother unto his daughters.
text.KJV: And they said, The Lord commanded my lord to give the land for an inheritance by lot to the children of Israel: and my lord was commanded by the Lord to give the inheritance of Zelophehad our brother unto his daughters.
text.NABRE: They said: “The Lord commanded my lord to apportion the land by lot for a heritage among the Israelites; and my lord was commanded by the Lord to give the heritage of Zelophehad our kinsman to his daughters.
text.DRB: Our Lord hath commanded thee our lord that thou shouldest by lot divide the Land to the children of Israel, and that to the daughters of Salphaad our brother thou shouldest give the possession due to their father:

text.NRSV: But if they are married into another Israelite tribe, then their inheritance will be taken from the inheritance of our ancestors and added to the inheritance of the tribe into which they marry; so it will be taken away from the allotted portion of ours."
text.Rotherham: When, therefore, unto any one from among the sons of the other tribes of the sons of Israel, they become wives their inheritance shall disappear out of the inheritance of our fathers, and be added to the inheritance of the tribe to which they shall be received,—and so out of the lot of our inheritance, shall it disappear,
text.KJV: And if they be married to any of the sons of the other tribes of the children of Israel, then shall their inheritance be taken from the inheritance of our fathers, and shall be put to the inheritance of the tribe whereunto they are received: so shall it be taken from the lot of our inheritance.
text.NABRE: But if they marry into one of the other Israelite tribes, their heritage will be withdrawn from our ancestral heritage and will be added to that of the tribe into which they marry; thus the heritage that fell to us by lot will be diminished.
text.DRB: Whom if men of another tribe take to wives, their possession shall follow, and being translated to another tribe, it shall be a diminishing of our inheritance.

text.NRSV: When the jubilee of the Israelites comes, then their inheritance will be added to the inheritance of the tribe into which they have married; and their inheritance will be taken from the inheritance of our ancestral tribe."
text.Rotherham: And when the jubilee shall come to the sons of Israel, then shall their inheritance be added unto the inheritance of the tribe to which they shall be received—and, out of the, inheritance of the tribe of our fathers, shall their inheritance disappear.
text.KJV: And when the jubile of the children of Israel shall be, then shall their inheritance be put unto the inheritance of the tribe whereunto they are received: so shall their inheritance be taken away from the inheritance of the tribe of our fathers.
text.NABRE: When the Israelites celebrate the jubilee year, the heritage of these women will be added to that of the tribe into which they marry and their heritage will be withdrawn from that of our ancestral tribe.”
text.DRB: And so it shall come to pass, that when the Jubilee, that is the fiftieth year of remission is come, the distribution of the lots shall be confounded, and the possession of one shall pass to others.

text.NRSV: Then Moses commanded the Israelites according to the word of the Lord, saying, "The descendants of the tribe of Joseph are right in what they are saying."
text.Rotherham: Then Moses commanded the sons of Israel, at the bidding of Yahweh saying,—A right thing, are, the tribe of the sons of Joseph, speaking,
text.KJV: And Moses commanded the children of Israel according to the word of the Lord, saying, The tribe of the sons of Joseph hath said well.
text.NABRE: So Moses commanded the Israelites at the direction of the Lord: “The tribe of the Josephites are right in what they say.
text.DRB: Moyses answered the children of Israel, and as our Lord commanded, said: The tribe of the children of Joseph hath spoken rightly.

text.NRSV: This is what the Lord commands concerning the daughters of Zelophehad, 'Let them marry whom they think best; only it must be into a clan of their father's tribe that they are married,"
text.Rotherham: This, is the thing which Yahweh hath commanded, as to the daughters of Zelophehad saying: Unto whomsoever it may be good in their eyes, let them become wives,—save only unto the family of the tribe of their father, let them become wives.
text.KJV: This is the thing which the Lord doth command concerning the daughters of Zelophehad, saying, Let them marry to whom they think best; only to the family of the tribe of their father shall they marry.
text.NABRE: This is what the Lord commands with regard to the daughters of Zelophehad: They may marry anyone they please, provided they marry into a clan of their ancestral tribe,
text.DRB: And this law is promulgated of our Lord touching the daughters of Salphaad: Let them marry to whom they will, only that it be to the men of their own tribe:

text.NRSV: so that no inheritance of the Israelites shall be transferred from one tribe to another; for all Israelites shall retain the inheritance of their ancestral tribes."
text.Rotherham: So shall no inheritance belonging to the sons of Israel go round from tribe to tribe,—for the sons of Israel shall, each one, cleave, unto the inheritance of the tribe of his fathers,
text.KJV: So shall not the inheritance of the children of Israel remove from tribe to tribe: for every one of the children of Israel shall keep himself to the inheritance of the tribe of his fathers.
text.NABRE: so that no heritage of the Israelites will pass from one tribe to another, but all the Israelites will retain their own ancestral heritage.
text.DRB: lest the possession of the children of Israel be mingled from tribe into tribe. For all men shall marry wives of their own tribe and kindred:

text.NRSV: Every daughter who possesses an inheritance in any tribe of the Israelites shall marry one from the clan of her father's tribe, so that all Israelites may continue to possess their ancestral inheritance."
text.Rotherham: And, every daughter possessing all inheritance from among the tribes of the sons of Israel, unto one of the family of the tribe of her father, shall become wife,—to the intent that the sons of Israel may possess each one the inheritance of his fathers;
text.KJV: And every daughter, that possesseth an inheritance in any tribe of the children of Israel, shall be wife unto one of the family of the tribe of her father, that the children of Israel may enjoy every man the inheritance of his fathers.
text.NABRE: Every daughter who inherits property in any of the Israelite tribes will marry someone belonging to a clan of her own ancestral tribe, in order that all the Israelites may remain in possession of their own ancestral heritage.
text.DRB: and all women shall take husbands of the same tribe: that the inheritance may remain in the families,

text.NRSV: No inheritance shall be transferred from one tribe to another; for each of the tribes of the Israelites shall retain its own inheritance.'"
text.Rotherham: and no inheritance go round from one tribe to another tribe,—for the tribes of the sons of Israel shall cleave, each one unto his own inheritance.
text.KJV: Neither shall the inheritance remove from one tribe to another tribe; but every one of the tribes of the children of Israel shall keep himself to his own inheritance.
text.NABRE: Thus, no heritage will pass from one tribe to another, but all the Israelite tribes will retain their own ancestral heritage.”
text.DRB: and that the tribes be not mingled among themselves, but remain so

text.NRSV: The daughters of Zelophehad did as the Lord had commanded Moses."
text.Rotherham: As Yahweh commanded Moses, so, did the daughters of Zelophehad.
text.KJV: Even as the Lord commanded Moses, so did the daughters of Zelophehad:
text.NABRE: The daughters of Zelophehad did exactly as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.DRB: as they were separated by our Lord. And the daughters of Salphaad did as it had been commanded:

text.NRSV: Mahlah, Tirzah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Noah, the daughters of Zelophehad, married sons of their father's brothers."
text.Rotherham: Thus then did Mahlah, Tirzah, and Hoglah, and Milchah, and Noah, the daughters of Zelophehad, become wives, unto the sons of their father’s brethren.
text.KJV: For Mahlah, Tirzah, and Hoglah, and Milcah, and Noah, the daughters of Zelophehad, were married unto their father’s brothers’ sons:
text.NABRE: Mahlah, Tirzah, Hoglah, Milcah and Noah, Zelophehad’s daughters, married sons of their uncles on their father’s side.
text.DRB: and Maala, and Thersa, and Hegla, and Melcha, and Noa were married to the sons of their uncle by their father,

text.NRSV: They were married into the clans of the descendants of Manasseh son of Joseph, and their inheritance remained in the tribe of their father's clan."
text.Rotherham: Unto men from among the families of the son of Manasseh son of Joseph, became they wives,—and so their inheritance remained with the tribe of the family of their father.
text.KJV: And they were married into the families of the sons of Manasseh the son of Joseph, and their inheritance remained in the tribe of the family of their father.
text.NABRE: They married within the clans of the descendants of Manasseh, son of Joseph; hence their heritage remained in the tribe of their father’s clan.
text.DRB: of the family of Manasses, who was the son of Joseph: and the possession, that had been allotted to them, remained in the tribe and family of their father.

text.NRSV: These are the commandments and the ordinances that the Lord commanded the Israelites through Moses in the plains of Moab by the Jordan at Jericho.
text.Rotherham: These are the commandments and the regulations which Yahweh gave in charge, by the hand of Moses, unto the sons of Israel,—in the waste plains of Moab, by Jordan near Jericho.
text.KJV: These are the commandments and the judgments, which the Lord commanded by the hand of Moses unto the children of Israel in the plains of Moab by Jordan near Jericho.
text.NABRE: Conclusion. These are the commandments and decisions which the Lord commanded the Israelites through Moses, on the plains of Moab beside the Jordan opposite Jericho.
text.DRB: These are the commandments and judgments, which our Lord commanded by the hand of Moyses to the children of Israel, in the champion countries of Moab upon Jordan against Jericho.

16.36: Chapter 17 The Lord said to Moses:
16.37: Tell Eleazar, son of Aaron the priest, to remove the censers from the embers; and scatter the fire some distance away, for they have become holy—
16.38: the censers of those who sinned at the cost of their lives. Have them hammered into plates to cover the altar, because in being presented before the Lord they have become holy. In this way they shall serve as a sign to the Israelites.
16.39: So taking the bronze censers which had been presented by those who were burned, Eleazar the priest had them hammered into a covering for the altar,
16.40: just as the Lord had directed him through Moses. This was to be a reminder to the Israelites that no unauthorized person, no one who was not a descendant of Aaron, should draw near to offer incense before the Lord, lest he meet the fate of Korah and his faction.
16.41: The next day the whole Israelite community grumbled against Moses and Aaron, saying, “You have killed the people of the Lord.”
16.42: But while the community was assembling against them, Moses and Aaron turned toward the tent of meeting, and the cloud now covered it and the glory of the Lord appeared.
16.43: Then Moses and Aaron came to the front of the tent of meeting,
16.44: and the Lord said to Moses:
16.45: Remove yourselves from this community, that I may consume them at once. But they fell prostrate.
16.46: Then Moses said to Aaron, “Take your censer, put fire from the altar in it, lay incense on it, and bring it quickly to the community to make atonement for them; for wrath has come forth from the Lord and the plague has begun.”
16.47: Aaron took his censer just as Moses directed and ran in among the assembly, where the plague had already begun among the people. Then he offered the incense and made atonement for the people,
16.48: while standing there between the living and the dead. And so the scourge was checked.
16.49: There were fourteen thousand seven hundred dead from the scourge, in addition to those who died because of Korah.
16.50: When the scourge had been checked, Aaron returned to Moses at the entrance of the tent of meeting.

29.40: Chapter 30 So Moses instructed the Israelites exactly as the Lord had commanded him.
